PAGE: Admiral Taidha Covington
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Admiral_Taidha_Covington
======================================================================

This article is about the world boss.  For the event schedule, see
Kill Admiral Taidha Covington
.
Admiral Taidha Covington
Race
Human
Level
50
Rank
Legendary
Location
Laughing Gull Island
(
Bloodtide Coast
)
Organization
Covington Pirates
Pirates
“
After you meet Grenth, be sure to tell him the Hydra Queen sent you!
—
Admiral Taidha Covington
Admiral Taidha Covington
, also known as the Hydra Queen, is the infamous leader of the
Covington Pirates
and can be found on
Laughing Gull Island
. She leads a
pirate
alliance consisting of various crews which oppose the
Captain's Council
and the
Lionguard
. Killing her is the final step of the area's
meta event
,
The Campaign Against Taidha Covington
. The Hydra Queen shows reverence towards
Grenth
, the
God
of Death, invoking his name when in combat.
Biography
Admiral Taidha Covington of the
Covington Pirates
was once a member of the
Captain's Council
of
Lion's Arch
but eventually left due to disagreements.
She
captured
the
Capricorn
, a
magical
ship, from
Captain Peter the Lost
but could not figure out how to tell the ship to weigh anchor. She had to abandon the ship as a result, leaving the
Capricorn
to sail the seas as an elusive ghost ship.
Taidha fortified her base on
Laughing Gull Island
atop the ruins of
Port Noble
and persuaded or forced several pirate crews to work for her, becoming a noteworthy rival to the ships sailing under the flags of the Captain's Council. Her pirate alliance began harassing locals in
Bloodtide Coast
and beyond, leading to a war between her crews and the
Lionguard
.
The Hydra Queen's reign of terror was contested in 1325
AE
when the
Order of Whispers
began a campaign to eliminate her and replace her with one of her bribed allies,
Captain Penzan
.
While Whispers agents hampered the crews of the pirate alliance in the region, the Lionguard and various adventurers besieged
Covington Keep
from land and sea and
broke through the keep's sturdy gate
. Ever defiant, Taidha confronted the assaulters and
fought
them head on before she was forced to retreat deeper into the base, proving herself to be a skilled combatant throughout the assault.
The Hydra Queen ultimately lost her life atop her keep, and her last words invoked
Grenth
, the
God
of Death, with the desire to meet him in the
Underworld
. Following Taidha's demise, the keep was overrun by
Risen
until these
dragon minions
were pushed back as well, and the Lionguard established a foothold on the island.
Location
Kryta
Bloodtide Coast
Laughing Gull Island
Covington Keep

[Group Event]

Notes
Killing Taidha Covington will reward the
achievement
Kill the Hydra Queen
. Prior to the update of
4/15/2014
it was difficult to accomplish due to bugs and particular battle mechanics.
She drops the
pirate
variant of the
Embroidered Coin Purse
.
In the past,
players
would only receive credit for the
Kill the Hydra Queen
achievement if
Lionscout Jenny
was alive when the Admiral died.

Aetherblade Foreman Spur
Race
Charr
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Depth of Despair
(
Twilight Arbor
)
Organization
Aetherblades
Aetherblade Foreman Spur
is a powerful, high-ranking
charr
member of the
Aetherblades
who oversees the
Aetherblade Shipyard
in
Scarlet's
factory beneath
Twilight Arbor
. He's the second boss encountered in
Aetherpath
of
Twilight Arbor (explorable)
and is initially surrounded by several large groups of Aetherblades who must be dealt with beforehand.
In combat, he dual-wields
axes
and rapidly deals high Point Blank
AoE
damage while possessing multiple methods of inflicting
bleeding
,
crippled
, and
vulnerability
. His unique skills include creating small, high-damage AoE fields; creating a wide, impassable PBAoE ring; and performing a PBAoE whirling attack that
pulls
and
launches
.
Locations
Maguuma Jungle
Caledon Forest
Twilight Arbor (explorable)
Depth of Despair
Aetherblade Shipyard
Combat abilities
Abilities
Throws Axes
Spins
Behavior
Foreman spur raises his arms briefly and then performs a point-blank area of effect
Pull
, immediately followed by a
channeled
whirling attack similar to
Whirling Axe
that lasts for 3 seconds. While spinning, he'll
reflect
hostile projectiles and
charge
toward a random direction, dragging any enemies that were pulled along his path. As he travels, he'll also deal point-blank area of effect damage while inflicting
cripple
,
Vulnerability
, and
Bleeding
multiple times per second. When the whirlwind is finished, it causes a point-blank area of effect
Launch
at his location.
Foreman spur creates a wide radius, point-blank area of effect ring of sound that enemies cannot cross, similar to
Ring of Warding
. The ring
knocks down
on contact, but it deals no damage and the foreman will be unable to perform his channeled whirling attack while it's active.
Foreman spur continuously gains 5 seconds of
Fury
and/or
Swiftness
every 1-3 seconds throughout the encounter, each
boon
stacks up to a 30 second duration.
Effects
Skills
Dual Strike
- Melee
auto-attack
that hits twice,
crippling
for 3 seconds with each hit.
Cyclone Axe
- Frequently used point-blank
area of effect
attack that hits twice, inflicting
Vulnerability
for 4 seconds with each hit.
Throw Axe
- Foreman spur throws 5
projectile
axes above his head that fly out in random directions and createsmall, high-damage area of effect fields where they land. These fields pulse every second for 15 seconds; inflicting
cripple
for 3 seconds,
Bleeding
for 5 seconds, and 5 stacks of
Vulnerability
for 8 seconds with each pulse.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ai, Keeper of the Peak
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Ai,_Keeper_of_the_Peak
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Sunqua Peak Fractal
.
In this
Canthan
name, the
family name
is Li.
Ai, Keeper of the Peak
Race
Human
Profession
Elementalist
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Sunqua Peak Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Image(s)
Click to enlarge.
“
My powers can really take over when I'm upset.
—
Ai, Keeper of the Peak
Li Ai, Keeper of the Peak
is the
challenge mote
version of
Sorrowful Spellcaster
who awaits at the summit of the
Sunqua Peak Fractal
.
Biography
Early years
Li Ai lived on
Shing Jea Island
in
Cantha
during
Emperor Usoku
's reign. She first met her husband-to-be,
Li Bo
, when he found her painting at the
Panjiang Peninsula
in spring. Bo interrupted Ai while she worked, causing her to ruin the painting which in turn infuriated her. Despite the bad first impression, Ai eventually warmed up to Bo and began a relationship with him. Although Bo had planned to take Ai back to Panjiang to propose to her years later, it was Ai who proposed to him first. Seven years after the proposal, Ai gave birth to their daughter
Li Lan
whom Bo loved with all his heart.
When Emperor Usoku began the unification of Cantha circa 1637
CC
(1127
AE
), Bo, along with several other Shing Jea clansmen, was called to serve in the imperial army and join the emperor's forces on the mainland for the upcoming offensive against the
Kurzicks
of
the Echovald Forest
.
Bo often sent letters home and described his experiences in the army to his wife and daughter while training both his combat and music playing skills as well as befriending fellow soldiers such as the unusually tall and strong woman Hyo-Sonn.
After witnessing the horrific acts committed by Usoku and the
Ministry of Purity
during their campaign to unite Cantha, Bo and Hyo-Sonn defected from the army and fled back to Shing Jea.
Bo eventually perished at the age of 32, however, leaving his family in Hyo-Sonn's care. His last letter was addressed to both Ai and Lan, and he professed his love for them while encouraging them to keep on living.
Two years after Bo's death, Lan passed away at the age of 12.
It is not clear whether Ai tried to save Lan but failed, or if Ai's
magic
was unleashed during her grieving and she accidentally ended up killing her daughter. However, the deaths of her family left a lasting mark on Ai whose grief would only increase as she began blaming herself for Lan's death.
Sunqua Peak Fractal
Unbeknownst to Ai, however, Lan continued existing as a
ghost
and observed Sunqua Peak where her mother Ai, who was wracked with grief, was meditating. Ai's inner turmoil attracted
demons
to her who feasted on her doubt, fears, guilt, and sorrow, and her elemental magic deeply disturbed the natural and spiritual balance on the mountain as a result. Scared to confront her unhinged mother, Lan disguised herself as the deer-like
Spirit of the Mountain
and sought aid from travelers to save her mother and the mountain.
By the time
Dessa
and her team of
fractal
explorers ended up in
Sunqua Peak Fractal
which depicted a distorted view of the historical event, they encountered Lan, disguised as the Spirit of the Mountain, who asked them to help her restore harmony to the mountain. The spirit's rather casual tone and mispronounced words made Dessa suspicious, but she nevertheless suggested that the fractal explorers do as the spirit asked.
By the time the party reached the mountain's summit, they discovered Ai as the
Sorrowful Spellcaster
meditating in grief. Lan was hesitant to approach her mother after witnessing how much in turmoil Ai really was, and she believed that her presence would only agitate Ai more. Lan decided to drop the act of posing as an enlightened spirit by turning to more casual talk although she still refused to reveal her true form. Dessa began putting the pieces of the mystery together after realizing how much the spirit cared for Ai, but decided to see the quest to the end.
Ai lashed out at the explorers, however, first telling them to leave her alone with her pain and then threatening them before attacking them. Although the explorers were able to quell the elementalist's rage after a clash, they soon learned the true source of the elemental imbalance as Ai's
doubt
,
fears
,
guilt
, and
sorrow
manifested as demons which took her over and attacked the party. After a fierce battle, the explorers were able to weaken the demons enough that Ai managed to drive them out, thus calming down the weather around the summit.
Once the explorers had collected letters written by the late Bo to his family and delivered the letters to Ai for closure, the disguised Lan finally approached her mother to tell her that she was safe. By the time the spirit began praising Lan for her qualities, Ai realized that the spirit was none other than Lan herself, forcing Lan to reveal her true form.
When Ai questioned why her daughter had hidden herself, Lan responded that she had been scared of her mother's magical outburst and had been unsure how to approach her until the explorers had assisted her. Ai apologized to her daughter about what her unleashed powers had done and how she felt responsible for Lan's death, and admitted to missing her and feeling alone. Lan comforted her grieving mother, stating that Ai would never be alone.
Location
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Sunqua Peak Fractal

Trivia
"Ai" has different meanings, depending on the character it is written with:
哀 happens to be a Japanese first name (read in On-Yomi as Ai), which roughly translates as pathetic, grief, sorrow, pity, but can also mean to sympathize.
愛 on the other hand, means "love".
Interestingly, both characters have almost the same reading in Japanese and Chinese, which makes it unclear what the origin of the name is. However, the surname "Li" implies it is Chinese.

CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Albax
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Albax
Race
Dragon
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Longfeather Plaza
(
Arborstone
)
Jade Promenade
(
Dragon's End
)
Organization
Saltspray dragons
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
You've not seen what I've seen... Not lost what I've lost. To see that there still may be good fortune in the world, and to see a future for my kind... That is enough to bring an old dragon to tears.
— Albax
Albax
is one of the last remaining
saltspray dragons
. He can be found in his
roost
in
Dragon's End
after the
Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
has been
successfully subdued
. Once his
riddles
have been solved, he joins
Kuunavang
in
Arborstone
.
Biography
Early years
Albax is an ancient
saltspray dragon
who claims to have seen the rise and fall of many empires over the ages.
Sometime prior to the
Jade Wind
, Albax spoke with
Kuunavang
, and began to look up to the dragon.
Guild Wars Factions
In 1382
CC
(872
AE
),
Shiro Tagachi
caused the Jade Wind, corrupting most of Albax's fellow saltspray dragons but for some reason sparing him, much to the saltspray's dismay.
Sometime around the mid-15th century
CC
, the
Luxon
ranger
Maxeles
and his crew mistook Albax for a drake and attacked him, taking one of his scales. The group of Luxons soon suffered bad luck, with the majority of them wiped out by
Kurzicks
. Coming to be known as Infortunatos Maxeles and realizing in hindsight Albax's identity, the ranger begged the
Hero
of
Cantha
to find Albax and return the necklace he had made from the saltspray's scale, explaining that he believed that Albax was roaming the
Silent Surf
. When the hero reached Albax, the dragon explained his history and thanked them for returning his scale. Inspired by the hero's courage, he chose to lift his curse upon Maxeles.
Interim years
Following the
Rising of Orr
, Albax observed as the saltspray dragon
Kuunavang
built a relationship with the
Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
to help the people of
Cantha
survive and recover, but did not have the courage or energy to join them.
End of Dragons
After watching his race become corrupted again, this time by the
Void
, Albax thought he had lost his luck, and fearing the worst, hid from the Void in 1845
CC
(1335
AE
). Following the
subjugation
of
Soo-Won
and the defeat of the Void, he regained hope, and tasked the
Pact Commander
with solving his riddles to prove that luck still existed in the
world
.
Once the Commander had gathered various tokens of good luck around the world based on the dragon's clues, Albax decided to travel to
Arborstone
where he was reunited with Kuunavang. The two dragons' meeting was bittersweet as they understood that they had lost many of their kind over the years, including
Ishan
, but they also resolved to seek out other dragons if any still existed out there. During the following conversations, Albax thanked the Commander for restoring hope in his heart and admitted to Kuunavang that the fear of losing himself to corruption had made him keep his distance initially. Both the Commander and Kuunavang comforted Albax, however, as they were happy that he no longer needed to live alone.
Locations
Cantha
Dragon's End
Jade Promenade
Saltspray Roost
(after successfully completing
The Battle for the Jade Sea
and before completing
Albax, the Unlucky
)
Arborstone
Longfeather Plaza
(after completing
Albax, the Unlucky
)

Abilities
The Lucky
Effects

— Vulnerable while broken.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Dialogue
See also:
Albax, the Unlucky#Dialogue

Arborstone
(after completing
Albax, the Unlucky
)
Albax:
All of these items you've found represent the luck of many different cultures. To see that there is still good will, and the luck that brings, in the
world
...
I didn't take you for a sentimental
dragon
.
You've not seen what I've seen... Not lost what I've lost. To see that there still may be good fortune in the world, and to see a future for my kind... That is enough to bring an old dragon to tears.
I'm happy I could help. What's next for you?
Kuunavang
and I have a lot of catching up to do. After that, there are others like us out there. Maybe I can find the strength to find them.
Thank you, <Character name>. I am lucky to have met you.
I hope you and Kuunavang enjoy each other's company.
Kuunavang:
We do. Thanks to you.
See you around.
Likewise, Albax.
Be well, Albax.
It's a lot, isn't it?
Ambient dialogue
In
Arborstone
(after completing
Albax, the Unlucky
)
Albax:
So many gone. But we're still here. How many others do you suppose are left?
Kuunavang:
I'd like to find out.
Albax:
So this is where
Ishan
fell
?

(sigh) Yes... defending mortals until the

took him...

Albax:
Your colors...they've changed.
Kuunavang
Mmm. As my bond deepened with
Soo-Won
, my colors followed. It's all I have left of her now.

Aldus Stormbringer
Race
Icebrood
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Palace of Gathering
(
Honor of the Waves
)
Organization
Sons of Svanir
Aldus Stormbringer
is a powerful
Son of Svanir
located in
Honor of the Waves
that serves as a boss in the butcher path of
explorable mode
. He's seen rallying a group of Sons of Svanir, encouraging them to embrace their rage and blood lust.
Location
Shiverpeak Mountains
Frostgorge Sound
Honor of the Waves
Palace of Gathering
Combat abilities
Abilities
Uses frost magic
Behavior
Aldus summons a randomized group of six corrupted Sons of Svanir, including:
Icebrood Berserker
Icebrood Claymore
Icebrood Hunter
Icebrood Seer
Icebrood Mauler
At intervals of 25% health, Aldus lets out a battle roar, rapidly gaining 40 stacks of
Shield of Jormag
over 2 seconds. The shield
reflects
projectiles and
blocks
melee attacks until all of its stacks are removed. Each time a melee attack is blocked, the shield loses 1 stack.
Effects
Shield of Jormag
- Immune to damage. Reflects projectiles. Attacks remove shield.
Skills
Frostbolt
-
Projectile
auto-attack
.
Frostbolt Volley
- Fires a projectile at every enemy in the room in a frontal
area of effect
.
Frozen Ground
- Aldus thrusts his staff into the ground, creating a large area of effect field of ice at a target location. The ice persists for 10 seconds and pulses every second, dealing high damage and
chilling
for 5 seconds with each pulse.

======================================================================
PAGE: Archon Iberu
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Archon_Iberu
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
Archon Iberu
Race
Awakened
,
Human
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Heretic's Arena
(
Domain of Istan
)
Organization
Mordant Crescent
Image(s)
Iberu as an Awakened.
“
It's unwise to intervene in our affairs, outlander.
—
Archon Iberu
Archon Iberu
is a high-ranking officer in the
Mordant Crescent
. In the past, he had opposed
Palawa Joko
, but was eventually converted to serve him. Iberu rose to the high rank of
Archon
while still
human
and has taken to converting
Elonians
, who have fled north to locations such as
Amnoon
, into following Joko.
Biography
Early years
Iberu, like many other
Mordant Crescent
, was originally a member of the
Order of the Sunspears
before he was converted to
Palawa Joko
's cause. Once he had pledged allegiance to Joko, he hunted his former Sunspears in the lich king's name, using diplomacy, bribes, threats and outright violence where needed. Iberu eventually became an
Archon
, a high-ranking officer in Joko's military, giving orders to
Awakened
marshals and expecting Joko to eventually honor him with Awakening for his services.
As Archon, Iberu enjoyed many privileges such as having his personal dwelling, the
Archon's Retreat
, at the
Fortress of Jahai
. He discreetly took a nibble of every royal tribute for his own use via extortion, usury, bribery, and blackmail, and stored
his treasure
in the labyrinthine
Inner Keep
.
Path of Fire
Iberu extended the
Free City of Amnoon
an offer of Awakened aid against their plights of the
Branded
and
Forged
incursions in 1330
AE
. As part of his attempts to convince
Amnoon's City Council
to accept Joko's aid, he put pressure on
Zalambur
's trade and casino business so that the rich businessman would in turn pressure the councilors,
one of whom
had already been bribed to support Joko's cause. After learning that Zalambur had been working behind his back to stir the people of Amnoon against Joko, Iberu visited Zalambur and killed the businessman's bodyguards before unleashing his Awakened escort on the guests at the casino. However, the
Pact Commander
, who had come to visit Zalambur as well, thwarted the massacre but was unable to stop the Archon himself from leaving the city unscathed.
When the Commander and other members of
Dragon's Watch
worked to trick Palawa Joko's military leaders into gathering an army which they intended to use to thwart the Forged, they went after Iberu whose high standing in the military was a key to the success of their daring plan. They lured the elusive Archon back south of
the Bone Wall
by faking a Sunspear uprising in Joko's domain. As Iberu made his way towards
the Bone Palace
to quell the would-be insurrection, Dragon's Watch ambushed him and revealed the truth of the fake rebellion, angering the Archon who retaliated by attacking them.
Despite fighting ferociously and attempting to siphon the lifeforce of his Awakened reinforcements to overcome his enemies, Iberu was ultimately slain. With help from
Kasmeer Meade
, the Commander ended up under the effect of a
mesmer
glamour to pose as the Archon. Using Iberu's authority granted by the illusion, the Commander convinced Joko's marshals to rally their Awakened forces for the
defense
of
Kodash Bazaar
against the approaching Forged army which intended to get to the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
.
Living World Season 4
Iberu's corpse was eventually found and Awakened, and the Archon traveled to the
Domain of Istan
where he oversaw the defenses of
Palawadan, Jewel of Istan
. He faced a group of adventurers and rebelling Sunspears during the
raid on Palawadan
and was felled once again after a fierce clash against the Sunspears and their allies in the
Heretic's Arena
.
Iberu was revived once again, however, and returned to the Elonian mainland after Palawa Joko's demise in the
Battle of Gandara
. He eventually traveled to the Fortress of Jahai in the
Jahai Bluffs
to attend a political summit, where various factions debated Elona's future, and was even willing to consider accepting
Spearmarshal Zaeim
's proposed treaty until
Aurene
, the
dragon
who had devoured Joko, suddenly appeared in the fortress much to everyone's surprise.
Angered by seeing the dragon which he believed the Pact Commander had sent to intimidate him and the delegates, Iberu changed plans and sent word for his reinforcements. The Archon met with the Commander, who had arrived at the fort to calm down Aurene, and pretended to be diplomatic and willing to negotiate while he was actually waiting for his troops to arrive so he could ambush the Commander and the rest of the delegates. By the time the people attending the summit realized what Iberu was up to, the Archon's forces moved in while he used his magic to stop the Commander from acting and accused the Commander and the rest of the delegates of being a threat to Elona. Iberu's ambush was cut short, however, by the sudden arrival of
Awakened Koss Dejarin
, who stopped the Archon's plans by killing him with a single blow.
Location
Crystal Desert
Domain of Istan
Heretic's Arena
Crucible of the Apostate
(As Awakened Archon Iberu, during event only)
Story involvement

======================================================================
PAGE: Arina
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Arina
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Arina
Race
Norn
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Memory's Hollow
(
Inner Nayos
)
Mantle's Arrival
(
Bava Nisos
)
Organization
Astral Ward
Image(s)
Arina without her helmet.
Interactive map
Outrider Arina Copperwolf
is a
norn
from the
Astral Ward
sworn to the
Bastion of the Obscure
.
She is the daughter of
Ragna Copperwolf
and
Frode
, the
Warden
of the Ward.
Biography
Early years
Arina Copperwolf was born on the third resting day of winter to
Ragna Copperwolf
and
Frode Wurmsbearer
some time after 1309
AE
.
She was also gifted a handwoven doll from
Gaerta Whitebear
.
Later, when Arina was still very young and training with the elders, her father Frode, a shaman of
Wurm
, lost faith in the
Spirits of the Wild
after witnessing harrowing events involving his friend
Sejm
in the north and left the homestead and family to be on his own as he could no longer look them in the eye and believed that Arina would not miss him. She was brought to the
Astral Ward
by
Mabon
of the
Wizard's Court
after she had been looking for her father.
When Arina joined, Frode had already been a member of the Ward for at least a decade, and the two reunited in a rare occasion as not many members were able to keep in touch with their loved ones from their life before joining.
Secrets of the Obscure
Act 1
In 1336
AE
, Arina got lost in the
demonic
realm of
Nayos
after being pulled there by a
Kryptis Rift
while she was leading a mission to contain the
Kryptis
in
Gendarran Fields
. She was also instructed to avoid any interaction with the
Pact Commander
, who was reported to become likely involved in the mission, while also handling them with the utmost respect if necessary.
However, she was forced to stay in Nayos with her unit as the rescuing effort was focused on the Commander instead,
though her father held the belief that she would survive if she had at least half of the strength of her mother.
Following the successful
rescue
of the wizard
Isgarren
, the Commander recovered Arina's
dog tag "Arina 892 Snow 0XV1 Obscure"
from a pile of bones in a cave near the Gendarran campsite while Arina herself was still missing in action. The Commander also helped the wizard
Dagda
by locating the scattered divination cards of
Waiting Sorrow
, one of which was referring to Arina and titled
XV: The Fiend
.
Through the Veil
After entering
Inner Nayos
, the Pact Commander encountered Arina and other surviving members of her unit, learning that they had managed to build a camp in the enemy's territory while they also had met
Peitha
and
Ramses
who decided to aid the stranded members before a contact with the Astral Ward could be made. Arina remained at the camp whilst others progressed matters in Inner Nayos until joining the assault on
Heitor's Gate
.
The Realm of Dreams
After Heitor's defeat, Arina returned to the
Astral Ward Camp
and participated in the
War Council
.
While the
Wayfinder
caused distractions across
Inner Nayos
, Arina had stayed with
Peitha
at the
Astral Ward Camp
managing the search for
General Nephus
. Once recruited, General Nephus planned an assault on
Eventide's March
while he deployed Peitha with Ramses and Arina with Frode as the horns of the attack striked from the flanks whilst Nephus himself had begun a frontal assault with the Wayfinder. The three groups reunited on the steps of
Eventide's March
and pressed the attack until
Queen Labris
joined the fight. Once Labris had been subdued, Arina supported Nephus' entreaties to just kill her immediately but Peitha decided on a bolder course of action. Arina stayed with Peitha and Frode whilst the Wayfinder lit the beacons summoning Kryptis to an audience at the
Coliseum of the Midnight King
. Arina, like all other
Astral Ward
present, was immobilized when
Eparch
arrived. After he had left, Arina answered the Wayfinder's questions about the situation and possible effect on morale.
The Midnight King
Stub. Key elements of this npc article are incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
involved in spider lair meta and story
Janthir Wilds
Arina continued coordinating
Rift Warden
operations in the following months to deal with the remaining Kryptis followers of Eparch who attempted to breach the Tyrian mainland even after the Midnight King's fall. She joined the
Tyrian Alliance
's assault on the fallen
mursaat
city of
Bava Nisos
to end the encroaching
titan
threat to
Janthir
in 1338
AE
, learning that the titan
Saevus Saxum
had grown so large that he and the city had fused and essentially become one. Unlike her battles in Nayos, Arina chose to stay in camp to coordinate troop movements this time around. She had also earned a reputation of punishing Astral Ward members who complained loudly about their posts as noted by
Ward Member Bub
and
Ward Member Sloan
.
After the titan threat had been dealt with, Arina accompanied Frode to participate in the
victory celebration
held in
Lion's Arch
. Upon reuniting with the Wayfinder, she expressed empathy for the
Pact
's organizational struggles that were not so different from the Astral Ward's struggles. She admitted that she could even see a future where the Ward might join the Pact as the fourth
order
to assist them more closely someday.

Dialogue
Memory's Hollow
next to the
Southern Beacon
What's on your mind, Wayfinder?
Why can't we just knock down Heitor's gate?
Heitor's magic protects the gate, and she's strong. We'll need some extra firepower to knock it down.
It's never easy, is it? One more question.
That's enough for now.
How do the beacons work?
They're powered by some form of magical energy native to Nayos. Ramses called it Beacon's Flame, I think. Said they used to be the only security needed around here. Apparently Eparch came to disagree.
Apparently. One more thing ...
Nothing else for now.
Why didn't Heitor destroy the beacons?
Powered by this Beacon's Flame, they were never powerful enough for more than taking down individual targets, I'm told. But we're thinking that could change if we fill them full of Kryptis essence...
I hope it works. Can I ask something else?
(same as "What's on your mind, Wayfinder?")
Got it. Thanks.
Talk to you later, Arina.

Jennifer Hale
.
Arina is younger than
Braham
.

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Chaos Isles Fractal
,
Nightmare Fractal
, and
Shattered Observatory Fractal
.
Arkk
Race
Asura
Mech. race
Aetherblades
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Chaos Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Nightmare Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Shattered Observatory Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Image(s)
Arkk's hologram
Arkk wielding
Horrik
's Airship Cannon
Arkk wielding
Legendary Archdiviner
's Hammer
Arkk wielding a unique peacemaker weapon
“
Now you're in my domain. I set the parameters. I control the variables. Try to keep up.
—
Arkk
Arkk
, originally known as the
Mysterious
Asura
, is a recurring antagonist and a moving plot involving the
Fractals of the Mists
that runs parallel to
Living World Season 3
. His identity and plans remain shrouded in mystery, though it is known from
journals
he left behind that he—and perhaps the
krewe
he led—had been studying
the Mists
and, more specifically, on destabilizing the fractals within.
Arkk has a device he has dubbed the
Diaphanous Diffraction Randomizer
(or
DDR
) which he uses to cause "chaotic instabilities" to destabilize and reduce cohesion of the fractals. He also has the ability to block
Dessa
's means of communication.
Biography
In the Chaos Fractal, Arkk (As the Mysterious Asura, and printed in the dialogue box as “Mysterious Voice”) was first introduced to the players. He claimed that he’s been in the Chaos Isles for some time, and was irritated because the players might disturb him. As the player explored further into the Chaos Isles, he tried everything he could to stop these intruders, including obstructing
Dessa
, sending golems, the
Chaos Anomaly
, a blizzard and finally the
Legendary Brazen Gladiator
. When the players defeated the Brazen Gladiator, he was furious, saying he would kill player, but his DDR’s been out of energy, then he teleported away.
In the
Nightmare Fractal
, Arkk finally revealed his name and claimed to be chased by (and having a debt toward) the
Inquest
. After a brief alliance with the adventurers visiting the fractal who helped him clear a path through the reimagined
Tower of Nightmares
, he ran off with the core of the fractal which he had recovered after the defeat of
Ensolyss
, warning the adventurers not to follow him.
In the Shattered Observatory Fractal, Arkk was revealed to be
Dessa
's son, with a plan to try to break himself and her out of the fractal loop. However, he ultimately realized that neither he nor Dessa were real, as they were only fractal echoes of their former selves. Coming to terms with his existence, Arkk assisted Dessa with stopping his machine from destroying the fractals and shared one last moment with her before the fractal would eventually loop again and put him and her right back where they started.
Locations
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Chaos Isles Fractal
(as Mysterious Asura)
Nightmare Fractal
Shattered Observatory Fractal

Artificer Mullenix
Race
Ghost
Profession
Monk
Rank
Legendary
Location
Eye of the North
(
Shiverpeak Mountains
)
Organization
Ebon Vanguard
Image(s)
Interactive map
Artificer Mullenix
is a ghost guarding the Eye of the North. In life he was a man of many talents as well as a storehouse of information about the Ebon Vanguard, their mission in the north, and the state of affairs in
Ascalon
, although his fellow Vanguard soldiers viewed him more as a glorified record keeper. When he was stationed at the Eye of the North, Mullenix worked closely with Captain
Gwen Thackeray
, taking care of the base when the captain was out on missions.
Location
Far Shiverpeaks
Eye of the North (instance)
Dialogue
Welcome to the Eye of the North. Ages ago, when
the Great Destroyer
stirred, the Ebon Vanguard held this bastion of hope and joined forces with a special hero to prevent the destruction of us all.
Are you dead?
My body may be long gone, but I continue to guard this sacred place—and recognize heroes of all races.
That's noble of you.
Carry on.

Effects
Trivia
The
Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Artificer Mullenix
.
This NPC is named after
Joe Mullenix
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Asgeir Dragonrender
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Asgeir_Dragonrender
======================================================================

This article is about the lore figure.  For the statue object, see
Asgeir Dragonrender (statue)
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
.
Asgeir Dragonrender
Race
Vision
,
Norn
Rank
Legendary
Location
Asgeir's Legacy
(
Bjora Marches
)
Image(s)
Asgeir without his
helm
.
“
Asgeir Dragonrender. Battle Master, Favored of the
Spirits of the Wild
, Builder of
Hoelbrak
. May his legend live forever.
—
Eir Stegalkin
Asgeir Dragonrender
was the grandfather of
Knut Whitebear
. He is renowned for severing one of the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
's
fangs
from its maw, earning the name Dragonrender, and for leading the
norn
south to found
Hoelbrak
during the
norn exodus
.
Biography
Early years
Asgeir was a
norn
hero who lived during the
rise
of the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
and their push south into the
Far Shiverpeaks
after 1165
AE
. When the norn were on the brink of annihilation by the Elder Dragon, Asgeir was given the aid of the
Spirits of the Wild
to fight them. In addition, he had a powerful
jotun
scroll
with him as well; the scroll has been lost since, though believed to be somewhere in the Shiverpeaks.
Asgeir slew the greatest of Jormag's
champions
,
Frostfang
, with a single blow and proceeded to battle Jormag directly.
Tales describe how Asgeir gathered a hunting party and headed north to confront Jormag although the stories do not say whether this happened before or after the slaying of Frostfang; however, what is known is that Asgeir was the only one to return many weeks later and refused to tell anyone what had happened to the rest of the hunting party.
Though Asgeir had failed to kill Jormag during his hunt, he had rent one of
its teeth
from its maw as a trophy and proof that the Ice Dragon can be harmed, being the first to do so.
This act earned him the name Dragonrender.
During the battle with Jormag, Asgeir's bravery began to falter after witnessing the demise of
Owl
and his fellow norn, and it was then that he was struck with a vision. Stories say that the four Great Spirits of the Wild called
Bear
,
Raven
,
Snow Leopard
, and
Wolf
revealed themselves over the field of battle and spoke to him of a sanctuary in the
Southern Shiverpeaks
. Asgeir rallied all norn still alive and told them of his vision. He spoke of the Spirits giving all norn shelter, telling them that their fight against Jormag would come another day.
With the guidance of the four Great Spirits, Asgeir
led the norn south
through blinding snow and around deep chasms while the Spirits of
Ox
,
Eagle
, and
Wolverine
stayed behind to distract and battle Jormag.
Unbeknownst to his fellow norn, however, Asgeir had written and left behind a personal journal with his thoughts on the battle against Jormag and what had actually transpired during it.
In the southern valleys, Asgeir established
Hoelbrak
which he named after the lowlands there, carved new shrines to honor many of the surviving and lost Spirits of the Wild who had aided the norn, and placed the
Fang of the Serpent
in
the Great Lodge
.
During Hoelbrak's creation, an iceberg was threatening to crush the new settlement, so Asgeir sent
Hrothbeir
to break it, creating
Lake Mourn
.
Asgeir had changed since his confrontation with Jormag although details of this change are not elaborated on either in tales or eyewitness accounts.
Elder Ulf
suggests that Asgeir died to Jormag, though left it ambiguous as to how.
Legacy
Asgeir left behind a family and a legacy as one of the greatest norn heroes to have ever lived. His grandson,
Knut Whitebear
, would continue to keep Hoelbrak a safe haven for all norn.
Eir Stegalkin
would honor Asgeir by carving a
statue
in his honor, placing it in
Stonewright's Steading
. Asgeir's legend inspired generations of norn, including Eir and her son
Braham
, to attempt to damage the Fang of the Serpent. According to a
prophecy
, the one who can damage the fang will be destined to lead the norn north to reclaim their fallen homelands and end the threat of Jormag once and for all.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
.
During the campaign against the
Sons of Svanir
in 1332
AE
, the
Vigil
made a shocking discovery within the frigid, mountainous confines of
Asgeir's Legacy
: a personal journal of Asgeir Dragonrender himself. In the journal's pages, Asgeir revealed that the Spirits of the Wild had helped him slay Frostfang. He had also had a private conversation with Jormag after rending the Elder Dragon's fang, and Jormag had explained how its violent actions had only been a form of self defense. Jormag had come to view Asgeir as a worthy warrior and had realized how much he cared for the norn, so the Ice Dragon let him leave alive with a choice: Asgeir could return with more warriors and watch them all die as the dragon continued defending itself, or he could use the severed fang to convince the norn to move south and live in peace with Jormag promising not to follow them as it had come to view them as a race worthy of being preserved.
Realizing that the norn would stand no chance against the Elder Dragon even with the enchantments of the jotun scroll and the blessings of the Spirits of the Wild, Asgeir had lied that the Spirits, not Jormag, had instructed him to lead the norn to a southern sanctuary. Despite believing that the lie had been necessary, Asgeir had nevertheless felt deep down that he had given up when victory might have been within his reach, and that uncertainty was a burden he would carry for the rest of his life.
Location
Shiverpeak Mountains
Bjora Marches
Asgeir's Legacy
Combat abilities
Effects
Crippled
Gallery
A vision shown by
Raven
of Asgeir with
Jormag
's
tooth
.
A
statue
of Asgeir.
Notes
Using the
Raven Lens
special action prompt near the spot where
Asgeir's journal
is found in
Asgeir's Legacy
after the
Storms of Winter
meta event
, it is possible to summon a
vision
of Asgeir which shows him leaving the journal behind, hooking up the
Fang of the Serpent
and dragging the fang south.
Trivia
Asgeir is likely referring to the old Norse name, Ásgeirr, derived from the roots
áss
meaning 'god' and
geirr
meaning 'spear'. It is also a current Icelandic and Norwegian name, and the name 'Oscar' is derived from the same bases.
Asgeir has earned many names during the course of his life, including Dragonrender, Battle Master, Favored of the
Spirits of the Wild
, and Builder of
Hoelbrak
.
At an unknown time after the release of
Whisper in the Dark
, the model of Asgeir's memory was updated with the
Dragonrender Helm
, and his armor dye changed from red to orange-brown. The change was not listed in the patch notes.

PAGE: Ayumi

======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Ayumi
Race
Tengu
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Mori Village
(
The Echovald Wilds
)
Xunlai Jade Junkyard
(
The Echovald Wilds
)
Organization
Kestrels
Tyrian Alliance
“
Echovald is free territory, but when freedom comes at the expense of everyone else's welfare, someone has to push back.
—
Ayumi
Ayumi
is a young
tengu
and a member of the
Kestrels
. Her origins as a city-born tengu are sometimes used against her. She is an outsider but open to new ideas, much to the dismay of some of the other traditionalist Kestrel members. Ayumi was brought into the Kestrels by her uncle
Viri
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
After the
defeat
of
the Dragonvoid
, Ayumi has been in consideration to become the de facto leader of the Kestrels to keep
the Echovald Forest
safe from gangs and other forces that threaten the free territory.
Biography
Early years
A city-born
tengu
, Ayumi was introduced to the
Kestrels
, the peacekeepers of
the Echovald Wilds
, by her respected uncle
Viri
. Many Kestrels such as
Zuru
were not happy seeing a city tengu joining their ranks and upsetting the status quo, especially after Viri had passed away and Ayumi had begun proposing openminded and somewhat radical ideas to improve Kestrel operations.
End of Dragons
Act 3
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
Ayumi communicated with her fellow Kestrels frequently, keeping an eye out on Echovald's health and how the warring factions of the
Jade Brotherhood
and the
Speakers
were affecting it and the people living in it.
When an unidentified, corrupting force suddenly began spreading across Echovald and affecting the people and animals in it in 1335
AE
, Ayumi did her best to help whom she could. Even the Kestrels' efforts did not prevent losses, however, when the corruption found Arborstone and killed or twisted the people in it.
Act 5
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
After the
defeat
of
the Dragonvoid
, Ayumi was in consideration to become the de facto leader of the Kestrels to keep the Echovald Forest safe from gangs and other forces that threaten the free territory.
What Lies Within
Months after the Void incident and after having experienced harrowing events while solving the jade crisis in
Gyala Delve
, Rama gathered enough courage to attempt to pursue a romantic relationship with Min. To make their date less stressful, he invited Gorrik,
Taimi
, the Commander and the latter's chosen date from three available local friends to join him and Min for an
outing
at the
Red Duck Tea House
. Despite the occasional awkward topics of conversation and shyness almost overcoming Rama again, the Commander managed to lighten the mood just enough to allow Rama to steer the date in the right direction and give Min a good time. If Ayumi was chosen as the date, she and the Commander ended up having a good time as well with some good-natured flirting between the two. Ever vigilant, Ayumi inquired if the Commander might have largest wardrobe in all of Tyria and coyly asked if the Commander might accompany her to
Club Canach
one day with the Commander tentatively being on board for another date in the future.
Janthir Wilds
In 1337
AE
after the global
Kryptis
invasion attempt
, Ayumi accompanied
Empress Ihn
to attend a
meeting
in
Lion's Arch
with several faction leaders from around the known world with the intention of forming the
Tyrian Alliance
as a united defense force against Tyrian and
Mistborn
threats. She also attended a
second meeting
where the founding members of the alliance met with
Stoic Alder
,
Claw
of the
Harvest Den
Lowland Kodan
who was considered to become a future member of the alliance, and voted for whether to use the
Scepter of Orr
against the
titans
plaguing
Janthir
and the lowlanders or not. Despite having been present in the inaugural meeting of the alliance and representing Echovald as a free territory, Ayumi did not get to vote and only observed Ihn voicing her stance regarding the scepter. She spent some time chatting and getting acquainted with
Kalidris Sparrowhawk
who acted as the representative of one of the Great Houses of the tengu
Dominion of Winds
.

Trivia
Ayumi is
voiced
by
Joy Osmanski
.
Ayumi wields a
Tengu Echo Sword
in her main hand and a
Tengu Echo Dagger
in her off-hand.
Ayumi
(あゆみ or 歩美) is a Japanese given name with multiple meanings, including "progress", "truth", "peaceful" or "walking beauty".
Blue beak and face aside, her appearance appears to be based off of the
Secretarybird
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Bengt Tovasson
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Bengt_Tovasson
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the
Living World Season 1
and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Bengt Tovasson
Race
Norn
Profession
Mesmer
Rank
Legendary
Location
Forced Entry
(
Bava Nisos
,
Mistburned Barrens
)
Organization
Mesmer Collective
“
I have some personal stake in seeing Mabon's legacy upheld, even if that means bearing some of his burdens.
—
Bengt Tovasson
Bengt Tovasson
is the Fourth
Veil
of the
Mesmer Collective
, follower of
Raven
, and father of
Kurt Bengtsson
. He sends a
letter
to
Kasmeer Meade
on behalf of the Collective, which can be found on a table in
the Dead End
. He later assists
Councillor Ludo
and
Livia
in the
Tyrian Alliance
's
assault
on
Bava Nisos
.
Biography
Early years
While Bengt Tovasson was showing talent as a budding
norn
mesmer
and was learning the ways of
Raven
, he came across a curious
magic
user sealing an anomaly in the
Shiverpeak Mountains
. After aiding the disguised stranger, Bengt was invited into the
Mesmer Collective
where the stranger held the high position of Fifth
Veil
. He rose through the ranks over the years, eventually becoming a member of the Collective's inner council. When Bengt's recruiter revealed his
true self
and identity as
Mabon
to the Collective, Bengt stood by him as a close friend and pupil.
As time passed, Bengt eventually became the Fourth Veil of the Collective working directly under Mabon. At some point he fathered a son named
Kurt Bengtsson
who would join the
Durmand Priory
in adulthood.
Living World Season 1
In 1326
AE
, Bengt wrote an encouraging
letter
to one of the Collective's newest recruits,
Kasmeer Meade
, who had become impoverished after
her father
's untimely demise due to her brother
Kyle Meade
's actions and the
Ministry
seizing the family's assets in the resulting scandal. Bengt promised Kasmeer that her status in the Collective would remain unchanged despite her financial hardships because an enigmatic sponsor had vouched for her talents and most importantly her discretion. He also assured Kasmeer that the Collective would provide her with a reference should any
would-be employer
require such when considering hiring her.
Janthir Wilds
In 1338
AE
,
Countess Anise
wrote a
letter
to the Mesmer Collective's inner and outer councils, asking for any available Veil to assist her in exploring the dangerous isle which had the ruined mursaat city of
Bava Nisos
. Bengt answered the call, travelling to Bava Nisos to assist the
Tyrian Alliance
in dealing with the
titan
and
spectral
threats within as well as to keep an eye on
Livia
whose rogue actions with her recent theft of the
Scepter of Orr
had made the Alliance uneasy about her trustworthiness. Upon
meeting
with the
Pact Commander
, Bengt admitted to having heard his fellow Collective members Anise and Kasmeer gossip about the Commander's prowess for the past decade and could easily recognize the Commander's looks based on the provided information. He also pointed out that he had a personal reason for travelling to Bava Nisos aside from Anise's request: he intended to uphold Mabon's legacy and handle the fallout of his late mentor's burdened choices from the past.
Bengt accompanied
Councillor Ludo
, Livia, and the Commander on a
push deeper into the ruined city
which had been fused with the titan
Saevus Saxum
whose life Mabon had spared in the past but whose actions had caused the spreading titan threat in
Janthir
. During this time Bengt and Livia had a heated discussion about Bengt's purpose for being in the city with Livia questioning if his main duty was to help defeat the titan or to deal with her if she stepped out of line with the scepter.
The Alliance's army encountered various threats along the way, including the monstrous
Lextalion the Merciless
originating from the fused souls of the mursaat's sacrificial victims to the
keystone
who had been released upon the shard's cracking, as well as Saevus's exposed organs such as his lungs and heart. As the party split up to assault the titan's organs simultaneously on multiple fronts, Bengt's forces
targeted the northern lung
and ultimately managed to damage it to weaken the massive titan. The parties rendezvoused at Saevus's injured heart to celebrate their victory once the battle was over with Bengt expressing relief that Livia had turned out to be a trustworthy ally after all. However, the Alliance's forces were soon forced to retreat when the titan used magic seeping from the open
Mists Gate
near the heart to regenerate himself and to expel them from the heart chamber.
The Tyrian Alliance was ultimately successful in defeating Saevus for good when the Commander's party, assisted by
Mabon
's ghost,
entered
the Mists Gate to deal with the problem within and having Mabon use the Scepter of Orr to seal the gate permanently from the other side to prevent any more magic or
demonic
forces from coming through. Although Bengt did not accompany the party on their dangerous journey into
the Mists
, he nevertheless boasted about dealing with Lextalion and exorcising the angry spirits of the mursaat's victims so they could find rest at last, and how he intended to tell a great tale about it to his son one day.

Notes
Lore discrepancy
:
Originally in
Forced Entry
set in 1338 AE, Bengt says he is "Fourth Veil of the Mesmer Collective and havroun of Raven." However,
Havroun Weibe
was still shown alive during
Whisper in the Dark
set in 1332 AE.
A Spirit of Legend
states that "There is only one havroun per Spirit of the Wild; there may be another in training, if the current havroun has grown old or weary and is preparing for their final crossing into the Mists." It is unclear if Weibe has passed on the mantle of havroun to Bengt since his last appearance despite Bengt working for the
Mesmer Collective
. Bengt's spoken line in Forced Entry cuts off abruptly right before he mentions being a havroun while the text retains the mention, leaving the canonicity of his statement uncertain. The written line about being a havroun has been removed by the
August 19, 2025
update.
Bengt did not exist in the
original Sky Pirates release
; he was retroactively written in for the
2022 re-release of Sky Pirates
.
Trivia
Bengt Tovasson is
voiced
by
Darin De Paul
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Bloody Prince Thorn
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Bloody_Prince_Thorn
======================================================================

This content was introduced in
Blood and Madness
and is subsequently only available during
Halloween
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Halloween
.
Bloody Prince Thorn
Race
Halloween creature
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Mad King's Labyrinth
(
Mad King's Realm
)
Image(s)
Interactive map  (floor  -27)
“
He
doesn't hear the music. He doesn't hear what symphony murder makes. How pure it is.
—
Bloody Prince Thorn
Bloody Prince Edrick Thorn
(also known as
Edrick the Bloody
and nicknamed Eddie by his father, self-titled Painlord) was the son of
Mad King Oswald Thorn
of
Kryta
. In life, he was a lanky boy, with a head of spiky hair, both lips and eyes smeared with pitch, and was said to have two facial expressions: scowl or smirk.
Biography
Early years
Prince Edrick Thorn was born as the son of
King Oswald Thorn
of
Kryta
circa 805
AE
.
Not much of Edrick's history is known due to Oswald removing knowledge of him from history. Edrick's mother was killed by his father via beheading on Oswald's birthday, and his favorite stepmother,
Henrietta
, was killed by Edrick himself when he nailed her hands to a
drake
during a game of "Pin the Tail on the Drake"—though Edrick does not recall this.
Edrick was fond of coming up with what he considered to be fearsome titles such as "Painlord" and the Bloody Prince, but Oswald was embarrassed by the titles.
When Edrick was barely twenty, Oswald had him thrown into a dungeon for still-unknown reasons, though he escaped and bit off the guards' tongues to keep them from talking. Upon escaping, the Bloody Prince burned down the Krytan village of
Pelchalice
, killing 600 villagers in approximately 825
AE
.
It was the fifth act that season, rumors claiming to be due to a woman, and done in order to incite the populace against his father to take the crown for himself. The angry mob that formed remained outside
Castle Thorn
for three days before King Oswald learned it was Edrick's doing and that the prince was waiting for him in the throne room.
When the Mad King walked in to chastise his son about his acts of punless violence that were unbefitting of the Thorn name, he
witnessed
the prince sitting on his throne. Edrick announced his intention to overthrow Oswald with the help of the mob, angering Oswald. However, the Bloody Prince pressed his advantage by delivering an ultimatum to his father: Oswald would either surrender his reign willingly and Edrick would drive the mob off, or he would kill Oswald himself and make the mob think he put an end to their Mad King.
Seemingly cornered and outwitted, Oswald successfully persuaded the victorious Edrick to take him to the undercroft which, according to him, held a wealth of rare and valuable armaments that the prince would be inheriting. However, when the pair reached the undercroft, they found only an ornate metal reliquary which Oswald revealed to be an infamous torture device known as "
The Shell of Insanity
" which his counselor
Dribbin
had enchanted. While the Bloody Prince was recovering from the revelation of having been tricked, the Mad King shoved him into the reliquary and stuffed the prince's mouth with
candy corn
. Oswald explained that if any other person had threatened him, he would have publicly executed them for treason, but Edrick would not share such a fate as it would make the prince famous. The Mad King had something far worse in mind for his son: Edrick would either starve to death or suffocate in the box and be driven mad in his last moments in order to become worthy of the Thorn name.
Before the rioting mob was able to breach the castle, Oswald ordered his scribes to purge Edrick's name from the royal archives; effectively erasing the Bloody Prince from history with the exception of accounts by
Mad Baron Wycker
. The Shell of Insanity was then sent to the former
Istani
desert island estate of the Mad King's late wife
Princess Zola
but was stolen multiple times before it arrived in
Nolani Academy
.
Blood and Madness 2013
The Thorn Reliquary containing the Bloody Prince deep within the
Nolani Academy Ruins
.
After a mysterious—and dangerous—artifact dating back to King Oswald Thorn's reign was found in the
Nolani Academy Ruins
nearby the
Black Citadel
in 1326
AE
,
Magister Tassi
of the
Durmand Priory
sought aid in containing the object, after it had killed an unsuspecting
charr
, to prevent further loss of life. While Magister Tassi and the
Pact Commander
investigated the ancient artifact known as the Thorn Reliquary, Price Edrick Thorn, also known as the Blood Prince overpowered Magister Tassi's containment field and broke free from the reliquary. Stricken from history, Bloody Prince Thorn vowed revenge against his father, Mad King Thorn. By exploiting the Bloody Prince Thorn's weakness to candy corn, Magister Tassi and the Commander managed to temporarily force Bloody Prince Thorn back into the reliquary in which he was imprisoned. However, as the candy corn in the reliquary room was too old and dry, the seal on the reliquary would not be able to contain the Bloody Prince Thorn's strength forever.
Determined to contain the potential threat from being released upon Tyria, Magister Tassi went in search of living candy corn. With the adventurers' aid, Tassi acquired a living candy corn elemental to use in a prototype candy energy rifle. Using the new weapon, the team managed to coat Bloody Prince Thorns reliquary in a candy glaze, permanently sealing him within the Mad Realm.
Shadow of the Mad King 2021
Following
Palawa Joko
's
demise
in 1331
AE
,
Brigadier General Kernel
and the
Pact Commander
schemed to
gather
real and fabricated spoils of war from
Elona
to fool Mad King Thorn into thinking that Kernel's forces had merely forced the lich king to retreat. However, Oswald eventually began suspecting that his servants were keeping something from him, so Kernel and the
Lunatic Court
resolved to settle the matter in their own way to avert the king's potential, destructive tantrum. They brought what appeared to be the spirit of Joko himself to the Mad King's Labyrinth, delighting Oswald who had Joko replace the Bloody Prince as his new rival to exchange taunts with.
The ousted Edrick was placed on a bony shipwreck which was suspended above the labyrinth. Realizing that his father no longer cared about their family rivalry after reconnecting with the lich king, the Bloody Prince began pondering his role in the Mad Realm. While there, he met with and conversed with adventurers, including the Commander, while lamenting his fate. However, his interactions with visitors boosted his morale, and he decided to consider their encouraging words to seek his own path carefully.

Combat abilities

Boneskinner#Walkthrough
Abilities

Summons Wisps
Inhibited by Light
Effects
(unlocked periodically)
Spiritually Wounded
— Vulnerable to the
Spirit Nova
skill.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Skills
Cascade
- The boneskinner launches a cascade of darkness that rapidly deals medium damage per hit.
Charge
- Charges towards a torch and permanently destroys it.
Crushing Cruelty
- Leaps to the center after a delay and deals heavy damage.

- The
boneskinner
spews a noxious cloud, launching enemies and applying torment. Extinguishes torches.

- Pulls enemies towards the center of the room. Extinguishes torches.

).
Tormenting Aura
- Does damage every second. Damage increased per stack.
Unnatural Aura
- The
boneskinner
pulses a volatile aura that deals damage every 3 seconds to all foes within a range of 2,000.

Throw Cursed Artifact
Trivia
Upon release, the encounter within the raid encounter (previously strike mission) was bugged, with issues including faulty damage output, malfunctioning breakbar punish and inconsistent AoE and debuff usage.
This was corrected in a
subsequent game update
.
Boneskinners were originally called Wendigo,
referring to the
Wendigo
, a mythological spirit that originates from Algonquian tribes. They should not be confused with the unrelated
Berserking Wendigo
from
Guild Wars: Eye of the North
.
The Boneskinner was teased during the
Extra Life
charity stream, with the text: "𝕊𝕌𝕄𝕄𝕆ℕ 𝕋ℍ𝔼 𝔹𝕆ℕ𝔼𝕊𝕂𝕀ℕℕ𝔼ℝ : At $100k we'll reveal the date and title of the next Living World episode!"
A teaser video was shown after hitting the $100k mark.
The Boneskinners have been designed by the following developers
:
Kate Picchi
— hair cards, textures and cloth settings, overall game model/topology, textures, and bone constraints for the necklace.
Matthew Pugnetti
— concept art
Chris Bennett
— high poly sculpt
Nathaniel Hubbell
,
Nick Ajer
,
Lee Bledsoe
— effects
Justin Reilly
,
Taylor Southerland
— designers

======================================================================
PAGE: Boticca
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Boticca
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
and
The Icebrood Saga
.
Boticca
Race
Charr
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Dredgeways
(
Thunderhead Peaks
)
Ice Floe
(
Thunderhead Peaks
)
Olmakhan Camp
(
Dragonfall
)
Organization
Olmakhan
Image(s)
Appearance during
The Crystal Dragon
.
“
We have to go on the offensive. Drive the invaders out of our lands.
—
Boticca
Boticca
is a
charr
stormcaller from the
Olmakhan
tribe. She advocates taking a stand against forces which threaten the tribe.
Biography
Living World Season 4
A Bug in the System
Born to the
Olmakhan
tribe of charr living in
Atholma
, Boticca was raised with the tribe's values and became a formidable stormcaller. She was captured by the
Inquest
, who had arrived on
Sandswept Isles
, and was placed in a containment tank in
Lab Sigma-05
where the
asuran
researchers were planning to test the effects of the
Scarab Plague
on her in 1331
AE
. Despite having witnessed the demise of the rest of the captives, Boticca remained defiant in the face of death and swore to make the Inquest pay for what they had done.
When the sudden arrival of trespassers distracted the Inquest, Boticca broke free from containment and attacked the researchers with the newly arrived group's help. Deciding to join forces with the newcomers, she informed them how to get out of the facility as well as the where the main Inquest complex,
Rata Primus
, was located. As the lab self-destructed, Boticca and the rest of the group jumped out just in time to avoid the explosion although they were shaken up by the blast as they washed ashore.
After recovering, Boticca learned more about the group which consisted of the
Pact Commander
, the
norn
Braham Eirsson
, the charr
Rox
and the
devourer
Frostbite
. When the Commander suggested that they and Boticca's tribe should work together to thwart the invading Inquest and
Awakened
in the region, the stormcaller agreed and offered to escort the group to her village after mounting her summoned
skimmer
Ghostwind
. As they traveled to the village, Boticca told the group the name of her people and how they had fled from the
Flame Legion
long ago. She inquired whether Rox was of the Flame Legion and did not care about Rox's
gladium
status much to Rox's surprise.
The villagers were delighted to see Boticca return, but the guards only let the Commander accompany Boticca to the council chamber due to being suspicious of outlanders. The tribe's elders welcomed Boticca but were suspicious of the Commander even when Boticca supported the Commander in asking for the Olmakhan to take the fight to the invaders who were threatening their way of life. The arguing was interrupted by the arrival of
Efi
who inquired about her missing father; when the elders tried to keep the truth about his demise from Efi, the cub stormed off. Worried for what the grieving cubs might do, Boticca asked the Commander to check up on them while she would tell the elders about her experiences with the Inquest.
Shortly after, the Inquest sent a raiding squad of
golems
to the village to capture more test subjects and kill any who refused.
Elder Narn
died protecting his fellow elders and Boticca from
Mark II Beta Exterminator Golem
much to everyone's shock. After the golem raiding squad had been destroyed, the Olmakhan gathered to honor Narn's passing while the elders decided to pledge the tribe to Boticca and the Commander's cause.
With all Olmakhan standing behind them, the Commander's party and Boticca began planning a way to reach the heavily defended Rata Primus. Boticca learned that the group had a
dragon
ally,
Aurene
, who scouted the cube's perimeter. Boticca provided the Commander with intel on how to breach the complex's communications tower to hamper Inquest coordinating their troops. She also pledged to send the Olmakhan to the sites the Commander had already sabotaged in order to keep the Inquest from reclaiming and repairing the sites.
Once the Inquest perimeter facilities had been sabotaged, the Commander's party and a group of Olmakhan stormcallers led by Boticca boarded the airship of the Commander's
corsair
ally
Sayida the Sly
who had arrived to offer her assistance for the assault on Rata Primus. Boticca and other stormcallers summoned a storm to destroy and hamper the Inquest's defenses, and the airship successfully breached the cube, allowing the Commander and Braham to search the complex while Rox led a group of Olmakhan against the Inquest and Awakened in the area.
Boticca was among the people welcoming the Commander and Braham back just as Rata Primus exploded. She noticed that the pair were now accompanied by new acquaintances:
Blish
,
Gorrik
, and various domesticated insects. Talking with the Commander afterwards, Boticca expressed relief over no longer having to worry about further Inquest meddling. The Olmakhan would have to elect a new person to take the late Elder Narn's place in the Council of Five, but the stormcaller knew that the Olmakhan had faced hardships before and would persevere.
All or Nothing
Boticca was with the other Olmakhan who joined the fight with
Kralkatorrik
in
Thunderhead Keep
.
The production of the
Dragonsblood Spears
required oil to quench the alloy. Boticca, together with Braham and Rox, approached the dredge for help.
Miner Mikhail
told them that the oil drills were offline and surrounded by his Branded comrades. They set out together to repair the drills and to give peace to the branded dredge. Boticca with a group of adventurers protected the second drill, while others continued to repair the last drill. After all three drills were repaired, the oil overflowed courtesy of the
volatile magic
that had slipped in. Boticca helped clean up the spill after which she noticed something bad happening further away. She left with the Olmakhan to
Freedom's Catchment
where the overflow of oil was sent by Miner Mikhail. There she helped fight
Volatile Oil Slug Sulypix
. After the fight she asked if the oil was sufficient. Miner Mikhail replied that the flame of revolution would keep the dredge warm.
Boticca also fought in
battle
with Kralkatorrik in the
the Auditorium
.
War Eternal
The Olmakhan were with the fighting force that landed on
Dragonfall
as they prepared to face off against the injured Kralkatorrik whom the Commander and Aurene had driven to Tyria from
the Mists
. Rox took the Olmakhan and Boticca to the southwest, the domain of
Grenth
, to establish a beachhead. Boticca was unhappy with how unsteady the newly formed island was. She spent the journey encouraging Rox who was very disturbed by both the Elder Dragon as well as the environment. Boticca helped Rox and the other Olmakhan establish the
Olmakhan Camp
in the
Underworld
. As they oversaw the construction and sent the Olmakhan on missions to secure the perimeter and gather useful materials against Kralkatorrik, the two charr talked about the metaphysical aspects of the Underworld and their personal experiences.
After all the camps were fully established and the preparations for the grand assault against Kralkatorrik were complete, Boticca took part in the
final assault
from the southwest side. After the assault was finished she noticed that Kralkatorrik was planning something. She got evacuated to
Pact Command
, while Kralkatorrik crystallized himself to heal his wounds. Despite the Crystal Dragon's actions, a team led by the Pact Commander ultimately managed to slay him, and Boticca witnessed Aurene succeeding Kralkatorrik as a new, prismatic Elder Dragon. As the allied forces made preparations to leave, Rox decided to stay behind to live with Boticca and the Olmakhan in Atholma as a member of the tribe.
The Icebrood Saga
News eventually reached the Olmakhan about the
All-Legion Rally
held in
Grothmar Valley
in 1333
AE
, but the Olmakhan did not send a representative there to observe the proceedings. When Tyrians sent calls for aid as the
Icebrood War
escalated and the clashing forces of the Elder Dragons
Jormag
and
Primordus
threatened to destroy everything in their wake in 1334
AE
, the Olmakhan decided to send some members of their tribe to aid their northern allies with Rox acting as the Olmakhan field leader for the duration of the war. After Boticca had learned that Imperators
Crecia Stoneglow
and
Malice Swordshadow
were headed for
Bloodtide Coast
to fight against the amassing
Frost Legion
there, she initially thought that it was another imperator vanity exercise as the All-Legion rally had turned out to be for Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
. However, the female imperators proved that their main focus was on saving lives than boosting their ego. Once the rival Elder Dragons had been
defeated
and the Olmakhan had returned home, Rox let Boticca know that the Commander had told her to say hi to Boticca on the Commander's behalf.

Trivia
Boticca is
voiced
by
Kimberly Brooks
.
During the development of
A Bug in the System
, Boticca was originally intended to be a villain whom the players would have fought against.
Boticca has a
Dajkah Lantern
skimmer
named
Ghostwind
.
She wears an
Olmakhan Mantle
,
Tribal Vestments
, no gloves,
Tribal Leggings
, and
Tribal Walkers
.
Boticca's name may be a reference to
Boudica
(or Boudicca), a queen of the British Celtic Iceni tribe who led an urprising against the occupying forces of the Roman Empire.
Boticca uses a male charr hairstyle, despite being female, and unique horns inaccessible to players.

Broken King
Race
Elemental
Rank
Legendary
Location
Ice Wastes
(
Hall of Chains
)
“
Dhuum's reign... Brutal...uncompromising...
— Broken King
The
Broken King
is a mini boss who is part of the
Statues of Grenth
event, guarding the
Statue of Ice
in the
Hall of Chains
. He used to be
King Frozenwind
, a loyal
elemental
servant of
Grenth
, who has since been twisted into
Dhuum
's servitude.
Location
The Mists
Hall of Chains
Ice Wastes
Statue of Ice
Combat abilities
Abilities
Turned Elemental
Behavior
Targets the player with the highest toughness.
Effects
Stun
— Vulnerable while broken.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Shield of Ice
(at 1% health)
— Shields the
Broken King
from death.
Skills
Biting Aura
— Pulses damage to all players in range.
Frozen Demise
(upon reaching
4
Frozen Wind
)
— Your skin freezes through as death takes hold.
Hailstorm
(every 2% health)
— A whirling orb of sleet and ice that deals heavy area-of-effect damage if not absorbed upon landing.
A large chunk of hail will descend onto a shrinking green circle over the course of 15 seconds. Deals moderate amount of damage to any players in the green circle when it lands, and applies a stack of
Frozen Wind
. If no players are in the green circle, then the Broken King will use Ice Breaker.
Ice Breaker
— Failing to absorb
Hailstorm
results in heavy arena-wide damage.
King's Wrath
(variant 1)
— Icy fingers rip through the ground, bursting and driving shrapnel into nearby foes.
The Broken King raises its left hand spread a web of cone-shaped AoEs in all directions.
King's Wrath
(variant 2)
— Icy fingers rip through the ground, bursting and driving shrapnel into nearby foes.
The Broken King raises its left hand and fires a fan of icicles at the tank.
Numbing Breach
— Frozen breaches in the ice that deal heavy damage and apply chill.
Stolen skills
Detonate Plasma
Notes
The
Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
King Frozenwind
.
Broken King has 2748
armor
(1374 toughness and 1374 defense).
Compared to regular
Raid
bosses with 2597
armor
, this translates to about -5.5%
power
damage
.
Broken King has an enrage timer of 3 minutes and 30 seconds.
Broken King has an 160 range diameter
hitbox
.
Broken King will focus on the player with the highest
toughness
attribute of squad members still alive.
Broken King has 9,831,000 HP.

raid boss
.  For the event boss in

Cairn the Indomitable
Race
Jade Construct
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Bastion of the Penitent
(
Heart of Maguuma
)
Cairn the Indomitable
is the first boss in the
Bastion of the Penitent
raid
wing.
Location
Woodland Cascades
Bastion of the Penitent
The Yard
Cairn's Perch
Combat abilities
For walkthrough, see:
Bastion of the Penitent#Cairn the Indomitable
Abilities
Guard of the Penitent
Behavior
Targets the player who is farthest away from itself.
Effects
Skills
Impact
-
Cairn
swings with its right hand, dealing damage in a two-part arc.
Impact
-
Cairn
follows up its right-hand swing with a left, dealing damage in an arc.
Meteor Swarm
-
Cairn
swings its left hand back, creating an expanding fan of projectiles that drag targets back. Throws approximately 8 projectiles that cause a short distance
Knockback
and damages targets for a very small amount of damage. These projectiles can be
blocked
,
evaded
,
reflected
, and/or
converted
. Each projectile also adds stacks of
Unseen Burden
.
Cosmic Aura
-
Cairn
pulses with malevolent cosmic energy.
Damages all players in combat with
Cairn the Indomitable
for a small amount of damage.
Displacement
-
Cairn
opens a rift into the skin of
Tyria
, displacing targets a fixed distance away.
Cairn
places 3 red circles in close proximity. After a short duration, these circles will teleport players to a random edge of the fighting arena.
Energy Surge
- Cairn the Indomitable surges forward, dealing damage and knocking back all targets in its path.
Cairn
teleports to 5 different, set locations based on the percentage of its health. Standing between his teleport points - which can be detected by seeing a rift of disturbed air or by a directional arrow at his feet - will cause a moderate amount of damage and a long range
Knockback
.
Gravity Wave
- A cascading wave of energy that pushes targets back.
Cairn
creates a cascading wave of black energy that expands outward from it over a total of 3 waves. It will cause a long distance
Knockback
and a large amount of damage.
Orbital Sweep
- A sweeping attack that knocks targets back.
Cairn the Indomitable
slams a large blade down in one of the cardinal directions (depending on his HP), then spins counter-clockwise. This attack deals a large amount of damage and causes a large distance
Launch
.
Shared Agony
-
Cairn
causes agony so intense that it spreads to the target's nearby allies.
Cairn
initially selects a target at maximum range to place the effect
Shared Agony
on. From that point on, every twenty seconds the furthest player will receive
Shared Agony
at a set interval up to a maximum of 3 players being afflicted at one time.
Spatial Manipulation
- An unleashed wave of energy that damages, stuns, and applies
Unseen Burden
.
The damage and effects of this attack occur when a player is unable to get in a green circle that meets the required minimum number of people for the circle. The attack will cause
Float
, a large amount of damage, and add stacks of
Unseen Burden
.

Unstable Artifact

Notes
Cairn The Indomitable has 2597
armor
(1374 toughness and 1223 defense).

hitbox
, which is considered large.
Trivia
"Cairn The (noun/adjective)" was already a common naming scheme for
Jade Construct
Bosses in the original
Guild Wars
probably in reference to Scottish
cairns
and the Jade's rocky appearance.
Cairn the Indomitable’s Orbital Sweep was shown as a large, blue stick in early testing. Testers called it the 'pool noodle' attack.

PAGE: Calogero, Avatar of the All Seer
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Calogero,_Avatar_of_the_All_Seer
======================================================================

This article is about the
seer
First Elder.  For the
charr
researcher, see
Calogero the Elder
.
Calogero, Avatar of the All Seer
Race

,
Seer
Level
80

Legendary
Location

(

)
Organization
Seer constructs
Calogero, Avatar of the All Seer
is a projection of
the All Seer
. In life, Calogero was the First Elder of the
seers
.
When the seers abandoned
Castora
to keep it from the
mursaat
, Calogero stayed behind to watch over the island alongside several other seers, acting as the All Seer's avatar by offering their soul essence to protect the island.
