Captain Ashym
Race
Human
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Nightmare Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Urban Battleground Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Image(s)
Captain Ashym wielding a
staff
Captain Ashym wielding a
Fiery Greatsword
Captain Ashym
is a
legendary
boss
found at the end of the
Urban Battlegrounds Fractal
. He will have a permanent
Protection
effect on him and gains a permanent
Retaliation
effect as the fight progresses. He will occasionally summon a
Veteran Ascalonian Squire
, which will constantly grant
Might
to Ashym, up to a stack of 20. At some points, Ashym will swap his sword for a staff, using powerful
fire attunement
-based spells, before switching back. Defeating Ashym will complete the fractal.
Location
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Nightmare Fractal
Urban Battlegrounds Fractal
Combat abilities
Abilities
Blinds
Burns
Summons Minions
Behavior
Begins battle with Sword and Shield, switches to Staff at 75% and to Fiery Greatsword at 25% health.
Effects
Retaliation
— Reflect incoming damage back to its source; stacks duration.
Determined
— Determined to survive and immune to damage.
(In the
Nightmare Fractal
)
Skills
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Verify Growing Rage and weapon phase mechanics. Sometimes he doesn't summon an FGS?
Growing Rage
- Changes fighting styles for each quarter of health lost.
Summon
- Summons a
Veteran Ascalonian Squire
. The squire carries a banner that grants Ashym a direct boon depending which banner it carries. Can provide
Fury
,
Might
,
Protection
, or
Swiftness
Sword and shield skills:
Blinding Blade
-
Leap
at your foe. Inflicts
blindness
and heals you for each foe hit.
Fire Shield
-
Blocks
all incoming sources of damage and inflicts
Burning
on attackers.
Savage Leap
-
Lunge
at your foe and strike them with your sword.
Staff skills:
Meteor Shower
- Call down a meteor shower onto the target area.
Damage is reduced per target each time they are struck by this ability.
This version covers a much larger area.
Meteor Shower
- Blasts the surrounding area with flames or
agony
. Signified by sparks of flame at his feet.
Flamewall
- Create a wall of flame at the target area that
burns
foes.
Fiery Greatsword skills:
Fiery Eruption
- Ram your sword into the ground and make fire erupt near your foes.
Fiery Whirl
- Advance forward, slashing foes in your way.
Flame Wave
- Shoot a fireball at your foe with each swing.

Name
Type
Rarity
Quantity
Utility Backpack
Container
F
Exotic
1
Notes
One of the possible land transformations from the
Endless Fractal Tonic
.
It's possible to negate the effect of retaliation by walking away from him to about 1500 units and then returning to combat range.
Captain Ashym has a 50 range diameter hitbox, which is considered small.

======================================================================
PAGE: Captain Mai Trin
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Captain_Mai_Trin
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 1
and
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Captain Mai Trin
Race
Human
Profession
Thief
Revenant
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Captain Mai Trin Boss Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Aetherblade Hideout
(
Seitung Province
)
Organization
Aetherblades
Xunlai Jade
Image(s)
Alternate appearance.
Mai Trin as a
hologram
.
Mai Trin as a
revenant
.
“
This is what your curiosity gets you.
—
Captain Mai Trin
Captain Mai Trin
is the leader of the
Aetherblades
and one of the generals of
Scarlet's Alliance
. Working at the behest of
Scarlet Briar
, she was responsible for
assassinating
Captain Theo Ashford
in order to weaken
Lion's Arch
from within.
Mai was captured after a
Lionguard
raid on the
Aetherblade Retreat
; however, the Aetherblades eventually
freed
Mai from captivity and fled with her into the
Edge of the Mists
as Scarlet had a specific task in mind for her.
While several Aetherblades fought under Scarlet in the
Battle for Lion's Arch
, Mai herself was absent, presumably continuing her secret mission in
the Mists
with the rest of the Aetherblades.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
While in the Mists, Mai became a
revenant
, channeling the
Echo of Scarlet Briar
. The deaths of her shipmates and Scarlet haunted Mai, making her fellow Aetherblades start to question her competence. Her First Mate,
Ankka
, initiated an uprising and took the title of Captain for herself. Mai was killed during an
attack
on the
Yong Reactor
, posthumously receiving the title of "Hero of Kaineng" from
Empress Ihn
.
Biography
Early years
The
Aetherblades
started out as a particularly greedy and bloodthirsty
pirate
crew in which Mai Trin held high rank; when the
sylvari
terrorist
Scarlet Briar
found them and presented them with
Inquest
magitech and allies, the crew became far more dangerous and far more successful at the business of pirating. Officers like Mai kept the Aetherblades organized and active, and their ranks swelled as more and more unaffiliated pirates and pirate bands signed on, lured by the promise of easy money and power. Once they were in, however, they found it problematic to get out again, as Scarlet had plenty of work for them to do and kept them hard at it or they would face her wrath.
The Aetherblades managed to steal
Pact
airships
and modified them into their own vessels called aetherships.
Living World Season 1
Sky Pirates
Captain Trin attempts to help
Garrot Ambercowl
to his feet.
Mai Trin was first seen attending the
Dragon Bash
in
Lion's Arch
while disguised as a festive onlooker in 1326
AE
. She offered her help as a healer during the Aetherblade
attack
on Dragon Bash after the ceremonial effigy had exploded and caused the death of
Captain Theo Ashford
as well as injured several people nearby. The enigmatic
E
wrote letters to the
Pact Commander
and other able adventurers, asking them to seek out
Marjory Delaqua
in order to figure out the mastermind behind the attack before anything happened to the rest of the
Captain's Council
.
Logan Thackeray
got involved in the case as well by covertly
hiring
Marjory and her assistant
Kasmeer Meade
to bring the killer to justice as he did not want the death of his family friend Theo Ashford go unavenged in case the
Lionguard
were unable to find the culprit.
Inspector Ellen Kiel
of the Lionguard, Marjory and the Commander began investigating the crime scene soon after. Marjory provided the group with
Magister Tassi
's device which could read energy fluctuations. The trio
discovered
that the effigy's energy discharge had been released on purpose. Their initial investigations led them to Mai Trin whose aura glowed with energy residue, but they deemed her innocent once they realized that she had been tending the fatally injured
charr
representative
Garrot Ambercowl
whose residue they believed had passed on to her.
Further investigations revealed that the Aetherblades had tampered with the
kodan
representative
Trembling Song
's offering and threatened the racial representatives
Astorra
and
Morraloo
to carry unknown offerings to the effigy. With the suspects' testimonies and additional ingredients to finetune the Tassi device to perform a new scan, Ellen Kiel realized that Mai Trin had actually been the culprit behind the sabotage all along. Angered, Mai
summoned
her Aetherblades and First Mate Horrik to distract the party while she retreated into the concealed
Aetherblade Retreat
within the perimeter of Lion's Arch to command her troops from there.
Ellen Kiel eventually discovered the hideout and led a group of Lionguard, including the Pact Commander who had offered to help them with the investigations, to bring the elusive Aetherblade captain back to face justice for her crimes. The Lionguard fought their way through the retreat, eventually cornering Mai Trin and her first mate
Horrik
who were in the later stages of evacuating the breached base. Upon being discovered, Mai lamented that the sudden Lionguard invasion had foiled her scheme and would make Scarlet mad. The mention of this mysterious Scarlet clued the Lionguard in to the fact that the Aetherblades were actually working for some unseen mastermind.
Realizing that Scarlet might not forgive her for her failure and that the Lionguard wanted to avenge their fallen, Mai resolved to face combat on her own terms. Horrik perished in the following battle, but Mai was apprehended instead after pleading mercy as Ellen wanted the information the Aetherblade captain had.
Captain Magnus the Bloody-Handed
declared that Ellen would be eligible for the title of captain thanks to Mai Trin's
airship
which Ellen had confiscated during the raid.
Ellen would eventually rename the airship the
Phoenix Dawn
.
Battle for Lion's Arch
Mai spent time incarcerated in
Fort Marriner
and
taunted
both Ellen Kiel and the Pact Commander, who had tried to question her for her motives, while ominously stating that they should keep their eyes to the sky. She was later
freed
by her Aetherblades in 1327
AE
and taken to the
Edge of the Mists
, pursued by the Commander's allies
Braham
and
Taimi
. Braham vowed to wring Mai's neck for escaping, as the resulting chaos had led Taimi into a dangerous area where the young
asura
could have been killed. The Aetherblade captain eluded capture, however, and learned that Scarlet wanted her alive for another mission suited for her talents.
Aftermath
Marjory's later investigations revealed that Mai had been acting at the behest of Scarlet Briar, who had wanted an ally on the council. Kasmeer and Marjory speculated that it was so Scarlet could weaken the city's defenses from within.
These fears were founded once
Scarlet's Alliance
invaded
Lion's Arch.
Mai Trin and the
Molten Alliance
and
Toxic Alliance
leaders did not participate in the assault on the port city. Instead, Mai focused on carrying out the mission Scarlet had given her in the Mists; however, she did muster, stage, and send out Aetherblades to
reinforce
Scarlet's forces in Lion's Arch.
Even after Scarlet's
fall
, Mai remained elusive in
the Mists
along with some of the Aetherblades who had accompanied her there. The Aetherblade captain's evasiveness led the gossiping
Wild-Eye Miller
to speculate that Marjory might have deliberately let her go, and he claimed that Mai was actually Marjory's long-lost sister and how it was supposedly a great family shame.
End of Dragons
Interim years
After getting stranded in
the Mists
, the Aetherblade needed materials to repair their ships.
Ankka
, an Asuran Aetherblade and first mate replacing Horrik after his death, convinced Mai Trin to kill
Fractals of the Mists
versions of themselves to steal and repair their ships using the spare parts from the fractal's ships.

Notes
Mai Trin is one of the possible land transformations from the
Endless Fractal Tonic
.
Trivia
Mai Trin was originally
voiced
by
Rachel Robinson
. Since
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
, she is voiced by
Kelly Hu
.
She wields
Bonetti's Rapier
and an
Aetherized Pistol
. She wears an
Inquest Guise
,
Aetherblade Medium Shoulderpads
,
Aetherblade Medium Armguards
,
Aetherblade Medium Leggings
and
Heritage Boots
.
During
Ceremony and Acrimony
as well as
Every Piece Matters
, she wore a
Scout's Coat
,
Ascalonian Sentry Shoulderguards
,
Scout's Gloves
,
Aetherblade Medium Leggings
and
Privateer Boots
.
Mai
is a
Japanese
given name typically meaning "dance". It is also a
Chinese
name meaning "ocean" or "elegance".
During
The Future in Jade
you get to visit
Mai's apartment
where she has a number of cats, each named after a deceased Aetherblade:
Horrik
is from the
Sky Pirates
story chapter and
Captain Mai Trin Boss Fractal
.
Stormsplit
,
Tarron
and
Zonn
are elite Aetherblades that assist Mai in her Boss fractal on higher tiers.
Sparki
and
Slick
are twin mini-bosses in the
Twilight Arbor's Aetherpath
, along with
Spur
who is the foreman there.

Aetherblades
Scarlet's Alliance

Cardinal Adina
Race
Djinn
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Basalt Arena
(
The Key of Ahdashim
)
Cardinal Adina
, the earth
cardinal
, is one of the four cardinal
djinn
who rule over
Ahdashim
and decide the fate of all djinn. When
Qadim
invaded Ahdashim, Adina joined his forces and slew the
Water
and
Fire Cardinals
who opposed Qadim.
Locations
Crystal Desert
The Key of Ahdashim
Basalt Arena
Combat abilities
For walkthrough, see:
The Key of Ahdashim#Cardinal Adina
Abilities
Master of Earth
Behavior
Targets the player with the highest toughness
Effects
Unnamed defiance skill
— ...  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Pillar Pandemonium
- Damaged pillars are empowering this creature. Outgoing damage is increased by 20% per stack. Incoming damage is reduced by 20% per stack.
Radiant Blindness
- All outgoing attacks are rendered useless.
Skills
Boulder Barrage
- Bombards random foes with boulders that can be blocked by pillars. Boulders will damage the pillars and down players with
Radiant Blindness
and
bleeding
.
Diamond Palisade
-
Adina
turns her skin into diamond, reflecting projectiles and applying boons to herself. After two seconds, her skin will cast
Radiant Blindness
to those who are not facing away.
Perilous Pulse
- Creates a tremor that
knocks out
foes and destroys raised pillars within its area of effect.
Quantum Quake
-
Adina
channels deadly earth beams and scatters them around the arena.
Sand Geyser
- A geyser of sand launches foes into the air and blinds them.
Seismic Suffering
- Seismic activity that damages foes.
Stalagmites
-
Adina
conjures stalagmites that cannot be evaded or blocked. They will detonate if anyone gets close, inflicting heavy damage and destroying the underlying pillar.
Stone Fist
-
Adina
hurls forth a damaging cascade of stone and applies might to herself.
Tectonic Upheaval
-
Adina
raises a pillar that
kills
nearby foes and sends out a shock wave that
cripples
and
immobilizes
those who don't jump over it.
Terraform
-
Adina
sends out a shock wave that transforms the arena into a new shape.
Stolen skills
Detonate Plasma

Cardinal Sabir
Race
Djinn
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Fractured Conservatory
(
The Key of Ahdashim
)
Cardinal Sabir
, the air
cardinal
, is one of the four cardinal
djinn
who rule over
Ahdashim
and decide the fate of all djinn. When
Qadim
invaded Ahdashim, Sabir joined his forces and slew the
Water
and
Fire Cardinals
who opposed Qadim.
Locations
Crystal Desert
The Key of Ahdashim
Fractured Conservatory
Nirvana's Ascent
Combat abilities
For walkthrough, see:
The Key of Ahdashim#Cardinal Sabir
Abilities
Master of Air
Behavior
Targets the player with the highest toughness
Stays in the center of the arena
Changes platforms at 80% and 60% health
Effects
(only when affected by
Ion Shield
or
Repulsion Field
)
Bolt Break
—
Sabir
unleashes a series of lightning strikes that targets all foes.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Ion Shield
- Increases outgoing damage by 3% per stack.   Reduces incoming damage by 5% per stack.   Grants immunity to vulnerability.
Repulsion Field
- Generates a repelling defensive barrier. Break
Cardinal Sabir
's defiance to interrupt its generation.
Violent Currents
- Electrical currents surge through you, increasing incoming and outgoing damage. Stacks 5 times. Outgoing damage +5% Incoming damage +5%
Eye of the Storm
- The center of the storm is safe from its fury.
Skills
Bolt Break
(when defiance bar is broken)
-
Sabir
unleashes a series of lightning strikes that targets all foes.
Chain Lightning
-
Cardinal Sabir
channels electricity into his hands, launching chain lightning at his foes.
Will chain to additional players even those on far away on wisp platforms.
Dynamic Deterrent
-
Sabir
unleashes a line of electricity that knocks foes back.
Line AoE used during the main defiance bar phases. Will create 3 of them at a time in normal mode and 5 during challenge mode.
Electrical Repulsion
- Constant damage and push back during defiance bar phase. Can be blocked. Puts the
Electrical Repulsion
on players.
Fury of the Storm
- Powerful lightning bolts bombard those outside of the eye of the storm.
Storm's Edge
-
Cardinal Sabir
swings his right-hand sword, sending out electrical projectiles in an arc.
Storm's Edge
-
Cardinal Sabir
follows up his right-hand swing with a left swing, dealing damage in an arc.
Unbridled Tempest
- An expanding wave of violent electricity that deals lethal damage.
Sends out a wall of wind that downs players hit by it. Need to take a tornado into the air over it, teleport past it, use invulnerability to ignore it, or otherwise use a skill that ignores lethal damage such as
A.E.D.
or
"Rebound!"
.
Walloping Wind
(at 80% and 60% health)
-
Sabir
summons powerful winds to blow foes away.
Deals damage and knocks nearby players back. Can be blocked or evaded.
Voltaic Wisp
skills
Electrospark
- The
voltaic wisp
channels electricity into its tail, leaving time-delayed explosions behind.
Electro-Repulsion
- Constant damage and push back while it has a defiance bar. Can be blocked. Puts the
Electro-Repulsion
on players.
Paralyzing Wisp
skills
Venomous Voltage
- The voltaic wisp
[
sic
]
immobilizes foes with steady streams of paralyzing shocks from its tail.
Tornado
skills
Shocking Barrage
- Violent currents stimulate the tornado's expanding waves of charged projectiles.
Caused by touching a tornado with stacks of
Violent Currents
. Removes all stacks and sends out 4 bars of electricity across the ground that apply weakness.
Titanic Twister
skills
Dire Drafts
- Caused by touching the large red tornado, lifts you up into the air and deals massive damage until you die.
Stolen skills
Unstable Artifact

-
Movement speed
decreased by 50%; stacks duration.

regeneration decreased by 50%. 50% of hits are

Blows (50% damage).

- A powerful electrical force that repels foes and deals continuous damage. (Sabir)

- A powerfully charged force repels foes and deals continuous damage. (Voltaic Wisps)

======================================================================
PAGE: Cerus
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Cerus
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Cerus
Race
Demon
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Lonely Tower Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Temple of Febe
(
Nayos
)
Organization
Kryptis
“
Even though my brother is...gone, his dream is mine. Eparch will see that Deimos and I are truly the same. I am just as capable.
—
Cerus
Cerus, the Glaive of House Nephus
is a
Kryptis
Lord from
Nayos
, the Realm of Dreams.
He is the brother of
Deimos
and
Peitha
in House Nephus, and the mate of
Heitor
, their cousin.
Eager to prove himself to be as capable a warrior as his brother under the eyes of Midnight King
Eparch
, Cerus intends to hunt his greatest quarry yet.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
After
the World Spire
's breaking, Cerus leads his fellow Kryptis to assault
Amnytas
and the rest of
Tyria
while he singles out the
Seer
wizard
Isgarren
as his primary target to present to his master. During the invasion he
crosses paths with
the
Pact Commander
who has drawn his attention. Cerus eventually subjugates both Isgarren and the Commander but is
betrayed
by Peitha during the moment of his triumph as she chooses to side with the Commander and Isgarren instead to kill him and declare her rebellion against Eparch's tyranny in an attempt to claim the title of Midnight King for herself.
Biography
Early years
Cerus and his siblings
Deimos
and
Peitha
were born to the evolving
Kryptis
society in
Nayos
, the Realm of Dreams. As their uncle Midnight King
Eparch
and aunt
Queen Labris
tightened their hold over the realm and made preparations to one day invade
Tyria
to assault Eparch's rival, the
Seer
wizard
Isgarren
, the siblings gained power as well. The trio would be named as members of House Nephus after
General Nephus
, one of the Kryptis lords who acted as Eparch's right hand, although it remains unclear why they were associated with him over their uncle. Cerus eventually became the mate of his cousin
Heitor
.
Cerus always felt that Eparch respected Deimos more than him, however, which drove him to prove himself so that the Midnight King would realize that Cerus was just as capable as Deimos.
The Midnight King gave the Kryptis orders to invade other realms in
the Mists
and consume and corrupt their landscape and inhabitants to add these victims' power into their own, while he would feed on his subjects in turn and continued scheming for ways to send his forces en masse to invade
Amnytas
and Tyria. Cerus was personally responsible for fighting heroes from other worlds during these campaigns.
Although most Kryptis were
intimidated by their powerful king
to the point of fighting to their last breath rather than anger him, a select few had the courage to speak against Eparch's tyranny at the cost of their lives. Cerus encouraged
Kanaxai
to follow
Aurkus
's orders to execute
Lady Mosyn
, an influential Kryptis lord, at the
Coliseum of the Midnight King
for her defiance against Eparch.
Febe
, another respected lord and friend to Mosyn, spoke about autonomy and also opposed Eparch openly but was slain by Deimos in the
Temple of Febe
with Cerus and Eparch in audience. Eparch was said to have smiled upon watching Febe's fall.
Lonely Tower Fractal
After the
Astral Ward
's founding in 604
AE
, Eparch's Kryptis possessed the first
Warden of the Wizard's Court
,
Taryn
.
This ultimately led to Eparch's first invasion attempt into Tyria in 635
AE
,
where the Kryptis
targeted
the Wizard's Tower with Eparch himself leading the incursion alongside his nephews. During the assault, Cerus and Deimos killed the
Forgotten
wizard
Vass
before being forced back into
Nayos
by Astral Ward defenders, including the
mursaat
wizard
Mabon
. Isgarren and his allies were able to banish the Midnight King and the remaining invaders back into Nayos as well despite the tower sustaining heavy damage in the ensuing clash.
Interim years
When Deimos was stuck in the wards of the
Bastion of the Penitent
and slain by the
Pact Commander
and raiders many years later in 1330
AE
,
Eparch was merely content to watch and did nothing to help his champion. Peitha would remember it as another form of the Midnight King's indifference towards his subjects, while Cerus appeared to have accepted Eparch's silent judgement without voicing any overt disapproval.
Secrets of the Obscure
Act 1
Cerus during the
Kryptis
invasion of
Tyria
.
Following
the World Spire
's fracturing in the wake of the
Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
's
death
and
Aurene
's ascension affecting the
magical
balance of
the All
in 1335
AE
, the Kryptis were able to breach the
Veil
to invade Tyria en masse at last. Eparch sent Cerus,
Asthenes
and several other Kryptis champions to lead the
assault
as they targeted Isgarren and his wizards to neutralize the Astral Ward's greatest defenders one by one.
Cerus chose to subjugate Isgarren and present the Seer wizard to Eparch and thus prove his worth as a hunter.
As Cerus targeted Isgarren for possession, he sensed a solitary being from Tyria
appearing
in the Temple of Febe from a
rift
in 1336
AE
. Approaching the disoriented trespasser, he decided to toy with this flickering soul, tasting many conflicting emotions on this person whom he would later learn was the
Pact Commander
. The Kryptis Lord did not respect the Commander's personal space as he got closer while the Commander tried to keep some distance between them, all the while he tried to figure out what kind of creature he was dealing with given the depth of the Commander's emotions. However, when the Commander suddenly gained the knowledge to call the Kryptis Lord as Cerus during their clash, Cerus grew angry with his quarry's audacity to utter his true name and decided to end their little game then and there. However, the Commander was able to elude Cerus in the ensuing chase and escaped back to Tyria while the Kryptis Lord refocused on his mission to subjugate Isgarren, remaining unaware that his sister had been aiding the Tyrian interloper in secret.
Act 2
Asthenes finally succeeded in its grim task to subjugate Mabon after the mursaat wizard had spent much of his strength in saving his
dwarven
comrade
Lyhr
from possession as well as
opening
the Wizard's Tower
. Despite the possession, however, Mabon was still able to control some of his actions to set up wards in the
Bastion of Strength
in an attempt to shield parts of it from the Kryptis although his resistance continued eroding under the Kryptis's influence.
The Pact Commander who was now known as the
Wayfinder
,
Zojja
as well as members of the Wizard's Court and Astral Ward eventually
tracked down
Mabon to the Bastion of Strength. However, it turned out to be a trap as the possessed mursaat wizard was accompanied by several Kryptis which ambushed the would-be rescuers. The wizards ended up splitting up in the ensuing battle, leaving the Commander and Zojja to face Mabon alone. Asthenes emerged from Mabon's body to battle the pair directly while its comrade, Cerus, observed the battle on the sidelines via a projection. Regaining some of his willpower, the mursaat wizard urged the pair to slay Asthenes despite what it would cost him. After a fierce struggle, Asthenes was defeated albeit it led to Mabon's demise as well. Wishing to torment his quarry further, Cerus let go of Isgarren's mind for only a moment, long enough for the Seer wizard to sense his mursaat friend's demise but not long enough to let him do anything about it.
Act 3
Peitha
being playful with Cerus before
betraying
him.
When the Astral Ward had finally located Isgarren, the Wizard's Court and the Wayfinder set off to rescue Isgarren and cleanse him of possession. After chasing the Seer across the
Bastion of the Celestial
and fighting his many fragmented magics which manifested and amplified Isgarren's negative traits much to Cerus's amusement as he taunted the Astral Ward, a
final confrontation
occurred atop the World Spire itself. However, instead of simply removing the possessing Kryptis from Isgarren, a new rift opened and dragged both Isgarren and the Wayfinder into the Temple of Febe in Nayos, where Cerus had first met the Wayfinder and was now waiting for the pair.
Unable to properly move, Isgarren gave the Wayfinder what little help he could so that they could stand up and
confront
Cerus. It was not enough, however, but rescue came in the unexpected form of Peitha who distracted Cerus with her inquiries about his prize just in time. Cerus admitted to his sister that while Isgarren was reserved for Eparch, the Wayfinder had become Cerus's unexpected prize, and the Kryptis Lord now had to decide what to do with the hero. Positioning herself between her brother and his captives during the talk, Peitha used Cerus's hesitation to her advantage and lectured him about Eparch's indifference towards Deimos's demise that had finally inspired her to take a stand, while she accused Cerus of being just as bad as Eparch towards their fellow Kryptis and not as honorable as Deimos. Angered by his sister's accusations and that she had chosen to not only aid his quarry but oppose him and the Midnight King so brazenly, Cerus engaged the trio in combat. Despite proving to be a mighty opponent, Cerus ultimately fell while the trio successfully escaped from Nayos. Cerus's demise would signal the true beginning of Peitha's rebellion to liberate the Kryptis from Eparch's tyranny.
Legacy
Through the Veil
As he had done with Deimos, Eparch did not care for Cerus's fate either, deeming him just as expendable. When Peitha brought the Wayfinder to
Inner Nayos
and had her fellow Kryptis rebels shelter the stranded
Astral Ward
from Eparch's loyalists as they planned to take the fight to the Midnight King later that year, however, Heitor learned of Cerus's demise and swore to make Peitha pay for her treachery. Despite her efforts, Heitor failed to avenge her fallen mate when Peitha's party
killed her
as well.

Nayos

Notes
The
Endless Kryptis Lord Combat Tonic
originally had the
player
assume the form of Cerus. However, a later update changed the tonic form to resemble
Peitha
instead.
Trivia
Cerus is
voiced
by
Noshir Dalal
.
Cerus's name was first revealed in the press preview of the
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
expansion
.
The name may be derived from Cerus, the masculine Latin version of
Ceres
the Goddess of Agriculture and Fertility. The name stems from Proto-Indo-European *ḱerh₃-os ('nourishment, grain'), a derivative of the root *ḱerh₃- ('to feed').
The "Mysterious Voice" in the
Silent Surf Fractal
has been confirmed to be Cerus.

======================================================================
PAGE: Chief Councilor Imann
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Chief_Councilor_Imann
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
Chief Councilor Imann
Race
Human
Rank
Normal
Legendary
Location
Office of the Chief Councilor
(
Free City of Amnoon
)
Organization
Amnoon's City Council
Tyrian Alliance
“
It's been too long since this city...since these people have had anything to celebrate. Or heroes to embrace.
—
Chief Councilor Imann
Chief Councilor Imann
is the head of
Amnoon's City Council
in the
Free City of Amnoon
. Trying to weigh the options presented to her, she has to make tough decisions to ensure Amnoon's survival in the conflicts that ravage the
Crystal Desert
.
Biography
Early years
A noble, Imann eventually became the Chief Councilor of the
Free City of Amnoon
. As head of the city council, she did her best to ensure Amnoon's future even if at times her attempted neutrality brought her into conflict with her fellow nobles who felt she was being too lenient towards commoners while forgetting her upbringing.
During this time Imann came into conflict with the ambitious businessman
Zalambur
who often criticized her and the council's decisions. She also met with emissaries of
Palawa Joko
who tried to persuade her to annex Amnoon into Joko's kingdom.
Path of Fire
In 1330
AE
, the rogue
god
Balthazar
's army of
Forged
invaded the desert and had an effect on trading with their blockades and raiding parties while also driving many inhabitants of the desert to flee to
Tyria
as refugees. At Imann's behest, Joko's government sent representatives of the
Mordant Crescent
led by
Archon Iberu
to Amnoon to offer military support against the Forged. During this time Imann met with delegates from Tyria: the
Krytan
ambassador
Kasmeer Meade
and the
charr
Rytlock Brimstone
. She also witnessed the arrival of other outlanders from organizations like the
Consortium
,
Durmand Priory
and the
Black Lion Trading Company
to the city.
The
Pact Commander
eventually met with Imann and the rest of the council as well to learn more information with which to track down Balthazar and end his rampage. Imann had already heard much about the Commander's heroic deeds from the Tyrian delegates and decided to ask for the Commander's help in settling a sensitive political matter. Her fellow councilors were indecisive about whether to keep Amnoon independent or align with the
Order of the Sunspears
or Palawa Joko's kingdom for help. Each option had its pros and cons, and Imann wished to hear the opinion of an outsider who had earned a respectable reputation. After some deliberation, the Commander chose one of the three options which the Chief Councilor supported in turn.
While the Commander was away chasing after Balthazar, Imann faced the consequences of her and the Commander's choice and did her part to shape the future of Amnoon. Imann focused on helping the relief effort of displaced refugees instead of prioritizing on the needs of nobles whose houses had been buried by a recent sandstorm, which earned her the ire of some citizens in Amnoon while others were grateful for her aid or understanding of her priorities.
After the Commander had defeated Balthazar in the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
, Imann welcomed representatives from Kryta and Joko's kingdom to Amnoon for a victory celebration and met with the Commander's party. After introducing the Commander to
Grand Vizier Utumishi
, she was puzzled by the Grand Vizier's surprised reaction and hasty departure from the party. Choosing to set aside her troubling thoughts about the Grand Vizier's behavior and Joko's ambitions for Amnoon for the time being, Imann insisted on the Commander giving a speech to everyone present to lift their spirits after all the horrors they had endured.
The Chief Councilor reacted appropriately to the Commander's speech and promised to have a proper chat with the Commander later when time allowed. She also urged the Commander to enjoy the festivities in the meantime. However, the celebration came to a sudden halt when a
Brandstorm
conjured by the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
hit Amnoon.
Living World Season 4
Although the initial Brandstorm was over quickly, another storm hit Amnoon soon after and brought with it a horde of
Branded
. Imann, Zalambur, and the Pact Commander's allies from
Kryta
evacuated a number of civilians via airship while the Commander, local defenders and the
dragon
Aurene
fought off the invaders.
Janthir Wilds
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.

Trivia
Imann is
voiced
by
Mara Junot
.
In initial story development Imann was intended to collaborate with Joko more closely before the pro-Joko views were given to
Councilor Ayman
in the final draft. Some shades of these early ideas are still present with ambient dialogue involving citizens complaining about Imann's decision-making in Amnoon.

======================================================================
PAGE: Chul-Moo
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Chul-Moo
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
In this
Canthan
name, the
family name
is Park.
Chul-Moo
Race
Human
Profession
Engineer
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Brotherhood Woodlands
(
The Echovald Wilds
)
Outer Terrace
(
Dragon's End
)
Organization
Jade Brotherhood
“
If you put the minds of the Brotherhood on the problem, we'll have it solved in minutes.
—
Chul-Moo
Park Chul-Moo
is the leader of the new
Jade Brotherhood
. Coming from one of the wealthiest
Canthan
families as the son of the influential
Matron Park
, he was the co-founder of
Yu Joon
's
dragonjade
company.
However, they eventually had a fallout as Chul-Moo had started proposing less ethical ideas, to which Joon was very opposed. He is very charismatic and a great businessman.
He has a soft spot for ex-Brotherhood member
Zhou Yao
.
Biography
Early years
Park Chul-Moo was born to
Matron Park
who led one of the wealthiest
Canthan
families. He eventually became the co-founder of
Yu Joon
's
dragonjade
company in 1829
CC
.
However, ultimately they had a fallout as Chul-Moo had started proposing less ethical ideas, to which Joon was very opposed. He went as far as calling her a "visionless husk of a woman with a stick lodged so high up her rear end, it's a wonder she can bend at the waist."
Frustrated Chul-Moo founded the new
Jade Brotherhood
in 1833
CC
,
taking the name from the infamous gang that had reigned in Cantha's past. The Brotherhood began scavenging tech from Joon's
Xunlai Jade
, repurposing it for their use as well as selling knockoff, cheaper copies to Canthan families that could not afford the more reliable but more expensive Xunlai Jade tech. The Brotherhood established a base of operations within
The Echovald Wilds
, leading them into a new gang war with the ruthless nature-preserving
Speakers
led by the
warden
Tetra Earthcall
.
After
Yao
had defected from the gang and Brotherhood agent
Renho
spotted them working for Xunlai Jade in New Kaineng, the agent informed Chul-Moo about it and asked for his permission to eliminate Yao before the kid could spill the Brotherhood's trade secrets. However, Chul-Moo insisted that the agents simply watch and monitor Yao's actions for the time being and warned that if Yao came to any harm, the person responsible would see it returned threefold.
Even after Chul-Moo became a wanted criminal who could not set foot in New Kaineng or he would be arrested on sight, Matron Park kept trying to contact him via his right-hand woman
Marii
. The matron asked Chul-Moo to attend his cousin Aiya's wedding and reminded him that he should never have left Xunlai Jade and gone to the wrong side of the law.
End of Dragons
Act III
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Act V
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
The Battle for the Jade Sea
During the
dragonvoid
outbreak, Chul-Moo and the Jade Brotherhood supported
Captain Fa
's fire team on the western prong of the assault on the
Harvest Temple
, ultimately leading to a coordinated offensive against the corrupted
Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
.
Chul-Moo's forces consisted of "fearless and elite" members of the Jade Brotherhood—not including
Brotherhood Scrapper Doh
.
What Lies Beneath
At some point after the Battle for the Jade Sea, the Jade Brotherhood resumed a mining operation in
Gyala Delve
, where they discovered
Ley-Infused Jade
. After some time, Chul-Moo contacted Yao to inform them that some of the Brotherhood miners had gone missing while others were acting strangely aggressive.
During the following investigation by the Commander and the
Friend's Detective Agency
, Chul-Moo was attacked by
Jin-Lee
, a member of the Jade Brotherhood driven mad by the mysterious Haze. Despite Yao's encouragement, Chul-Moo remained reluctant to venture deeper into the mines to rescue his men
, instead relying on adventurers to help maintain order and reclaim territory throughout Gyala Delve. During this time, Chul-Moo remained at
Jadepillar Point Base
while Yao and their allies progressed deeper into the mines.
It was discovered that at the depths of the Brotherhood mine, a supposedly-unkillable demon feeds upon a
ley-line
, known as the
Ravenous Wanderer
. This demon preys upon emotional vulnerability, and its influence is responsible for driving Chul-Moo's men to madness.
What Lies Within
Emboldened, Chul-Moo coordinates an offensive to rally the Brotherhood and drive the Ravenous Wanderer to the surface using
siege turtles
. Upon driving the demon to the surface and sealing the passage, Chul-Moo and his allies employed a
Jade Behemoth
against the Ravenous Wanderer.
This would prove to be only a temporary solution, however.
In an attempt to properly lure and defeat the Ravenous Wanderer, the Commander took up the
Recollector
, a device that allowed them to revisit and capture the emotional energy of past memories.
Chul-Moo met with the Commander at
Blazeridge Steppes
, where there the two confronted the Commander's remorse over the death of
Cinder Steeltemper
. Here, Chul-Moo sympathized with the Commander's burden, offering encouragement.
After testing the Recollector's capabilities, Taimi and the Commander concluded that their supplies were insufficient in luring the demon. Chul-Moo offered his help by providing Brotherhood supplies, in hopes that something useful might be found. Among the supplies was a Kurzick rune (of questionable legality) which Rama believes can be used to seal the caves once the demon was lured away from the ley-line.
With the plan in motion, Chul-Moo joined the Commander and their allies in luring the Ravenous Wanderer, much like before, except now equipped with the Kurzick Rune. Under the demon's influence, Chul-Moo was tormented by his own anguish and fears. Distorted memories of Yao,
Binna
, Jin-Lee, and
Mal-Chin
are turned against Chul-Moo. The Commander and their allies, especially Yao, protect and encourage Chul-Moo. As Chul-Moo and the Commander progress deeper into the caves, Chul-Moo's earlier memories of Yao are revealed, showing Chul-Moo's fatherly, caring (albeit rough) nature.
Chul-Moo aids the Commander and their allies in subduing the Ravenous Wanderer for good.

Sorry. Didn't mean to be accusatory.
Nah, I get it. You're just looking out for a friend. And if there's anything that kid deserves, it's good friends. Glad they've got ya in their corner.
Same goes for you. Need a hand again, let me know.

======================================================================
PAGE: Commander Lonai
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Commander_Lonai
======================================================================

"Mysterious Figure" redirects here.  For the historical
Dry Top
NPC
, see
Mysterious Figure (Dry Top)
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
.
Commander Lonai
Race
Awakened
Profession
Necromancer
Rank
Legendary
Locations
The First City
(
Churrhir Cliffs
,
Fahranur, the First City
)
The Charge
(
Divinity's Reach
,
Mount Maelstrom
,
Frostgorge Sound
,
Rata Primus
)
Organization
Mordant Crescent
“
These fools don't know what they have: Freedom to do as they please. No master to control them.
—
Commander Lonai
Commander Lonai
is one of
Palawa Joko
's high ranking
Mordant Crescent
Awakened
. She has been leading the Awakened forces invading
Central Tyria
via
Rata Primus
asura gates
.
Biography
Early years
In life, Lonai was a member of the
Order of the Sunspears
. She was part of an elite unit led by
Nerashi
and accompanied with
Ahtok
and
Rojis
. After helping
Kormir
and
the Sunspear Hero
prepare the Sunspears for war against the ambitious
Kournan
Warmarshal
Varesh Ossa
in 1072
AE
, Lonai's unit (sans Ahtok, who joined Kormir and was subsequently killed by his greatest fear of dying: siege artillery) led the second assault on
Gandara, the Moon Fortress
.
During the assault, Lonai's unit got cut off due to
Kournan Firespitters
, though they were able to rejoin after Kormir and the Sunspear Hero had destroyed the firespitters. Creating an army of
minions
, Lonai helped lead
the charge
into the
Plaza of the Five Gods
. There, Varesh had summoned a horde of
demons
that scattered the Sunspears and captured Kormir who was blinded by one of the demons known as
the Hunger
.
Witnessing the gruesome losses had left Lonai suffering from post-traumatic stress, and she found herself unable to fight or raise minions.
As such, she remained within the
sanctuary
that was established by the Sunspear Hero and their allies and managed the outpost, all the while pressing for the Hero to lead the Sunspears after Kormir's disappearance. Even after the defeats of Varesh and the fallen
god
Abaddon
in 1075
AE
, Lonai remained with the Sunspears.
In late 1093
AE
, Lonai joined
Koss Dejarin
in gathering a group of volunteers to siege
the Bone Palace
in hopes of ending the threat of
Palawa Joko
before he could gather too much power.
This assault failed, however, and both Koss and Lonai were
Awakened
by Joko.
Living World Season 4
Daybreak
After the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
in 1330
AE
, Lonai appeared in the
dragon
Aurene
's
vision
which depicted Lonai leading an Awakened invasion of
Tyria
from within a mysterious location which turned out to be
Fahranur, the First City
where
Palawa Joko
had forcibly taken the
Pact Commander
's
asuran
ally
Taimi
to access the
Inquest
's
asura gate
network within. Lonai stood next to Taimi's tampered
golem
Scruffy 2.0
and ordered several Awakened to step through the activated gates before entering one of the gates as well while the Commander's party
faced
the Awakened rear guard and the reprogrammed Scruffy 2.0 in combat.
A Bug in the System
While the Awakened raided various locations in
Central Tyria
in 1331
AE
, Lonai joined Joko in securing the
Scarab Plague
samples from
Rata Primus
with the Commander's party in pursuit. When
confronted
, Joko ordered Lonai to kill the Commander and
Braham
, and the Awakened commander was left to carry out her orders while Joko fled through an asura gate. During the battle, the gates malfunctioned and began teleporting the battling trio across Tyria.
Their first stop was
Divinity's Reach
where Lonai attempted to Awaken local citizens while calling them fools for not understanding the luxury of freedom that they had. The second stop was in
Mount Maelstrom
where they fought within the volcano itself near an enraged
Megadestroyer
. The third stop was
Frostgorge Sound
where they were harassed by the
Claw of Jormag
. Gradually realizing the Commander and Braham's strength after their continuous defiance and that she might lose, Lonai pleaded for the pair to place her body somewhere Joko could not find and Awaken her again once she had fallen in combat. Her wish was ultimately granted on a remote rock at their fourth stop which was an unknown location in the Mists similar to the
Fractals of the Mists
, far away from Joko's reach.
Story involvement
Living World Season 4
Episode 1:
Daybreak
Eye of the Brandstorm
(cinematic only)
The First City
(as Mysterious Figure)
Episode 2:
A Bug in the System
The Charge
Combat abilities
Abilities
Awakens the Dead
Channels the Sun
Effects
Skills
Solar Strike
- Lonai swings her Sunscythe once, in a regular melee attack.
Sun Slam
- While in melee range, Lonai will do a
point emote
, and place a filling circle under the target. When filled, she will slam her Sunscythe down, dealing approximately three times her normal melee damage, and apply
3
Burning
for 5 seconds.
Desert Sun Field
- At any range, Lonai will periodically raise her Sunscythe up into the air and place a yellow AOE under a target. After a long delay, a red beam will fall on the marked zone, dealing large amounts of damage to all targets within the area every second.
Summon Dying Stars
- After her first phase (i.e. after exiting Rata Primus), Lonai will periodically gain
Shade's Protection
, and summon three
Dying Stars
. Player need to destroy each to reveal a
Solar Core
. Picking up a
Solar Core
enables the use of
Touch of the Sun
.
Summon Shade
- At 70% health, and every 10% below, Lonai will stop all attacks, and summon a Shade. While the Shade is active, Lonai gains
Shade's Protection
, causing her to be immune to all damage until the Shade is destroyed with
Touch of the Sun
Stolen skills

with no headgear,
Nightmare Court Collar

Shackles of Madness

======================================================================
PAGE: Corrupted Eagle Spirit

======================================================================

This article is about the
Spirit of the Wild
.  For the
ambient creature
, see
Eagle
. For the tamable
pet
bird
, see
Juvenile Eagle
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
.
Corrupted Eagle Spirit
Race
Spirit of the Wild
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Lost Spirits Found
(
Bjora Marches
)
Voice in the Deep
(
Drakkar Lake
,
Rime Glen
,
Eye of the North
)
Pantheon of the Wild
(
Drizzlewood Coast
)
Shrine to Eagle in
Bjora Marches
.
“
May my vision guide you to the victory I once sought.
—
Eagle
Corrupted Eagle Spirit
is one of the lesser
Spirits of the Wild
, and the natural embodiment of all
eagles
,
who stayed behind to fight the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
during the
norn exodus
.
Biography
Early years
As the natural embodiment of all
eagles
,
Eagle was revered as one of the
Spirits of the Wild
among the norn. In the past, the norn considered Eagle as one of the great Spirits.
Following the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
's
awakening
in 1165
AE
, the norn tried to defend their lands in the
Far Shiverpeaks
from its
corruption
and destruction, but they were no match for the Ice Dragon. Eagle was one of the Spirits of the Wild—alongside
Ox
and
Wolverine
—who stayed behind after
Owl
's apparent demise and fought the Elder Dragon to give the norn the time to prepare for their
exodus
to the south.
The norn did not know what had happened to Eagle since the exodus,
and the Spirit was not mentioned as often as the other Spirits. Unlike other Lost Spirits, Eagle did not get a dedicated shrine at
Lost Spirits' Hallow
in
Hoelbrak
.
No norn had been blessed to serve as
havroun
to Eagle in generations although it was unknown if this was because of Eagle's demise or because it was common for weaker Spirits, or those not close to the physical realm of
Tyria
, to be without a havroun.
The Icebrood Saga
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add events from Shadow in the Ice and Jormag Rising
During the
Vigil
's exploration of
Bjora Marches
in 1332
AE
, several enhanced
Sons of Svanir
were discovered wielding what appeared to be Eagle's spiritual essence.
Researcher Yarixx
found a way to
manipulate their spirit essence
of Vigilance to turn them strong and weak against other minions of Jormag—
Aberrant
and
Fallen
—who had been infused with the essences of Valor and Resilience related to other lost Spirits of the Wild. Beyond the Sons of Svanir being infused with Eagle's essence and building many of their camps at Eagle's abandoned shrines, there was no other clue about the ultimate fate of Eagle.
As the years passed, the fleeing
kodan
of
Still Waters Speaking
who were stranded in Bjora Marches discovered the Lost Spirits' actual fate, however. Ox, Eagle and Wolverine had failed to defeat Jormag, and had been taken and corrupted via the Ice Dragon's tempting whispers which had offered them power.
Drakkar
, Jormag's
champion
, gained the corrupted Spirits' protection while it fed on their infinite well of magic.
In 1333
AE
, Wolf summoned
Braham Eirsson
, who had fulfilled the first part of a norn prophecy via
cracking
Jormag's
impervious fang
, to
Sifhalla Ruins
in Bjora Marches. There, the Great Spirit
instructed
Braham and his companions to travel to the corrupted Spirits' shrines and demand their power in order to use their magic against Drakkar and stop the far-reaching whispers, which were plaguing the Far Shiverpeaks, so Braham could become a true norn.
Braham fulfilled Wolf's quest by
visiting the shrines
. With the help of the
Pact Commander
, norn shamans and other adventurers who were dedicated to the Spirits, he was able to summon each of the Spirits so he could ask for their aid. Once the party had
cleansed the corrupted Eagle shrine
, the Spirit initially considered Braham unworthy, prone to act without thinking of the consequences, and too dependent on the commander's plans. Braham countered Eagle's argument, reminding her that every plan has risks, and that a plen's failure doesn't necessarily mean failure, as learning from mistakes gives the foresight to lead. With Braham's words resonating with her own philosophy, Eagle accepted to give him her vision for the battle ahead.
After Braham had gained all the three Spirits' blessings and the Commander had smashed the idols of Jormag whose magic had been rejuvenating the dragon champion, the party
tracked Drakkar into its lair
where the whispers tried to turn the party members against one another.
Crecia Stoneglow
and
Rytlock Brimstone
fell to the whispers and attacked their companions, but the Pact Commander successfully tricked them to direct their attacks on the icy walls of the lair instead to force Drakkar into the open. As the dragon champion emerged from the ice, Braham channeled the powers of Ox, Eagle, and Wolverine who manifested in the lair. Stripped of its magical protection, Drakkar was defeated in a blinding flash, and the Lost Spirits vanished as the dragon champion drew its last breath.
Despite their victory over Drakkar, the corrupted Spirits were unable to wrestle free from Jormag's influence.

Corrupted Ox Spirit
Race
Spirit of the Wild
,
Fallen
Rank

(

Eye of the North
)
Pantheon of the Wild
(
Drizzlewood Coast
)
Image(s)
Portrait
“
May my strength guide you to the victory I once sought.
—
Ox
Corrupted Ox Spirit
(also known as
Dolyak
) is one of the lesser
Spirits of the Wild
, and the natural embodiment of all
oxen
,
who stayed behind to fight the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
during the
norn exodus
. He is known for his teachings in strength and perseverance.
Biography
Early years
Shrine to Ox
in
Hoelbrak
.
As the natural embodiment of all
oxen
,
Ox was revered as one of the
Spirits of the Wild
among the
norn
.
Following the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
's
awakening
in 1165
AE
, the norn tried to defend their lands in the
Far Shiverpeaks
from its
corruption
and destruction, but they were no match for the Ice Dragon. Ox was one of the Spirits of the Wild—alongside
Eagle
and
Wolverine
—who stayed behind after
Owl
's apparent demise and fought the Elder Dragon to give the norn the time to prepare for their
exodus
to the south.
The norn did not know what had happened to Ox since the exodus, and they revered him with great sorrow, along with the other Spirits who had stayed behind, at the dedicated
shrines
of
Lost Spirits' Hallow
in
Hoelbrak
.
No norn had been blessed to serve as
havroun
to Ox in generations although it was unknown if this was because of Ox's potential demise in the battle against Jormag or because it was common for weaker Spirits, or those not close to the physical realm of
Tyria
, to be without a havroun.
Forgal Kernsson
was the son of Kern, the last Dolyak Shaman.
The Icebrood Saga
During the
Vigil
's exploration of
Bjora Marches
in 1332
AE
, several
Fallen
creatures with a red aura—ranging from norn and
kodan
to local wildlife—were found roaming around various ruined shrines dedicated to the Spirits of the Wild in the
Aberrant Forest
and the
Fallen Mountains
. After learning that the Fallen were minions of Jormag,
Researcher Yarixx
found a way to
manipulate their spirit essence
of Resilience to turn them strong and weak against other minions of Jormag—the
Aberrant
and the
Sons of Svanir
—who had been infused with the essences of Valor and Vigilance related to other lost Spirits of the Wild. Beyond the Fallen's ties to Jormag and their spirit essence being related to Ox, the Vigil initially found no other clues about the ultimate fate of Ox.
As the years passed, the fleeing
kodan
of
Still Waters Speaking
who were stranded in Bjora Marches discovered the Lost Spirits' actual fate, however. Ox, Eagle and Wolverine had failed to defeat Jormag, and had been taken and corrupted via the Ice Dragon's tempting whispers which had offered them power.
Drakkar
, Jormag's
champion
, gained the corrupted Spirits' protection while it fed on their infinite well of magic.
In 1333
AE
, Wolf summoned
Braham Eirsson
, who had fulfilled the first part of a norn prophecy via
cracking
Jormag's
impervious fang
, to
Sifhalla Ruins
in Bjora Marches. There, the Great Spirit
instructed
Braham and his companions to travel to the corrupted Spirits' shrines and demand their power in order to use their magic against Drakkar and stop the far-reaching whispers, which were plaguing the Far Shiverpeaks, so Braham could become a true norn.
Braham fulfilled Wolf's quest by
visiting the shrines
. With the help of the
Pact Commander
, norn shamans and other adventurers who were dedicated to the Spirits, he was able to summon each of the Spirits so he could ask for their aid. Once the party had proven themselves in a
test of strength
to
draw Ox to his shrine
, the corrupted Spirit initially dismissed Braham as a weakling who had relied on the strength of others—his
mother
's
bow
and the magic of the
jotun scroll
—to crack the Ice Dragon's fang and how he was not worthy of his blessing. Braham countered Ox's argument by explaining that true victory came from sharing strength with others rather than hoarding it. Braham reminded Ox how the Spirit had chosen to submit under the will of Jormag and questioned if he was going to doom the norn to the same fate. Realizing the weight of Braham's words and his own fall, Ox relented and gave Braham his strength for the battle ahead.
After Braham had gained all the three Spirits' blessings and the Commander had smashed the idols of Jormag whose magic had been rejuvenating the dragon champion, the party
tracked Drakkar into its lair
where the whispers tried to turn the party members against one another.
Crecia Stoneglow
and
Rytlock Brimstone
fell to the whispers and attacked their companions, but the Pact Commander successfully tricked them to direct their attacks on the icy walls of the lair instead to force Drakkar into the open. As the dragon champion emerged from the ice, Braham channeled the powers of Ox, Eagle, and Wolverine who manifested in the lair. Stripped of its magical protection, Drakkar was defeated in a blinding flash, and the Lost Spirits vanished as the dragon champion drew its last breath.
Despite their victory over Drakkar, the corrupted Spirits were unable to wrestle free from Jormag's influence. Ox's Fallen minions continued fighting alongside the icebrood and the corrupted charr of the
Frost Legion
who had emerged in the
Drizzlewood Coast
.

Notes
Lore discrepancy
:
According to
A Spirit of Legend
, the Spirit of Dolyak is also called Ox. However, the
Shrine to Ox
in Hoelbrak and
Dolyak Totem
in
Angvar's Trove
differ in appearance. (The first is a bull, while the second is a dolyak.) During the Icebrood Saga, Ox resembles a bull.
Trivia
Ox is
voiced
by
Jocelyn Blue
.
Despite the gender of the voice actor, all references to Ox in-game use he/him pronouns, and the wiki reproduces this approach to the topic. No further information is known about Ox's gender identity.
Fallen
enemies in
Bjora Marches
drop
essence orbs
of Resilience which share the likeness of Ox.

======================================================================

======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
.
Corrupted Wolverine Spirit
Race
Spirit of the Wild
,
Aberrant
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Lost Spirits Found
(
Bjora Marches
)
Voice in the Deep
(
Drakkar Lake
,
Rime Glen
,
Eye of the North
)
Pantheon of the Wild
(
Drizzlewood Coast
)
Image(s)
Portrait
Shrine to Wolverine
in
Hoelbrak
.
“
May my valor guide you to the victory I once sought.
—
Wolverine
Corrupted Wolverine Spirit
is one of the lesser
Spirits of the Wild
, and the natural embodiment of all wolverines,
who stayed behind to fight the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
during the
norn exodus
. After failing to defeat Jormag, the Wolverine Spirit has been taken and
corrupted
via the Ice Dragon's tempting whispers to empower the
champion
Drakkar
. He is tied to the
Aberrant
.
Unlike most corrupted beings, he has occasional moments of lucidity where he recalls the way he used to be.
Braham Eirsson
eventually
confronts
Wolverine and reminds him of the Spirit's past valor while proving his worth as the Norn of Prophecy to the Spirit. Wolverine's blessing, along with the other Lost Spirits, allows Braham to
defeat
Drakkar and
open the way
to the
Sanctum of the Wild
to seek out Jormag. Despite these triumphs, the corruption remains in Wolverine who admits that he can never return to the way he was.
Biography
Early years
As the natural embodiment of all wolverines,
Wolverine was revered as one of the
Spirits of the Wild
among the
norn
.
Following the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
's
awakening
in 1165
AE
, the norn tried to defend their lands in the
Far Shiverpeaks
from its
corruption
and destruction, but they were no match for the Ice Dragon. Wolverine was one of the Spirits of the Wild—alongside
Ox
and
Eagle
—who stayed behind after
Owl
's apparent demise and fought the Elder Dragon to give the norn the time to prepare for their
exodus
to the south.
The norn did not know what had happened to Wolverine since the exodus, and the Spirit was revered with great sorrow, along with the other Spirits who had stayed behind, at the dedicated
shrines
of
Lost Spirits' Hallow
in
Hoelbrak
.
No norn had been blessed to serve as
havroun
to Wolverine in generations although it was unknown if this was because of Wolverine's demise or because it was common for weaker Spirits, or those not close to the physical realm of
Tyria
, to be without a havroun.
The Icebrood Saga
Whisper in the Dark
As the years passed, the fleeing
kodan
of
Still Waters Speaking
who were stranded in
Bjora Marches
discovered the Lost Spirits' actual fate. Ox, Eagle and Wolverine had failed to defeat Jormag, and had been taken and corrupted via the Ice Dragon's tempting whispers which had offered them power.
Drakkar
, Jormag's
champion
, gained the corrupted Spirits' protection while it fed on their infinite well of
magic
.
During the
Vigil
's exploration of Bjora Marches in 1332
AE
, several
Aberrant
creatures with a green aura—ranging from affected Vigil members to local wildlife—were found roaming around various magically potent locations such as the
Aberrant Forest
and the
Ravenfrost Caverns
. In the latter area,
Sons of Svanir
necromancers
were conducting
macabre rituals
on victims with
Ritual Anchors
and turning the summoned spirits into hostile Aberrant.
Boneskinners
, monsters created from beings who had succumbed to corrupted Wolverine's hunger in Aberrant Forest and had resorted to cannibalism, were among these Aberrant as they stalked anyone trespassing on their territory.
After learning that the Aberrant were minions of Jormag,
Researcher Yarixx
found a way to
manipulate their spirit essence
of Valor to turn them strong and weak against other minions of Jormag—
Fallen
and the Sons of Svanir—who had been infused with the essences of Resilience and Vigilance related to other lost Spirits of the Wild. Beyond the existence of the Aberrant and their ties to Jormag, there was no other clue about the ultimate fate of Wolverine at the time.
Shadow in the Ice
In 1333
AE
,
Wolf
summoned
Braham Eirsson
, who had fulfilled the first part of a norn
prophecy
via
cracking
Jormag's
impervious fang
, to
Sifhalla Ruins
in Bjora Marches. There, the Great Spirit
instructed
Braham and his companions to travel to the corrupted Spirits' shrines and demand their power in order to use their magic against Drakkar and stop the far-reaching whispers, which were plaguing the Far Shiverpeaks, so Braham could become a true norn.
Braham fulfilled Wolf's quest by
visiting the shrines
. With the help of the
Pact Commander
, norn shamans and other adventurers who were dedicated to the Spirits, he was able to summon each of the three Lost Spirits so he could ask for their aid. Once the party had
cleansed the corrupted Wolverine shrine
, the Spirit initially dismissed Braham as a coward without valor, figthing only for himself, only to impress and to prove out of fear. Braham assuredly admitted that he indeed wanted to prove himself and earn his legend, and added that doubt was not a weakness, but made him want to be stronger, knowing he could be better. Wolverine was convinced enough to give Braham his blessing of valor for the battle ahead.
After Braham had gained all the three Spirits' blessings and the Commander had smashed the idols of Jormag whose magic had been rejuvenating the dragon champion, the party
tracked Drakkar into its lair
where the whispers tried to turn the party members against one another.
Crecia Stoneglow
and
Rytlock Brimstone
fell to the whispers and attacked their companions, but the Commander successfully tricked them to direct their attacks on the icy walls of the lair instead to force Drakkar into the open. As the dragon champion emerged from the ice, Braham channeled the powers of Ox, Eagle, and Wolverine who manifested in the lair. Stripped of its magical protection, Drakkar was defeated in a blinding flash, and the Lost Spirits vanished as the dragon champion drew its last breath.
Jormag Rising

.
