======================================================================
PAGE: General Nephus
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/General_Nephus
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
General Nephus
Race
Demon
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Nyedra, Dreamer's Sanctum
(
Inner Nayos
)
Spire of Dreams
(
Inner Nayos
)
Organization
Kryptis
“
Bring us something other than death.
—
General Nephus
General Nephus
is an enigmatic
Kryptis
residing in
Nayos
, the Realm of Dreams. The denizens of the realm are divided on him; some seem to almost revere him while others get angry when his name is mentioned.
Nephus is said to be a kind of Kryptis folk hero and is believed to live deep within Midnight King
Eparch
's territory despite having some disagreements with the Midnight King.
Peitha
and
Ramses
both respect him.
He shares the name with House Nephus which includes
Cerus
,
Deimos
, and Peitha, but it is unknown what his actual relation to the siblings is.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Nephus is eventually revealed to be one of the founders of Kryptis nobility and Eparch's former right hand before their disagreements on Kryptis future made Eparch exile him. Due to Peitha's
manipulations
, Nephus is ultimately forced to end his neutrality in the civil war and side with her rebellion to preserve the lives of his followers from Eparch's wrath. In the resulting conflict Nephus assists the alliance of Kryptis rebels and
Astral Ward
in
neutralizing
Queen Labris
and
assaulting
Eparch's headquarters in the
Citadel of Zakiros
where the Midnight King is
cornered
and ultimately
slain
. Relieved to see that Peitha has chosen to discard Eparch's essence instead of hoarding it for herself upon her ascension to the throne, Nephus acknowledges her as the new Midnight King and
agrees to the treaty
with the Astral Ward. Although an air of uncertainty hangs around Nayos regarding whether the Kryptis might become a threat to
Tyria
again in the future, Nephus personally promises the Ward to uphold the peace as long as he draws breath.
Biography
Early years
Nephus was born in
Nayos
and eventually founded House Nephus, being one of the respected
Kryptis
leaders. Sometime during the previous
dragonrise
,
Eparch
met with the founders and told them of his experiences on
Tyria
and how the
Seer
wizard
Isgarren
had banished him and was supposedly planning the invasion of Nayos and the extermination of all Kryptis. Eparch's dire warning infuriated the founders and eventually rallied them behind him, and they began preparing the Kryptis for war against Isgarren and any who stood with the Seer.
Nephus was named the Grand General of the Kryptis forces and acted as Eparch's second-in-command. He gained a reputation for being "a brilliant strategist, a peerless warrior, and a noble leader," and earned
Peitha
and
Ramses
's respect.
Eparch and Labris's niece
Peitha
and nephews
Cerus
and
Deimos
were named as members of House Nephus.
As Eparch's influence grew, he named himself Midnight King and made
Labris
his queen. He, the founders, and various advisors and generals gathered periodically at the
Council of Houses
in what would eventually be turned into Eparch's base of operations in the
Citadel of Zakiros
where the nobles would offer their counsel to the king.
The Kryptis sent their forces in varying numbers to find a way through the
Veil
to invade Tyria over the following centuries although their attempts were thwarted every time by local heroes or Isgarren's fellow wizards and the
Astral Ward
.
Eparch grew more ambitious and greedy over time, and he began controlling and influencing Kryptis society more and more with his twisted vision. The Midnight King eventually stopped seeking his advisors' counsel and began quelling any unrest among his subjects violently, choosing to devour his own people to gain more strength as he had come to believe that the only way for the Kryptis to thrive against Isgarren and other threats was for him to become the most powerful Kryptis in existence.
Nephus served Eparch loyally for a time during various times of Kryptis unrest and uprisings known as Disquiets,
but he eventually began disagreeing with Eparch's brutal methods and refused to carry out the Midnight King's orders.
Eparch was angered by Nephus's reluctance and decided to act before Nephus could begin another uprising. The loyalists slaughtered many of Nephus's followers in public although Nephus and his remaining forces managed to march all the way to the edge of Zakiros to confront the king's superior army. Nephus ultimately relented at the last possible moment and agreed to a truce rather than allowing his remaining forces to fall in what appeared to be a losing battle. The disgraced general was spared only to serve forever as proof of the king's "mercy"; he was exiled to the corners of Kryptis society to live out his life with his remaining followers.
However, Eparch had another reason for sparing Nephus: the general commanded too much loyalty from soldiers, and his death would only cause a greater uprising which the king could not afford at the time.
Secrets of the Obscure
The Realm of Dreams
The
Pact Commander
, using the title of
Wayfinder
, and the Astral Ward first heard about General Nephus from whispers in
Inner Nayos
in 1336
AE
, initially unsure of what to make of these rumors.
When the moment came to push further into Nayos in early 1337
AE
, Peitha
suggested
enlisting Nephus's help. The Astral Ward was not ready to commit entire legions of soldiers, only volunteers, and so Peitha needed to find local Kryptis who would be willing to not follow Eparch's orders and follow someone else instead, someone who commanded respect, such as Nephus. Peitha and her lieutenant
Ramses
explained how the general had been Eparch's former second-in-command and had been kept contained in his exile while remaining elusive.
Peitha's scouts eventually tracked Nephus down to his last known location on the outskirts of
Nyedra, Dreamer's Sanctum
. Peitha, Ramses, and the Wayfinder ventured deeper into the region,
finding
the disgraced general in the company of his defensive followers who believed that Peitha was approaching them with ill intent. Before a brawl could ensue, Nephus told his soldiers to calm down and offered Peitha a chance to speak her mind. Before her speech could gain momentum, Queen Labris and her royal guard arrived and tried to subdue Peitha's party by force. Peitha urged the onlookers to join with her and fight, but Nephus, in an attempt to remain neutral and protect his people from slaughter, ordered them not to take up arms against the queen's forces. The Kryptis queen, stately yet menacing, eventually called off her forces and made her proposal: in exchange for helping her turn Peitha, Ramses, and the Wayfinder over into loyalist custody, Nephus and his people would be granted clemency and left in peace in Nyedra with both the queen and king's blessing.
Nephus seemed initially receptive to the proposal. However, upon realizing how callously Labris was disrespecting the honor code of guests and threatening Nephus and his followers despite them having nothing to do with Peitha's visit, the general's anger finally boiled to the surface. He chose to swear fealty to Peitha and renounced Labris and Eparch as the alternative would be to live in fear forever. His and Peitha's combined forces turned the tide and made Labris flee. When Peitha thanked Nephus for siding with her, the general answered in tightly controlled fury that his hand had been forced. He challenged Peitha to make up for the violence and misery that her brother and uncle had inflicted upon the Kryptis, and stormed off from the scene.
The party rendezvoused with Nephus outside of
Eventide's March
, a sacred landmark that Queen Labris and her dwindling army were using as a base of operations after being
cornered
there by the spreading uprising in Nyedra. The general split the rebel forces, sending Peitha, Ramses, and the Astral Ward representative
Arina
to attack the queen's position from the flanks while he and the Wayfinder led a direct frontal assault to the Kryptis queen's last line of defense. With nowhere left to run, Labris made her stand and fought fiercely but was ultimately defeated. Nephus was prepared to execute the weakened queen; however, seeing an opportunity to rally the Kryptis to her side with a show of force, Peitha stayed Labris's execution as she intended to present the queen as her prisoner and a bargaining chip in a public showing at the Coliseum of the Midnight King to prove that the growing rebellion could successfully defy Eparch. Realizing Peitha's plan after initial hesitation, Nephus decided to carry it out. He asked the Wayfinder to locate an old Kryptis,
Tecla the Venerable
, who had knowledge of an array of beacons spread across Inner Nayos which could be used to summon a large crowd to the coliseum.
Eparch finally made an appeararance when Peitha presented the chained Labris to a large Kryptis crowd and made her rallying speech for the king's subjugated people to join the rebel cause. The Midnight King showed his power by immobilizing the Wayfinder and all the members of the
Astral Ward
, and proceeded to mock Peitha and ask if any of the listening Kryptis wished to follow her, watching in delight as the crowd hesitated. He then made Peitha an offer to spare Tyria and allow her to live there in exile if she would release his queen. However, even in her weakened state, Labris would not allow Peitha to use her as leverage to undermine all that she and Eparch had built. The defiant queen sacrificed herself to her beloved king's cause, forcing the last of her essence into Eparch as he and the rest of the coliseum looked on in horror.
The crowd erupted in chaos—some declaring allegiance to Peitha, some to Eparch. The rest fled in terror. Even the king was stunned by his queen's martyrdom. Roaring in fury, he vowed to devour all who opposed him and challenged any who stood with Peitha to bring the fight to him in the Citadel of Zakiros. Eparch did not exact vengeance at that moment against expectations, however, as he chose to retreat to the citadel hastily. Despite the seeming reprieve caused by the king's exit, Nephus told his allies to stay on their guard nevertheless as Labrir's death would not only end all attempts at diplomacy as Eparch would focus on exterminating the entire rebellion, while many Kryptis would rally behind their king to honor the queen's sacrifice.
The Midnight King
Despite his promise to consume everything and the rebels forces having been weakened by constant battles in the following months, Eparch remained in the safety of his citadel behind a layer of protective fog that would harm any intruder. Nephus grew impatient with the Kryptis rebels' and the Astral Ward's lack of progress against the loyalists while also growing worried about Eparch not having retaliated yet. By the time the Wayfinder
visited
the rebel headquarters, Nephus was having a heated debate with
Frode
about whether to wait for reinforcements or proceed against Zakiros to take the fight to Eparch even though Nephus was expecting some trick from Eparch if they did so. Peitha's arrival ended the debate for a moment only for Eparch's elite
assassins
to target her and forcing Nephus and his allies to defend her. After a successful defense, Peitha pointed out that she had sensed fear on the assassins' remains, suggesting that Eparch might not be as strong as he had led the rebels to believe. On Peitha's orders, the combined rebel and Astral Ward forces marched to the coliseum to begin the assault on Zakiros.
Fortifying the allied forces' position at the coliseum and receiving unexpected help from the wizards
Dagda
and
Zojja
, Nephus acted the part of Grand General and began issuing orders for separate teams to carry out their duties simultaneously to destroy the citadel's defenses while using the opportunity to divide and thus weaken the remaining loyalist defenders. As Frode and Peitha led the separate teams deeper into Eparch's lair, Nephus kept a contingent outside to secure the courtyard and protect their flanks in case loyalist reinforcements arrived.
With help from the Astral Ward reinforcements brought by the wizards
Dagda
and
Zojja
, the strike team successfully penetrated into the
Spire of Dreams
where the Kryptis houses of old had convened prior to Eparch's tyranny. While there, they witnessed another
grand entrance
from Eparch: this time, however, the Midnight King had been consumed by his hunger and had taken on a massive monstrous form, using his power amassed over the centuries to battle the intruders while summoning zealous reinforcements via rifts. After a lengthy battle, the rebels succeeded in drawing out many of Eparch's consumed essences by attacking his hardened carapace as well as damaging him from the inside when he tried to devour some of his opponents. Drained, the Midnight King reverted to his humanoid form and retreated up his central tower to his throne while muttering that he needed even more power.
Wasting no time, Peitha chose to pursue the weakened Eparch despite sensing his army still lying in wait within the citadel, and Nephus supported her decision. The team came to the realization that Eparch might still triumph if his army came to assist him, so the strike team split up: Dagda and Frode would lead most of the assault force to secure Zakiros and cover the flanks and, if the battle ended up badly, evacuate as many Kryptis rebels to Tyria to avoid the vengeful Midnight King's wrath. Meanwhile, Peitha led the Wayfinder, Nephus, Ramses, and Arina to
finish the fight
or die trying.
While ascending the tower, the group came across Kryptis memories that revealed details about both the Midnight King's past as well as the sensations of his many victims. They suddenly sensed a change in Eparch's army ahead and hurried onward, only to find themselves in a reliquary room where they learned the grim truth: there was no army left as countless number of Kryptis had been sacrificed and turned into essence-filled relics to feed the king's hunger as he had consumed them just moments before to recover his lost strength.
As the group reached the Midnight Throne, Eparch lay in wait, accompanied by his twin commanders,
General Alkaeus
and
General Zantharon
, and some of the remaining loyalists. The Midnight King was no longer willing to negotiate terms: he demanded nothing less than Peitha's life as compensation for the rebellion. Instead of submitting, however, Peitha insulted both Eparch and the late Labris by claiming that the latter had been the real strategist of Eparch's campaign and had died for nothing. Snarling in rage at the insult to his wife and queen, Eparch's control snapped. Hungry for even more power, he shockingly consumed his own general, Alkaeus, in blind fury. Zantharon, seeing his own brother murdered and absorbed by the king he had sworn fealty to, turned against Eparch. The throne room erupted, loyalists shouting accusations and changing sides as Eparch himself attacked indiscriminately.
Despite facing not only Peitha's rebels but Zantharon's turncoats, Eparch was practically invincible. Fast as the attackers could hurt the Midnight King, the strength of the Kryptis essence healed him faster. Just before Eparch could triumph, a portal ripped open, and Isgarren stepped through. The wizard and the Midnight King faced each other for the first time in centuries as the battle raged around them. Eparch continued exuding control due to his empowered state, however; even Isgarren could not harm him now by himself. To even the odds, the wizard lord freed Peitha's forces from Eparch's spell and urged her to join her power with his while also telling the Wayfinder to use the
Heart of the Obscure
to further boost their joint attack. The resulting blast of
magic
was strong enough to prevent the Midnight King from healing himself.
Through their combined efforts, Eparch finally fell. In his last moments, the king of the Kryptis conceded that Peitha would rule. He acknowledged that his niece would take the throne, that she would be the Midnight King. Knowing his fate was sealed, Eparch did not scream in defiance; he instead urged Peitha to consume him, to take his power and use it, as he had, to lead the Kryptis from a position of strength, reminding her that the Kryptis had to be predators or else they would become prey and be destroyed. Peitha lifted Eparch into the air, and then, in a display of both strength and willpower against expectations, she blasted him into pieces instead of consuming his essence to empower herself, stating that this strength had never been Eparch's to give and she would not rule the same way as he had.
Nephus was angry with Isgarren for only showing up at the last possible moment when his survival was all but assured. When the Seer countered the general's accusation by stating that he never intended to risk anything for the Kryptis and was willing to sacrifice them to rid both worlds of Eparch if needed, Nephus coldly stated that it would be best if the two of them never ended up standing on the opposite sides of the battlefield. As emissaries of various Kryptis houses began appearing in the throne room, Nephus reassured the Wayfinder that they had come to pay their respects to their new king and would not become an immediate threat as the power vacuums would be filled in the wake of the civil war that had cost many Kryptis lives on both sides.
Nephus, Ramses, and Zantharon
accompanied
Peitha to
the Wizard's Tower
where Isgarren formally acknowledged Peitha as the new Midnight King while making her and Nephus agree to stop the Kryptis invasions and allow a group of Astral Ward to remain in Nayos indefinitely and hunt down remaining Eparch loyalists to ensure that the now atoning Kryptis would keep their word. In subsequent conversations, Nephus answered the Wayfinder's lingering questions, assuring them that he had no desire for the Midnight Throne and preferred his role as a general and an advisor as he had once been. The general was pragmatic about Zantharon's changing allegiance and the reason for sparing him despite the latter's crimes as Zantharon could use his influence to help sway some of the remaining loyalists to Peitha's side. Nephus also comforted the Wayfinder by saying that as long as he drew breath, he would not allow another war between Nayos and Tyria.

General Zantharon
Race
Demon
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations

(
Citadel of Zakiros
)
Sovereign of Nayos
(
The Wizard's Tower
)
Organization
Kryptis
“
I have given everything for the survival of the Kryptis. I have acted always for the future of Nayos. But I see what you cannot—that the Kryptis can only be led through strength. I did what needed to be done. Do you have the conviction to do the same?
—
General Zantharon
General  Zantharon
is a powerful
Kryptis
general and the twin of
Alkaeus
who resides in
Nayos
.
Eparch
keeps both of them close for his own comfort but has occasionally unleashed them to commit horrific acts against his enemies.
He is encountered in the throne room during the related quest. After Eparch consumes his fellow general,
General Alkaeus
, in a fit of rage after
Peitha
and her forces breaches the throne room and threatens Eparch, this causes Zantharon to turn on Eparch and ally himself with Peitha.
General Zantharon is later present during the peace treaty signing at
The Wizard's Tower
, though it is clear that, despite siding with Peitha against the Midnight King in the spur of the moment, he doesn't hold her in high esteem and remains a prominent voice within the beaten loyalist side.

Giganticus Lupicus
Race
Risen
Level
82
Rank
Legendary
Location
The Grand Temple of the Pantheon
(
The Ruined City of Arah
)
The
Giganticus Lupicus
was once the
Temple's Guard
of
The Grand Temple of the Pantheon
in
the City of Arah
, but is now just another servant of
Zhaitan
without a cause after the defeat of its
Elder Dragon
master.
In life, it was one of the
Great Giants
, a race whose extinction is thought to predate the arrival of the
Gods
on
Tyria
by thousands of years. As such, it is unknown how it came to be a Temple Guard in service of the Gods, or how it was affected by the
Exodus of the Gods
,
the Cataclysm
, or the
Rising of Orr
(saving that the latter ended in this creature's reanimation). It is also possible that the creature did not come from
Orr
originally, but was commanded there by Zhaitan post-reanimation to protect the Dragon's greatest secrets, but this still leaves the mystery of where it was from, and how Zhaitan obtained its corpse.
Locations
Ruins of Orr
The Ruined City of Arah
The Grand Temple of the Pantheon
Temple's Guard
Combat abilities
For a specific phase, please click on one of the following:
All phases
Abilities
Summons Insects
Shadow Walk
Drains Life
Effects
Stun
— Vulnerable while broken.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Behavior
See also:
The Ruined City of Arah (explorable)#Giganticus Lupicus
The giant uses different attacks in three different phases; when switching from phase to another, it briefly becomes
determined
.
Phase 1
Effects
Empowered
- upon devouring a grub
Skills
Summon Grub
- curses one player, who will be
knocked down
after a few seconds, while a
Risen Grub
spawns. Lupicus will attempt to consume the grub to
empower
itself, greatly increasing its damage and armor.
Summons Insects
- summons several
Elite Locust Swarms
which attack players, dealing moderate damage and causing
Bleeding
.
Mighty Kick
- short-range heavy damage kick that
knocks back
. Because Lupicus remains mostly stationary and does not chase, he will do this attack only when players move into melee range (<250 range).
Phase 2
This phase occurs at 75% HP. The giant will stop summoning grubs and insects and will start attacking players more directly. While transitioning into phase two, he will infect the closest ally with a grub which is unavoidable.
Skills
Lupine Blast
- shadow walks to a targeted player, charging towards them and dealing heavy damage and
knocking down
in a moderately large area when it reaches the targeted player. Evades all attacks while casting this ability.
Necrid Bolt
- casts a powerful projectile at a single target, exploding on contact to cause
Poisoned
and moderate damage.
Necrid Barrage
- casts a rain of projectiles, covering much of the arena with area of effect circles; projectiles explode upon hitting the ground, doing damage in an area. These projectiles fly outwards and can be reflected.
Melee claw attack
- kicks players in mele range with its right claw, doing massive damage,
Knockback
, and three stacks of
Bleeding
.
Phase 3
During the final phase, the giant will switch from heavy-hitting "spread" attacks to more specialized
life stealing
attacks.
Life Drain
- points at a player and
drains their health
. Ticks 4 times, can be avoided with a double dodge.
Necrid Bolt
- casts a powerful projectile at a single target, exploding on contact to cause
Poisoned
and moderate damage.
Necrid Trap
- casts a
ward
dome spell which traps players within,
draining
a large amount of their health with each pulse for 5 seconds. The wall of the dome
knocks back
players.
Frenzied Blast
- casts a rain of projectiles in a small AoE in front of it. Can be reflected. Will not cast if players are too close to him.
Life Drink
- levitates and
drains health
from every unit in an ~800 range PBAoE every second.
Knockback
(~300 range) at the end of the cast.
Lupine Death
- Upon defeat, Lupicus will fall, causing a moderate amount of damage to any players nearby.

has an article on

.

jackal
, which, with the creature being a temple guard, is reminiscent of some cultural depictions of
Anubis
.

Reason:
Missing informations about EoD story
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
,
The Icebrood Saga
,
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
, and
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Gorrik
Race
Asura
Profession
Engineer
Holosmith
Level
80
Rank
Normal
Legendary
Locations
Thaumanova Reactor Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Allied Encampment
(
Domain of Kourna
)
Sun's Refuge
(
Jahai Bluffs
)
The Grotto
(
Thunderhead Peaks
)
Pact Command
(
Dragonfall
)
Skyscale Eyrie
(
Sky's Reach
)
Rusty Meadows
(
Grothmar Valley
)
Hall of Monuments
(
Eye of the North
)
Howling Caves
(
Gyala Delve
)
Organization
Brim & Scarab Detective Agency
Dragon's Watch
Inquest
Services
Renown Heart
Part of
Ready your forces for the assault on Kralkatorrik
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
Rest assured, you've got expertise and a fair dose of brilliance on your side.
—
Gorrik
Gorrik
is an
asuran
entomologist,
holosmith
and foremost expert on
scarabs
who formerly worked for
Inquest
. His older brother,
Blish
,
is a
college
friend of
Taimi
's.
Biography
Early years
Gorrik got to know
Taimi
when she and his older brother
Blish
befriended one another while attending the
College of Synergetics
. Gorrik was a member of the
Inquest
early on, while Blish was still in college, working at the
Thaumanova Reactor
alongside
Ankka
, who would later join the
Aetherblades
.
In 1326
AE
, Gorrik studied the
Elonian
sand moth, as part of his research in the field of entomology.
Later, Blish eventually transferred his consciousness into a
Mark I Golemite
body. This technology was developed by the Inquest, but until the
M.I.G.
project by
Hronk
's
krewe
, always proved fatal.
Living World Season 4
A Bug in the System
In 1331
AE
, Blish and Gorrik were working at
Rata Primus
on the
Scarab Plague
samples taken from
Fahranur, the First City
. Gorrik was against testing the samples on living subjects but was nevertheless fascinated by the results for scientific reasons.
The brothers discovered the later
Awakened
invasion that was being covered up by the megacomplex's upper echelons. They took it upon themselves to send out distress signals and barricade the plague samples, as well as attempted to tamper with the Primus gate network that allowed travel without a receiving gate in order to hamper with Awakened attempts to use the gates for their own purposes.
Blish and Gorrik were contacted by the
Pact Commander
and
Braham Eirsson
who had spearheaded an
Olmakhan
assault on Rata Primus to investigate what the Awakened and Inquest were up to. Blish was surprised to hear Taimi's voice coming out of the Commander's personal remote communicator but quickly set the surprise aside as the brothers instructed the team where to find the gate hub so it could be shut down.
The brothers kept Awakened invaders from reaching the bottom lab and hid there long enough for the Commander and Braham to come to the rescue. Blish introduced himself to the newcomers, but before Taimi could inquire why his voice sounded different, the Commander suggested that they keep the reveal of Blish being a golem from Taimi until after they had reunited with her in person. Blish and Gorrik revealed that the Awakened presence had triggered the decontamination protocol which would make the complex self-destruct with no way of stopping it.
Teaming up with the Commander, they rushed to the vaults containing the plague samples, only to discover the samples had already been taken. They headed for the gate hub next, witnessing
Palawa Joko
and
Commander Lonai
in possession of the samples and about to flee through an asura gate. Joko taunted the Commander by promising to unleash the Scarab Plague on
Central Tyria
before entering the portal with the samples, leaving Lonai behind to deal with the group. During the resulting clash the gate, which Joko had tampered with to prevent anyone from pursuing him, began malfunctioning and sucked the Commander, Braham and Lonai in.
Blish and Gorrik began working on the gate to stabilize it and return the Commander and Braham from wherever the gate had sent them, using Lonai's energy signature to direct a portal to the trio's location. However, the attempts failed at first as the battling trio were being teleported around Tyria before they ended up in an unknown location somewhere in
the Mists
where Lonai was finally defeated. The brothers were ultimately successful in teleporting the Commander and Braham aboard
Sayida the Sly
's airship outside of Rata Primus and fled the complex with Gorrik's pets just as it was destroyed in an explosion.
When reunited with Taimi, she expressed fascination with Blish's condition, a welcomed change to the usual disgust or horror the brothers saw. After learning that Blish had managed to back up Inquest data on the Scarab Plague and the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
, Gorrik warmed up to the idea of working with Taimi as long as they discussed authorship hierarchy for any published research and stipulated that Taimi did not have supervisory authority.
Long Live the Lich
After traveling to the
Free City of Amnoon
, Gorrik and Blish began researching the Scarab Plague. Gorrik discovered that the scarabs only infected humans and planted
Petey
, another species of beetle, under his skin to test another theory of his. But locals discovered what Gorrik was up to and arrested him after mistaking him for planning to unleash some pestilence in the city. While Gorrik was put on trial, the Cavaliers impounded his materials at the docks, including Blish who was shoved into one of the crates with his neural net cut off.
After the Commander and Taimi had helped clear the charges against Gorrik, he and Taimi went to the docks to retrieve those impounded materials. While checking his things, Gorrik incautiously released some of his bugs, and the commander and Faren, who was there for the upcoming Tyrian delegation ship, had to squash these bugs. Then, Gorrik and Taimi found the power downed Blish. Upon being rebooted, Blish returned to consciousness with a horrified scream, letting his companions know of his lack of sensory experiences in what he described as nothingness while he had been shut down. Taimi and Gorrik hastily promised never to let him experience that kind of state again and monitored him while the Commander left them to meet with
Lord Faren
and
Councilor Mayameen
who were waiting for the supply ship to arrive at the docks.
Blish, Gorrik and Taimi went to the docks later and learned that the Commander had barely prevented an outbreak of the first generation of Scarab Plague carried by the crew of the supply ship whom Joko had secretly infected.
While his brother was setting up the portal to Kourna, Gorrik continued his research about the scarab plague. But then, Petey, which is the beetle Gorrik planted under his skin, jumped out from under Gorrik's skin and ate all the plague samples, by doing that Petey grew bigger. So Gorrik thought he could grow Petey big enough to ride, and started the study immediately. He also asked the Commander to collect various things to help Petey.
Gorrik continued his research until the
Battle of Gandara
. He and the others followed the Commander to the front gate of the
Moon Fortress
, but was stopped by the third generation of scarabs, and had fall back to Rytlock's position while the Primeval Queens
Nahlah
and
Dahlah
cleaning the scarabs while the commander chasing after Braham.
Gorrik and Taimi went inside the fortress after the third-generation epidemic was averted and learned that Joko was dead. Gorrik then surprisingly found lots of pestilence samples to study, but Taimi decided everything about the plague will be destroy after Gorrik collect the data.
A Star to Guide Us
While the rift anomalies of
Jahai Bluffs
were being investigated, Gorrik was with Taimi, observing the data obtained by the Commander and theorizing with Taimi and Blish over what might have caused them. In
Sun's Refuge
, Gorrik helped Blish to calm down all the refugees. After the Commander returned from the Mist without Blish, Gorrik walked away from the group with sorrow.
All or Nothing
In
Thunderhead Peaks
, Gorrik helped with logistics for the Pact and allied forces, setting up a shop in the Grotto, though he had requested a help from the Commander.
Later, Gorrik and Taimi used the tracker Blish made to evacuate several villages before Kralkatorrik’s minions broke through. When the
final battle
initiated, Taimi and Gorrik stayed high above the fight and gave the Commander information from above. After Kralkatorrik got away and
Aurene
was killed, Taimi and Gorrik found her branded body. When the Commander got there, they were grieving for her.
War Eternal
When the Commander returned to Thunderhead Peaks to
prepare Aurene’s body
, Gorrik and Taimi was there waiting. After Aurene was revived, Gorrik sent Kralkatorrik telemetry to help track down him.
At
Dragonfall
, Gorrik asked the Commander to
plant his instruments
to collect data about the terrain. The data shown that Kralkatorrik was healing throught ley line energy on the island. To cut down Kralkatorrik from ley line, Gorrik and Taimi helped the Commander made the new
Dragonsblood Spear
.
Gorrik was not on the airship in the aftermath of Aurene's
ascension
. According the Taimi, Gorrik was doing research on the
skyscales
, and the Commander eventually found themselves helping Gorrik
save
the skyscales from extinction by
infusing the eggs
of the first skyscale generation with various types of magic in order to stabilize the life cycle of the second generation. Gorrik acted as the newborn skyscales' caretaker to monitor their health while instructing the Commander to carry out various tasks such as locating skyscales which had
fled
from the nest. Upon discovering how one of the skyscales had bonded with the Commander, Gorrik suggested that the Commander and the skyscale learn to work together and close various rifts which had formed around Elona and Tyria because of Kralkatorrik's rampage through the Mists. To ensure that the Commander could always call on their dragon mount, Gorrik gave them a whistle which the skyscale would respond to.
The Icebrood Saga
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography with events from Champions and aftermath.
Prologue: Bound by Blood
Gorrik joined Dragon's Watch as they attended the
All-Legion Rally
in
Grothmar Valley
where the charr
High Legions
were celebrating Kralkatorrik's demise. Taimi chose not to attend the festivities as she focused on working on her treatise, titled "The Elder Dragons Reconsidered." However, she insisted that Gorrik should accompany the Commander's party to Grothmar so he could "live a little." Gorrik reluctantly did what Taimi had asked but only for a chance to study the local fauna.
While in Grothmar, Gorrik mostly spent his time on
Erasmus Fangstorm
's farm helping
remove
devourers
from killing the cattle. He complained whenever the comm system was interrupted with unimportant conversations between the Commander and various Dragon's Watch members.
Gorrik was approached by a group of charr during the festivities. He was forced to accompany them to the
War Room
in the
Blood Keep
where the charr, revealed to be
Renegades
, interrogated him about what he knew about Aurene's powers and weaknesses and threatened to harm him if he did not tell them anything of value. Gorrik managed to
contact the Commander
about his kidnapping before the Renegades broke the comm device, and the Commander rushed to the rescue while using Kasmeer's
mesmer
spell to appear as the famed
Blood Legion
Centurion
Ryland Steelcatcher
. Using either guile or brute force, the Commander saved Gorrik from the Renegades who were revealed to be working for Ryland and
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
. Gorrik was sent to safety while the Commander continued investigations in the valley about what the Blood Imperator was up to.

Research in the Eye of the North
After witnessing the fatal clash between the two Elder Dragons, Taimi and Gorrik returned to the Eye of the North. They set up a laboratory near Aurene so they could study and investigate the dragon cycle and any changes to the world from the magical fallout.
As there were no visible consequences from the deaths of the dragons yet, Taimi suggested that either Aurene was able to contain the excess magic alone, something else got its share of magic, or that the world is simply short on time.
To help with the research, Gorrik proposed to the Commander that they could use the Scrying Pool to search the Commander's memories of their past battles and struggles against the Elder Dragons. Although the Commander initially protested, in the end they accepted and went to relive their memories, starting with
following the disturbance
in
Brisban Wildlands
while Gorrik and Taimi went onto studying and recollecting provided data.

Act 1

Gorrik received a message from
Ankka
, a friend and colleague from his
Inquest
days, asking to meet up in
Lornar's Pass
. This was a trap, and Gorrik was kidnapped by Ankka and the
Aetherblades
. The Commander gave chase but was unable to rescue Gorrik without crash-landing an Aetherblade ship in
Cantha
.
While in Cantha, Gorrik spent time with
Marjory Delaqua
and
Detective Rama
and became infatuated with the idea of starting a
Friends' Detective Agency
together, as he dabbled in investigative forensics as a hobby.

Spectrum Shield
-
Exceed
.
Break
stun
effects and create a holographic shell that reduces incoming
damage
and grants
stability
. This skill grants more stability and lasts longer while above the
heat
threshold.
Corona Burst
- Strike nearby foes and begin to store up energy, gaining
boons
and
heat
each pulse. After charging, the energy explodes, inflicting
conditions
on nearby foes.
Photon Wall
-
Exceed
.
A defensive barrier appears in front of you that blocks you and your allies from incoming attacks. Reactivate Exceed to fire the barrier at foes as an attack. This skill grants projectile reflection if you are above the heat threshold.
Particle Accelerator
- Fire a crippling bolt of light at your target. Allies the bolt passes through are granted swiftness. Projectile velocity and damage increased while above the heat threshold.
Photon Blitz
- Fire multiple piercing blasts at your target.

Trivia
Gorrik is
voiced
by
Ike Amadi
.
In an earlier story draft for
A Bug in the System
,
Taimi
played the part of
Blish
and Gorrik.
Gorrik's original name was Glubber.
Gorrik is on the autism spectrum, often causing him to be misunderstood.
Gorrik, alongside his brother, seems to have been inspired by the
Fullmetal Alchemist
manga series, in which two alchemist brothers (one of whom is trapped in armor, similar to Blish) are searching for the philosopher's stone to restore their bodies.
Gorrik wears the Tier 1 Human Cultural Armor
Researcher's armor
. He wields a pistol and shield that use the
Inquest Mark II weapon skins
.
When the
player
is in first person they can see flies and hear a buzzing sound around Gorrik.
Gorrik and
Ankka
were retroactively added to the
Thaumanova Reactor Fractal
in the
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
release.
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Gorseval the Multifarious
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Gorseval_the_Multifarious
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Spirit Vale
.
Gorseval the Multifarious
Race
Ghost
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Gorseval's Perch
(
Spirit Vale
)
Organization
Bloodstone ghosts
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
We fight together! And we will have our revenge!
—
Gorseval the Multifarious
Gorseval the Multifarious
is  the second boss in the
Spirit Vale
raid
wing. It is a soul construct
formed from the angered
ghosts
trapped within the
Spirit Woods
.
Biography
Early history
After establishing a foothold in the
Forsaken Thicket
in their exile from
Kryta
, the
White Mantle
ordered their
bandit
allies to capture people, and those who were deemed
magically
potent were sacrificed atop the Maguuma
Bloodstone
for dark rituals over two centuries. The bandits of
Spirit Vale
left the bodies of the unworthy prisoners to rot in the
Spirit Woods
. The
Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
's
demise
caused a magical shockwave in 1328
AE
, however, which hit the Forsaken Thicket and released some of the spirits trapped in the Maguuma Bloodstone.
The freed
ghosts
, having forgotten their cause of death at first, alternated between being confused and scared to becoming angry and bloodthirsty when memories of their suffering periodically returned. The bandits quickly learned not to leave more bodies in shallow graves or piles within the woods as the restless spirits increased in number and attacked anyone they came across. When threatened, the ghosts learned to merge together into a single, monstrous entity which the bandits eventually named Gorseval. Some bandits even thought of entertaining themselves by leaving helpless prisoners in the woods and watch them be devoured by Gorseval.
Forsaken Thicket
Later in 1328
AE
, a squad of raiders assisting the
Pact
ventured into the Spirit Vale in search of a missing squad which the bandits had captured earlier. The raiders encountered Gorseval in the woods and heard it voicing its desire for both revenge and merging the souls of more beings into its mass to grow in power. Gorseval was ultimately defeated after a fierce clash, and the raiders conjectured that the ghosts forming Gorseval might have been slaughtered and bound by the bandit leader
Sabetha the Saboteur
. This conjecture was proven untrue later when the Pact discovered more evidence of the bandit and White Mantle activities in the region, however.
Scholar Glenna
, the raiders' associate, recorded Gorseval's true origins in her
field journal
for posterity.
Janthir Wilds
In 1338
AE
, the Pact Commander encountered a being similar to Gorseval—
Lextalion the Merciless
, another soul construct naturally formed from souls, unlike other soul constructs encountered by the Commander, such as the
titans
forged in the
Foundry of Failed Creations
.
Location
Heart of Maguuma
Forsaken Thicket
Spirit Vale
Gorseval's Perch
Walkthrough
Primary article:
Spirit Vale#Gorseval the Multifarious
Combat abilities
Abilities
Many Become One
Behavior
Spectral Darkness
- Gorseval summons spectral orbs which slowly create an expanding AoE. Players who enter this field are afflicted with 10 stacks of the
Spectral Darkness
debuff. Destruction of a Spectral Darkness orb leaves behind two small golden energy orbs which persist for a short time. Each golden orb removes 5 stacks of the Spectral Darkness debuff from an
affected
player when they walk over them and is consumed.
If not destroyed, Spectral Darkness orbs can grow to cover about a quarter of the arena before naturally timing out.
Updrafts and Spirit Barrier
- During Gorseval's World Eater attack, players have the choice to either phase Gorseval (bring him to either 66%, 33%, or 0% health) in order to interrupt the attack; or must destroy a
Spirit Barrier
wall at the edge of the arena to access an
updraft
, gliding up to avoid the attack, then return to the arena.
The fight begins with four updrafts. Once an updraft is used, it disappears. Each updraft is marked on the minimap with a
icon.
Disappearance of updrafts is activated by player proximity, with each disappearing 15 seconds after a player moves close to it (very edge of the platform).
Note that if Gorseval has walked away from the center of the arena then upon being phased he must walk back to the center before World Eater will cancel.
Effects
(unlocked while channeling
Ghastly Rampage
; inflicts knockdown for 3 seconds when broken)
Unnamed defiance skill
— Break this to stun
Gorseval
.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Protective Shadow
- The souls have channeled their strength, rending
Gorseval
invulnerable.
Spirited Fusion
- Gains 10% damage for every ghost devoured by bloom.
Note that Bloom was the previous name for World Eater
Vivid Echo
- Reflects incoming damage. Can't be removed.
Skills
Ghastly Prison
- You have been captured by the spirits of
Gorseval
.
Encases players in an egg-like prison that can be broken through a combination of other player's attacks and the special action skill
Rebel
the captured player receives.
Ghastly Rampage
- A ring of horrible, ghostly energy spikes up through the ground. Damages foes for 50% of their health.
Gorseval gains
Vivid Echo
and slams the ground repeatedly, creating patterns of black, ghastly material on the floor. After 3 seconds, the black material detonates and damages players while applying 25 stacks of
Vulnerability
to them. Breaking Gorseval's breakbar interrupts this skill.
Haunting Aura
-
Gorseval's
mere presence causes all foes within range to take damage every 3 seconds.
A passive damage dealing effect which comes into effect as soon as Gorseval is engaged into combat.
Spectral Impact
- Smashes the ground, dealing impact damage to foes in front and underneath.
Gorseval slams its fist into the ground damaging
knocking back
nearby players and applying
Torment
.
Wicked Swipe
- Swipes the air, dealing damage at close range.
World Eater
- ...
Slow casting Area of Effect (AoE) which fills the arena; once fully cast, it will kill any player still standing in the arena. This skill also consumes any remaining spirits in the arena applying one stack of
Spirited Fusion
to Gorseval per spirit consumed.
Angered Spirit

Gree, the Bringer
Race
Titan
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Blightbringer's Court
(
Mount Balrior
)
Convergence: Mount Balrior
(
Mount Balrior
)
Image(s)
Interactive map
Gree, the Bringer
is a
Greerling
titan
found alongside
Greer, the Blightbringer
and
Reeg, the Blighter
in the
Mount Balrior
raid
and
convergence
.
Location
Janthir
Convergence: Mount Balrior
Mount Balrior
Blightbringer's Court
Combat abilities
Abilities
Poison
Area Control
Boon Corruption
Effects
5
Might
(30s)
- Increased outgoing
damage
; stacks intensity.
(Granted by
Ereg, the Enabler
during challenge mode, quickly stacks to 25)
Resolution
(30s)
- Incoming
condition damage
decreased by 33%; stacks duration.
(Granted by
Reeg, the Blighter
during challenge mode)
Behaviors
If there are non-players within Gree's attack range, Gree uses
Empowering Blast
on
Greer
.
Flee every 25% of their health.
Skills
Aura of Corruption
- A pool of putrescence constantly seeps from the cracks in the Greerling's armor, damaging and poisoning anything that gets too close to it.
Empowering Blast
- Fires a projectile of corrupted life energy at Greer. If it hits Greer, it grants him 2
empowered
stacks. If it hits a player, it will knock them back and continue on. If it is blocked by a player, the projectile is destroyed.
Eruption of Rot
- The Greerling places a stacking indicator on several players. If the indicated number of players are within the circle when time runs out, the
rot eruption
is stabilized and only does damage. If a player is within more than one stacking circle, they are instantly killed. If there are not enough players within the circle, a
rot eruption
occurs and the players are given a debuff of
Infectious Rot
and the Greerling gains 2 empowered stacks.
Rot Eruption
(Failed Green) - Rot walls burst up from the ground, knocking players back and creating a
noxious blight
at the eruption point. Players hit by a rot eruption take damage and gain poison, bleeding, and a stack of
infectious rot
.
Applies
Infectious Rot
, which lasts for 20 seconds or until the player is downed.
Noxious Blight
(Boon Corrupt AoE) - Noxious blights form persistent pools of corruption on the ground. Foes in a noxious blight take continuous damage, and their boons will be corrupted while they remain in the noxious blight.
Rain of Spores
- The Greerling targets 3 players, preferring the farthest away, and then fires a torrent of exploding spores along a line that moves towards the initial position of the target. The spores explode on impact, inflicting damage, poison, and confusion.
Ripples of Rot
- The Greerling leaps into the air towards a targeted player. When the Greerling lands, targets directly under it will be knocked back. It then fires cascades of rot walls that ripple outward, inflicting damage, knockback, and bleeding on anyone they hit. The final rot wall stays in place, blocking player projectiles that hit them.
Applies
Infectious Rot
, which lasts for 20 seconds or until the player is downed.
Stomp the Growth
- The Greerling stomps 3 times in quick succession. The first two stomps hit all targets in a circle around the impact point dealing damage, inflicting poison, and applying vulnerability. The third stomp creates a
wave of corruption
.
Wave of Corruption
- An expanding circular shockwave thats spreads outward. Players hit by the wave of corruption take damage, gain vulnerability, are knocked down, and have a number of their boons corrupted. This attack cannot be blocked, but players can avoid the effects by jumping over it.
Sweep the Mold
- The Greerling sweeps its claw in a 60-degree cone
[
sic
]
, damaging, bleeding, poisoning, and knocking back targets. This attack sets up
Rake the Rot
, the next attack in the chain.
Rake the Rot
- The Greerling sweeps its claw in a 60-degree cone, damaging, crippling, poisoning, and knocking back targets. This attack sets up
Stomp the Mould
[
sic
]
, the final attack in the chain.
Stolen skills

expanding it
.

“
The multiplicity of your bestial nature flickers dull without air... I am Greer. And you are small.
—
Greer, the Blightbringer
Greer, the Blightbringer
, or
Godspoil Greer
, is an
extremely powerful
titan
and the brother of
Decima, the Stormsinger
.
He holds domain over rot, decay, and blight, and uses
boon
corruption in combat.
Alongside his sister, he intends to drain the
magic
of
bloodstones
located on
Janthir Syntri
.

Convergence:
Mount Balrior
Abilities
Poison
Area Control
Boon Corruption
Effects
(unlocked when casting
Rot the World
)
Unnamed defiance skill
—
Greer
's armor shatters and leaves him vulnerable. He loses 10 stacks or 25% of total stacks of
Empower
, whichever is greater. He is knocked down and exposed. Some armor fragments transform into
Empowering Beasts
on impact.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Empowered
- Increases damage by 5% per stack. At 99 stacks, Greer's attacks become unblockable.
Damage Immunity
- Immune from damage but vulnerable to crowd control.
Skills
Aura of Corruption
- A pool of putrescence constantly seeps from the cracks in Greer's armor, damaging and poisoning anything that gets too close to him.
Blob of Blight
- Greer targets up to 3 foes, preferring the most distant combatants. He slams his fist down, creating a damaging sphere that will then fire a blob of blight that follows the targeted enemy for 12 seconds, firing multiple smaller projectiles as it moves. If it hits a targeted foe, it will also create a stationary blob of blight that fires projectiles for several seconds.
Blob of Blight
- If one of Greer's blobs of blight hits a targeted player, it explodes and places a stationary blob of blight that will continue to fire projectiles for several seconds.
Cage of Decay
- Greer creates a fan of 5 cascades, starting at the edges of a 180-degree arc and moving toward the center. Rippling thorn barriers travel along these lines, damaging and knocking back anything struck by them. When the barriers reach the end of their track, they create a circular rot cage of barriers surrounding a zone of
noxious blight
.
Noxious Blight
(Boon Corrupt AoE) - Noxious blights form persistent pools of corruption on the ground. Foes in a noxious blight take continuous damage, and their boons will be corrupted while they remain in the noxious blight.
Enfeebling Miasma
- Greer exhales lingering clouds of poisonous gas that travel outward to form 3 roughly conical areas. The gas clouds stay in place for several seconds, poisoning foes inside them.
Actual affected area is much larger than indicated.
Eruption of Rot
(Green) - Greer places a stacking indicator on several players. If the indicated number of players are within the circle when time runs out, the
rot eruption
is stabilized and only does damage. If a player is within more than one stacking circle, they are instantly killed. If there are not enough players within the circle, a
rot eruption
occurs, and the Greerling gains 2 empowered stacks.
Rot Eruption
(Failed Green) - Rot walls burst up from the ground, knocking players back and creating a
noxious blight
at the eruption point. Players hit by a rot eruption take damage and gain poison, bleeding, and a stack of
infectious rot
.
Applies
Infectious Rot
, which lasts for 20 seconds or until the player is downed.
Noxious Blight
(Boon Corrupt AoE) - Noxious blights form persistent pools of corruption on the ground. Foes in a noxious blight take continuous damage, and their boons will be corrupted while they remain in the noxious blight.
Rain of Spores
- Greer targets 3 players, preferring the farthest away, and then fires a torrent of exploding spores along a line that moves towards the initial position of the target. The spores explode on impact, inflicting damage, poison, and confusion.
Ripples of Rot
- Greer leaps into the air towards a targeted player. When Greer lands, targets directly under him will be knocked back. He then fires cascades of rot walls that ripple outward, inflicting damage, knockback, and bleeding on anyone hit by them. The final rot wall stays in place, blocking player projectiles that hit them.
Applies
Infectious Rot
, which lasts for 20 seconds or until the player is downed.
Ripples of Rot
(challenge mode only)
- Greer leaps into the air toward a targeted player. When he lands, targets directly under him will be knocked back and gain a stack of
Plague Rot
. Greer then fires cascades of rot walls that ripple outward, inflicting damage, knockback, and bleeding on anyone hit by them. The final rot wall stays in place, blocking player projectiles that hit it.
Rot the World
- Greer rains explosive spores down on the battlefield as he musters his power. This leaves him vulnerable, opening his defiance bar. If players do not break him, he will spawn rot cages under the feet of every player in the raid, as well as extra ones, choking the battlefield. Breaking him will interrupt the attack.
Scattering Sporeblast
- Greer rapidly fires spores in a cone in front of him. Players in this cone will be directly targeted by the spores.
Seeds of Decay
- The shattered fragments of Greer's armor rain down on the arena. Smaller pieces explode on impact, but larger fragments transform into titanspawn, ready to help Greer in the fight.
Sweep the Mold
- Greer sweeps his claw in a 60-degree cone
[
sic
]
, damaging, bleeding, poisoning, and knocking back targets. This attack sets up
Rake the Rot
, the next attack in the chain.
Rake the Rot
- Greer sweeps his claw in a 60-degree cone, damaging, crippling, poisoning, and knocking back targets. This attack sets up
Stomp the Mould
[
sic
]
, the final attack in the chain.
Stomp the Growth
- Greer stomps 3 times in quick succession. The first two stomps hit all targets in a circle around the impact point, dealing damage, inflicting poison, and applying vulnerability. The third stomp creates a
wave of corruption
.
Wave of Corruption
- An expanding circular shockwave thats spreads outward. Players hit by the wave of corruption take damage, gain vulnerability, are knocked down, and have a number of their boons corrupted. This attack cannot be blocked, but players can avoid the effects by jumping over it.
Single large AoE indicator followed by shockwave indicator. Greer stomps 3 times.  First two inflict
Poisoned
and
Vulnerability
, last stomp creates a wave of boon corruption.

Enfeebling Miasma
(challenge mode only)
- Greer exhales lingering clouds of poisonous gas that travel outward to form 3 conical areas. The gas clouds stay in place for several seconds, poisoning foes inside them. If a victim of the gas gains 20 stacks of poison, they will be converted into 1 stack of
Plague Rot
.
Eruption of Rot
(Green) - Greer places a stacking indicator on several players. If the indicated number of players are within the circle when time runs out, the
rot eruption
is stabilized and only does damage. If a player is within more than one stacking circle, they are instantly killed. If there are not enough players within the circle, a
rot eruption
occurs, and the Greerling gains 2 empowered stacks.

noxious blight
at the eruption point. Players hit by a rot eruption take damage and gain poison, bleeding, and a stack of
infectious rot

Infectious Rot
, which lasts for 20 seconds or until the player is downed.
Noxious Blight
(Boon Corrupt AoE) - Noxious blights form persistent pools of corruption on the ground. Foes in a noxious blight take continuous damage, and their boons will be corrupted while they remain in the noxious blight.
Invulnerable Barrier
- Greer encircles himself in a barrier, protecting himself from further harm while his Greerlings attack his foes. Players entering the barrier will be knocked back.
Rain of Spores
- Greer targets 3 players, preferring the farthest away, and then fires a torrent of exploding spores along a line that moves towards the initial position of the target. The spores explode on impact, inflicting damage, poison, and confusion.
Ripples of Rot
- Greer leaps into the air towards a targeted player. When Greer lands, targets directly under him will be knocked back. He then fires cascades of rot walls that ripple outward, inflicting damage, knockback, and bleeding on anyone hit by them. The final rot wall stays in place, blocking player projectiles that hit them.

Infectious Rot

(challenge mode only)

Plague Rot

If players do not break him, he will spawn rot cages under the feet of every player in the raid, as well as extra ones, choking the battlefield. Breaking him will interrupt the attack.
Rot the World
(challenge mode only)
- Greer rains explosive spores down on the battlefield as he musters his power. This leaves him vulnerable, opening his defiance bar. If players do not break it, he will kill all players in the raid.

In the raid Greer has ?
armor
(1374 toughness and ? defense).
In the raid Greer has a 800 range diameter hitbox, which is considered large.

achievement,
Emboldened Mote
must be disabled.
Trivia

voiced
by
David Sobolov
.
Greer and
Decima, the Stormsinger
might be a reference to the show
Person of Interest
, which features the character John Greer and a company Decima Technologies.

Seer constructs

Guardian of the Sacred

Dagda
during
The Defense of Amnytas
meta event
. It is the combination of the guardians of
Balance
,
the Celestial
,
Knowledge
,
the Natural
,
the Obscure
, and
Strength
.

Horn of Maguuma

Guldhem
Race
Jotun
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Bastion of the Penitent
(
Heart of Maguuma
)
Guldhem
appears during the confrontation with
Samarog
in
Bastion of the Penitent
. Guldhem will appear alongside
Rigom
when Samarog has reached 66% and 33% health.
Locations
Woodland Cascades
Bastion of the Penitent
The Holding
The Warden's Office
Combat abilities
Abilities
The Brute
Redirects
Behavior
Effects
Stun
— Vulnerable while broken.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Classic Misdirection
- Damage and conditions are redirected to a nearby patsy.
Fixate: Guldhem
- Fixate Guldhem
Strengthened Bond: Guldhem
- Outgoing damage increased by 10%. Stacks are applied when in close proximity to Rigom.
Skills
Cleave
-
Guldhem
swings his sword viciously.
Anguished Bolt
-
Guldhem
launches an arcing projectile at the farthest target from him. The projectile stuns all those around the target when it lands.

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Gwen Thackeray
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Gwen_Thackeray
======================================================================

This article is about for the legendary hero.  For a child playing the part of Gwen, see
"Gwen"
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Hall of Chains
and
Living World Season 4
.
Gwen Thackeray
Race
Ghost
Profession
Mesmer
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Winter's Vigil
(
Hall of Chains
)
Mist Warden Camp
(
Dragonfall
)
Organization
Mist Wardens
Image(s)
Gwen as she appears since All or Nothing.
Interactive map
“
Glint's general is Gwen Thackeray. The Goremonger. Killer of charr.
—
Rytlock Brimstone
in
Glory of Dragons
In life,
Captain Gwen Thackeray
was a commander of the
Ebon Vanguard
and the founder of
Ebonhawke
. In death, Gwen's ghost is a general of the
Mist Wardens
. Gwen is a legendary figure among both
humans
and
charr
; to the former, she is a hero and ancestor to
Logan Thackeray
, famous
captain
of the
Seraph
and member of
Destiny's Edge
. To the latter, she is a terrifying villain who viciously slaughtered hundreds of their people, earning herself the title
Goremonger
.
Biography
Early years
Born in
Ascalon
as the daughter of
Sarah
, Gwen's earliest years were peaceful; her biggest worry at the time was leaving her flute by a nearby river and being unable to reclaim it from aggressive wildlife until a hero
returned her broken flute to her
. These peaceful days came to a sudden end when
the Searing
struck
Ascalon
on Gwen's tenth year in 1070
AE
. She lost her family, her home, and her childhood in a matter of hours as the sky-summoned fury of the
charr
shamans
shattered the world around her. Thousands of Ascalonians died that day, and thousands more fled before the
charr invasion
. Gwen wandered alone in the wreckage of a nation searching desperately for the hand of a friend. Instead, she found the charr. More to the point, they found her.
The charr did not kill young
humans
. They enslaved them. For seven long years, Gwen toiled under the constant lash of her taskmasters. As fellow prisoners broke under the strain or were slaughtered out of hand, the embers of fear in her soul sparked into a blaze of hatred. When she finally saw her chance, Gwen fled into the wilderness, vowing never to be enslaved again. She did not make it far...the charr recaptured her easily. They judged her useless to them. She should have been slain on the spot, but her captors decided she could serve them, at least one more time, as entertainment.
The charr built arenas within their military camps dedicated to the glory of the
legions
. Inside these cruel stadiums they set unarmed
humans
against wild creatures, revelling in the blood sport and applauding as the beasts disembowelled helpless humans. Gwen was just another prisoner in a holding pen awaiting a brutal death for mere amusement and prayed for the strength to endure her ordeal and a chance to strike back.
Gwen defeated the charr's pet monster through guile and good aim, and
escaped
into a maze of tunnels beneath northern Ascalon. In those ruins she found more evidence of the charr's handiwork...the entombed remains of years of death matches. In the depths of this abattoir she also discovered power, in the form of
mesmer
spells within the journals of a long-dead researcher.
Stronger for her ordeal, but still alone in the heart of charr territory, Gwen set her sights on the distant mountains and the promise of freedom they offered from the pursuing
warbands
. She knew that if she were caught again, her death would be slow and painful. She would not be given the luxury of a merciful execution. Gwen's prayers were answered when she stumbled upon a group of free humans, a squad of Ascalonian soldiers operating deep behind enemy lines. They called themselves the
Ebon Vanguard
and their leader was
Captain Langmar
, a seasoned veteran.
Gwen joined the Ebon Vanguard and served them well. Years of forced labor and punishments turned this innocent girl into a ruthless woman, her hands guided by rage and a deep fear of her captors. Over time she learned to cope with her emotions. She learned when to strike and when to hold back. In her short life, Gwen had known hatred as well as mercy, and knew when to choose between the two. She became a true champion of Ascalon.
The Goremonger
Gwen during the campaign against
the Great Destroyer
in 1078
AE
.
When
destroyers
began appearing in the
Depths of Tyria
in 1078
AE
, the
dwarf
Ogden Stonehealer
traveled north in the company of various
heroes
to find allies to fight back the invading menace. At the
Eye of the North
, he found Gwen, who had temporary command of the Ebon Vanguard while Captain Langmar was on a mission in the
Blood Legion Homelands
.
Langmar's return was long overdue, however, and a worried Gwen led a small force into charr territory,
freeing
the rebellious charr
Pyre Fierceshot
from
Flame Legion
captivity and kickstarting the
charr rebellion
in the process. During this time she learned to tolerate and trust Pyre and his warband and came to see that not all charr were enemies to be slain, although she made it clear to him that she did not fear but
hate
his kind. This amused the impressed Pyre, who would form a friendship with the belligerent human. Later, Gwen aided the dwarves against the destroyers, and was present for the
defeat
of
the Great Destroyer
,
champion
of the
Elder Dragon
Primordus
. Around this time Gwen visited the
Underworld
where she had a chance to speak with the ghost of her mother Sarah, who offered her advice and allowed her closure so she could move on.
Gwen attained command of the Ebon Vanguard during the
Krytan Civil War
, when Captain Langmar traveled to
Kryta
with the
Ebon Falcons
(the leader of whom,
Lieutenant Keiran Thackeray
, she was romantically involved with) to come to the aid of Ascalon's former enemy. When the
White Mantle
marched on
Lion's Arch
, Langmar escorted the
necromancer
Livia
, and the
asura
pair
Blimm
and
Zinn
into the city, but lost her life to the
mursaat
near the
Ascalon Settlement
.
Gwen, now in command of the Ebon Vanguard, led a search for Captain Langmar and Lieutenant Kieran Thackeray, who had also gone missing in the fighting, and eventually learned of Langmar's death and
rescued Keiran
. She had slowly come to realize her growing feelings for the lieutenant and that out of all the horrors she faced in her life, nothing had scared her so much as the thought of being happy.
Gwen
accepted Keiran's wedding proposal
after their reunion, and they
were wed
in the presence of long-time allies and friends at
Olafstead
with the
norn
Olaf Olafson
acting as the vow master to make the pair's union official. Some time after the wedding, Gwen returned to the Underworld to present her husband to the ghost of Sara who was both happy and surprised to see her daughter all grown up and approved of the couple's union.
In 1080
AE
, 10 years after the Searing,
King Adelbern
recalled the Ebon Vanguard to
Ascalon City
, where they were given a heroes' welcome. They were redeployed to the mountains separating Ascalon from the
Crystal Desert
to guard against a dire threat which the king warned was coming. Although the mission seemed noble on the surface, some speculated that the increasingly paranoid king had simply wanted to get rid of undesirable people and remove a source of a potential threat from his presence. On the journey, the expedition lost many soldiers and civilians to charr. When they found their destination, an old mining town at the mouth of the mountain pass, they began to build what would become the
stronghold of Ebonhawke
. When the Kingdom of Ascalon finally gasped its last breath in the
Foefire
in 1090
AE
, Ebonhawke was just out of reach of the spell's effect, leaving Gwen's people the last humans in Ascalon.
The decades Gwen spent in defending Ebonhawke earned her the title "The Goremonger" and a legendary reputation among the besieging charr who both hated and feared her.
Gwen eventually passed away and was buried in Ebonhawke which would be inspired by her dedication and withstood the charr siege for decades to come. Her
grave
can be visited in the stronghold's graveyard,
Defender's Field
, to this day. Her deeds were written about in
Gwen's Story
which inspired
future generations
to show courage at the face of adversity. One of Gwen's descendants,
Logan Thackeray
, would play a major role in the wars to come centuries later.
Living World Season 4
Hall of Chains
When the former
god
Dhuum
broke free from his prison and began taking over the
Hall of Chains
in the Underworld, Gwen's soul could be found within the
Ice Wastes
. It is unclear if she had been in the Ice Wastes since her passing, or if she was there due to the conflict against Dhuum. Either way, she did not help in combating the former
God
of Death as she would vanish upon approach.
All or Nothing
When the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
invaded the Mists in 1331
AE
, Gwen joined the
dragon
Glint
's army of
ghosts
to combat the spreading
Branded
menace. In 1332
AE
, she
relayed a message from Glint
to the
Pact Commander
that Kralkatorrik had been wounded in battle against Glint's forces, but had subsequently scattered the ghost army, invaded the
Domain of the Lost
and begun feasting on the souls there to regain power. After delivering the message, Gwen returned to the Mists.
War Eternal
Gwen would once again return to the lands of Tyria in 1332
AE
when
Aurene
and the Pact Commander
drove out
Kralkatorrik from
the Mists
. As the Elder Dragon traversed through the domains of
Melandru
,
Grenth
and
Balthazar
, he dragged parts of their domains back into Tyria, and ultimately formed a new landmass called
Dragonfall
which he was buried under. Gwen and the
Mist Wardens
join the battle alongside the
Pact
and the
Olmakhan
by assisting Logan and
Rytlock Brimstone
in establishing a camp within
Melandru's Lost Domain
.
Logan was surprised to see his ancestor leading the Mist Wardens, while Gwen was surprised to see a charr like Rytlock fighting on Logan's side. Logan was quick to explain that he and Rytlock were friends and he would trust the charr with his life, while Rytlock tried to convince Gwen that the charr had changed their ways. Although Gwen was skeptical if the charr could truly change, she admitted that she had been friends with a charr, Pyre Fierceshot, once and accepted Rytlock after Logan's explanation.
When Gwen mentioned how Melandru had abandoned her domain and that the agitated creatures which the Mist Wardens and Pact were fighting did not deserve any pity, Rytlock found himself agreeing with her. Logan tried to argue for Melandru by stating that the goddess had brought humanity to Tyria and helped them survive. However, Gwen countered Logan by stating that the gods had chosen to leave rather than help mortal races confront the Elder Dragons, and she viewed this abandonment of Tyria as indefensible.
Gwen informed Logan and Rytlock about the existence of
Melandru's Tears
, magical sap that would give the allied army an edge against the Branded. After Rytlock had sent people to
collect the sap
and distribute it to other allied camps for their coordinated assaults on the Branded, Gwen called Rytlock a good charr much to the latter's annoyance. Rytlock felt increasinly uncomfortable staying around Gwen as he had grown up learning the legend of the infamous Goremonger.
Gwen accompanied Logan and Rytlock to join the other allied army commanders in the assault on the recovering Kralkatorrik. Although they successfully managed to hurt the Elder Dragon, they were unable to kill him at first and had to retreat when Kralkatorrik generated a
Brandstorm
around himself. Gwen pointed out that they would have to charge at Kralkatorrik and inflict wounds several times before the Crystal Dragon finally died for good. While the rest of the allied forces returned to their base of operations to regroup, Gwen revealed that Kralkatorrik could not brand ghosts, so she and her Mist Wardens would hold their ground until their mortal allies returned.
The allied forces were ultimately successful in incapaciting Kralkatorrik's physical body, while Aurene and the Pact Commander pierced the Crystal Dragon's heart from within. The allied army watched as Aurene, who had taken in Kralkatorrik's magic,
ascended
and succeeded her grandfather as an Elder Dragon.

Trivia
Gwen is
voiced
by
Kari Wahlgren
, the same actress who provided her voice in
Guild Wars: Eye of the North
.
Gwen had her own theme, "Gwen Thackeray", in
Guild Wars
soundtrack composed by
Jeremy Soule
. In
Guild Wars 2
the theme is one of
M.O.X.
's playback options in the player character's
home instance
if M.O.X. has been unlocked.
An ethereal flute can be heard playing a theme near
Gwen's Grave
when entering
Defender's Field
in Ebonhawke. The theme, as well as dialogue from
Sentinel Miner Fireeye
, act as a clue to what melody needs to be played at the Ebonhawke
pipe organ
to reveal the hidden
Descendant's Chest
.
If you get near Gwen in the
Hall of Chains
, she will quickly disappear, and a brief flute sound can be heard.
In Dragonfall, Gwen wields a spectral version of the
Tormented Scepter
.
See also

Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Gwen Thackeray
.

legendary

gwyl
seneschal
tasked by the

with forever protecting the forests of
Castora

Kela

Inquest
asura
Sebb
has plans to control it.

Harrower Veltan
is a
Lunatic Courtier
who aided in bringing
Palawa Joko
's spirit to the
Mad King's Realm
.
Location
The Mists
Mad King's Realm
Mad King's Labyrinth
(only during related events)

[Group Event]

Combat abilities
Behaviors

Halloween creatures
and players, excluding the
Labyrinthine Horror
.
Abilities
Burns
Dashes
Traps
Effects
Skills
Sword Arc
- Melee attack. Cleaves.
Sword of Wrath
- Melee attack. Cleaves.
Sword Wave
- Melee attack. Cleaves and
knocks back
.
Flame Rush
- Charges forward, leaving behind a trail of fire for 10 seconds that pulses and inflicts
Burning
for 4 seconds. Used twice in a row.
Bonfire
- Throws three torch projectiles in a fan formation. Each projectile creates a fire field for 12s that pulses damage and inflicts
Burning
for 4 seconds.
Blazing Blades
- Marks a target location and
teleports
there after a short delay. Whirls in place at the location for 5 seconds, rapidly dealing damage to all nearby enemies while
reflecting
projectiles and
knocking back
foes that attempt to enter or leave the area of effect.
Shouts to gain
Protection
for 8s.
Stolen skills
Bone Crack
Consume Plasma
Throw Gunk
Dialogue
That was an entertaining battle. Here in the Realm, one has to stay sharp, especially when the doors draw near to other worlds.
I've heard you were involved with Joko's appearance here.
Hmm, I don't know who would tell you that. I couldn't say, although it SOUNDS like an amazing feat. If it's true.
I believe Brigadier General Kernal mentioned you in this report.
You went up the chain of command, eh? Very well... Yes, I guarded several courtiers in recovering certain items to bring that...overlord.
Where did you go, and what were you after?
We journeyed all over Tyria, from Elona to the far north... Searching for some trinkets they wanted. Relics from his kingdom, records of speeches, royal regalia. The like.
I'll take that testimony, but I have my eye on you.
(After completing
Court Duty: Untangling the Labyrinth
)
But you must know something about this soul jar?
An empty pot? I suppose you could smash it and use the glass as a weapon...but surely you can do better.
Hmm, that approach didn't work.
(After completing
Court Duty: Terror of the Tower
)
I happen to have a guide to the Clock Tower here.
A guide? If you're going to climb, climb with your own strength and fall from your own failures.
Hmm, that approach didn't work.
(After completing
Court Duty: Race to the Precipice

My investigation turned up this claw. A gargoyle's, if I'm not mistaken.
There's gargoyle bits all over the place in the Labyrinth! You can hardly call that a trophy of battle.
Hmm, that approach didn't work.

Court Duty: Deference to the King

Is this remnant of Awakened garb perhaps familiar to you?
The court hasn't had a real throw down with the Awakened for years!
Wodalr
hasn't even been carrying his hammer lately... A pity.
Hmm, that approach didn't work.
Oh, I'll uncover the truth of this.
And I've got a lot to do during Halloween. So I'll be off, then.

PAGE: Heitor
