Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Heitor
Race
Demon
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Heitor's Gate
(
Heitor's Dominion
)
Organization
Kryptis
“
Eparch is our dream.
—
Heitor
Heitor
is a
Kryptis
general loyal to
Eparch
. She is the cousin to the Nephus siblings, of whom
Cerus
is her mate.
Biography
Early years
Heitor enjoyed the life of a
Kryptis
noble in Midnight King
Eparch
and
Queen Labris
's court in
Inner Nayos
. She became the mate of her cousin,
Cerus
of House Nephus, and thought that Cerus's sister
Peitha
had been "spoiled to the horn." Even the life of a noble was not easy during Eparch's harsh reign; Heitor and Peitha did what they could to survive in the
Citadel of Zakiros
, and Heitor embraced the cruelty of her fellow nobles as she preferred tormenting and devouring lesser Kryptis than letting herself be devoured by Eparch and Labris in turn. At some point, Peitha left the court and voiced her disapproval of the Midnight King's tyrannical ways. Heitor, meanwhile, submitted herself to Eparch. Heitor did not share Cerus's optimism for Peitha ever amending her sentiments of Eparch.
Secrets of the Obscure
In 1336
AE
, Heitor learned that Peitha had
betrayed and slain
Cerus in combat, and she grew angry with her cousin. Eparch had sent her and Commander
Ignaxious
to oversee the fortress of
Heitor's Gate
and prevent any intruders from entering Nayos heartlands via a barrier while the king sealed all paths to his castle. Heitor ordered several of her forces to scout the surrounding countryside, and some scouts
ran into
Astral Ward
stragglers from
Tyria
led by
Arina
who had been stuck in Nayos for months after a rift accident had plunged them there.
Astral Ward speculated that Ignaxious was accompanying Heitor not only to serve as her lieutenant to guard the fortress but as a means for Eparch to keep an eye on Heitor to ensure her loyalty after Peitha's rebellious actions.
After convincing a few of the local Kryptis to offer their assistance, adjusting the fearful locals to the presence of Tyrians, and slaying several of Heitor's roaming officers to weaken loyalist hold on the region, Peitha and her lieutenant
Ramses
used the forces provided by Arina's Astral Ward and the
Pact Commander
to take down Heitor's Gate and slay Ignaxious, after which the four rebel commanders sought out Heitor deeper within the fortress. Meanwhile, Heitor and Ignaxious's grunts had managed to capture
Irja
, a fearful Kryptis commoner who had been sympathetic to the rebellion, and brought her to Heitor.
Peitha and her fellow rebel commanders encountered not only Heitor but the captive Irja in the heart of the fortress and witnessed Heitor and Peitha exchanging barbed words where both accused the other of fighting for the wrong side while Peitha offered Heitor one last chance to join the growing rebellion. When Peitha suggested that Heitor was not driven by zealous loyalty but fear of being punished by Eparch, however, Heitor grew angry and demonstrated the fate of all who defied the Midnight King: she cruelly consumed Irja's life force to empower herself and attacked the shocked Peitha while voicing her desire to avenge Cerus's death. Despite her best efforts, however, she was ultimately no match for the strike team who overpowered her. Heitor, unhinged, desperately clung to her allegiance to Eparch right up to her unceremonious death. Peitha would remark after the battle that Heitor wanted to die, and death had saved Heitor from a much more miserable existence.
Story involvement

Through the Veil

Icebrood Construct
Race
Icebrood
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Fraenir of Jormag
(
Bjora Marches
)
The
Icebrood Construct
is creature summoned by the
Fraenir of Jormag
in the
Sanctum Arena
.
Location
Shiverpeak Mountains
Bjora Marches
Sanctum Arena
Fraenir of Jormag
Combat abilities
Abilities
Knocks Back
Massive Shock Waves
Behavior
Periodically summons
Bound Ice Elementals
, which may be destroyed.
Periodically, the Fraenir and Construct may gain a Defiance Bar (1000 strength). This may be broken easily using the
Night Terror
Special action
skill.
Effects
(unlocked when channeling
Seismic Crush
)
Stun
— Stuns and applies vulnerability.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Skills
Frigid Fusillade
- Players on the left side of the construct will be targeted by a series of AoEs. Each quickly fills with color and deals a moderate amount of damage to all players within the AoE when it hits. This can occur up to three times in quick succession.
Frostbite Aura
- Icebrood construct pulses a volatile aura that deals damage every 3 seconds to all foes within a range of 2,000.
Ice Arm Swing
- Sweeps its right arm across half the arena, dealing heavy damage and knocking players back.
Ice Flail
- The icebrood construct swings its arm, doing damage and knocking enemies down. Breaks free any
Bound Ice Elementals
that are hit.
Ice Shock Wave
- The
Icebrood construct
slams its arm down, doing damage and creating a shock wave that knocks enemies down. The shock wave can be jumped.
Seismic Crush
- The
Icebrood construct
slams its arm down, doing damage and creating a shock wave that knocks enemies down. Ice shards rain down over the area. The shock wave can be jumped. At the same time, targets players with time ice bombs, which must be dodged or blocked in order to avoid damage.
Shatter Arm
- When the construct reaches 70% health its left arm will shatter. Shortly after, it will collapse before spinning around and knocking players back.

======================================================================
PAGE: Isgarren
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Isgarren
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
,
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
, and
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
Isgarren
Race
Seer
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Lonely Tower Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Outer Sanctorium
(
The Wizard's Tower
)
Soaring Sands
(
Shipwreck Strand
)
Organization
Wizard's Court
Tyrian Alliance
Image(s)
Echo
of Isgarren.
Isgarren with the
Arcane Flow
aura.
“
Not much is known about Isgarren. He keeps to his castle, and is rarely ever seen or heard. What is known, is that he has a kind gracious heart, and he cares for and loves the people of this town.
—
Lasha
Isgarren
the Curator is an enigmatic wizard who resides in, and is the current owner of,
the floating castle of
Wizard's Tower
in
Kessex Hills
according to the townsfolk of
Garenhoff
.
He is eventually revealed to be the last known survivor of the
Seers
as well as the founder of the secretive
Wizard's Court
and patron of the
Astral Ward
who has safeguarded the world of
Tyria
against various threats from
the Mists
with help from
the World Spire
in
Amnytas
. Isgarren and Midnight King
Eparch
of the
Kryptis
have a centuries-spanning rivalry that has led to conflict between their respective forces.
Biography
Early years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Isgarren claims to have had many names and shapes over his millennia-spanning existence, and those who know him from ages past even refer to him as "Your Highness," suggesting a royal origin of some sort although he prefers not to use such titles on
Tyria
.
He was once a member of the
Seer
horde, or fhogos, and assisted them in their alliance with the
elder races
of
Tyria
against the ravenous
Elder Dragons
in the previous dragonrise millennia ago. However, just before the Seers were to aid the
mursaat
in the attack against the Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
, the Seer doyen
Sidony
suddenly decided against Seer involvement in the battle and left the mursaat and the
Forgotten
to fend for themselves while the Seers focused on the creation of the
Shadowstone
and the
Bloodstone
instead. Isgarren felt sorry for the mursaat, but Sidony stopped him from interfering before he could lend them aid.
The mursaat, who had suffered many losses alongside the Forgotten in the battle, never forgave the Seers for this perceived act of betrayal, leading to a bloody conflict between the two races millennia later in which the mursaat exterminated most Seers, including Sidony, from Tyria despite their numbers being fewer compared to the Seers.
During the seer-mursaat war, Isgarren continued to attempt to aid the other races of Tyria despite Sidony's orders.  When he used the
Rite of Divinity
to save the sanity of the young kodan who would become known as
Waiting Sorrow
, Sidony saw that as a step too far.  Sidony banished Isgarren from the fhogos and from the seer's ritual center on the island of
Castora
, enforcing the banishment with a powerful curse.
It is unclear if the
Beacon of Ages
and other
beacons
scattered throughout Tyria predated Isgarren and he discovered and claimed them for his use, or if he created the beacons himself.
Either way, at some point he was inspired to found the
Wizard's Court
to study
magic
and safeguard Tyria from threats from
the Mists
, and he extended his influence by having the wizards be assisted by what became known as the
Astral Ward
and the
Rift Hunters
. His first recruit to the court was the
mursaat
Mabon
whom he had been fighting during the mursaat-Seer conflict but whom he had later befriended when Mabon had professed a desire to end bloodshed as he was ashamed of his people's actions.
Later the
djinn
Akeem
, the
Forgotten
Vass
, and the
kodan
Waiting Sorrow
joined as well, and Isgarren and Vass grew close over the centuries. All wizards were required to undergo the ancient Seer rite of
ascension
to tap into the flow of magic of the world itself albeit with the caveat that the wizard's personal memories may be erased as a side effect.
Sometimes during the previous
Dragon cycle
, Isgarren rescued a highly evolved
Kryptis
named
Eparch
, who somehow managed to get into Tyria but found himself chased by the elder dragon
Mordremoth
.
Isgarren was intrigued and brought the Kryptis back to the Wizard's Tower, offering him a chance to learn more about Tyria and the opportunity to eventually join the ranks of the Wizard's Court.
However, Isgarren denied the
demon
entry to the court's ranks and exiled him to
Nayos
, the Realm of Dreams where Eparch had come from, because he found Eparch to be ignorant and hungry after the demon had torn an entire village to innards and scraps of meat.
This led to an intense rivalry and eventual war between the two as Eparch swore to bring Isgarren and
Amnytas
down and claim the wizards' secrets and power for himself. To achieve his ends, Eparch began uniting the Kryptis into a coalition under his command, dubbing himself the Midnight King and aiming their aggression against the wizards of Amnytas and Tyria as a whole while Isgarren and his supporters fought to keep them out.
Lonely Tower Fractal
Eparch
confronts Isgarren during the
assault
on
the Wizard's Tower
.
After the
Astral Ward
's founding in 604
AE
, Eparch's Kryptis possessed the first
Warden of the Wizard's Court
,
Taryn
.
This ultimately led to Eparch's first invasion attempt into Tyria sometime around 635
AE
,
where the Kryptis
targeted
the Wizard's Tower with Eparch himself leading the incursion alongside his nephews
Cerus
and
Deimos
. The assault led to the deaths of the wizards
Akeem
and
Vass
and culminated in Isgarren and Eparch's second confrontation. Mabon was unfamiliar with the magic that the Kryptis were using to block the ward's path but did his best to stabilize the tower so
Dagda
and the ward could push onward and banish Cerus and Deimos. The battle had drained Mabon's strength, so he asked Dagda to open a portal to Isgarren's current location so they might offer the wizard some assistance against Eparch.
Isgarren asked Mabon to lend him his strength so they could finish a banishment spell and expel all Kryptis from the tower while the ward battled Eparch directly. They were ultimately able to force the Midnight King and the Kryptis back into Nayos despite the tower sustaining heavy damage in the ensuing clash. Mabon expressed disbelief at having seen Eparch's humanoid form and that the demon had brought an army with him this time around. When Mabon tried to ask Isgarren for more information, the Seer wizard dismissed any further commentary as the ward should focus on recovering from the assault and tending to their fallen and wounded.
Life Above Garenhoff
After the Wizard's Tower had relocated close to the town of
Garenhoff
, the townsfolk began relying on the wizard's protection. He was praised by many denizens of Garenhoff for providing
elemental
guardians to protect the town and its people, but he was rarely seen or heard.
Some of the townspeople viewed the gifted elementals with skepticism as they believed that something felt off about the elementals.
Despite his reclusive nature, Isgarren was said to have occasionally sent invitations to some of the townsfolk to come live in his castle; none of the people who entered the Wizard's Tower were ever heard from again.
In the past, Isgarren visited the
Mystic Forge
in search of special compounds.
Zommoros
, the
djinn
owner of the Forge, only learned of the visit afterwards. Due to lack of proper
skritt
documenting, he was unable to find any record of what items Isgarren had tossed into the Forge and what the wizard had received in return. The djinn hoped that Isgarren returned one day so he could inquire the wizard about the nature of his work and where he had been. However, no one had heard from Isgarren for a long time, and Zommoros pondered if something had happened to the wizard since the previous visit.
At some point, the
charr
merchant
Brine Clutchall
ran a shop at Garenhoff but had to leave in a hurry after he had supposedly overcharged a person he believed to have been Isgarren; he began working for the
Guild Initiative
in exchange for them protecting him from the wizard.
Secrets of the Obscure
Act 1
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Could still be expanded.
Following the death of
Soo-Won
and the outbreak of the
Void
, the
Spire's Heart
became fractured from the unleashed magic. Though
Aurene
quickly mended things, the defenses of the World Spire were still injured, and required fixing. Eparch's Kryptis took advantage of this as Isgarren moved the
Wizard's Tower
to a new location, and assaulted the Wizard's Court directly. Isgarren was attacked by
Cerus
, and left to go into hiding at
The World Spire
to prevent harming others as he fought off the possession.
Act 3
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information on post-story achievements.
When the Astral Ward had finally located Isgarren, the Wizard's Court and
Wayfinder
set off to rescue Isgarren and cleanse him of possession. After chasing the Seer across the
Bastion of the Celestial
and fighting the ancient seer's many fragmented magics, a final confrontation occurred atop
the World Spire
itself. However, instead of simply removing the possessing Kryptis from Isgarren, a new rift opened and dragged the Wayfinder and Isgarren through into
Nayos
itself, where
Cerus
was waiting for the pair. Unable to properly move, Isgarren gave the Wayfinder what little help he could so that they could confront Cerus. It wasn't enough, however, but rescue came in the unexpected form of
Peitha
, Kryptis Lord and sister of Cerus, who turned on her brother to assist the Wayfinder. After Cerus' defeat, Peitha opened a new rift for the three to leave Nayos and return to the Wizard's Tower. There, Isgarren had a brief moment to recovery before confronting Peitha and her intentions. With the Wayfinder's opinion considered, Isgarren agreed to a tentative alliance with Peitha to confront and kill Eparch once and for all.
Through the Veil
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
When the
Wayfinder
has returned to the
tower
, Isgarren could be seen in company of
Peitha
. Contrary to the statements issued previously by both her and
Frode
, the wizard was visibly and audibly upset by the presence of the
Kryptis
in his home. Despite this, he listened to Peitha's reports about ongoing escalation happening in
Nayos
— the homeland of Kryptis. Reluctantly, he agreed to consider aiding the cause — not out of sympathy towards the Kryptis, but out of necessity, believing it better to stop the threat before it grows. According to him, no meaningful effort could be launched until current incursions are stopped. Yet, while speaking privately with the Wayfinder, he decided to allow volunteers to follow them into Nayos. At the time, he also shared that according to Peitha's words
Arina
, Frode's daughter, is still alive inside Nayos.
As Isgarren was preparing to send
Peitha
and the
Wayfinder
to
Nayos
, a
Kryptis Rift
emerged inside
The Wizard's Tower
. When efforts to neutralise it proved futile, Isgarren urged his allies to instead use it to cross into Nayos and disable it from the other side. By combining efforts with Peitha, Wayfinder and newly
ascended
Zojja
, enemy Kryptis were defeated and the rift was sealed. Still inside of Nayos, Isgarren issued a last warning to the Wayfinder as they followed Peitha deeper into unfamiliar lands. He then stepped into a
Wizard Portal
, presumably heading back into the tower.

Dialogue
The Wizard's Tower
After completing
Treachery
and before beginning
Secrets Obscured
Wayfinder
, after everything... I must thank you. For protecting my people, my home. This place is a reflection of myself, so to say. Without it... I don't know what would become of me. Or
Tyria
.
Anything else I can do?
These events gave me pause and a moment to reflect on things I've left unsettled for too long. I...have a task I'd request of you.
What is it?
<Character name>:
What is it?
Isgarren:
We
have kept the walls around Tyria up for ages now. There has been much conflict in that time, all across the world.
Isgarren:
There are items that have been left hidden in the process. It seemed the safest approach, but the situation has changed.
Isgarren:
They must be recovered. Our gift to the people of Tyria is that they never know just how much danger they've been in.
I've got plenty of tasks already.
You're welcome.
While Secrets Obscured is active
Are you making progress on the task I gave you?
What was I supposed to be doing again?
You were given a list of items that need to be retrieved, lest the citizens of Tyria discover the nature of our work.
Ah, that's right.
Can you give me any advice on where to find them?
Very well. What do you need to know?
Where is this jeweled amulet?
I suspect it fell from the
tower
when it was moved.
Garenhoff
would be the logical place to start.
Back.
Leave.
Where can I find "Planning for Tyria's Future"?
The tome was sealed away in one of the
beacons
in
Orr
, then it was lost when the continent
fell
. If it has survived, it will be in a
tower
there.
Which one though?
Its caretaker
went mad and renamed it. I believe he started calling himself a vizier. Perhaps that will narrow it down.
Back.
Leave.
Where should I look for this tainted
bloodstone
censer?
The cursed censer was locked away in the
Bloodstone Caves
, where I had hoped it would remain. But alas, here we are.
Back.
Leave.
Do you know what
Mabon
's favorite food from
Divinity's Reach
was?
I never asked him about it, but he often spoke fondly of cuisine from
Elona
, and also of the value of bloodstone as an ingredient. Perhaps some combination of them?
Back.
Leave.
What about this
Kryptis
research document?
The research was being done at
Rata Pten
before its demise. I wouldn't tarry long—I've heard talk of excavations happening in the area.
Back.
Leave.
What do I need a Kryptis body for?
It's less about needing one and more about not leaving any behind. Incursions in Tyria proper increase the risk of their remains being found.
Back.
Leave.
Where should I find this missing stone?
The stone was last in possession of a
ward
member at the
former beacon
in
Ascalon
. It is mistakeable for an ordinary stone and likely still resides there.
Back.
Leave.
Leave.
I'm on it.
After completing Secrets Obscured
You did well in gathering those items I requested. Now, what can I do for you?
Have
Peitha
and the Kryptis kept their word?
(after completing
Sovereign of Nayos
)
What choice have they? I've little concern for Peitha's rule in the short term; return to me in two hundred years and ask me this question again. The answer will be more meaningful then.
I have other questions.
I'll try.
How's your recovery coming along?
No need to worry. I have learned to bear the burden of this role over time, and I don't break easily. I will be fine.
I have other questions.
I'll leave you to rest.
Are there still Kryptis attacks happening?
(before completing
Sovereign of Nayos
)
Of course. Those likely will not subside until
Eparch
himself is dealt with. Your continued assistance in combating those invasions would be of great value.
I have other questions.
I'll leave you to rest.
Are you ever going to reveal yourselves to
Tyria
?
That is not a decision that can be made lightly. Secrecy has been very important, both for our cause and for Tyria. But...perhaps someday. If the situation changes.
I have other questions.
I'll leave you to rest.
I'm curious. Can you tell me about the
Seers
?
Ah. My fhogos—my kin. I would not be surprised if I was the only one you'll ever meet.
Mabon
and I were both birthed by stubborn beings. And like the Tyrian
mursaat
, my kind are all dead.
I'm so sorry.
We didn't age well. And we lived a very difficult experience... Of all my thousands of years of self-reflection, I barely spent my adolescence among them. I parted from the horde after...
...After?
We once tried to fell the
dragons
. The
Elder Races
of Tyria. The Seer doyen,
Sidony
, cried for war. But...just before we were to aid the mursaat in their attack on
Zhaitan
... Sidony decided against.
(Listen intently.)
The mursaat never forgave us, and I empathized with that. They were turned to bone scraps and bile... But they would hunt us for thousands of years after. A few hundred of them, for all of us.
You and Mabon, then...
An unlikely alliance. He was different, and more so resembled the mursaat I've met external Tyria: brutal, yes, but fair. He's endured as much as I have.
Mursaat...external Tyria.
Some, at least. Who could say? Mabon searched, but... He stayed here all this time, after all. Perhaps, they were not worth finding...?
I'm sorry, about Mabon...
I am, too.
I have other questions.
I'll leave you to rest.
I think I'm good for now.
Soaring Sands
Before completing
Path of Divinity
Yes, Wayfinder? Keep it brief, will you?
Are you feeling all right?
No. Sea travel does not suit me. And somehow I am still feeling its effects, despite having my feet on solid ground again. Figuratively, that is.
Sorry to hear that. Maybe you need to rest.
My pleasure.
After completing Path of Divinity
...
Should I take your silence as shame, guilt, or indignation?
I have regrets. These are not the first. Does such an admission bring you joy? Regardless, I'm contemplating
Vloxx
's motivations. What does he truly seek with
Ancora
?
I don't know. But I need to be able to count on you to help me find out.
During
Memories of Another Time
Primary article:
Memories of Another Time#Dialogue
Eternity's Garden
Before completing
A Brief Reprieve
(If not holding
Medication
)
Wayfinder. Just...give me some time. I need to rest.
(If holding
Medication
)
(groans) I need rest, Wayfinder. I won't be much help to you until this curse is broken. Or until I'm dead.
Don't be so dramatic. Try this.
I...I don't know how much a mortal remedy will... Ah, never mind that. Thank you, Wayfinder.
Happy to help.
I'll leave you be.
After completing A Brief Reprieve
Thank you. The remedy you provided was no curse-breaker...but the gesture has dulled some of the pain. I appreciate the thought.
We'll figure it out, Isgarren.

Trivia
Isgarren is
voiced
by
Xander Berkeley
.
Narrative designer
Peter Fries
has once jokingly suggested that Isgarren is the son of Wasgarren, and his son is Wilbigarren, the joke being about the English verb tenses 'is,' 'was,' and 'will be.'
Grand Wizard Garren
was a mage who designed the gardens in
the Upper City
of
Divinity's Reach
during
King Roderick
's reign circa 1265
AE
.
The similar names and titles have left some questioning if there is a relation, which remains unanswered.
Isgarren's involvement in
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
was first teased in a blog post.
Between the release of
Bastion of the Penitent
and the development of
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
,
Bobby Stein
,
Crystal Reid
and
Jason Reynolds
had a pitch of Isgarren's story taking place in a
raid
themed around the Wizard's Tower. Isgarren would invite people over for a tour but had ulterior motives: he intended to experiment on the visitors in order to sustain the life of an ill family member. The story's tone was meant to be bittersweet, showing that Isgarren was not acting out of malevolence but had a sympathetic reason for his dubious actions.
Narrative Director
Bobby Stein
has jokingly suggested that the reason Isgarren's weapon pierces his shoulder when he folds his arms is due to Isgarren being a
masochist
.
Despite Seers being
genderless
,
Isgarren is only referred to with masculine pronouns, and is fine with being called "he."
Isgarren is also referred to as:
The Curator
The Mask Scourge
See also
Associated items
Mini Isgarren
Relic of Isgarren
References

Issvano, Avatar of the All Seer
Race
Construct
,
Seer
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Temple of the Blessed Mantle
(
Eternity's Garden
)
Organization
Seer constructs
Issvano, Avatar of the All Seer
is a projection of
the All Seer
. In life, Issvano was the Sacred Arms Weaver of the
seers
.
When the seers abandoned
Castora
to keep it from the
mursaat
, Issvano stayed behind to watch over the island alongside several other seers, acting as the All Seer's avatar by offering their soul essence to protect the island.

Knockback
to foes in a small circular
AoE
around them.
Spiral Burst Skill
— A field of mines made of chains appears on the ground around them, exploding after a short delay. Standing on a mine while it explodes damages and causes
Knockback
.
Issvano's Bombardment
— Rains down
projectiles
on the other avatars' platforms. Only used when not in combat.
Luce's Lashing Vortex
— Floats in the air and then moves to the side, generating nine whirling chain vortices around them in a Y-shape. Each chain vortex in a row is larger than the last. Standing in a chain vortex deals extreme damage multiple times a second and applies
2
Bleeding
(10s)
and
Crippled
(3s)
.

Vulnerability
(10s)

Streaks of Calogero
—
Leaps
forward, damaging and causing
Knockback
to targets in their path.

— Slams a chain into the ground in front of them, striking as two overlapping AoEs. A rectangular AoE extends out in front of them, dealing damage and applying
5
Vulnerability
(5s)
, while the large circular AoE beyond it launches struck targets into the air, dealing heavy damage and applying
3
Confusion
(3s)
.
Unnamed skill
—  Chain two enemies together with a
Linked Edict
, inflicting damage to them both when separated too far.

This article or section contains information about Guild Wars 2 which is known (or is likely) to be out of date.
Please read this information with caution and update it with current information if you can.
Details: "This boss saw updated mechanics with the aquatic fractal update, including updated skills."
Jellyfish Beast
Race
Jellyfish
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Aquatic Ruins Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Image(s)
Interactive map  (floor  24)
The
Jellyfish Beast
is the final enemy encountered in the
Aquatic Ruins Fractal
. It summons smaller jellyfish and also has a tendency to swallow its foes whole. If it eats a character at low health it will defeat them instead of downing them regardless of downed penalty.
Location
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Aquatic Ruins Fractal
Combat abilities
Abilities
Slashes
Summons Allies
Effects
Eat
- The jellyfish beast
heals
and takes reduced damage while
eating
.
Electrified Shield
- Electrified shielding protects the boss, rendering attacks ineffective and damaging attackers.
Gnawed
- Your struggles to escape are in vain.
Weakened
- Takes additional damage.

Notes
Justiciar Agatha spawns at the very beginning of the White Mantle counter-attack on Saidra's Haven (usually 2-3 minutes after start) at the main gate of
Fort Evennia
and slowly walks to Saidra's Haven. If there's players waiting for her at the spawn point, she dies quite quickly and it's therefore recommended to wait ahead of time directly at the spawn point as not to miss her.
She will not spawn until the pre-events for
White Mantle Control: Saidra's Haven
have been completed (specifically
Liberate Saidra's Haven from the White Mantle (Control Points)
) and Saidra's Haven is liberated from White Mantle control. If you have been waiting for some time for the event to spawn, complete the events in Saidra's Haven in order to trigger the event.
The event chain for the Justiciar to spawn is on a timer. Check
Lake Doric
for the timer "Saidra's Haven".
See also
Legendary Cairn the Queenslayer
Siegemaster Immelhoof

======================================================================
PAGE: Kalidris Sparrowhawk
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Kalidris_Sparrowhawk
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
,
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
, and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Kalidris Sparrowhawk
Race
Tengu
Level
80
Rank
Veteran
Legendary
Location
Dragon Response Mission: Caledon Forest
(
Caledon Forest
)
Organization
Tyrian Alliance
Image(s)
Appearance as a member of the
Tyrian Alliance
.
“
I can't speak for all of us, but my family—everyone in the Great House—thought humans would destroy us. You know, like they almost did with the charr. It was always safer to just stay inside our walls. There's a part of you that knows it's not all true. It can't be. The world's too big. Hopefully I can change a few minds. The way you changed mine.
—
Kalidris Sparrowhawk
Kalidris Sparrowhawk
is a
tengu
messenger who brings word of the
icebrood
's siege of the
Dominion of Winds
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
When the
Tyrian Alliance
reaches out to the tengu to join the international organization after the worldwide
Kryptis
invasions, the Dominion appoints Kalidris as the royal messenger to hear the delegates out with Kalidris hoping that the talks will eventually lead to the Dominion opening up their borders and embracing the rest of the world in full.
Biography
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Early years
Kalidris is a young tengu, notable for her speed as a messenger. She was born to one of the four Great Houses of the
Dominion of Winds
but grew up friendless, dedicating her time to being a courier in adulthood. She claimed to know every route through the aerie. However, like many
tengu
, she was isolated within the Dominion, with very little knowledge of the outside world. By her admission she had only been outside the walls twice before her encounter with the
Pact Commander
.
The Icebrood Saga
The Dominion of Winds's western entrance was
besieged
by a large force of
icebrood
in 1333
AE
. The icebrood attacked the nearby
Town of Cathal
,
Caledon Haven
and
Hanto Trading Post
on their way to the city. Kalidris was tasked with escaping across the bridge to Caledon Forest before the
Frost Colossus
sealed it. As she had only been across the wall two times before, she was uncertain of how she could summon aid for the besieged tengu. However, she encountered
Canach
and the Pact Commander who were rushing to stop the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
's attacks. En route, Kalidris admitted that she had never even met a
sylvari
before and was curious if all were like Canach, to which Canach replied that most sylvari were similarly naive as Kalidris.
The reinforcements were stranded on the bridge, with the tengu unwilling to open the gates to prevent the Frost Colossus from breaking through. Kalidris was able to assist in defeating the Colossus and breaking the siege. Her fellow tengu were stunned that Kalidris had supposedly gathered an army and led the counter-attack in mere hours. In her conversations with the Pact Commander, Kalidris revealed the tengu's fears that the humans would exterminate them. The Commander recognised that Kalidris knew this was not true, and assured her that they would come to the tengu's aid. Kalidris had little influence on tengu society and was uncertain if the tengu would change their isolationist policies, but said she would begin sharing her tales of this collaboration in the tavern.
End of Dragons
Kalidris accompanied several volunteers from the
Pact
and
Elona
to
Cantha
by
airship
in 1335
AE
to aid Canthans in
fighting
the maddened Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
who was threatening to spread the all-devouring
Void
beyond the confines of
the Jade Sea
. The combined forces of the three continents were ultimately able to weaken Soo-Won enough for the Pact Commander's party to
defeat
the manifested
Dragonvoid
and end Soo-Won's life so the Void invasion would cease at last.
Janthir Wilds
Act 1
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
In 1337
AE
after the worldwide
Kryptis
incursions,
Queen Jennah
began the process of unifying Tyrian nations under a common alliance in
Lion's Arch
so the nations could better protect themselves as a united front against future threats. She sent invitations to talks to various nations, including the
Dominion of Winds
. Kalidris was sent as the tengu representative to the first
Tyrian Alliance
conference, where she was surprised at the new and strange experiences outside the Dominion, including the appearance of the ancient
Seer
Isgarren
.
Absolution
In 1338
AE
, Kalidris was present in another Tyrian Alliance meeting where
Countess Anise
and the Pact Commander informed the delegates about the details of their
successful mission
to end the
titan
threat in the ruins of
Bava Nisos
. Kalidris attended a subsequent victory celebration held in Lion's Arch, congratulating the Commander on a job well done. During the ensuing conversation she revealed that
Empress Ihn
and
Kestrel
leader
Ayumi
had been in talks with the Dominion of Winds about the Dominion possibly opening up to the rest of the world one day. Kalidris expressed hope that the talks between the two nations' leaders would bear fruit and that the Dominion's elders would agree to open the gates within their lifetimes.

Trivia
Kalidris Sparrowhawk is
voiced
by

.
Kalidris's name is based on the Greek word
σκαλίδρις
, sometimes anglicized as
kalídris
, meaning "a kind of speckled waterbird".

======================================================================
PAGE: Kanaxai, Scythe of House Aurkus
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Kanaxai,_Scythe_of_House_Aurkus
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Silent Surf Fractal
and
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Kanaxai, Scythe of House Aurkus
Race
Demon
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Silent Surf Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Organization
Kryptis
“
I don't often mingle with prey, but you've earned an introduction. I am Kanaxai, Scythe of House Aurkus, bringer of nightmares—and yours is just beginning!
—
Kanaxai
Kanaxai, Scythe of House Aurkus
is a
demon
who once dwelled in
the Deep
in
Cantha
, and serves as the final boss of
Silent Surf Fractal
. The leader of the
Nightmare Horde
connected to the
oni
, he drove many
Luxons
and other denizens of
the Jade Sea
mad. He was
defeated
by a group of
heroes
during the
return
of
Shiro Tagachi
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Kanaxai and the oni are eventually revealed to have been
Kryptis
originating from
Nayos
.
Biography
Early years
Kanaxai's exact origins and allegiances remained unknown to
Tyrians
, though rumors across
Cantha
suggested that his
oni
came from
the Mists
,
and, according to these rumors, they first appeared shortly after the
Jade Wind
of 872
AE
(1382
CC
), which was when Kanaxai was supposedly trapped beneath
the Jade Sea
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Kanaxai and the oni were actually
Kryptis
originating from
Nayos
, the Realm of Dreams within the Mists. While all the Kryptis noble houses, including Kanaxai's House Aurkus, served the will of Midnight King
Eparch
, many Kryptis and oni called the
Wanderers
had not sworn fealty to Eparch and operated independently instead.
At some point, Kanaxai executed
Lady Mosyn
, the founder of House Mosyn, at the
Coliseum of the Midnight King
under orders of his house's master
Aurkus
for Mosyn's treason against Midnight King
Eparch
.
Silent Surf Fractal
Days after the Jade Wind,
Thess
, a
Luxon
captain, was sent with his crew to look for survivors from the cataclysmic event. There, they instead encountered corrupted creatures and mysterious whispers, some of which turned out to have come from Kanaxai who was debating with an unidentified being about how long he should be hunting in the depths. Thess lost his life during the expedition, and the remaining members of the group came face to face with Kanaxai himself deep within the Jade Sea. Impressed with the
humans
' combat prowess, Kanaxai introduced himself properly and fought against them in a fierce battle. The group turned out to be stronger than the demon had expected, so he decided to leave and let the humans live but promised to return one day.
Guild Wars Factions
Not long after
Shiro Tagachi
's
defeat
in the Imperial Sanctum in 1072
AE
(1582
CC
), a group of Luxons mining the Jade Sea for valuable resources came across a subterranean chasm, which, unbeknownst to them, housed Kanaxai and a number of demonic oni. Through Kanaxai's malevolent whispers, the miners fell to corruption, and became known as the
Outcasts
. These twisted Luxons—shadows of their former selves—quickly garnered the attention of the Luxon clans inhabiting the nearby regions of the Jade Sea, and Luxon clan leaders met and agreed to send a scouting party to find the source of these Outcasts and put a stop to it.
Calling upon their greatest warriors, including the
Hero of Cantha
, the Luxons dispatched a team into the depths of the newly discovered cavern. Inhabiting the chasm were not only Outcasts, but oni,
leviathans
, terrestrial
fish
,
irukandji
, and
nightmares
. Throughout their foray into
the Deep
, the demon of the depths (eventually identified as Kanaxai) sent forth shadowy aspects of himself in an attempt to stop the intruders. After fighting through numerous aspects, the team finally came face to face with Kanaxai himself in the very bottom of the cavern, and through their combined efforts,
slew
the demon once and for all.
End of Dragons
In 1336
AE
, the
asura
Gorrik
sent his research on the demons and events encountered in
Gyala Delve
to the
Pact Commander
,
urging
them to investigate a related fractal to look for answers in the
Fractals of the Mists
to look for clues to the actions of the oni known as the
Ravenous Wanderer
. This led the Commander to relive the events surrounding Captain Thess's expedition and the first encounter with Kanaxai, revealing that the human form of the Wanderer encountered in Gyala Delve bore a curious resemblance to the late Thess.
Location
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Silent Surf Fractal
(Starts out as "Unknown Demon")

damage and

.
“
A face like a candle when the whole world seemed dark. Jory, you've always believed in me, even when I didn't believe in myself. This life we live has taken us everywhere, but as long as I wake up and see your face next to mine, I'm home.
—
Kasmeer Meade
Kasmeer Meade
is a member of the
Mesmer Collective
from a noble family. A staunch member of
Dragon's Watch
, pious follower of the
Six Human Gods
and lover of
Marjory Delaqua
, she has to balance her responsibilities with her adventuring companions and her duties in
Kryta
. Kasmeer has a
nervous tic
in the form of uncontrollable hiccups whenever she is afraid.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
.
Kasmeer's noble rank is restored by the time of the
Siege of Divinity's Reach
, and she becomes a valued member of
Queen Jennah
's court.
Biography
Early years
Kasmeer Meade was born to a wealthy noble family consisting of a
father
,
mother
, and her brother
Kyle Meade
who lived in
Divinity's Reach
. She became a childhood friend of
Lord Faren
with whom she exchanged kisses when they were eight although their relationship never advanced further despite Faren's attempts at flirting.
Kasmeer was tutored in the
mesmer
arts from a young age, and mastered the use of staff over time. After her mother had passed away, she inherited the mother's staff which had been in the family for generations from her mother's side.
At some point Kasmeer was admitted into the
Mesmer Collective
.
Living World Season 1
Kasmeer's family fell on hard times when her father was murdered in debtor's prison after spending everything to pay off the debts of Kasmeer's gambling brother, Kyle, in early 1326
AE
. When the creditors took all of the Meade family's possessions, they allowed Kasmeer to take a single item from the house: she took a stuffed bear she had since she was young that had been a gift from her father, and she had her mother's staff with her already. A minister moved into the Meade house shortly after.
The Secret of Southsun
Distraught by losing her father and possessions, Kasmeer decided to leave the unsympathetic nobility of Divinity's Reach. She began searching for a job, which led her to be employed by the detective
necromancer
Marjory Delaqua
to make ends meet. One of Kasmeer's first assignments was to travel to the recently opened vacation resort on
Southsun Cove
and meet with potential clients there. While traveling aboard a ship to reach her destination, she spotted Lord Faren sneaking aboard as well. By the time the ship anchored at the cove, Kasmeer
ran into
Inspector Ellen Kiel
who spotted Faren being a stowaway. She vouched for Faren not being a troublemaker, convincing the inspector to leave him alone. While staying at
Pearl Islet
, she spent time on the beach speaking with Faren and learned about attacks from
karka
who had been riled up by the rogue sylvari
Canach
's toxins.
Sky Pirates
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Missing content, please remove stub tag if all content seems to be here
Kasmeer was present when
Logan Thackeray
hired
Marjory to investigate the
death
of
Captain Theo Ashford
during
Dragon Bash
and noticed Logan's surprise at seeing a noble like her in a seedy bar like
The Dead End
. During this time she also learned about Marjory's mysterious spy contact whom she began humorously calling
"Mister E"
.
Tower of Nightmares
When an invisible wall was discovered in the middle of
Viathan Lake
later that year, Kasmeer joined Marjory in investigating the situation. She was able to shatter the powerful illusion causing the veil, revealing the concealed
Tower of Nightmares
guarded by the
Toxic Alliance
. The pair, along with the
Durmand Priory
and the Pact Commander, entered the tower and managed to put an end to
Scarlet Briar
's machinations within. It was during this time that Kasmeer and Marjory were properly introduced to the Commander's other allies,
Braham
and
Rox
, and talked with them.
The Origins of Madness
Kasmeer and Marjory were called to action once again when Scarlet attacked
Lornar's Pass
with a massive
Twisted Marionette
controlled from above by her airship,
The Breachmaker
, in an attempt to perfect a new weapon. The pair joined forces with the Commander, Braham, Rox and their new ally
Taimi
to defeat the construct and thwart Scarlet's latest plan. After the battle, they reunited at
The Dead End
where Kasmeer finally opened up about the fates of her family and the circumstances that had led her to be employed by Marjory. The necromancer felt sorry for Kasmeer and insisted on her staying with her for the night so Kasmeer would not have to be alone, an offer which Kasmeer eagerly took.
Battle for Lion's Arch
Kasmeer kisses
Marjory Delaqua
after
finding
her to still be alive.
Following Scarlet's
attack
on
Lion's Arch
, Kasmeer traveled to the beleaguered city to help with relief efforts. Once the deadly
miasma
had cleared, she joined the fight against
Scarlet's Alliance
, and eventually found herself aboard
The Breachmaker
itself, cornering the sylvari mastermind who had been injured fighting invading adventurers. Scarlet, broken but not beaten, severely injured Marjory and Braham, and the enraged Kasmeer, believing Marjory to be dead, engaged Scarlet in fierce combat. She distracted Scarlet with illusions and cruel taunts while the Pact Commander got close enough to
finish
the wayward
sylvari
off. Upon returning to Marjory, Kasmeer realized that the necromancer had survived Scarlet's attack and kissed her in relief, cementing their budding relationship.
The group evacuated the falling flagship while failing to prevent it from completing Scarlet's prime objective: drill into the ley lines and direct them west to the
Heart of Maguuma
. Shortly after the destruction of
The Breachmaker
, a loud cry resonated throughout Tyria although its origin remained unknown at first. As the surviving and beaten members of Scarlet's Alliance fled from the destroyed city upon their leader's demise, Lion's Arch was reclaimed.
Some time later, Kasmeer and the rest of the group returned to
The Dead End
bar to
discuss
the aftermath of the battle and their future plans, and she sent an invitation to the Commander to join them as well. While there, the group met with Marjory's big sister,
Seraph Belinda Delaqua
, who had come to check up on Marjory before departing on her latest mission to the
Brisban Wildlands
. During a conversation with the Commander, Kasmeer complimented the Commander for their teamwork in the battle against Scarlet and that her hiccups were gone. She also learned that the Commander suspected the loud cry originating from an
Elder Dragon
which Scarlet may have stirred on purpose. Kasmeer admitted to not believing herself to be a person who would overcome her fears and evil while the Commander told her that she was braver than she thought.
Living World Season 2
Festival of the Four Winds
At the
Festival of the Four Winds
, Kasmeer, along with the Commander, Rox, Marjory, Braham, and Taimi, were noted as heroes and introduced to the
Zephyrite
Master of Peace
. She, Marjory, and Taimi spent most of their time during the festival at the
Labyrinthine Cliffs
, with Kasmeer keeping a close watch on Marjory after her significant injuries, and noting both she and Marjory would be happy to return to their work with Delaqua Investigations.
Gates of Maguuma
Kasmeer traveled with Marjory to the
Seraph
outpost at
Tangle Root
at the request of the Commander, meeting up with them, along with Rox, Braham and Taimi. After they defeated the sudden
Inquest
attack and secured the outpost, she bade Belinda farewell, which was met with a stilted coolness on Belinda's part. This reaction prompted a brief discussion with the Commander over whether her noble background was to blame.
Upon encountering the Zephyrite
crash site
in
Dry Top
, Kasmeer, Marjory, and the Commander were chosen to enter the crash site, but her mesmer magic was blocked once they arrived. Fighting through the crash site and the Inquest's defenses, she tried to render aid to
Morning
, and told Marjory and the Commander that Morning had lied about not knowing why the Master of Peace was in danger. As their group investigated the crash and the slaughter of the Zephyrites, Kasmeer lamented not only the needless death involved but the possibility of another sylvari-at-large behind this destruction.
She continued to assist the Commander in their search for the Master of Peace, helping to fight and bring down the saboteur
Aerin
while wondering what would prompt the Master of Peace to leave his people in ruins elsewhere.
When their group began investigating
Scarlet Briar
's former room in
Prosperity
, Kasmeer found a copy of
Ogden Stonehealer
's "The Nature of Dragons", and was fascinated with the idea that the
elder dragons
were a natural part of Tyria.
Entanglement
After the destruction of Prosperity, Kasmeer noted the similarities of Aerin's broken speech and ramblings to the ramblings of Scarlet Briar, but quickly fell into nervous hiccups at the prospect that
Mordremoth
was awake and only beginning its attacks.
She followed the Commander to the
Cavern of the Shining Lights
using Scarlet's map, and helped reactivate the cavern defenses Scarlet had set up. Upon further discussion, she was excited at the prospect of investigating the waypoint at
Fort Salma
for the opportunity to spend time with Belinda.
At Fort Salma, Kasmeer assisted the Commander and Marjory in defending the stronghold, understanding that the fort held
krait obelisk shards
from the
Tower of Nightmares
that needed to be protected at all costs. After the fight, she was the first to notice the body of Belinda crushed by
Mordrem
vines, and tried to give some comfort to Marjory, insisting she help with the funeral proceedings. Her comfort and support were gently rejected, and she grudgingly agreed to continue on with the Commander on the condition she spent that night with Marjory.
Meeting back up in Dry Top, Kasmeer delivered the news of Belinda's death, reassuring the group that Marjory would join up with them when she could. At Taimi's announcement that she had discovered
Omadd's Machine
, Kasmeer expressed her worry about the potential damage the machine could do, citing the effect on Scarlet. After Taimi entered the machine, she shielded the Commander as they retrieved Taimi, but was then unable to immediately retrieve the Commander as well, admitting they only pulled them out once they started screaming. Talking to the Commander, Kasmeer voiced her doubts about pulling off an audience with the
Pale Tree
, but reaffirmed her commitment to see things through. She requested time to check-in on Marjory in
Divinity's Reach
before reconvening in
the Grove
, saying she'd send a message when she was ready.
The Dragon's Reach: Part 1
Meeting up with the Commander in the
Omphalos Chamber
, Kasmeer was recognized for her unveiling of the Tower of Nightmares, but deferred the credit to the whole group and watched attentively while the Commander spoke to the Pale Tree. Afterwards, as the group discussed their next moves, she stated her intent to obtain an audience with
Countess Anise
to try and sway
Queen Jennah
into attending the world leader summit, a nervous prospect considering her standing with the crown.
After meeting up at the
Ley Line Hub
, Kasmeer informed the Commander her audience with Countess Anise was a success and that Queen Jennah would be attending the summit.
The Dragon's Reach: Part 2
Before the summit could occur, Kasmeer sent a letter to the Commander, explaining Queen Jennah was now under suspicion of colluding with Scarlet Briar, and that unless the charges were dropped, Jennah would have to miss the summit to attend the hearing. She asked the Commander to meet her at a party in Divinity's Reach and help find the lying minister without arousing suspicion. Upon arrival, she explained the situation in detail and helped the Commander discover Minister Estelle's lies about the queen and convince her to recant her statements. Speaking with Countess Anise, she was invited to travel with the queen's entourage to the summit, which she accepted, hoping perhaps this would open up an opportunity to regain her title.
At the summit, Kasmeer participated in the queen's procession before standing in solidarity with the Commander during the summit talks. Once the Omphalos Chamber was attacked by
Mordrem
, her mesmer portal allowed the world leaders to escape. Cut off after the arrival of the
Shadow of the Dragon
, she fought with the Commander and Braham to protect the
Avatar of the Tree
.
Echoes of the Past
Once word spread that
Fallen Seraph
were causing trouble at Fort Salma, Kasmeer traveled with Marjory from the
Priory
to confront the ghost of Belinda. She helped fight through the ghosts to save
Explorer Campbell
, and was present to see Belinda's spirit fuse with her sword. After, she and Marjory returned to the Priory to continue their research looking for information to help the fight against Mordremoth.
Later, along with the Commander and Marjory, Kasmeer met with Ogden Stonehealer about the situation with Mordremoth, and was transported with the others to
Glint's lair
. As they fought through the various defenses of the lair, she and Marjory spoke on their joint knowledge of Glint, before finding a way to return to the Priory.
Tangled Paths
At Logan's request, Kasmeer was one of the group to rally at
Camp Resolve
in the Silverwastes, where the Commander revealed the presence of a dragon egg sired by
Glint
. There, the Commander asked her and Marjory to catch
Trahearne
up on the information they obtained at the Priory, and to tell only him about the dragon egg. She and the Commander spoke briefly on her worries about Marjory's dedication to kill Mordremoth, before the Commander asked them both to assist them in finding the Master of Peace.
Later, after the Commander tracked down the
Aspect Masters
, she, Marjory, Braham, Rox, Caithe, and the Commander followed the Master of Peace's trail into the Mordrem
labyrinth
, fighting through to find the Master of Peace dying in the center. Upon his death, they watched Caithe steal the dragon egg before disappearing and agreed to speak to the Pale Tree on a possible reason for the theft.
Point of No Return
Kasmeer had stayed behind to help prepare the launch into Maguuma against Mordremoth, and helped push back the Mordrem's attack on Camp Resolve until the Commander could call an airstrike. She followed the Commander to the final
memory seed
location, and helped fight through to the golden location the Commander had seen in their vision from the Pale Tree. Upon planting the memory seed, she stood as protection while the Commander watched the final memory, and listened as Caithe revealed the secret of the Sylvari - that they were created from Mordremoth. As Caithe slipped away, she helped the Commander take down the Shadow of the Dragon once and for all, and traveled with the group back to Camp Resolve to join the Pact Assault on Mordremoth.
Heart of Thorns
After the Pact fleet crashed in the
Heart of Maguuma
, Kasmeer and Marjory, scouting for
Caithe
, found that
Mordremoth
had attacked
The Silverwastes
, and they went to help
Amber Sandfall
while Braham and Rox went to
Red Rock Bastion
. Soon, the Commander arrived and aided Kasmeer and Marjory in defeating the
Mordrem
attacking the Pact survivors, leading the two to follow the Commander into the Heart of Maguuma. When reaching
Maguuma's Breach
, she expressed shock at how much wreckage there was, later following the Commander after their decision to either rescue the survivors or fortify the camp of survivors.
When encountering the
Itzel
hylek
, she acknowledged they seemed truthful, and after arriving in
Jaka Itzel
, she voted to stay and defend the village from the encroaching Mordrem instead of attack them. At the Mordrem camp, she described the area as 'part prison camp, part minion factory', and she would later create portals along the cliff face for the rest of the group while the Commander glided down to the main camp. Kasmeer witnessed the death of
Eir Stegalkin
at the hands of the
Mordrem Vinetooth
and aided in killing the beast.
Within the
Mordrem prisoner camp
in
Dragon's Stand
, Kasmeer and the rest of the group that had gone ahead were stalled until the Commander arrived, and she expressed curiosity over what the rest of the group had found in
Rata Novus
. She began scouting the region and found that there were two separate routes leading ahead; one to
Logan Thackeray
and the other to
Zojja
, though both were heavily guarded. When fighting the Mordrem
clones
of Zojja, she realised that they must have inherited the
asura
's will and were trying to resist. Afterwards, the Commander assigned her and Rox to protect Logan and Zojja as they were in no shape to fight.
She was absent for the
final confrontation
with Mordremoth within its mind.
Living World Season 3
Flashpoint
Kasmeer and Marjory, witnessing
Balthazar
's
reveal
.
Lady Kasmeer Meade met with the commander in
Rata Novus
, and promptly asked for an invitation to join
Dragon's Watch
. The conversation came to a halt as a few escaped dragon minions began wreaking havoc in the lab. After subduing and returning the minions back to their pens for research,
Taimi
revealed that they were to be the first test subjects of her modified Omadd's Machine,
Taimi's Machine
.
The group was then joined by
Marjory Delaqua
. Marjory had been following a being that claimed to be
Lazarus
, the last
Mursaat
. However, recent events on
Caudecus Manor
revealed that the real Lazarus had to be dead, as one of the missing pieces needed to complete the resurrection ritual was right there in the manor. Marjory told the group that this fake Lazarus being would never separate from a peculiar hand mirror. Kasmeer explained that mirrors have a long history with mesmer illusions, and how this hand mirror could be a magic mirror that hides its owner with an illusion.
Kasmeer proposed using several other mirrors, that she would enchant, to create a reflection loop, with the fake Lazarus standing between the mirrors, and the mirrors reflecting their own reflection. She theorized that the reflections would be enough to shatter the illusion, thus revealing who was hiding behind the Lazarus facade.
With the aid of the commander, Kasmeer redirected and enchanted several mirrors that Taimi had placed around the Rata Novus lab. The plan worked, and Kasmeer's mesmer magic shattered the fake Lazarus illusion. The fake Lazarus resisted, and a chaotic fight ensued. Eventually, they were forced to drop the charade, revealing themselves as none other than
Balthazar
, the
human god
of fire and war. Balthazar escaped the lab, but not before hurting Marjory with a powerful fire attack, and banishing, taking Taimi's Machine with him. Being a faithful believer in the gods, Kasmeer was paralyzed by shock. One of the Six Gods was right in front of her, and was attacking the very human race he helped bring into Tyria so many years ago.
Stunned by Balthazar's entrance and betrayal, and Marjory's severe injuries, Kasmeer excused herself in between hiccups, asking the commander to tell Marjory she loved her, and portaled herself away.
Path of Fire
Kasmeer and Rytlock arrived in Elona before the Commander. Upon the Commander's arrival in the
Free City of Amnoon
and being detained by
Captain Rahim
, Kasmeer, as part of the human delegation, vouched for the Commander and confirmed access to Amnoon. After catching up with Rytlock and Kasmeer, who expressed her fear about confronting one of the gods she had grown up worshipping, the trio entered Amnoon to speak again with Captain Rahim.
When the city was being assaulted by Balthazar's Forged troops, the city considered allying themselves with others for aid, but were at an impasse on which they should choose. Representatives from the Mordant Crescent, corrupted former members of the Sunspears, had arrived offering aid, but it would require allying themselves with Palawa Joko, a powerful undead Lich. Another option was allying with Kormir's priesthood and the remaining Sunspears, ancient guardians of Elona who may bring hope to the citizens, but doing so would incur Joko's wrath. A final option was to remain independent as before, and step up recruitment of cavaliers to protect Amnoon. Kasmeer was supporting the Sunspears, but the choice would be the Commander's. After their decision, Kasmeer pulled the Commander aside and spoke to them about why she supported Kormir and how Balthazar's goals may be noble even if his path was not.
Living World Season 4
Daybreak
After the defeat of Balthazar with Kralkatorrik and Aurene absorbing his powers, Kasmeer was sent with Marjory to keep an eye on the Elder Dragon's movements. They found Kralkatorrik perched on a mountain in Western
Kourna
and noticed him acting strange.
These actions were later revealed to be the Elder Dragon testing his newfound powers by creating remote Brandstorms and creating Death-Branded minions from corpses.
A Star to Guide Us
During the
political summit
between Elonian factions in the
Fortress of Jahai
in 1331
AE
, Taimi initially could not raise Kasmeer and Marjory on the remote communicator, which made her worry. Some time after, the pair did manage to get in contact with Taimi much to her relief, only to reveal that Kralkatorrik had suddenly vanished from the mountain.
The Crystal Dragon's disappearance was later revealed to be him mastering Balthazar's power to traverse the Mists, which he had entered to devour more magic and send
Branded
minions throughout Elona in search of Aurene.
All or Nothing
As the Pact and the Elonian alliance prepared to face Kralkatorrik in
Thunderhead Peaks
in 1332
AE
, Taimi contacted Kasmeer and Marjory and updated them on what had been happening in Elona since their last meeting. During the conversation Kasmeer and Marjory learned that Aurene had
devoured
Palawa Joko
, and they let Taimi know that they had been busy evacuating villages which had been targeted by the Branded.
The Icebrood Saga
Prologue: Bound by Blood
Some time after the
death
of Kralkatorrik, Kasmeer, Marjory and the rest of Dragon's Watch were invited to
Grothmar Valley
to attend the
All-Legion Rally
organized by
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
. Kasmeer and Marjory met with the Commander, Braham, Rytlock and Gorrik at the
Black Citadel
where they boarded a
chopper
to the
Blood Keep
.
While in Grothmar, Kasmeer ran across a small group of Charr cubs that had been excluded from practice by other cubs in the
fahrar
due to being runts and believing in gods. Kasmeer decided to
help them train together
by creating illusions of herself for them to fight. When the cubs had proved that they could handle something more difficult,
she encouraged them on a patrol as a warband
where she created illusions of various enemies such as the
Ascalonian ghosts
present throughout Ascalon and minions of each of the known Elder Dragons, for them to face. Once the warband had successfully defeated these illusions the group was
ambushed by a large devourer
. Kasmeer ordered the cubs to retreat behind her so she could protect them but the cubs, believing it to be an illusion, joined her in taking it down. Once it had been defeated Kasmeer escorted them back to the farar training area where they thanked her and decided on 'Obsidian Warband' as their name.
Whisper in the Dark
A shaken Marjory, who had been subjected to a traumatic vision of her
dead sister
created by
Jormag
's whispers in the
Bjora Marches
desperately attempts to contact Kasmeer on her communicator but receives no response.
No Quarter
Kasmeer and
Logan
arrived in
Drizzlewood Coast
midway through a parley held between the
Pact Commander
, the
United Legions
and
Ryland Steelcatcher
. They were sent by
Queen Jennah
, alongside a troop of
Seraph
soldiers to repay
Blood Legion
for their help during the
Siege of Divinity's Reach
. Kasmeer and Logan's sudden arrival angered Ryland who called off negotiations with the United Legions, leaving the Commander,
Crecia
and the remaining Imperators to decide how to proceed. A combination approach was decided on as a strategy and Kasmeer offered the help of herself and the Seraph
mesmers
in regaining the territory to the West of Drizzlewood Coast, alongside the Blood and Ash legions.
Kasmeer rallied a group of Seraph mesmers together and conferred with
Malice Swordshadow
in
Umbral Grotto
, as the United Legions prepared to take back the western settlements held by the
Dominion
. Kasmeer assured Malice that her group of mesmers could confuse the enemy's senses and make them hear and see things that weren't there and that they'd be waiting on Malice's call to join the fight when they were needed.
Kasmeer and her group worked alongside Malice in taking back
Vloxen Mine
,
Fort Defiance
and
Leadfoot Village
from Dominion control. Her group later converged with the rest of the United Legions forces at
Wolf's Crossing
where they fought to retake the bridge from the
Steel Warband
. The Seraph mesmers accompanying Kasmeer opened portals during this battle to allow United Legions troops to easily access the vantage points that
Vishen
was using to snipe their forces from.
Jormag Rising
After the glacier blocking the northern half of Drizzlewood Coast had receded and the
Pact Commander
had cleared the caves leading to the
Forward Camp
, Kasmeer reconverged with the rest of the United Legions forces there.
Kasmeer joined
Efram Greetsglory
and
Ember Doomforge
in leading the assault on the
Frost Citadel
in northern Drizzlewood Coast. She worked alongside them to secure a landing site on
The Bloodfield
where they constructed cannons to take down the gate of the citadel. Once inside the group worked to clear out the
Frost Legion
forces inside and interrupted the conversion process being used to convert Charr into dragon minions using Jormag's blood, by destroying their machines. When the Frost Citadel began to collapse and flood with blood due the damage sustained, Kasmeer helped find an alternative exit out of the keep. Outside the keep the group was confronted by the
Claw of Jormag
and worked together to construct scrap cannons that could knock it out of the sky and give them the upper hand. The group succeeded in knocking the Claw from the sky and alongside the United Legions forces, defeated it where it landed on the cliffs below.
Champions
After the events in Drizzlewood, Kasmeer spent time working with Crecia to clear out remaining
Renegades
and Dominion forces, believing it to be very important for charr-human relations that they loudly and publicly go after those groups.
When
Thaddeus Soren
contacted Dragon's Watch for help with the increased destroyer activity in the
Fields of Ruin
and Crecia's contingent was the only allied faction nearby that was available to help immediately, Kasmeer was worried about the reaction the Charr imperator would get from the people of
Ebonhawke
due to the ongoing tensions with the
treaty
and the
Separatists
there.
Kasmeer traveled to the Fields of Ruin
ahead of Crecia and the Commander to act as an intermediary and reassure the Ebonhawke locals that the Charr imperator and her troops were there to help.
Bangar
's campaign had spread a lot of fear in the stronghold, Crecia was previously his second in command and would also be the first Charr Imperator to have ever set foot inside the stronghold. Kasmeer, Crecia, and the Commander worked to drive back the invading destroyers and protect Ebonhawke from the invasion. They eventually defeated the
Destroyer of the Third Fortress
, a powerful destroyer that had dug under the city defenses and made it to the inner district of Ebonhawke.
Whilst traveling back from Ebonhawke, Kasmeer received news about Jormag's
attack on Lake Doric
and decided to
spread the word about what was happening
to potential allies. Kasmeer's warning reached
Song of Final Exile
just in time, saving countless
Kodan
there from becoming the dragons agents. Kasmeer and the Commander met with
Braham
outside the walls of Final Exile where he was following the sound of pained screams that neither of the two could hear at first. As the group approached
Owl's Abattoir
the
Owl Spirit
audibly called out to Braham for help and upon reaching the shrine the group was met with the sight of
Ryland Steelcatcher
channeling the corrupted spirit magic from Owl. Kasmeer and the Commander fought Ryland in a bid to split his focus and distract him while Braham focussed on breaking the connection. Owl was released from Ryland's grasp but rather than succumb to the dragon's advances she sacrificed herself to prevent the shifting of balance.
End of Dragons
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Missing content, please remove stub tag if all content seems to be here
Imperial court with Kasmeer, the
Pact Commander
,
Empress Ihn
,
Joon
, and
Minister Li
.
When the Commander crash landed on the shores of Cantha, nearly triggering a international incident, (newly elevated) Ambassador Meade was sent by
Queen Jennah
on a diplomatic mission. She was accompanied by Marjory, having invited her along (leading to some tension regarding Marjory's lack of interest in her Canthan heritage, about which Kasmeer hadn't thought to ask). The party was there to welcome the recently released Commander to a Cantha whose doors were open for the first time in their lives.
Although she might have been nervous as an Ambassador, Kasmeer focused on confidence and embraced the role (despite the disconcerting politics and pressure), and worked to minimize or soothe ruffled feathers and advance both Kryta's interests and, later, those of Dragon's Watch.
Epilogue
Several weeks after Dragon's Watch had resolved the Void problem,
Kasmeer sent an invitation to the Commander and other Dragon's Watch members, including Taimi, Gorrik, Logan, Rox, Rytlock, Canach, and Braham, to meet her and Marjory in the Dead End bar in Divinity's Reach, which now belonged jointly to Marjory and Kasmeer.
Kasmeer and Marjory greeted everyone while being visibly excited, and they also made small talk till everyone arrived. Once the group had gathered, Kasmeer and Marjory recounted the time they had spent together since they first met and proposed to each other while all of their friends cheered on them. The whole group shared a toast and Marjory stated that while there might not be the need for Dragon's Watch anymore, they will always be a guild.

Ascalon

Tribunes' Trench
Kryta
Divinity's Reach
Walking through the city
(only during

Combat abilities
Effects
Skills (Living World Season 1)
Winds of Chaos
-
Bounce
an orb of energy between foes and allies. The first hit applies
torment
, while subsequent hits to enemies apply
confusion
. Allies that the orb bounces to gain
fury
and
might
.
Blink
-
Manipulation
.
Teleport
to a target location.
Phantasmal Defender
-
Phantasm
.
Summon an
illusion
that taunts your target and all nearby targets while
blocking
. When it finishes blocking, the illusion explodes, dealing increased
damage
for each attack it blocked.
Illusion of Defense
- A portion of incoming damage is transferred to the illusionary defender.
Skills (unsorted)
[
verification requested
]
Phase Retreat
-
Clone
.
Teleport
away from your target, summoning a
clone
that casts
Winds of Chaos
.
Phantasmal Warlock
-
Phantasm
.
Summon
illusions
that deal
damage
and inflict conditions.
Chaos Armor
-
Blind
and
confuse
nearby foes while you gain chaos aura.
Chaos aura grants you a random
boon
and inflicts your foe with a random
condition
whenever you are struck.
Chaos Storm
- Create a magical storm at the target location that applies random
conditions
to foes and
boons
to allies. The first strike of the storm dazes foes.
Time Warp
-
Glamour
.
Create an area that warps time, granting you and your allies
quickness
while
slowing
enemies.
Feedback
-
Glamour
.
Create a dome around your foes that
reflects
projectiles.
Portal Entre
-
Glamour
.
Create an entry portal at your location that
teleports
allies to your exit portal.
(Creating a new entrance portal while you have an active portal will destroy the active portal.)
(Scripted use)
Portal Exeunt
-
Glamour
.
Create an exit portal.
(Scripted use)
Dialogue
For previous dialogue:
Kasmeer Meade/dialogue
Kessex Hills
In
Thunder Ridge Camp
during
Making the Antitoxin
Oh, hello again. You and I seem attracted to trouble, don't we? I like that about us.
Nice work exposing that tower.
I never could resist peeking under the wrapping to spoil a surprise. And that tower's a huge surprise. No one expected the krait and
Nightmare Court
would work together like this.
Why are you and Marjory still here?
Jory's researching an elixir to counteract the toxins coming from that nightmare of a tower. I'm sure she'd welcome your help. She brought her
Mendel
weapons, so I know she's worried.
Her Mendel weapons?
It's a long story, but back when Marjory was a
Ministry Guard
, she saw a boy get killed. His ghost spoke to her and asked her to take his weapons into her custody. They held some significance for him.
What significance?
I don't know. But now, they hold significance for her. They're sharp as dragon's teeth. When she brings them, I know she thinks we may have to fight.
Good to know.
Got it. Good luck.
Here's hoping.
The Tower of Nightmares
I feel like this place knows us—what we're afraid of.
What do you mean?
The tower—and maybe Scarlet as well—keeps using our most personal fears against us.
Hm. You may be right.
It does seem that way.
The Battle For Lion's Arch
Jory is going to be the death of me.
What do you mean?
She nearly died in there. I'm going to have nightmares about her lying so still for the rest of my days. But now, she insists on continuing to fight.
How is she?
She's in a lot of pain. It's her ribs. They're bruised at best, maybe broken. It's sheer determination keeping her on her feet.
Are you okay?
Worried and tired, but fine. When you love someone like Jory, you have to accept moments like this. It's who she is.
She isn't going to die when she has someone like you to live for.
Thanks. That means a lot coming from you. We made a pretty good team in there, didn't we? I know I didn't do much, but I'm glad I had a chance to fight by your side.
You did great, Kasmeer. Stay safe. We'll all celebrate when this is over.
Hang in there.

Trivia
Kasmeer is
voiced
by
Janell Cox
.
She wields
Kasmeer's Staff
and originally wore the
Phoenix Light Armor Skin
, but changed to the
Kasmeer's Regal Outfit
in Living World Season 3, all of which were made available for purchase through the
Gem Store
.
During the Tower of Nightmares arc, Kasmeer admitted her clothes at the time were simply illusions, and that she was actually naked.
This has also been the only time she made allusions to such since.
Kasmeer is commonly used on the Guild Wars 2 Wiki in images used for size comparisons.
ArenaNet
playfully mentioned this in-game in the
Living World Season 2
instance
Fallen Hopes
when she mentioned she had been turned into a unit of measurement.
Kasmeer's house with Marjory,
The Delaqua & Meade Residence
, is located in the
Eastern Commons
of
Divinity's Reach
.
Kasmeer is one of the only characters who accompanies the Commander in every
Expansion
within the
Elder Dragon Saga
.

Welcome to Paradise

Keep Construct
Race
Construct
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Stronghold of the Faithful
(
Forsaken Thicket
)
“
Enjoy my creation. It'll be the last you see.
—
Xera
Keep Construct
is a
magical
construct made from ambient architecture pieces. It serves as the second boss of the
Stronghold of the Faithful
raid wing.
Location
Heart of Maguuma
Forsaken Thicket
Stronghold of the Faithful
Zealot's Bastion
Walkthrough
Primary article:
Stronghold of the Faithful#Keep Construct
Combat abilities
Abilities
From the Ruins of the Twisted Castle
Behavior
Xera's Energy
- At both 66% and 33% health, Keep Construct will become invulnerable. At the same moment white and red orbs will begin moving from the statues to the Keep construct. Players may stand in the way of the orb of the same color they are attuned to and absorb the incoming orb.
Each orb that reaches the Keep Construct will give it a stack of
Magic Blast Intensity
, increasing the damage done by Magic Blast.
Construct Core
- Keep Construct withdraws from the battle temporarily exposing three phantasms protecting its core. If the three phantasms are overcome, the Construct's Core becomes exposed and can be pushed (or pulled) through unstable rifts. Pulling the core through rifts will add a stack of
Compromised
, increasing the damage Keep Construct takes upon its return.
Insidious Projection
- When two (white) projections merge, they will form an Insidious Projection.
Radiant and Crimson Phantasms
- When radiant and crimson (white and red) phantasms spawn on the battlefield, each player will receive a random attunement. Players may only kill phantasms of the same attunement as themselves.
Xera's Fury
- Large circles will spawn under each player and a red circle will slowly expand outward. Once the circle is fully filled, all players within the circle will be dealt a moderate amount of damage.
Effects
(unlocked when
Xera's Embrace
is fully removed)
Unnamed defiance skill
—   Occurs when defiance is broken.
When the defiance bar breaks, players get access to the Keep Construct's core.
Compromised
- Incoming damage increased by 75% per stack.
Gaining Power
- Increases direct damage by 10% and condition damage by 3%.
Magic Blast Intensity
- Additional stacks will increase Magic Blast damage.
Xera's Boon
- The
Construct
is protected from damage.
Xera's Embrace
- Magic protects the
Keep Construct
's core from external damage. Can be removed.
Enraged
- Increases damage dealt by 200%.
Skills
Backhand
- Keep Construct slams his right arm behind him.
Construct Aura
- A passive aura which damages all players in the arena.
Hail of Fury
- Keep Construct leaps into the air and drops debris around the area. Players hit are
Stunned
for 1 second.
Magic Blast
- Keep Construct unleashes an explosion that scales with the number of stacks of
Magic Blast Intensity
once it comes out of invulnerability at 66% and 33%.
Phantasmal Blades
- Keep Construct bunkers down and casts rotating area of effect attacks around him.
Swipe
- Keep Construct swipes at the players.
Tower Drop
- Keep Construct leaps into the air and drops onto players
knocking down
for 3 seconds.
Xera's Fury
-
Xera
's horrible spell bears down on you, dealing heavy damage.
Stolen skills
Unstable Artifact
Drops
Keep Construct Chest

(doubled with

Notes
Keep Construct has 1910
armor
(687 toughness and 1223 defense). Compared to regular
Raid
bosses with 2597
armor
, this translates to +36%
power
damage
.
Keep Construct has a 200 range diameter
hitbox
, which is considered large.

Stronghold of the Faithful teaser
.
