======================================================================
PAGE: Kela, Seneschal of Waves
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Kela,_Seneschal_of_Waves
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
Kela, Seneschal of Waves
Race
Crustacean
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Guardian's Glade
(
Shipwreck Strand
)
Image(s)
Kela's Scalding Wave.
Kela, Seneschal of Waves
is an
extremely powerful
crustacean
seneschal
shackled by
seer
-made chains. She is a target of the
Inquest
's
Principal Biomancer Sebb
, who seeks to control her.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
The seneschals—Kela and
Gwyllian
—were both assigned to forever guard
Castora
and its secrets when the seers left the island.
Biography
Early years
Prior to the
seers
' departure to
face
the
mursaat
in battle in
Janthir
,
Sidony
bound Kela and a few other noteworthy beasts with powerful
magic
to guard
Castora
's secrets from both trespassers and the seer
Isgarren
whom Sidony had banished from the island. These bound beasts became known as seneschals, and the magic would keep them alive for millennia and compel them to carry out the seers' last command as they would guard specific territories of Castora.
Kela, Seneschal of Waves, would sink countless trespassing ships near Castora while remaining unseen, helping in part to prevent interlopers from entering and leaving the island alive. The castaways who survived her attacks would name
Seneschal Sands
, the area where Kela was seen patrolling, after her on
Shipwreck Strand
. Drunken castaways would boldly boast that they could fight Kela bare-handed to make themselves seem more impressive to their fellow castaways.
Despite the seneschal's vigilant patrols, however,
Captain Weyandt
managed to reach Castora intact somehow millennia later and hid three pieces of the aquabreather
Agaleus
across Shipwreck Strand. Some crew members, including
Ryumond Lakes
, mutinied and were cast off on the island as a result, while Weyandt seemingly returned home safely without being sunk by Kela unlike any other castaways who remained stuck on Castora.
Visions of Eternity
When the
Inquest
sailed to Castora in 1338
AE
, they lost the majority of their ships to arcane and natural hazards in Castoran waters. Kela killed four Inquest scientists when they trespassed on her territory, and the Inquest learned her name from local castaways.
As the
Tyrian Alliance
pursued the Inquest weeks later, their ships were also
ambushed
by Kela who successfully sank the
Martingale
while hiding out of sight beneath the waves although her roar was heard by the voyagers.
However, the seneschal was unable to sink the other alliance ship,
The Dark Leviathan
, due to it being magically warded by the seer
Isgarren
who was onboard.
After making landfall, the alliance was constantly being harried by aggressive crustaceans which were working for Kela. The
Pact Commander
,
Rytlock Brimstone
, and
Sayida the Sly
tracked the Inquest's movements and eventually found Sebb and
Overseer Kuda
burying artifacts in the sand. Upon being found out, Sebb decided to test his theory and tossed one of the artifacts at the interrupting trio, rousing Kela who arrived to claim the objects. The Inquest pair escaped while the alliance trio had to
face the enraged seneschal in combat
. The trio quickly noticed the magical chains binding Kela and realized that her actions were compelled by magic rather than her free will, so they decided to calm her down by unearthing and tossing artifacts to her instead of trying to kill her. After Kela had acquired enough artifacts, she calmed down and no longer acted hostile towards the trio who watched her vanish into the sea. The Commander recognized one of the artifacts as a
shadowstone
piece and realized that the artifacts and the source of the strange magic were related to the seers.
Location
Castora
Shipwreck Strand
Guardian's Glade

Kenut
Race
Largos
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Arcane Springs
(
Mythwright Gambit
)
Image(s)
Render by
HyunBo Park
.
“
The water draws close. Your hourglass is nearly empty.
—
Kenut
Kenut
, along with her brother
Nikare
, is one of the
Twin Largos
found in the
Mythwright Gambit
fighting for
Qadim
.
Location
Crystal Desert
Mythwright Gambit
Fountain Room
Walkthrough
Primary article:
Mythwright Gambit#Twin Largos: Nikare and Kenut
Combat abilities
Abilities
Largos Assassin
Effects
(unlocked when channeling
Aquatic Barrage
)
Stun
— Vulnerable while broken.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Enraged
(when
Nikare
is defeated)
— Seeing their kin fall in combat, the largos has become enraged, greatly increasing the damage they deal.
Behavior
Fixates on the player with the highest toughness on the platform.
Skills
Aquatic Assassin
(upon reaching
10
Waterlogged
)
— You spent too much time in the largos' domain, allowing them to hunt you down.
Aquatic Aura
— Aquatic magic ripples through the foe's body and inflicts damage.
Aquatic Barrage
(at 50% health and below)
— The largos unleashes powerful water magic in an indescriminate
[
sic
]
flurry of water bolts.
Spins and barrages other platforms with water. Break defiance bar to stop attack.
Aquatic Slash
— Stabs foes with an aquatic blade that inflicts damage.
Vaporous Strike
— Stabs foes with a blade of caustic vapor that inflicts damage.
Aquatic Vortex
— A vortex that roams the platform, inflicting damage and allowing the largos to more easily hunt its prey.
Cyclone Burst
— Water flows out from the largos on all sides, damaging anyone caught in its path.
Largos' Domain
— The water is the largos' domain while they remain alive. Being in it will damage you and apply
waterlogged
.
Sea Swell
— A large swell blasts across the platform, knocking back any who are caught by it. The largos gains protection for each foe struck.
Unnamed skill
(at 25% health and below)
— Inflict
Aquatic Aura
to foes every 3 seconds.
Vapor Jet
— The largos disappears into mist and teleports to their target, releasing a vapor jet that deals damage.
Stealth
(2s), then teleports to furthest target and steals boons in front of her.
Stolen skills
Detonate Plasma
Notes

armor
(1374 toughness and 1361 defense).
Compared to regular
Raid
bosses with 2597
armor
, this translates to about -5%
power
damage
.
Kenut has a 120 range diameter
hitbox
.

Kimmes the Historian
Race
Ghost
Profession
Mesmer
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Eye of the North
(
Shiverpeak Mountains
)
Hall of Monuments
(
Eye of the North
)
Organization
Ebon Vanguard
Services
Merchant
Kimmes the Historian
traveled from
Ascalon
long ago to study the
Hall of Monuments
and the memories imbued therein. He discovered and restored long-dormant magics that enabled him to view the deeds of different adventurers who had traveled to the
Eye of the North
over the years. After returning to Ascalon and dying in
Ebonhawke
, Kimmes's spirit returned to the Eye where he remains to this day, waiting for a hero who he sees fit to inherit the artifacts once owned by the
slayer
of
the Great Destroyer
.

Dialogue
In the old Eye of the North instance
If the player owns
Guild Wars: Eye of the North
I recognize the worthiness of your soul, hero. I cling to this world in order to guard these items, destined for you. Centuries have passed, and you're finally here. Collect your legacy. The time has come.
Show me my legacy.
What items have you been keeping?
(If the player unlocked all the rewards)
They belonged to a
hero
who earned all these relics during their life. I've waited centuries for someone to come who embodies the same heroism. You are that person. I give it all to you.
(If the player unlocked some of the rewards)
They belonged to a hero who earned some, but not all, of these relics during their life. I've waited centuries for someone to come who embodies the same heroism. It's you. Claim what is yours.
How do I earn the rest?
Only the ancient hero, from the period following
the Guild Wars
, when
Prophecies
were fulfilled in
Tyria
, when
Factions
were united in
Cantha
, and when
Nightfall
fell upon
Elona
, can quest for these relics.
Why are you here, of all places?
I returned with the
Ebon Vanguard
when they were recalled to their final home in
Ebonhawke
. It was there that I drew my last breath. But, I've always known that this was where I belonged.
I'd like to ask something else.
If I pass your grave, I'll reflect on your determination.
(If the player unlocked all the rewards)
Why are you here, of all places?
(same as above)
I'd like to ask something else.
Thank you. I must go.
Good-bye.

A hero from another time once strode these halls. I see the potential for the same bravery and glory in your eyes.
Tell me more about this hero.
More than two hundred years ago, the hero honored here found glory after the Guild Wars, when Prophecies were fulfilled in Tyria, when Factions were united in Cantha, and Nightfall fell upon Elona.
How can I achieve these things?
In this life, you do not travel the same paths as those who came before. Only an ancient hero can quest for this glory.
I see.
I see.
I'll be on my way.

======================================================================
PAGE: King Adelbern
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/King_Adelbern
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Ghosts of Ascalon
and the
personal story
.
King Adelbern
Race
Ghost
Profession
Warrior
Level
30
Rank
Legendary
Location
Foefire's Heart
(
Ascalonian Catacombs
)
Organization
Ascalonian ghosts
Image(s)
Interactive map  (floor -2)
“
I will have my vengeance! Join me in death!
—
King Adelbern
King Adelbern
(1011–1090
AE
), known to the
charr
as the
Sorcerer-King
,
was the last monarch of
human
-controlled
kingdom
of
Ascalon
. To prevent the charr from conquering
Ascalon City
in the late stages of the
human-charr conflict
, the Sorcerer-King used his sword
Magdaer
to unleash the
Foefire
, a spell that destroyed the capital and led to the creation of the
Ascalonian ghosts
who fight for their king even in death and infest the countryside to this day. The ghost of Adelbern resides deep within the
Ascalonian Catacombs
, guarding his kingdom against all threats from both above and below ground while waiting for the return of his son,
Prince Rurik
.
Biography
Early years
Born circa 1011
AE
, Adelbern was blessed by the
god
Balthazar
.
In his younger days during the time of
the Guild Wars
, Adelbern was the champion of his
guild
,
Ascalon's Chosen
, and fought against
Kryta
.
He was said to be a good strategist.
Adelbern was well-liked by the citizens of
Ascalon
because of his easy demeanor and battlefield heroics, and
Duke Barradin
appointed him the commander of the army after the Battle of
Rin
.
After the assassination of the royal family in the
Third Guild War
,
Adelbern was crowned king at
King's Watch
by the people's demand despite not being next in line for the royal
crown
who would have been the previous king's younger brother Barradin.
However, the duke did not object to Adelbern becoming king and stepped aside to honor the wishes of the people. Barradin and Adelbern's children,
Lady Althea
and
Prince Rurik
, were intended to be wed to unite and strengthen the two bloodlines.
However, some Barradin loyalists disagreed with Adelbern's crowning and became rowing bandits known as the
Royalists
whose mission was to harass Adelbern's supporters and place the duke on the throne. By 1070
AE
, the threat the Royalists posed was great enough that
Devona
asked local adventurers to
investigate
whether people at the duke's estate were actually showing signs of disloyalty towards Adelbern's regime and if Barradin was supporting the Royalists' actions. These investigations ultimately revealed that Barradin and others at Lakeside County were fully loyal to the crown, thus putting an end to rumors of high treason.
Guild Wars Prophecies
Adelbern started out as one of the most popular kings in the history of the walled nation, but towards the end of his reign, he was driven increasingly mad and obsessed by the
war
against the encroaching
charr
and suspicion towards Kryta which he had fought against in the Guild War. Although he was in need of supplies, he refused aid from
Ambassador Zain
, who had traveled to Ascalon from Kryta as a representative of the
White Mantle
, and intended to arrest him and place him on trial for suspected treachery until Prince Rurik intervened on the ambassador's behalf.
The Searing
caused by the charr drove the Ascalonians from Rin in 1070
AE
, a source of shame and ire for the king. Not willing to stand down, Adelbern was determined to defend the city from the charr. However, Prince Rurik, the king's firstborn son,
advocated making peace with Kryta and taking shelter within its borders. This remained a source of tension between father and son, culminating in Adelbern
disowning
Rurik who led a faction of Ascalonians into Kryta. Rurik ultimately
perished
during conflict with the
Stone Summit
dwarves
while traversing the
Shiverpeak Mountains
in 1072
AE
. The king was devastated by his son's death and hardened his resolve towards the defense of his nation.
After
Vizier Khilbron
had unleashed the
titans
into
Tyria
, Adelbern fought against the titan invasion in Ascalon and pushed them back from the
eastern section
of the
Northern Wall
until he and a handful of soldiers were surrounded by the enemy. The king was saved, however, when the
dragon
prophet
Glint
sent a group of
heroes
to his aid at this critical moment, which allowed the Ascalonians to prevail against the titans.
Guild Wars Beyond
During the
Krytan Civil War
in 1079
AE
, the increasingly paranoid Adelbern was approached by
Master Exemplar
Evennia
of the
Shining Blade
who pleaded him to commit troops to aid
Princess Salma
's rebel forces against the White Mantle zealots in Kryta in exchange for Salma assisting the king in driving the charr from Ascalon for good. Evennia disappeared without a trace some time after reaching Old Ascalon, however. White Mantle propaganda would claim centuries later that Evennia and her fellow Krytan emissaries had been assassins whom Adelbern had hanged from the walls of Ascalon City.
Fall of Ascalon
Adelbern witnesses the fall of
Ascalon City
.
In 1080
AE
, Adelbern recalled the
Ebon Vanguard
, who consisted of both Ascalonians who had followed Rurik on the trek through the Shiverpeaks and slaves saved from the charr, from the
Eye of the North
. After celebrating them for their bravery, the king ordered the Vanguard, led by
Gwen Thackeray
, to fortify the
Stronghold of Ebonhawke
in an attempt to solidify Ascalon City's supply lines and establish a last-ditch holdout position against Ascalon's enemies.
Kimmes the Historian
, who chronicled these events, pondered about the king's decision to add specific civilians and families to bolster the Vanguard's forces on their trek south as it was unclear whether these additions were a sign of support from the king or an act to remove those questioning his rule from sight.
Adelbern's efforts to keep Ascalon isolated and rely only on his troops proved futile, however.
Ascalon City
, the Ascalonian capital after Rin, eventually fell to the encroaching charr army in 1090
AE
. Accounts of this conflict differ between
human
and charr records.
In human accounts, Adelbern's forces fell to the charr, and Adelbern himself was left to fight the charr alone. He eventually fought his way to the
Imperator
of the
Flame Legion
, wielder of the
Claw of the Khan-Ur
. The Claw clashed with
Magdaer
, Adelbern's sword, and caused a spark of power that engulfed the land for leagues around. This event became known as the
Foefire
.
Adelbern unleashes the
Foefire
with
Magdaer
.
The charr accounts offered an alternate version of the events. Rather than fighting to the death, Adelbern's forces retreated from the city, leaving their king behind. In his rage and despair, Adelbern struck the ground with Magdaer, producing the immense burst of energy now referred to as the Foefire. The king used an ancient divine ritual with his inherited crown and the sword Magdaer to cast the Foefire. Following the destruction of Ascalon City, charr forces found the crown and toyed with it, losing it in the process as its fragments were scattered throughout Ascalon.
Adelbern suffered the same fate as other
Ascalonian ghosts
due to the Foefire and became a ghost driven to fight the charr and any other opposing force on Ascalonian soil to the end of time or until the curse he cast can be lifted. According to a legend, the curse can only be lifted in a ritual performed by the current Ascalonian ruler from
King Doric
's lineage who wields Adelbern's crown and either Adelbern's sword Magdaer or its sister sword
Sohothin
which had been wielded by Prince Rurik.
After successfully driving charr scavengers away from Ascalon City, Adelbern spent his time patrolling the ruins of his fallen capital and collecting various armors, weapons and artifacts which he stored into his royal vault underground as trophies. One of these valuables was the Claw of the Khan-Ur which had once been wielded by the Imperator of the Flame Legion himself who had perished in the Foefire.
Mysterious maps of Ascalon City, with detailed accounts of the valuables in the royal treasury, were released in the wind in the decades to come, encouraging daring treasure hunters to explore the ghost-filled ruins.
Ghosts of Ascalon
In 1324
AE
, an adventuring guild consisting of the humans
Dougal Keane
and
Riona Grady
, the charr
Ember Doomforge
, the
norn
Gullik Oddsson
, the
sylvari
Killeen
, and the
asura
Kranxx
braved the dangers of Ascalon to retrieve the Claw of the Khan-Ur for
General Almorra Soulkeeper
of the
Vigil
. The general, Imperator
Malice Swordshadow
of the
Ash Legion
, and
Queen Jennah
of Kryta needed the Claw so they could use it to begin talks for a ceasefire between humans and the charr
High Legions
so that both races could focus their attention on the growing threat of the
Elder Dragons
.
Killeen died to the
Branded
on the way to Ascalon City Ruins, but the rest of the party managed to sneak into the ruins intact. However, they soon alerted the ghosts to their presence and would have perished if they had not been saved by
Savione
, the chief courtier of King Adelbern and the only friendly ghost in all of Ascalon due to having died moments before the Sorcerer-King had cast the Foefire curse.
The party learned that Savione had been responsible for the suspiciously detailed maps of Ascalon City and the royal vault as he had hoped that adventurers would help him have his revenge on Adelbern who had stabbed him when he had tried to stop the king from casting the Foefire curse. The courtier also revealed that the charr account of the events of the Foefire was closer to the truth and that Adelbern had cast the Foefire out of desperation and madness rather than facing the Flame Imperator in a duel.
With Savione's help, the party were able to decipher the clues of the Claw's current whereabouts in Adelbern's trap-filled vault and proceeded to secure it. When they believed Gullik to have perished to the ghosts, Riona convinced Ember to accompany Kranxx as a decoy to lure the ghosts away so Riona and Dougal could sneak into the king's treasure vault unnoticed. Although the distraction worked for a time, Adelbern eventually spotted the intruders. Dougal attempted to continue distracting the Sorcerer-King by truthfully stating that he was a descendant from an Ascalonian family, and then lying that he had come to the ruins on the behalf of Rurik. The treasure hunter's statements caught Adelbern off guard, but the ghost king quickly regained his composure when he remembered how Rurik had betrayed his trust in the past.
Before Adelbern could attack the party, however, he was distracted again when Savione appeared near him and renounced his position in the king's court. Angered, Adelbern pulled out a dagger from Savione's chest—a ghostly version of the weapon he had originally killed the courtier with—and sliced him in two. Releasing Savione from the weapon's hold, the Sorcerer-King unwittingly freed the ghostly courtier from his remaining burden, which allowed Savione to fade away into
the Mists
. The courtier had bought enough time for Dougal and Riona to slip away much to Adelbern's frustration.
Despite the party's attempts to evade the ghosts, the Sorcerer-King eventually found them in the ruins. Adelbern summoned his spectral army to kill the trespassers, promising to forcibly recruit the party as his
undead
soldiers and then lead an invasion of the surface world so he would finally have his revenge against the charr invaders and any who sympathized with charr. For a time Dougal's party believed that Ember had perished as well when the ghosts closed in on them.
Before the Sorcerer-King could defeat the party with his
magic
, however, Kranxx arrived in the nick of time. The asura battled Adelbern and the ghost army with a massive
golem
made from the bones of Ascalon's long-dead soldiers and infused with the magic of the late inventor
Blimm
's ruby known as the
Golem's Eye
. Noticing the reanimating power of the ruby, Adelbern declared that he wanted that power for himself. Although Kranxx perished in the clash, he successfully managed to collapse
Sunrise Tower
and his golem on Adelbern, evaporating the Sorcerer-King for a time with the sheer mass of the golem and the city's architecture. The act granted the rest of Dougal's party valuable few moments to locate the Claw and flee from the ruins before the king and the rest of the ghosts would reform.
Ultimately Dougal, Ember, and Gullik were the only survivors from the journey into Ascalon City after Riona's attempted act of treachery and subsequent death. However, they had successfully recovered the Claw of the Khan-Ur, which allowed charr and human politicians to begin talks of a ceasefire and set the stage for the signing of the
Ebonhawke Treaty
.
Personal story
By 1325
AE
, Adelbern's ghostly form had reformed underground in the
Ascalonian Catacombs
and was preparing to take the fight to the surface. The arrival of an adventuring party, including
Eir Stegalkin
,
Rytlock Brimstone
and the
Pact Commander
, stirred Adelbern who came to greet them. At first the Sorcerer-King believed that his son Rurik had returned when he sensed the sword Sohothin's presence. However, he soon realized his error when the wielder of the sword turned out to be the charr Rytlock instead. Furious, Adelbern swore to make all charr pay for what they had done. He mocked the party's efforts, daring them to face his four champions who were already in later stages of preparing the ghostly army for the invasion of the land above.
The party proved to be better skilled than Adelbern had expected, however, when they successfully defeated the four champions and returned to the enraged king to claim Magdaer. Adelbern faced them in
battle
himself but was ultimately bested as well. Before disappearing, Adelbern's gave the party a dire warning about
greater horrors
lurking within the catacombs which the party's actions had unwittingly unleashed. Eir took Magdaer, which she intended to give to Queen Jennah's champion
Logan Thackeray
to mend his friendship with Rytlock, and announced that she would hand the sword over to a blacksmith to mend the sword's shattered pieces.

Ascalon

Trivia
King Adelbern is
voiced
by
Bob Joles
.
King Adelbern has a striking similarity to
Constantine XI Palaiologos
, last Emperor of the East Roman Empire who likewise died at the ultimate fall of his realm's capital city. Legend has it that he never died and awaits under the earth to reconquer his lost city as "the Immortal Emperor".
Adelbern means "noble bear" in
Old German
.
"Adelbern's Ghost" from the
Guild Wars 2 Original Soundtrack
is named after him.

Guild Wars

has an article on

======================================================================
PAGE: Korga the Mighty
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Korga_the_Mighty
======================================================================

Korga the Mighty
Race
Risen
Level
83
Rank
Legendary
Location
Arsenal of the Blessed
(
The Ruined City of Arah
)
Part of
The Ruined City of Arah (explorable)
Korga the Mighty
is a legendary
Risen Gorilla
who charges and knocks down foes.
He will revive the dead ape bodies surrounding the fight area.
The summoned apes have a 6 second knock down which used by a group of them can be permanent knock down resulting in sure death (even with break stun skills).
The summoned apes are slow moving and easily avoidable.
Recommended strategy here is to have a single player kite the summoned apes away from Korga, while the other 4 party members burn him down.
Korga is immune to any crowd control
use stability and break stun to avoid knock downs.
Also note, there may be an area to bring Korga in which he can only revive a few apes. (more information needed on this spot) However, letting Korga move around the arena results in more apes being summoned.
The summoned apes will return to being dead a short time after Korga is defeated (possibly once all aggro is lost).
Once Korga is defeated, a chest will spawn and the
Highborn Kings Waypoint
will open, allowing easier passage to the
Legendary Jotun Stargazer
should the party wipe.
Location
Ruins of Orr
The Ruined City of Arah
Estates of the Favored

Effects
Resilience
- Ignores Crowd Control
Skills
Charge
- Charges at a player and knocks them down and striking any players in its path.
Resurrect
- Resurrects any fallen gorillas around Korga.
Thump
- Korga slams the ground around him, launching foes. If Korga tries to use this move on a player outside the PBAoE radius, he will instead throw two spreading rocks.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kudu
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Kudu
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
Kudu
Race
Asura
Level
20, 62, 80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Inquest Base KD-4
(
Sorrow's Embrace
)
Crucible Access Platform
(
Crucible of Eternity
)
Organization
Inquest
Image(s)
Kudu under the influence of
dragon corruption
.
“
Enlightenment always comes at a price. Now you must pay for what you've learned.
—
Kudu
Kudu
is a high-ranking
overseer
of the
Inquest
,
father of
Overseer Kuda
,
and a former apprentice of
Snaff
whose research he has been using to pursue his own goals which involve the use of
dragon energy
. His methods put him at odds with Snaff's other apprentice
Zojja
. He is the head of research for an Inquest cell at the
Crucible of Eternity
.
After causing trouble in
Rata Sum
and beyond, Kudu is finally
slain
when fighting against Zojja,
Logan Thackeray
,
Rytlock Brimstone
, and the
Pact Commander
in the
Crucible of Eternity
. Despite his demise, his daughter carries on his work in the Inquest to further improve on his research.
Biography
Early years
Kudu was
Snaff
's earlier apprentice. Snaff's research led Kudu into major discoveries about
dragon energies
, particularly that
Elder Dragons
each exuded their own energies with unique
magical
signatures. Snaff discontinued this research because it was too dangerous, but Kudu picked it up again as part of the
Inquest
after parting ways with him. Following Snaff's death in the
battle
against the Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
in 1320
AE
, Kudu somehow acquired one of the arm pieces which his former mentor had once worn. He also stole the designs of a personal teleporter which he used to escape to safety whenever he was cornered. He rose through the ranks in the Inquest, eventually claiming the rank of
overseer
.
Ever a schemer, Kudu used his charm and reputation as a genius to sway promising
asuran
researchers such as
Plunka
to his side to help him with his various projects, sponsoring them to join the Inquest. Out of all the asura he worked with, Kudu eventually took
Tazza
as his apprentice.
Kudu treated his assistants poorly, however, but none of them left his side as they wanted to achieve great things under his leadership. He was also known to be charming, especially when complimenting female asura to get his way.
At some point Kudu fathered a child with an unknown female asura, and their daughter would grow up to become
Overseer Kuda
who followed in her father's footsteps in the Inquest.
When the
sylvari
Peredur
visited
Rata Sum
to learn about the asura, he was hired by Kudu who soon revealed his true colors and forced Peredur and other unfortunate captives to work for him. Whenever Peredur made a distraction and attempted to flee, he found out that Kudu was one step ahead of him and stopped each escape attempt.
Personal story
Interested Parties
In 1325
AE
, Kudu took note of the
Variable Atmospheric Conditioner
, the promising invention by the
Snaff Savant
. Seeing potential in it, he decided to plagiarize the work and improve it to turn it into what he called the "Weather-matic 5000" before inviting
Zojja
, his successor as Snaff's apprentice, to a demonstration of his weather machine for the
Arcane Council
.
Although Kudu believed that the Snaff Savant would not be bold enough to accuse him of plagiarism, he took no chances. To get rid of any evidence linking him to idea theft, he sent his lackeys to burn down the archive annex and the blueprints within it.
Kudu was certain that Zojja would want to investigate the matter by interrogating Plunka whose lab was close by. He told Plunka about Zojja's visit in advance and ordered her to keep his rival trapped alive so that he would enjoy Zojja's suffering. Zojja and the Snaff Savant
outsmarted Plunka
with the trapped Peredur's help, however, and ultimately made Plunka reveal that Kudu was testing the weather machine at
Mrot Boru
. Deciding to either enter the
testing grounds
or
Kudu's lab
to force answers out of him, Zojja and the Savant eventually cornered Kudu. However, Kudu used his personal teleporter to flee before he was caught while gloating about his upcoming show intended for the Arcane Council.
Kudu's plan was to permanently flood
Rata Sum
with the weather machine, seeding chaos and creating opportunities for Inquest power grabs. Zojja and the Savant
stopped Kudu's plan
before it could come to fruition, but that did not stop a skeptical
High Councillor Flax
, aware of the animosity between Zojja and Kudu, from ordering a full subcommittee investigation into whether Zojja had sabotaged Kudu's device.
Sorrow's Embrace
Later that year, Zojja heard of a connection between Kudu and a recent
dredge
revolution, and headed to
Sorrow's Embrace
to investigate, with
Caithe
and
Eir Stegalkin
in pursuit. Using Snaff's research, Kudu had rebuilt the
Iron Forgeman
, fusing the energy of a draconic minion with the ancient
dwarf
creation. Zojja managed to put down the revolution and destroyed the Forgeman with Caithe and the
Pact Commander
, but Kudu got away as usual.
Crucible of Eternity
Zojja tracked Kudu to the
Infinite Coil Reactor
, determined to stop him from using Snaff's research for evil. There, she was joined by the Pact Commander and the reunited
Rytlock Brimstone
and
Logan Thackeray
. They discovered that the
Crucible of Eternity
removed and concentrated dragon energies at the bottom of the complex. Kudu was corrupting his captives with this energy, turning them into dragon minions that only he could control.
Kudu, who had been experimenting with dragon energies on himself, was killed after a fierce battle. Before perishing, however, he unleashed his
greatest creation
to destroy the group: a
dragon champion
of his own, created from experiments on a captured
giant
. However, the monster ultimately perished, and Zojja's party fled from the facility, denying Kudu his revenge.
Legacy
When the Pact Commander
assisted
Agent Spire
of the
Order of Whispers
in a clean-up operation by infiltrating deeper parts of the Crucible of Eternity to lock it down and prevent Kudu's remaining experiments from escaping, they came across Kudu's other hybrid champion,
Subject Alpha
, who had gone rogue and had the uncanny ability to force rival dragon minions to serve it. After multiple battles against Subject Alpha, the Commander and Agent Spire were able to escape from the collapsing facility, while the surviving essence of Subject Alpha likewise fled to somewhere unknown.
Despite Kudu's demise, the Inquest did not abandon his research on dragon minions. The project continued, this time under the supervision of his daughter, Overseer Kuda, who
tested
the synchronization and combat abilities of various dragon minions, including Alpha's successor
Subject Beta
, in the
Specimen Chamber
on the
Sandswept Isles
in 1331
AE
. Kuda would further expand on the concept with the creation of magically enhanced asura known as the
Omega Team
during the Inquest's exploration of
Eternity's Garden
in 1339
AE
.

Trivia

.

======================================================================

Kudu's Monster
Race
Risen
Mech. race
Giant
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Crucible Access Platform
(
Crucible of Eternity
)
Image(s)
Interactive map  (floor  -11)
“
Behold my greatest work—the power of the dragons, invested in a creature under my control!
—
Kudu
Kudu's Monster
is an abomination, the culmination of
Kudu
's experiments with
dragon energies
. Like a mockery of an
elementalist
, it is capable of
attuning
to the powers of the
Elder Dragons
Jormag
,
Kralkatorrik
,
Primordus
, and
Zhaitan
; the nature of its attacks change based upon its current attunement. It is the final encounter in the story about the
Crucible of Eternity
.
Location
Maguuma Jungle
Crucible of Eternity
(
Story mode
)
Crucible Access Platform
Eternity's Focus
Combat abilities
Behavior
Periodically, the Monster will change its attunement between Primordus, Jormag, Kralkatorrik, or Zhaitan. The current attunement can be determined by the particle effects on the Monster.
While it is Zhaitan-attuned, it may spawn
Risen Grubs
and
Undead Tendrils
.
Effects
Skills
Attunement Swap
- With a burst of energy, the Monster changes affinity to a random corrupted element.
Zhaitan Attunement
Corrupted Shout
- Monster spits vile juices, inflicting
Weakness
and summoning
Risen Grubs
. Unblockable. Unevadable.
Giant Stomp
- It puts its right foot in. Summons
Undead Tendrils
.
Giant Stomp (chain 1)
- It puts its right foot out. Inflicts
Poisoned
and
Knockdown
.
Giant Stomp (chain 2)
- It puts its left foot in. Inflicts
Poisoned
.
Giant Stomp (chain 3)
- And it gives forth a shout. Inflicts
Poisoned
.
Primordus Attunement
Fiery Shout
- Monster screams, spreading flames in multiple areas in front of him. Inflicts
Burning
. Unblockable. Unevadable.
Giant Stomp
- Monster smashes ground for massive damage, spawning several areas of flame.
Giant Stomp (chain 1)
- It puts its left foot in, sending forth flames. Inflicts
Burning
.
Giant Stomp (chain 2)
- It puts its left foot in again, sending forth flames. Inflicts
Burning
.
Giant Stomp (chain 3)
- It puts its right fist in, sending forth eight flames. Inflicts
Burning
.
Jormag Attunement
Frozen Shout
- Monster lets go with an icy blast; enemies are
Chilled
. Unblockable. Unevadable.
Giant Stomp
- It puts its right foot in,
Launching
and badly damaging anyone nearby.
Giant Stomp (chain 1)
- It puts its left foot in, causing ice to erupt from the ground. Enemies are
Launched
and
Chilled
while spawning AoE.
Giant Stomp (chain 2)
- It puts its left foot in, causing ice to erupt from the ground. Enemies are
Launched
and
Chilled
while spawning multiple AoEs.
Giant Stomp (chain 3)
- It puts its right fist in, causing ice to erupt from the ground. Enemies are
Launched
and
Chilled
while spawning multiple AoEs.
Kralkatorrik Attunement

- Monster stomps its foot, summoning explosive crystals. Target is encased in an
Imprisonment Crystal
. Unblockable. Unevadable.

- Player is locked down within the crystal for 30 seconds. Player can be broken free. If full time elapses, crystal shatters for massive bleed on its prisoner.

Vulnerability
). Unblockable. Unevadable.

======================================================================
PAGE: Labyrinthine Horror

======================================================================

This content was introduced in
Blood and Madness
and is subsequently only available during
Halloween
.
Labyrinthine Horror
Race
Halloween creature
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Lunatic Inquisition
(
Mad King's Realm
)
Mad King's Labyrinth
(
Mad King's Realm
)
Part of
Slay the Labyrinthine Horror
Image(s)
Booby-trapped walls in the Labyrinth.
The
Labyrinthine Horror
is a massive, powerful
skeleton
that periodically spawns when a player approaches one of the random booby-trapped walls in the
Mad King's Labyrinth
, which can be identified by their red glow.
(See map for trap locations)
.
The Horror is fragile compared to the other Labyrinth bosses, but deals high damage and is extremely difficult to outrun. It can easily catch even the fastest of player characters and roams around the entire circumference of the Labyrinth, making it difficult to avoid and/or pin down as well. Most notably, players can accidentally summon it while they're fighting one of the other Labyrinth bosses if they don't watch where they're going.

Mad King's Realm
Lunatic Inquisition

that

for 20 seconds with each hit.
200
range
.

- The Horror raises up its greatsword and shouts, dealing damage and inflicting 6-10 stacks of
Torment
for 20 seconds to enemies in a point-blank
area of effect
while gaining
Swiftness
for 8 seconds and transferring
all
conditions
back to attackers. This skill has a low
cooldown
, a
range

line of sight

Bleeding
for 20 seconds with each hit.

Notes
Grants the
Approval of the Mad King
effect upon being killed.
Killing this creature awards the
Bonebreaker
achievement
.
It completes one lap of the Mad King's Labyrinth in approximately 2 minutes 30 seconds.
It's on a variable respawn timer, but averages
about 20 minutes
between last kill and respawning in the centre rotunda before being triggered, and can respawn faster than the Lich and Viscount.
Once the timer has elapsed, the Horror will spawn in the inaccessible rotunda area in the centre of the labyrinth (with the treasure chests and coins), ready to be triggered. From then on, once a player triggers any of the booby-trapped walls, it will spawn. The triggered wall will glow red intermittently as it spawns through the wall. These are at the same locations that alert Lunatics when approached by Villagers in
Lunatic Inquisition
.
Trivia
The Labyrinthine Horror was first introduced with the
October 15
th
, 2013
update, as part of the
Blood and Madness
release
, and has been a part of every
Halloween
festival since then.
It is identical in appearance to the
Skeleton
familiar summoned by the
Executioner Axe Toy
.
It wields a weapon, referred to internally as "Warrior Sword"(id 36151) that uses the
Greatsaw
skin.
The Labyrinthine Horror's unpredictable spawn and movement patterns and its tendency to ambush unsuspecting players has made it a popular target of jokes and memes and has led many players to refer to it by various nicknames, most commonly
Steve
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lazarus
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Lazarus
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
,
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
, and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Lazarus
Race
Mursaat
Profession
Elementalist
Necromancer
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Confessor's Stronghold
(
Fragmented Wastes
)
Dragon Vigil
(
Tarir, the Forgotten City
)
Taimi's Pet Project
(
Rata Novus
)
The Last Chance
(
Abaddon's Reliquary
)
Organization
White Mantle
“
I am the last mursaat. Many years ago, you knew me as Lazarus the Dire. I have returned from the brink of existence!
—
Lazarus
Lazarus the Dire
is the last surviving
mursaat
according to
Tyrian
scholars. He was one of the mursaat responsible for the creation of the
White Mantle
via influencing the cult's founder
Saul D'Alessio
. He survived the
Krytan Civil War
which saw many of his fellow mursaat slain and the White Mantle toppled by the
Shining Blade
who placed
Salma
on the vacant throne of
Kryta
. However, he was
gravely injured
and forced to split his essence into multiple
aspects
housed in various artifacts to await for his eventual revival but not before swearing vengeance on future generations for what had been done to him.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
.
However, after being
revived
and empowered by the exploding
Maguuma Bloodstone
in 1329
AE
, Lazarus claims to have turned a new leaf, choosing to focus his efforts on fighting the
Elder Dragons
rather than follow
Confessor Caudecus's
plans for the White Mantle to take over Kryta from Salma's descendant
Queen Jennah
. To achieve his ends, Lazarus aims to join forces with the
Pact Commander
and
Dragon's Watch
.
The Lazarus empowered by the Maguuma Bloodstone is later
revealed
to be the disguised
Balthazar
,
God
of War, who has used the infamous mursaat's appearance to trick the White Mantle into helping him in his campaign against the Elder Dragons, while the real Lazarus has lain dormant for centuries.
Livia
and the Commander locate and bring the scattered artifacts housing the mursaat's aspects together in
Abaddon's Reliquary
where they
revive and ultimately slay
Lazarus with the
magical
Shining Blade
to prevent him from becoming a threat to Kryta and the rest of Tyria ever again.
Biography
Early years
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Lazarus, along with other
mursaat
, originated from
Nayos
, the Realm of Dreams.
He was a member of a mursaat faction which was banished to
Tyria
as punishment although the reason for this punishment and why Tyria in particular was chosen for their place of exile remains unknown.
Compared to this banished faction, other mursaat external Tyria are purportedly brutal but fair.
The Tyrian mursaat eventually had children of their own;
it is unclear if Lazarus was one of the original mursaat exiles to Tyria or if he was their descendant.
Lazarus was among the most powerful of the Tyrian mursaat,
having mastered the use of
elementalism
and
necromancy
.
During the previous rise of the
Elder Dragons
, the mursaat allied with the other ancient race of
Forgotten
to fight the Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
, only to fail and suffer severe losses when none of the other
magically
gifted
elder races
—
dwarves
,
jotun
, and
Seers
—came to their aid; the Seers, despite having urged everyone to form the alliance, had decided to withdraw their support at the last moment for unknown reasons.
The mursaat viewed this lack of assistance as a betrayal and used their
shadowcraft
, which allowed them to travel through a shadowed pocket of existence within Tyria, to hide from the Elder Dragons and leave the rest of the Tyrian races to fend on their own. They bided their time until the Elder Dragons fell into slumber and returned to Tyria.
Sometime after the Elder Dragons had returned to their slumber, the mursaat and the Seers waged war for thousands of years. During that war, the mursaat began using a terrible weapon called
spectral agony
, which killed its victims while causing them indescribable amounts of pain. Although the few hundred mursaat were heavily outnumbered by the Seers, they nevertheless managed to make the Seers all but extinct during the lengthy conflict.
After defeating the Seers in
Janthir
and sending the few remaining survivors into hiding, the mursaat built their new capital,
Bava Nisos
, on the site of that battle.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
After learning to hide from those without the
gift of true sight
, the mursaat began manipulating Tyrian events in secret. Lazarus was instrumental in helping his people influence members of the other sentient races of Tyria.
Some mursaat elders wanted to continue their race's original mission of finding a way back to their homeworld, but the younger generations that aligned with the zealotry of
Optimus Caliph
, Lazarus, and
Sarlic the Judge
wanted to change the original mission into becoming rulers of Tyria instead. These disagreements ultimately split the mursaat in two with many of the elders leaving their zealous brethren behind instead of taking part in further atrocities and manipulations.
When the mursaat learned of the
dragon
prophet
Glint
's
Flameseeker Prophecies
which foretold their race's doom at the hands of the
titans
, they began searching for a way to prevent the prophecy from coming true. In order to ensure their survival, the mursaat of Janthir needed to keep the
Door of Komalie
of the
Ring of Fire
islands, which prevented the titans from entering Tyria, powered up with
soul batteries
and required magically potent souls for it. When the exiled
human
Saul D'Alessio
stumbled into the mursaat city of Bava Nisos and viewed them as gods,
the mursaat realized they had found the means to enact their plans. It was Lazarus who chose Saul to form a religious cult of the Unseen Ones in an effort to rule
Kryta
as puppets of the mursaat.
Conflict in Kryta
Lazarus and his fellow
mursaat
kidnap
Saul D'Alessio
.
Encouraged by the mursaat, Saul returned to his homeland of Kryta as a changed man and founded the
White Mantle
cult to worship these seemingly benevolent Unseen Ones. In time the cult grew into the protectors of Kryta who defended the kingdom against the invading
charr
when
King Jadon
had fled from the Krytan capital of
Lion's Arch
and left the kingdom leaderless. Saul and his missionaries spread the Unseen Ones' gospel beyond Kryta, and Saul personally traveled to the remote community of
Gavril
in Janthir to recruit the humans there to his cult.
Lazarus, along with his fellow mursaat Optimus Caliph and
Mercia the Smug
, offered assistance to Saul's forces during the
battle against the charr
by decimating the charr forces when Saul prayed for his gods to help his outnumbered forces. However, the mursaat's assistance came with a price: they turned on Saul's followers and killed those they deemed unworthy of seeing them, sparing only the most zealous. Realizing Saul's changed attitude at the sight of the massacre and to keep him from telling the Krytan population what he had seen, the mursaat took him with them to a faraway location.
The White Mantle, now under the leadership of
Confessor Dorian
, turned into a religious cult of fanatics who carried out the will of their Unseen gods, using the
Eye of Janthir
to locate
Chosen
, people identified as having magical potential, who would be taken to be sacrificed upon the
Bloodstone
of
Bloodstone Fen
and the other located bloodstones across Tyria to power up the soul batteries and to keep the Door of Komalie closed. The mursaat had thus accomplished two tasks: they had managed to keep their race safe from the titans, and they now ruled the Krytan population via their White Mantle puppets. However, a rebel group known as the
Shining Blade
discovered the true nature of the White Mantle and the Unseen Ones and brought the truth of their atrocities to light by 1072
AE
with the help of a group of
heroes
, ultimately leading to the fall of the White Mantle and the
temporary release of the titans
which destroyed many mursaat on Tyria as Glint had foreseen.
Lazarus had gone into hiding during the titans' rampage and survived for years, however, by splitting his soul into multiple
aspects
which he had hidden within unknowing White Mantle followers. In the final stages of the
Krytan Civil War
, Lazarus decided to return from the shadows and reclaim these aspects, although the acts led to the deaths of the aspects' human hosts. The last remaining host,
Justiciar Naveed
, realized the truth of Lazarus's "gift" and sought help from a Tyrian hero and the
asura
Glayvin
to remove the aspect, only for the attempt to
fail
. However, the trio discovered that the attempted removal had poisoned the aspect within Naveed; they decided to lure Lazarus to Naveed so they could transfer the corrupted aspect to the mursaat and severely weaken Lazarus. As the justiciar had predicted, Lazarus did arrive to claim the last aspect, which led to Naveed's death. The mursaat realized the change in the affected aspect too late, however, and swore to make countless human generations pay for what had been done to him, before he disappeared from sight.
Many believed that Lazarus was killed along the rest of his kind during the Krytan Civil War, but he had actually survived, albeit severely weakened due to his power being turned against him. He went into hiding to recuperate but, having learned from his previous actions, he decided to split his aspects into artifacts rather than living hosts. Once the splitting was complete and the artifacts had been scattered throughout the
Maguuma Jungle
and sites sacred to the White Mantle, Lazarus fell into a slumber in an unknown, remote location. His surviving White Mantle followers fled deep into the
Heart of Maguuma
to regroup and bide their time from 1079
AE
onwards.
Living World Season 3
Stronghold of the Faithful
Lazarus
impaled
by
the Shining Blade
.
As time passed, more and more White Mantle began doubting the mursaat's cause although the primary objective of the White Mantle was to revive Lazarus.
High Inquisitor Xera
left her fellow cultists to search for Lazarus's refuge while White Mantle spies continued searching for and gathering the artifacts containing the mursaat's dormant aspects. Xera found what she had been looking for and returned to the
Stronghold of the Faithful
, and the cult researched the Bloodstone in Bloodstone Fen to use its power in the mursaat's resurrection.
Following
Confessor Esthel
's
demise
in 1325
AE
, the White Mantle fractured into two factions: those following the new Confessor
Caudecus
's plans to overtake Kryta, and those following Xera's plans to use the Maguuma Bloodstone to revive and empower Lazarus, whom they viewed as their true leader, behind Caudecus's back.
Out of the Shadows
Lazarus was seemingly revived in 1329
AE
, just before a group of adventurers raided the White Mantle's Stronghold of the Faithful and defeated Xera who boasted about her mursaat's impending return. Shortly afterwards, the Maguuma Bloodstone
exploded
, and Lazarus absorbed much of its power. He
revealed himself
when the
Pact Commander
's party was fighting against Caudecus's forces. He ousted Caudecus, who had refused to submit to him, as a heretic and killed many of the Confessor's followers, thus furthering the splintering of the White Mantle before disappearing once more.
Rising Flames
Lazarus showed up some time after in
Tarir, the Forgotten City
to help the Pact Commander and the
Luminate
defend
Glint
's recently hatched child,
Aurene
, against invading
Destroyers
. After the
battle
, Lazarus made his intentions known: he claimed to have learned from the mursaat's past mistakes and wished to align himself with the Commander, believing that the two of them together, along with Lazarus's White Mantle faction, could be a great force against the Elder Dragons. Although the Commander's party was skeptical about Lazarus's earnestness given the mursaat's past deeds,
Marjory Delaqua
volunteered to accompany Lazarus to find out whether he was truly being genuine or not. Lazarus accepted Marjory's terms and left Tarir with her.
The Head of the Snake
After the Pact Commander
eliminated
Confessor Caudecus and investigated his personal quarters in the manor, it was revealed that Caudecus had held on to an aspect required to revive Lazarus and given Xera a fake artifact to keep her from resurrecting the last mursaat. The Commander realized that the current version of Lazarus whom Marjory had been accompanying was fake, and that the real Lazarus was still out there though it was unknown at the time whether Lazarus had revived during Xera's attempts or remained in his five aspect artifacts.
Flashpoint
After the illusion he was under was dispelled by the
joint efforts
of the Commander,
Kasmeer Meade
and
Taimi
, the mursaat imposter's true identity turned out to be none other than
Balthazar
, the
God
of War and Fire. Having betrayed his
White Mantle
followers and taken on
mercenary
armies, the exposed god stole Taimi's
device
designed to simultaneously kill the
Elder Dragons
Primordus
and
Jormag
and retreated to
Draconis Mons
to use it on Primordus. In the heart of the volcano before Primordus, he joined into the energy trails linking Primordus and Jormag and began absorbing their magic, as he had done to the Maguuma Bloodstone, in an attempt to slay the two Elder Dragons and take their power. However, his schemes were
foiled
by the Commander and Taimi, and he vanished once Taimi's device had exploded.
One Path Ends
The real Lazarus remained out there, however. Having thought Lazarus revived, the White Mantle had scattered the five artifacts they held across Tyria before they were imprisoned by Balthazar.
Elsewhere, the
Eye of Janthir
had left the
Bastion of the Penitent
following the death of the mursaat's prisoner Saul D'Alessio whom it had been overseeing. With its latest task completed, the Eye went out to search for its remaining mursaat masters. As such, it was tracking down and collecting the aspects of Lazarus.
Using this information to their advantage, the Shining Blade retrieved the aspects instead, including the one that had been in Caudecus's possession preventing Lazarus's earlier revival.
The final aspect, housed within a book, was still held by Balthazar who had taken it to
Abaddon's Reliquary
in
Orr
. The Eye of Janthir, and in turn the Pact Commander and Exemplar Kerida, entered Abaddon's Reliquary and recovered the final aspect,
reviving
Lazarus in 1330
AE
. Confronted, Lazarus revealed Kerida to truly be
Livia
, a human necromancer loyal to the Shining Blade from the time of the Krytan Civil War, and swore once more his vengeance against humanity. However, he did not survive the battle, his five aspects slain by the Commander and Livia, and himself slain by
The Shining Blade
, a powerful magical artifact of Seer origins. With Lazarus's death, the Eye of Janthir disintegrated, confirming that the last of its masters, the mursaat, were truly dead.
Legacy
Secrets of the Obscure
Unbeknownst to most people, Lazarus had not been the last mursaat on Tyria.
Mabon
, an atoning mursaat member of the secretive
Wizard's Court
, had stayed out of the Eye of Janthir's reach within the magically warded
Wizard's Tower
, so the Eye had been unable to locate him. While exploring the tower, the Pact Commander ran into Livia who revealed that she was also a member of the
Astral Ward
working alongside the Wizard's Court to defend Tyria from extraplanar threats. She explained that she had been aware of Mabon's existence during the hunt for Lazarus and that Mabon had been just as worried about Lazarus's return as she had been as the two mursaat had known one another during the war between the mursaat and the Seers. Livia pointed out that Kryta would be safe as neither Lazarus nor the White Mantle were able to threaten anyone again.
Lazarus's defeat was immortalized in both a statue and a
descriptive plaque
in the
Museum of Curiosities
found in the wizards' floating domain of
Amnytas
.
Janthir Wilds

Notes
Studio Audio Director
Drew Cady
added the "muted mask" sound to Lazarus's voice for
The Last Chance
, which has subsequently been used for all
mursaat
voices since.
Trivia
The "Lazarus" imposter is
voiced
by
Peter Jessop
.
The real Lazarus encountered in
The Last Chance
is voiced by
Rick Wasserman
.
During his
revival
, "Lazarus" mentions the White Mantle being in "a dire state." This is a reference to his name in the first
Guild Wars
, "Lazarus the Dire."
Lazarus's name is probably a reference to the Biblical figure
Lazarus of Bethany
, who was raised from death by Jesus after being dead for four days.
Lazarus splitting his soul into aspects is reminiscent of
liches
, particularly the
Dungeons & Dragons variant
which popularized the concept of an undead sorcerer who has split his soul into multiple soul artifacts (often called soul gem or phylactery) which anchor the lich's soul to the material world. If the corporeal body of the lich is killed, their soul will exist in spirit to resurrect themself with the help of the soul artifacts; the only way to permanently kill the lich is to destroy all the artifacts. The concept has existed earlier in myths and fairytales such as
Koschei the Deathless
of Russian folklore who made himself immortal by hiding "his death" inside nested objects.
See also
The
Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Lazarus the Dire
.
Aspect of Lazarus
Illusory Lazarus
Associated items
Mini Lazarus

Legendary Archdiviner
Race
Human
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Cliffside Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Organization
Bandits
The
Legendary Archdiviner
is the protector of the
Cliffside Fractal
and the boss for that stage. He wields the
Cultist Hammer
, which is the key to freeing the imprisoned
Colossus
.
Location
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Cliffside Fractal
Combat abilities
Abilities
Knocks Back
Pulls
Effects
Vulnerable while channeling to cast wells or charge overhead smash.
Protection
- gained at 50% health remaining
Swiftness
- gained at 25% health remaining
Might
- three stacks gained at 25% health remaining
Cultist Missile Reflection
- reflects projectiles while casting Nova
(historical)
Skills
Basic melee attack.
Leaping hammer pound for moderate damage and
knockdown
. Possible to dodge on reaction, but typically not fatal even if it connects. Prioritizes the furthest player away, using a shadowstep to get closer if necessary.
Hammer blow. A single, slow hammer blow that deals massive damage (25-30k). Indicated by an orange semicircular AoE briefly before hitting.
Wells. Channels to create multiple wells on the ground. Will be marked by a red circle on the ground a few seconds before they become active. Players hit by the mark will suffer damage and multiple conditions. At higher levels, also inflicts
Agony
.
Mass stun. Destroys the hammer and stuns the entire party for several seconds after the seal is damaged by the hammer. Stun breaks and stability can be used to avoid the stun effect but the loss of the hammer is unavoidable.
Cursed Grasp
- Sends out grasping hands that
pull
all players close to him.
Cage
- Casts a trap with an orange circular AoE indicator, teleporting any players in it after a delay into a random cage, removing them from the fight. Those players (or their party) must destroy the cage to allow them to return to the fight.

Throw Net
Use Scepter

when

Wields a

The event will be failed if no living players remain in the bubble; the Executioner can leave the bubble without consequences (such as to finish a downed player), but may reset if they go too far.

======================================================================
PAGE: Legendary Blue Dynamic Assault Knight
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Legendary_Blue_Dynamic_Assault_Knight
======================================================================

This page contains information about a Guild Wars 2 element, mechanic, or feature that has been removed or replaced.
The information on this page does not apply to the current version of the game or the content is no longer available.
Notes
:
This content was only available during the
Battle for Lion's Arch
(released March 4, 2014). Replaced with the
Prototype Indigo
in the
2022 re-release
.
Legendary Blue Dynamic Assault Knight
Race
Twisted Watchwork
Rank
Legendary
Location
Grand Piazza
(
Lion's Arch
)
The
Legendary Blue Dynamic Assault Knight
was a foe encountered during the
Battle for Lion's Arch living world
event. They guarded the entrance to
The Breachmaker
, where
Scarlet Briar
was located and fought. There were three different versions, but all versions had identical attacks and strategies. Upon defeating each of the three Knights, players could get the
Prime Light Attunement
.
Location
Historical location
Lion's Arch
Grand Piazza
, south of
Lion's Court
Event involvement
[Group Event]
Eliminate the blue Dynamic Assault Knight
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Mode Changes
Removes Conditions
Reflects Conditions
Knockdown
Effects
Unshakable
(immune to all control effects)
Calibration based on its type
Dynamic Calibration
- Deal more damage to assault knight; take more damage from assault knight.
Switches between:
Condition Reflect
- Reflects all incoming
conditions
.
Condition Crash
- Power level lowers the more
conditions
are applied to it.
Condition Counter
- Tracks the power level of each mode.
Skills
Assault Hammer, Hammer Head, Hammer Smash
Basic cleaving melee attack chain. Hammer Smash inflicts
Knockdown
.
Explosive Impact
A short range PBAoE stomp that inflicts
Burning
on the way up, then damage and
Knockback
upon landing.
Extraction
A large AoE
Pull
. The range is so far that moving out of range is nearly impossible. Players must dodge right as the AoE marker disappears to avoid it. Always followed by Hammer Swing
Hammer Swing (warrior skill)
Melee attack that inflicts
Daze
. Always used after Extraction.
Drops
Note that the following drops are not dropped by the knights themselves, but are awarded once after the three Assault Knight events are completed, but only if at least one knight was killed.
Deluxe Gear Box
(x6, guaranteed)
Chance at
Assault Knight Power Core
of the corresponding type:
Dynamic Assault Knight Power Core
Synergetic Assault Knight Power Core
Static Assault Knight Power Core
See also
Legendary Green Synergetic Assault Knight
Legendary Red Static Assault Knight

is a
charr
and is the final boss of the
Chaos Fractal
.
Location
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Chaos Fractal
Combat abilities
Abilities
Shielded
- The gladiator becomes invulnerable unless his shield is removed by luring him onto active electric panels.
Pulls
- Starting at 50% health, the gladiator begins channeling a 600 radius orange AoE. During this time his
defiance bar
is vulnerable. If it isn't broken in time, he will pull all players in the AoE and do high amounts of damage to them.
Behavior
Applies
Prey
to the furthest player, which puts a target indicator on their head and causes the Gladiator to chase them.
Will
aggro
on the closest player if no
aggro
indicator is above any player's head.
Effects
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Missing effects, remove if none
The defiance bar becomes unlocked when the Gladiator is channelling Pull.
Protective Shielding
until lured onto an energized aether grid panel
Shield Disruptor
- Applied when he stands on an energized panel. Duration is extended for every second that he remains on such a panel. When this effect ends, Protective Shielding is reapplied.
Swiftness
Skills
Slice
- Melee auto-attack.
Dice
- Melee auto-attack.
Spinning Cut
- Melee auto-attack that inflicts high damage and
Daze
.
Whirling Devastation
- Large AoE that pulls in players and deals massive damage. Use Crowd Control skills to break his bar and halt this attack.
Stolen skills

expanding it

Notes
Does not drop any loot. (All loot obtained is from the chest you get for finishing the fractal.)
Legendary Brazen Gladiator has a 50 range diameter hitbox, which is considered small.

Legendary Cairn the Queenslayer
is a powerful
Jade Construct
used by the
White Mantle
to
take control
of
New Loamhurst
in
Lake Doric
. It is a much larger version of a
Jade Bow
, with the same behaviours and attacks. During the
White Mantle counter-attack
on the fishing village, it will
provide reinforcements
from the south in
Eastern Divinity Reservoir
, rising from the stream flowing out the back gate to the
docks
of
Caudecus' Manor
and make its way to the assault. When New Loamhurst is under White Mantle control, if the
Seraph
successfully
launch an assault to retake it
, the Queenslayer will
appear inside the village to defend it
.
When defeated, it drops the
Queenslayer Jade Chunk
required for the
Cin Business
collection
involved with the
legendary trinket
Aurora
.

Notes
If defeated during
[Group Event]
Stop the Reinforcements
(80), the
Ancient Chest
rewarded will spawn in the village and not where it was destroyed.
The events it appears in are tied to the state of the village and the
meta event
. The White Mantle attack
scheduled every 2 hours
only occurs if the Seraph have control at the time. When the White Mantle have control, it will only appear during
[Group Event]
Liberate New Loamhurst from the White Mantle
(80), following a triggered Seraph
retake event
.
If there are a huge amount of players doing the meta event (such as during
special events
like
Return to Season 3
), when the White Mantle attack, it may die very quickly at its
spawn point
due to power creep, and the Seraph will likely have permanent control, resulting in it only respawning after 2 hours.

Legendary Cave Troll
Race
Troll
Level
35
Rank
Legendary
Location
Ascalonian Catacombs (explorable)
(

)
The
Legendary Cave Troll
is a powerful
troll
encountered within the
Ascalonian Catacombs (explorable)
. It has a delightful habit of knocking down apparently solid walls and rushing unsuspecting groups who get too close. It shares the same spawn locations as in the story version except the spawn point where the spider queen spawns where is a broken troll wall instead.

Legendary Chak Gerents
are powerful
chak
which spawn during the
King of the Jungle
meta-event in each of the four lanes around
Ley Line Confluence
in
Tangled Depths
. Killing all four of the Chak Gerents during King of the Jungle is required for the successful completion of the meta-event.
The Gerents are called the Chak's 'defensive measure' against the four cannons which are siphoning ley line energy for power.

Creates Pheromone Clouds

Tail Slam

Stun
— The
chak gerent
is temporarily stunned.  Occurs when
defiance

—
Damage
and
condition damage
taken are increased; stacks intensity.

— The
chak gerent
has burrowed. Find a way to make it resurface.

only)

Damage
and

taken are increased; stacks intensity.

and

(

only)
— Periodically
dazes

subject

Skills
Dodge
— The creature moves around its target in an evasive maneuver,
evading
attacks for 1-2 seconds.
1
Impale
— While burrowed, the creature attacks within its center dealing a massive amount damage to targets within, and
Knockback
players.
2
Lunge
— The creature charges towards a target, dealing a large amount of damage and will
knock
targets down for 3-5 seconds.
Pheromone Clouds
— The creature leaves a blue, donut-shaped
AoE
. While placed, the range expands and deals a high amount of damage to targets within.
Swipe
— The creature swipes with its left claw, dealing a large amount of damage and will
knock
targets down for 3-5 seconds.
Tail Slam
— The creature alternates slamming its tail behind it left and right,
knocking down
enemies caught by the impact for 3-5 seconds.
[
verification requested
]
Cave In
— Rubble falls from the ceiling as multiple
AoEs
, inflicting massive damage and
knocking down
enemies for 3-5 seconds.
Toxic Gas
— Spreads poisonous gas in an area as it retreats underground, forcing players to move on to a clearer section.
1
Note: This is not an offensive skill, but rather a defensive one. As a result, the name of the skill is unknown.
2
Note: This skill is only used after the each attack phases, while burrowed. It is not used before the creature is fought.

Trivia
Her name is a reference to the

, a tigress who killed an estimated 436 people in India and Nepal around the turn of the previous century.
Her appearance is based on the
Golden tiger
.

Legendary Clockheart

(

The
Legendary Clockheart
is a cybernetic
treant
that underwent heavy modifications by
Scarlet Briar
. It functions as the power generator of the
Aetherblade Shipyard
built within
Twilight Arbor
.
Location
Maguuma Jungle
Twilight Arbor (explorable)
Depth of Despair
Clockheart Generator
Combat abilities
Abilities
Armor-Charged Super Attack
Massive Ranged Attacks
Frontal Cone AoE
Effects
Aetherized Shielding
- Negates damage and conditions. Enables Super Attack if shielding persists. Susceptible to electric interference.
Aether Power Level
- Builds while Aetherized Shielding persists.
Skills
Short Jab
- Melee auto-attack that inflicts
Vulnerability
for 10 seconds.
Long Jab
- Melee auto-attack that inflicts
Weakness
for 10 seconds.
Clockwork Orb
-
Projectile
AoE
attack that creates several powerful
DPS
fields. These fields also inflict
Bleeding
,
Crippled
, and
Vulnerability
with each pulse. Will only use if player is at range.
Clockwork Throw
-
Launches
target, dealing damage twice.
Clockwork Thrash
-
Channeled
3 pulse frontal 180° cone
Torment
(duration 5s), followed by a powerful frontal 180° cone AoE shockwave that
Launches
targets.
Clockwork Binding
- PBAoE
Daze
(2s) and
Immobile
(5s). Causes Aether Power Level to increase.
Twilight's End
- Massive PBAoE shockwaves that
Launch
targets. Will only be used at 30 Aether Power Level stacks.

Dialogue
Greetings! You want to know more about collecting things, don't you?
What do you have that I might want?
(Only if a player has unlocked an achievement reward that Rednax sells)
Okay.
The most important things to know are how to get collections, and what can go in 'em.
What sort of things can you collect?
Collections can be completed by collecting items, using items, unlocking
skins
in your
wardrobe
, or any combination of the three. That simple!
What else is there to know?
Got it.
How do you get collections?
There are three sorts of collections: ones you have, ones you discover, and ones you unlock.
Ones I have?
Well, that's simple, you have them already and should be out there working on them. You've the sense of a cub.
What else is there to know?
Got it.
Discover?
Many collections make themselves apparent once you start finding things for them. Complete one of them, and you'll be the envy of your peers.
What else is there to know?
Got it.
Unlock?
Sometimes you need the proper item to start a collection. You can find these items all over the place.
Do you have such an item?
(Same as "Actually, I was wondering..." below)
What else is there to know?
Got it.
What else is there to know?
Got it.
Maybe later.
(If
Koutalophile
isn't unlocked yet)
Actually, I was wondering if you had anything for me.
I knew you had the collector's spirit! But do you have the collector's coin?
I'll take it!
I don't have room.
(If full inventory)
Not now.
Not now.
Notes
In the description of non-unlocked Koutalophile collection he is mentioned as Master Collector Rednax Youngblood.
Rednax will start selling the achievement rewards items once the achievement that rewards that certain item is completed.
E.g. Rednax will sell
Adelbern's Burden
once
Ascalonian Catacombs Collector
has been completed.

======================================================================
PAGE: Legendary Doppelganger
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Legendary_Doppelganger
======================================================================

"Crazed Doppelganger" redirects here.  For other uses of "Doppelganger", see
Doppelganger
.
Legendary <Character Name>
(This NPC's appearance may change based on the present characters.)
Race
Phantasm
Rank
Legendary
Location
Elon Riverlands
(
Crystal Desert
)
The
Legendary Doppelganger
is a boss that spawns as part of
The Path to Ascension
meta event
in
Elon Riverlands
. It will randomly select a player in the area and become an enormous clone of that character, mimicking appearance and specialization.
Locations
Crystal Desert
Elon Riverlands
Augury Rock
Champion's Gap
Prophet's Path
Skimmer Ranch
Skyward Reach
The Quickmire
Ustulate Barrens
Vulture Drifts
Event involvement
Shift beyond reality to confront the crazed doppelganger
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Relentlessly Pursues Targets
Creates Clones
Behavior
Mimicks the appearance and profession (with the
Path of Fire elite specialization
equipped
[
verification requested
]
) of a randomly-chosen player in the area.
Effects
Skills
Skills are determined by the Path of Fire elite specialization of the chosen player's profession. Only weapon skills are used:
Guardian
:
Axe
Revenant
:
Shortbow
Warrior
:
Dagger
Engineer
:
Sword
Ranger
:
Dagger
Thief
:
Rifle
Elementalist
:
Sword
Mesmer
:
Axe
Necromancer
:
Torch
Float AoE
[
verification requested
]
- The doppelganger selects one player and places a circle on them. When the circle runs out, a weaker doppelganger of that player is summoned, it lifts players inside it and disables all skills for the players except one. The player will be lifted high enough to take fall damage, so
gliding
is recommended. The only skill available depends on the player's profession:
Guardian
:
Wrath
Revenant
:
Essence Sap
Warrior
:
Throw Rock
Engineer
:
Throw Junk
Ranger
:
Throw Dirt
Thief
:
Trail of Knives
Elementalist
:
Discharge Lightning
Mesmer
:
Mind Blast
Necromancer
:
Life Leech
Stolen skills
None
Notes
The Doppelganger will take on an exact appearance of the copied player, and will be named
Legendary <Character Name>
.
Wields the
ascended weapon
corresponding to the chosen player's
Path of Fire specialization collection
.
Targeted player's ability to waypoint is disabled when they're being targeted by the doppelganger. The doppelganger appears as a friendly NPC until it hits the targeted player, after which it becomes hostile.
Related achievements
The
Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on

—

crazed doppelganger
at the end of the Augury Rock meta event.
(10
)

:

—

legendary doppelganger
group event.
(5
)

:
Exaltation of the Guardians
—

(3
)

:
Skyscale of Courage
—
Infuse your skyscale egg with courage.
(1
)

Legendary Dredge Power Suit
Race
Dredge
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Underground Facility Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Image(s)
Interactive map  (floor  18)
“
Now you die!
— Legendary Dredge Power Suit
Legendary Dredge Power Suit
is one of the two final bosses of the
Underground Facility Fractal
.
Location
Fractals of the Mists
Underground Facility Fractal
Combat abilities
Effects
Skills
Slash
- 2 wide melee hits, inflicts
Bleeding
Ground Smash
- 3 huge growing point blank AoEs in 3s, deals
Agony
, applies 3s of
Stability
to itself
Bomb
- 2 waves of multiple projectiles with delayed AoE, can be
reflected
, but deal no damage to the boss
Shockwave
- Multiple short-range projectile, hits 360 degrees of the area
Heal
- Channeled heal, automatically interrupted if the
Superheated
effect is applied
Fixate
- Applies
Fixated
onto a random player for up-to 30 seconds.
Stolen skills
Throw Gunk
Tooth Stab
Rusty Scrap Strike
Drops
Name
Type
Rarity
Quantity
Deluxe Gear Box
Container
F
Exotic
1
Notes
Legendary Dredge Power Suit has a 100 range diameter hitbox, which is considered medium.

Legendary Ellutherius Wintergust
Race
Djinn

(
Desert Highlands
)
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
Ellutherius Wintergust, chronically bored djinn, has taken to creating his own fun. Deadly fun.
—
Order of Shadows Agent
Legendary Ellutherius Wintergust
is a
Legendary bounty
in
Desert Highlands
. He is located in the
Palace of Aban
. His bounty can be picked up at the
Derelict Delve
Bounty Board
.
Location
For bounty map:
