Enchanted Bluffs#Ambient dialogue
Riddle 1
Thanks. That fight seems to have cleared my mind a bit, though boredom seems to have replaced my confusion. The prince used to enjoy my riddles. Would you like one?
Sure! Give it to me.
As you move forward, you leave this behind. Step carefully, for it is your person defined. What is it?
Your shadow.
No, that's not correct. You're close, though.
Shoot. Let me try again.
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
Your future.
No, that's not correct. You're close, though.
Shoot. Let me try again.
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
Your legacy.
Well done! You are either wise beyond your years or an excellent guesser. I must try to remember some of my trickier puzzles. I'll have another for you tomorrow.
I'll come back tomorrow, then.
Your friendships.
No, that's not correct. You're close, though.
Shoot. Let me try again.
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
Never mind.
I'm not interested.
Riddle 2
Another invigorating battle! For a moment, it seemed like the palace was filled with onlookers cheering us on. Did you perceive it? Never mind. Are you ready for another riddle?
Bring it on.
A glittering treasure sought by many. For the greedy, I am no good fit. I am made of gold and jewels, but my true value is where I sit. What am I?
A scepter.
A necklace.
A chalice.
A crown.
Yes, a crown! I was always proud of that one. Come back tomorrow if you'd like to try your hand at a new riddle.
See you tomorrow.
Never mind.
Riddle 3
I recognize you now. Good to see you again. You know, I'm starting to suspect you're only fighting me for my riddles. Well, I can't complain—and I've got a good one for you
I'm ready for it.
Every success brings you closer to my grasp. Every failure is a chance you will never see me.
Peace.
Hmm, no. Good try.
I want to give it another shot.
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
Old age.
Old age, indeed. Some rulers become arrogant after winning wars and gaining power. They forget they cannot conquer everything. I'll have another riddle for you tomorrow.
I'll be back again.
Heroism.
Hmm, no. Good try.
I want to give it another shot.
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
The throne.
Hmm, no. Good try.
I want to give it another shot.
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
Never mind.
I'll try later.
Riddle 4
Hmm. I never told the prince this one, because he wouldn't appreciate it, but I think you have a sense of humor. It should be easy for anyone who knows me well.
Go for it.
He looks elegant in his fine feathers, but all he does is squawk. What a tranquil place the world would be if these birdies couldn't talk! What is he?
A moa.
No, but I like the way you think. Try again?
Yes, please.
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
A politician.
No, but I like the way you think. Try again?
Yes, please.
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
A courtier.
Courtiers! Gab, gab, gossip, babble. I admit, it does seem empty in here without them. I have one more riddle for you, but you'll have to wait until tomorrow. No sense rushing things.
See you.
A royal guard.
No, but I like the way you think. Try again?
Yes, please.
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
Never mind.
Not right now.
Riddle 5
One final riddle. The older I get, the more I find this one has meaning for me. Are you ready?
I'm ready!
I can be given but not traded or sold. I cannot be stolen or paid for in gold. The poor and the rich alike yearn for me, a gift still treasured when the giver is but a memory.
Forgiveness.
No, that's not the answer I'm looking for. Think harder.
Let me start over..
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
Kindness.
Kindness, yes. When everything else fades, people will remember how kind you were. I have a feeling you understand that quite well. Good luck in your adventures, young one.
Thank you. Good-bye.
Scholarship.
No, that's not the answer I'm looking for. Think harder.
Let me start over..
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
Beauty.
No, that's not the answer I'm looking for. Think harder.
Let me start over..
(Back to the riddle.)
I give up. Good-bye.
Never mind.
I'll come back later.
Talking to him again after answering a riddle
One riddle a day. Be patient. Your life isn't so very short, is it, that you can't wait a day? Come back tomorrow, and I will have something new for you.
I'll see you tomorrow, then.
Talking to him again after answering all five riddles
You have bested me in combat and beaten all of my riddles. I don't have anything else to offer you, my friend.
Farewell.
Drops
Name
Type
Rarity
Quantity
Chest of the Bounty Hunter's Gloves
Container
F
Exotic
1
Dwarf-Cut Adamantine
(only drops if the
Glorify the Golden Heart
collection
is active)
Consumable
E
Rare
1
Treasure Hunting Kit
Gizmo
B
Basic
1
Notes
After defeating Ellutherius, the djinn will ask a riddle. He will only ask one riddle a day. Answer correctly to progress the achievement
Speak in Riddles
.
It is possible to solve multiple riddles with several characters if they were parked there before the event, giving time to quickly switch between them.
As of the
19th October, 2017 update
, this contract does not seem to respawn in 30 or 60 mins. Respawn may be randomized for Legendary contracts.
Related achievements
Desert Highlands
:
Highlands Bounty Tour
—
Complete a bounty for each bounty board location.
(6
)
Desert Highlands
:
Desert Highlands Legendary Bounties
—
Defeat each Legendary bounty in the Highlands at least once.
(6
)
Desert Highlands
:
Speak in Riddles
—
Fight djinn to earn a chance at solving a daily riddle.
(10
)
Ellutherius Wintergust provides 5 of the 10 riddles for this achievement.
Jubua the Wildfire
provides the other 5 riddles.
Desert Highlands
:
Smashing!
—
Defeat
Ellutherius Wintergust
without being hit by his pot attacks, three consecutive times.
(5
)
He will occasionally summon pots from the ceiling, which will clatter to the ground in red AoE cricles. If you successfully avoid the entire attack, you will gain one stack of
Ceramics Evasion
. This resets if you are hit by the pots in subsequent attacks, though there will be plenty of opportunities to regain three stacks.
The achievement will only be rewarded if you have three stacks at the moment Ellutherius reaches 0%.
Rare Collections
:
Glorify the Golden Heart
—
Collect 10 missing gems, and combine them with
Amalgamated Gemstones
in the
Lair of Zommoros
.
(3
)
Trivia
Ellutherius
may be a Latinized form of
Eleutherios
, an old Greek name meaning, "Free".

Legendary Flame Devourer
Race
Canine
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Chaos Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Legendary Flame Devourer
is a powerful
wolf
enemy in the
Chaos Fractal
, appearing during the blizzard section of the fractal. Its purpose is to extinguish flames from the player-created bonfires, and as such it cannot be killed.
Location
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Chaos Fractal
Combat abilities
Abilities
Consumes Flame
Freezing Aura
Invulnerable
Effects
Skills

devourer
race.

Legendary Giant
Race
Risen
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
The Shattered Cleft
(
The Ruined City of Arah
)
Legendary Giants
are
superior
minions of
Zhaitan
that were once
Giants
. In combat, they use skills similar to their living counterparts'.
Locations
Ruins of Orr
The Ruined City of Arah (explorable)
The Shattered Cleft
Combat abilities
Abilities
Stomps and Shouts
Behavior
Immune to hard CCs, including stun from breaking defiance bar.
If its health drops below 25%, it will roar and heal itself back to 100%. They can be killed before this animation completes, or by completing the associated event.
Effects
Skills
Giant Stomp
- The giant stomps its left foot, sending out a
piercing
projectile
toward target foe.
Giant Stomp
- The giant stomps its left foot, sending out a piercing projectile toward target foe that
knocks back
and
cripples
for 10 seconds.
Giant Stomp
- The giant stomps its left foot, sending out a piercing projectile toward target foe that
knocks down
.
The giant shouts twice in a large point-blank
area of effect
, inflicting
weakness
for 10 seconds followed by
fear
for 2 seconds after a 0.5 second delay.
This skill cannot be
dodged
.
Throw Boulder
- Projectile attack that knocks down for 2 seconds in a point-blank-on-target
area of effect
; 1 second
cast time
.
This skill only targets enemies outside of melee range.
Giant Stomp
- Medium radius point-blank area of effect attack that knocks down for 2 seconds and cripples for 10 seconds; 1 second cast time.
This skill is only used if an enemy is within melee range.

Club Shock Wave
Tooth Stab

PAGE: Legendary Green Synergetic Assault Knight
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Legendary_Green_Synergetic_Assault_Knight
======================================================================

This page contains information about a Guild Wars 2 element, mechanic, or feature that has been removed or replaced.
The information on this page does not apply to the current version of the game or the content is no longer available.
Notes
:
This content was only available during the
Battle for Lion's Arch
(released March 4, 2014). Replaced with the
Prototype Arsenite
in the
2022 re-release
.
Legendary Green Synergetic Assault Knight
Race
Twisted Watchwork
Rank
Legendary
Location
Sanctum Harbor
(
Lion's Arch
)
The
Legendary Green Synergetic Assault Knight
was a foe encountered during the
Battle for Lion's Arch living world
event. They guarded the entrance to
The Breachmaker
, where
Scarlet Briar
was located and fought. There were three different versions, but all versions had identical attacks and strategies. Upon defeating each of the three Knights, players could get the
Prime Light Attunement
.
Location
Historical location
Lion's Arch
Sanctum Harbor
, just west of
Moran Memorial
Event involvement
Eliminate the green Synergetic Assault Knight/historical
Combat abilities
Abilities
Mode Changes
Removes Conditions
Reflects Conditions
Knockdown
Effects
Unshakable
(immune to all control effects)
Calibration based on its type
Synergetic Calibration
- Deal more damage to assault knight; take more damage from assault knight.
Switches between:
Condition Reflect
- Reflects all incoming
conditions
.
Condition Crash
- Power level lowers the more
conditions
are applied to it.
Condition Counter
- Tracks the power level of each mode.
Skills
Assault Hammer, Hammer Head, Hammer Smash
Basic cleaving melee attack chain. Hammer Smash inflicts
Knockdown
.
Explosive Impact
A short range PBAoE stomp that inflicts
Burning
on the way up, then damage and
Knockback
upon landing.
Extraction
A large AoE
Pull
. The range is so far that moving out of range is nearly impossible. Players must dodge right as the AoE marker disappears to avoid it. Always followed by Hammer Swing
Hammer Swing (warrior skill)
Melee attack that inflicts
Daze
. Always used after Extraction.
Drops
Note that the following drops are not dropped by the knights themselves, but are awarded once after the three Assault Knight events are completed, but only if at least one knight was killed.
Deluxe Gear Box
(x6, guaranteed)
Chance at
Assault Knight Power Core
of the corresponding type:
Dynamic Assault Knight Power Core
Synergetic Assault Knight Power Core
Static Assault Knight Power Core
See also
Legendary Blue Dynamic Assault Knight
Legendary Red Static Assault Knight

raid encounter
boss.  For other uses, see

Legendary Icebrood Construct
Race
Icebrood
Mech. race
Giant
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Shiverpeaks Pass
(
Grothmar Valley
)
Legendary Icebrood Construct
is the boss in the
Shiverpeaks Pass
raid encounter
. It is a deadlier variant of the
Icebrood Construct
.
Location
Ascalon
Grothmar Valley
Shiverpeaks Pass
Combat abilities
Primary article:
Shiverpeaks Pass#Walkthrough
Abilities
Shockwaves
Creates Wind
Ice Magic
Behavior
Passive until attacked
Effects
Do Nothing
— Do Nothing  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Skills
Deadly Ice Shock Wave
- A more powerful version of Ice Shock Wave that shatters all ice wall platforms, creating an unavoidable shock wave that deals heavy damage and launches enemies.
Frostbite
- The ice wall platform deals low pulsing damage to players standing on it before it is ready.
Frostbite Aura
- Icebrood construct pulses a volatile aura that deals damage every 3 seconds to all foes within a range of 2,000.
Ice Arm Swing
- Sweeps its right arm across half the arena, dealing medium damage and knocking players back as well as propelling players to spin around it.
Ice Flail
- Sweeps its right arm across half the arena, dealing medium damage and knocking players back.
Ice Crystal
- Creates an area-of-effect projectile periodically at a target's position, dealing low damage and chilling players.
Ice Shatter
- The
icebrood construct
shatters a piece of its core, creating ice platforms that shoot multiple ice projectiles. Once the projectiles stop, the platform becomes safe to stand on.
Ice Shock Wave
- The
Icebrood construct
slams its arm down, doing damage and creating a shock wave that knocks enemies down. The shock wave can be jumped.
Shatter Arm
- Shatters its left arm before collapsing to the ground, dealing low damage and pushing nearby players away.
Snowball
- Summons a massive snowball periodically near the entrance of the strike.
Spinning Ice
- Slams into the ground, launching a shockwave and propelling boulders of ice to spin around itself that deal low damage.

Throw Enchanted Ice

Legendary Imbued Shaman
Race
Demon
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Volcanic Fractal
(

)
Image(s)
The Legendary Imbued Shaman protected by his bubble.
Interactive map  (floor  25)
Legendary Imbued Shaman
is the final boss of the
Volcanic Fractal
of the
Fractals of the Mists
dungeon. It is the second and final incarnation of the
Champion Grawl Shaman
found in the same fractal.
Location
Fractals of the Mists
Volcanic Fractal
Combat abilities
Abilities
Knocks Back
Shield Bubble
Effects
Protective Bubble
- Protective shield that gets weaker from repeated attacks.
Enraged
- Gains increased damage and a
fire aura
for several seconds after the bubble is destroyed.
Skills
Fire Storm
- Marks a large area to be assaulted with raining fire, causes
Burning
Burning Shot
-
Burning
attack on single target. If successful, target becomes inflicted with
Enveloping Flames
, emitting
Defiant Inferno
. This skill inflicts
Agony
from scale 28 and higher.
Enveloping Flame
- Deals additional
Burning
damage to target and surrounding foes each pulse.
Defiant Inferno
- The flames pulsing from Enveloping Flames, damaging foes.
Dive
- Rises into the air before diving back towards the ground, causing a large explosion that deals heavy damage to any foe caught in its radius. Leaves behind damaging fields.
Protective Bubble
- Summons a barrier that
blocks
all incoming damage and summons several
Lava Elementals
(number scales with fractal level).
Wing Buffet
- Channels a point-blank explosion at melee range that inflicts
Knockback
, indicated by a red-orange
AoE
around itself

Name
Type
Rarity
Quantity

1

Crafting material
C

1
Vicious Fang
Crafting material

Rare

Notes
This foe is a demon
and therefore a
Powerful Potion of Demon Slaying
can be used.
Due to the bonus provided by demon slaying sigils such as
Superior Sigil of Demon Slaying
being bugged, these should not be used versus this foe.
[
verification requested
]
One of the possible land transformations from the
Endless Fractal Tonic
.
Bubble actually loses stacks by activating offensive skills while having the shaman targeted. Hitting it is not required so you can just attack Lava Elementals.
Legendary Imbued Shaman has a 100 range diameter hitbox, which is considered medium.
Legendary Imbued Shaman wields a
Destroyer Longbow
.

Legendary Jotun Stargazer
Race
Ghost
Mech. race
Risen
Level
80, 83
Rank
Legendary
Location
Arsenal of the Blessed
(
The Ruined City of Arah
)
The
Legendary Jotun Stargazers
are four
jotun
ghosts
residing near ancient jotun stones gathered by the
Six Human Gods
and placed within
the Ruined City of Arah
. They protect
the Mystic Telescope
, challenging those who seek it with various tasks of slaying ancient creatures.
Location
Ruins of Orr
The Ruined City of Arah (explorable)
Arsenal of the Blessed
Walkthrough
For walkthrough:
The Ruined City of Arah (explorable)#Path 1: Jotun

Abilities
Fire Storm
Effects
Skills
Unnamed skill
- Fires a slow moving homing projectile orb that deals high damage.
Lava Font
- Deals high damage.
Flame Ring
- At the beginning of the fight, a fiery ring appears around the edge of arena, which downs anyone passing through and slowly becomes smaller.

Blinding Tuft
Throw Gunk

Exotic

Notes
Drops the non-Ascalonian
ghost
variant of the
Gilded Strongbox
.
Although lore-wise they are ghosts, mechanically the final Jotun Stargazer is a
Risen
and affected by Risen slaying potions and sigils. Likewise, he incorrectly uses Risen combat quotes.

This article is about the world boss.  For the event schedule, see
Defeat the Karka Queen threatening the settlements
.
Legendary Karka Queen
Race
Karka
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Driftglass Springs
(
Southsun Cove
)
Southsun Shoals
(
Southsun Cove
)
The
Legendary Karka Queen
is a
world boss
in
Southsun Cove
. When its event triggers, players are given 15 minutes to reclaim 4 settlements (if those settlements are contested by karka invasions or crazed wildlife) and kill the Queen. Once all of them are controlled by the
Lionguard
, the Karka Queen appears.
Locations
Kryta
Southsun Cove
Southsun Shoals
Driftglass Springs
Event involvement
[Group Event]
Defeat the Karka Queen threatening the settlements
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Heavily Armored
Acid Spit
Effects
Armored
Skills
Spit
-
Projectile
auto-attack
that
immobilizes
for 5 seconds and transfers 1
condition
.
Stomp
- The Queen jumps up and stomps twice in succession upon landing, dealing high damage and
knocking down
for 2 seconds in a point-blank
area of effect
with each stomp.
Rolling Charge
- The Queen jumps up and spins, pulling its body into a cylindrical shape and rolling forward when it lands. As it travels, it inflicts high damage and
knocks down
in a point-blank area of effect every second. The Queen gains
Might
each time an enemy is hit, but it will stop rolling upon reaching 25 stacks. Terrain and other impassable barriers prevent the Queen's movement but do not end the charge. This results in massive, pulsing point-blank area of effect damage that will quickly
down
or kill any players nearby.
Throw Egg
- Throws a
Karka Egg
which breaks if it hits a player.

Throw Coral Shard
Throw Gunk

Trivia
The Karka Queen was added with the
Last Stand at Southsun
release.
The open-world chest reward was originally limited to once per day per character.
The June 11th, 2013 update
removed this limit, allowing players to obtain the bonus award as often as the queen was killed, to encourage completion of the event. In February 2014, this was changed again to its current limit of once per day per account. It is speculated that the change was added purportedly in response to abuse of this mechanic.
Although it is called the "Karka Queen," this karka is hermaphroditic like all other karka. The title "Karka Queen" is given to the eldest
karka egg layer
.

The
Legendary Ley-Line Anomalies
are coalesced
ley line
energy which have taken humanoid shapes. Though they are not naturally hostile, their presence causes heavy magical disruptions around themselves.

Walkthrough
Primary article:
Defeat the Ley-Line Anomaly to disperse its destructive energy before it overloads#Walkthrough
Combat abilities
Abilities
Only Vulnerable While Attacking
Behavior
Will begin running across the map upon spawning and when hitting 75%, 50%, and 25% health.
Level of Anomaly changes based on
area
. When the level changes, it will disappear for a second and show a spawning animation before continuing on the run.
Will become untargetable, /cower, and spawn several
Anomaly Fragments
for a minute when defiance bar is broken; during this time, its defiance bar is "locked" from regenerating.
The defiance bar will be "locked" while it is vulnerable to attacks.
Effects
Unnamed defiance skill
— Stops to gather energy.  Occurs when
defiance
is broken.
Ley-Line Accumulation
— Increased outgoing damage; stacks intensity.
Enraged
(while vulnerable to attacks)
— This creature is fighting for its life.
Skills
Annihilation Smash
- After gathering energy for a second, indicated by a surging ley energy aura, the anomaly jumps up and slams into the ground, dealing extremely high damage. Used only when health is below 50%.
Ley-energy Overload
- After absorbing too many coalescences, the anomaly begins glowing and explodes in a massive burst of magical energy. Applies stacks of
Electrolyze
.
Ley-Line Burst
- The anomaly charges energy before blasting players back with both hands dealing moderate damage and causing
Knockback
.
Ley-Line Kick
- Does light damage and causes
Knockback
.
Ley-Line Punch
- Does light damage.
World-Line Pull
- Does light damage and
Launches
players high into the air.

Blinding Tuft
Throw Gunk
Tooth Stab

Notes
Drops the
elemental
variant of the Gilded Strongbox.
Before the
August 8th, 2017 update
, it dropped a
Utility Backpack
instead.
It spawns in one of the locations above every 2 hours and spawns in the same map every 6 hours. A
Ley-Infused Lodestone
will indicate which map it is in or just spawned in.
It spawns in
Timberline Falls
at
00:20
,
06:20
,
12:20
and
18:20
It spawns in
Iron Marches
at
02:20
,
08:20
,
14:20
and
20:20
It spawns in
Gendarran Fields
at
04:20
,
10:20
,
16:20
and
22:20
While the defiance bar is recovering or vulnerable to crowd control, only a negligible fraction of condition damage will damage it.
Due to high number of players, the anomaly's model may be behind its actual placement in the world, making it hard to target. The model and placement realign when it "respawns" for level adjustment.
The event will fail after 20 minutes if the anomaly is not defeated.
This Legendary-rank enemy counts toward the Wizard's Vault weekly quest [Defeat 1 Legendary-rank Enemy]
Trivia
Endless Ley-Line Anomaly Tonic
is a
novelty
that gives its appearance when used.

======================================================================
PAGE: Legendary Llama
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Legendary_Llama
======================================================================

Legendary Llama
Race
Animal
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Challenger Cliffs
(
Dry Top
)
Prospect Valley
(
Dry Top
)
Treadrock Uplands
(
Dry Top
)
Three
Legendary Llamas
are hidden in
Prospect Valley
,
Treadrock Uplands
and
Challenger Cliffs
areas of
Dry Top
; tucked away at hard-to-reach places. Players will have to locate them in order to earn three achievements, as a part of the
Dry Top achievements
.
Locations
Dry Top
Prospect Valley
Abandoned Mine
Treadrock Uplands
Raptor Prowl
Challenger Cliffs
Near
Azarr's Arbor
Walkthrough
Legendary Llama Locator
Inside the Abandoned Mine, at the end of the quicksand river. Entry into the Abandoned Mine is blocked off until the event
Rescue the captive Zephyrites
occurs.
It is east of the point of interest, accessed via the south side of the sand river. Once inside the abandoned mine pick up a Sun Crystal and a Lightning Crystal (or Wind Crystal). Look for a ramp heading down beside the quicksand river and go to the bottom. Directly ahead of you is a cave, aim for the right hand side. Use
Light Dash
to get you inside the cave, one or two uses will get you next to the ledge. You can either try and jump onto it or use
Lightning Pull
, or
Assisted Leap
to get up out of the quicksand.
Alternatively, you can get to the ledge using a glider, or traverse over the sand river on a
Skimmer
.
Learned Legendary Llama Locator
Southeast of
Raptor Prowl
. In the northeast corner of
Raptor Prowl
, climb up the rocks a bit and turn around to pick up the sky crystal. Use the
Assisted Leap
to jump up the rocks and run across the canyon edge to the next crystal. Again, use
Assisted Leap
to cross the three ledges, and enter the alcove to find the llama.
Alternatively, you can use a
Skyscale
and just fly up there, or a
Springer
to jump up there.
Licensed Learned Legendary Llama Locator
Northwest of
Azarr's Arbor
. Head east from the
Repair Station Waypoint
—
, then circle north and up a dune and past the Vine Bridge waypoint and wreckage. Run past the earth elementals, stay to the right and up a slight incline, past another 2 Earth Elementals, to the wall. Run along the wall, slightly downward (do not drop!) and through the bush on your left. The llama is inside.
Alternatively, you can use a
Skyscale
and fly up there. Be aware, it's easy to fly over the cave, but if you keep your eyes on the ground, you should easily spot the Llama from the sky.
Prospect Valley
Treadrock Uplands
Challenger Cliffs
Related achievements
Dry Top
:
Legendary Llama Locator
—
Find the
Legendary Llama
hidden at the
Abandoned Mine
in
Prospect Valley
.
(5
)
Dry Top
:
Learned Legendary Llama Locator
—
Find the
Legendary Llama
hidden in the uplands of
Dry Top
.
(5
)
Dry Top
:
Licensed Learned Legendary Llama Locator
—
Find the
Legendary Llama
hidden in
Challenger Cliffs
.
(1
)
Return to The Dragon's Reach
:
Legacy of the Llama Locator
—
Find the
legendary llamas
hidden in
Dry Top
.
(0
)
The llamas cannot be interacted with; simply getting close will give credit toward the achievement.
Notes
Once approached, it will begin to dance in a similar fashion to the
Llama Finisher
, without the flashy lights, however.
The Llamas may be smaller than expected, do not overlook them.

Legendary Mordrem Annihilator

Mordrem Husk
that has a low chance to appear in the
Southeastern Silverwastes
during the event to defend
Red Rock Bastion
. It can spawn regardless of the
Foothold
meta event
's status, but it does appear to have a better chance to spawn at higher defense levels.

Maguuma Wastes

(only during related events)

- Melee
auto-attack
.
The husk uses its left arm for this skill.

- Melee attack that inflicts
Bleeding
for 5 seconds.
The husk uses its right arm for this skill.
Binding Roots
- The husk stomps, sending out 1-4 ground-based
2
projectiles
in a frontal
area of effect
that each
immobilize
the first enemy it hits for 5 seconds. The number of projectiles fired depends on how many players are within range of the husk when it uses this skill.
1 second
cast time
. 1200
range
.
Unblockable
. Cannot
miss
or be
reflected
.

Legendary Mordrem Commander Axe
Race
Mordrem
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Gilded Hollow
(
Heart of Maguuma
)
Lost Precipice
(
Heart of Maguuma
)
Organization
Mordrem Guard
Legendary Mordrem Commander Axes
are powerful Mordrem protecting a
Mordrem Vinewrath
guarding the crystal throne within
guild halls
. Killing them is a main objective in claiming guild halls.
Locations
Heart of Maguuma
Gilded Hollow
Lost Precipice
Event involvement
[Group Event]
Kill the vinewrath knights to weaken the vinewrath
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Creates Fissures
Heavily Armored
Behavior
Begins heavily armored, but loses it when defiance bar is broken. Once the defiance bar is broken once, it remains grayed out the remainder of the fight.
Moves faster and becomes more aggressive once armor broken.
Rooted in place when attacking.
Effects
(permanently locked when broken)
Armored
- Takes reduced damage.
Skills
Chop
- Swing your axe three times towards a targeted enemy, dealing medium damage with each hit and the final hit dealing more damage.
Crippling Blade
- Launch an axe that tracks an enemy at range and inflicts
Crippled
.
Execute
— Targets an enemy and leap onto them, inflicting massive damage.
Fissure
— Plunge your axe into the ground, creating fissures that deal high damage and apply
Bleeding
.
Great Swing
— Targets an enemy then spin around, damaging nearby enemies and throwing four waves of indestructible axes in a fixed arcing pattern.Spin around and throw axes projectiles in all directions. Can be obstructed by nearby objects. Deals a medium amount of damage.

======================================================================
PAGE: Legendary Red Static Assault Knight
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Legendary_Red_Static_Assault_Knight
======================================================================

This page contains information about a Guild Wars 2 element, mechanic, or feature that has been removed or replaced.
The information on this page does not apply to the current version of the game or the content is no longer available.
Notes
:
This content was only available during the
Battle for Lion's Arch
(released March 4, 2014). Replaced with the
Prototype Vermilion
in the
2022 re-release
.
Legendary Red Static Assault Knight
Race
Twisted Watchwork
Rank
Legendary
Location
Fort Marriner
(
Lion's Arch
)
The
Legendary Red Static Assault Knight
was a foe encountered during the
Battle for Lion's Arch living world
event. They guarded the entrance to
The Breachmaker
, where
Scarlet Briar
was located and fought. There were three different versions, but all versions had identical attacks and strategies. Upon defeating each of the three Knights, players could get the
Prime Light Attunement
.
Location
Historical location
Lion's Arch
Fort Marriner
, south of
Gunnery Range
Event involvement
[Group Event]
Eliminate the red Static Assault Knight
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Mode Changes
Removes Conditions
Reflects Conditions
Knockdown
Effects
Unshakable
(immune to all control effects)
Calibration based on its type
Static Calibration
- Deal more damage to assault knight; take more damage from assault knight.
Switches between:
Condition Reflect
- Reflects all incoming
conditions
.
Condition Crash
- Power level lowers the more
conditions
are applied to it.
Condition Counter
- Tracks the power level of each mode.
Skills
Assault Hammer, Hammer Head, Hammer Smash
Basic cleaving melee attack chain. Hammer Smash inflicts
Knockdown
.
Explosive Impact
A short range PBAoE stomp that inflicts
Burning
on the way up, then damage and
Knockback
upon landing.
Extraction
A large AoE
Pull
. The range is so far that moving out of range is nearly impossible. Players must dodge right as the AoE marker disappears to avoid it. Always followed by Hammer Swing
Hammer Swing (warrior skill)
Melee attack that inflicts
Daze
. Always used after Extraction.
Drops
Note that the following drops are not dropped by the knights themselves, but are awarded once after the three Assault Knight events are completed, but only if at least one knight was killed.
Deluxe Gear Box
(x6, guaranteed)
Chance at
Assault Knight Power Core
of the corresponding type:
Dynamic Assault Knight Power Core
Synergetic Assault Knight Power Core
Static Assault Knight Power Core
See also
Legendary Blue Dynamic Assault Knight
Legendary Green Synergetic Assault Knight

Notes
Bug
:
The Legendary Risen Priest of Melandru loses the ability to enter his second phase when the event has been repeated a certain number of times. This "counter" is reset whenever there is a game update or server maintenance.(fixed)
Bug
:
The Legendary Priest that appears in the defense event doesnt actually attack the Aetheric Interrupter, instead he will walk in circles around the temple while the mobs are just standing near the Interrupter not doing anything then attacking players that get close.

lounge
in
Divinity's Reach
. To gain passage you must buy a
Royal Pass
from The
Black Lion Trading Company
store.
Location
Divinity's Reach
The Crown Pavilion
Dialogue
When attempting to walk inside the lounge without the permanent pass in inventory or the 2-week pass equipped as a bundle

(if
Royal Pass (2 weeks)
in your inventory)

Sorry.

May Balthazar strengthen you.
Tell me more.

Effects
Skills
Giant Stomp
- A powerful stomp that will
Launch
anyone still standing in its wake, hits 3 times, and if below 50% health, each stomp causes a small AoE to appear under each player. Being hit will cause a player to sink into the sand, become
Trapped
, and take damage until they
escape
.
Roar
- A two-phase attack
Phase One: Inflicts
4
Torment
and
4
Confusion
.

Fear
for 5 seconds.
Stamp
- Stomps his foot into the dirt twice, attempting to squash their target. Followed up by a punch attack.
Throw Boulder
- Tosses several heavy boulders at players for massive damage.

Legendary Searing Effigy
Race
Construct
Level
77
Rank
Legendary
Location
Sanguine Vault
(
Citadel of Flame
)
Organization
Flame Legion
“
This Searing effigy will bring fire to the whole of Tyria. And behind it will march the Flame Legion, over your bones and ashes.
—
Revered Forgedriver
The
Legendary Searing Effigy
is a powerful
Flame Legion Effigy
that was imbued with magic of
the Searing
. An
Iron Legion
engineer
named
Crea Irontooth
was forced to build it, but the Flame Legion could not control it after they activated it.
Location
Ascalon
Citadel of Flame (explorable)
Sanguine Vault
Combat abilities
Abilities
Healed by Searing Crystals
Burns
Double Damage to Burning Foes
Effects
Skills
Flame Jet
- Spray fire in a cone pattern while moving,
burning
foes on the final attack. Deals 10% bonus damage to burning targets.
Shockwave
- Create a shock wave that
bleeds
and
immobilizes
your target.
Flame Burst
-
Burn
foes at the target location.
Searing Crystals
- Calls down 12
Searing Crystals
at random locations after a brief delay, dealing damage and
launching
in an
area of effect
when each crystal lands. Each crystal will remain at the spot where it landed and continuously grants the effigy a stack of
Crystal Regeneration
and
Crystal Might
if it's nearby. Crystals are destructible
objects
, so players can get rid of them if the effigy's healing becomes a problem.

Legendary Sentient Conduit
Race
Titan
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Mount Balrior
(
Mount Balrior
)
Legendary Sentient Conduits
are
extremely powerful
rocky
titanspawn
of
Decima, the Stormsinger
found in
Janthir
.
Locations
Janthir
Mount Balrior
Titan's Pass
Combat abilities
Abilities
Absorbs Energy
Empowers Allies
Resolute
Effects
Resolution
(30s; reapplied every 10 seconds)
— Incoming
condition damage
decreased by 33%; stacks duration.
Charged
(when hit by their own or ally's electric skills)
- The conduit has become charged after absorbing arcane flux energy from its attacks or an attack form another ally.  Conduits can use this charge to empower themselves or their allies to use powerful skills.
Flux Barrier
- Grants barrier to the conduit and converts incoming extreme damage into stacks of
Supercell
.
Supercell
- While Supercell is active, all of the conduit's actions are faster.  While Arcane Thunderbolt is used, a stack of this effect is consumed to recharge the skill instantly.
Skills
Arcane Static
- Fire static towards a foe that inflicts
10
Vulnerability
, leaving behind an electric field that rapidly pulses
1
Vulnerability
.
Arcane Thunderbolt
- Channel a thunderbolt that strikes a foe at their current position. Consumes
1
Supercell
to recharge the skill instantly.
Flux Aura
(when charged)
- Pulse damage to nearby foes.
Swirling Winds
- Channel distant winds that push enemies either clockwise or counter-clockwise.

jotun
stargazer
in
the Ruined City of Arah
.
It will not appear until each of the 4 oil geysers has a player standing on it.

Ruins of Orr

Courts of the Highborn Kings
Walkthrough

The Ruined City of Arah (explorable)#Path 1: Jotun
Combat abilities
Behavior
The Shoggroth does not regenerate health while out of combat, unlike most NPCs.
Effects
Skills
Tar Spit
- sends out projectiles that place a AoE, spawning a
Tar Elemental
, and applying
Covered in Tar
to any players that walk inside. Projectile blocking and reflecting skills can be used to prevent any
Tar Elementals
from spawning.
Tar Spit
(Burning)
- if
Legendary Shoggroth
is burning, then
Tar Spit
instead place down fire fields that burn players on entry, but do not apply
Covered in Tar
or spawn a
Tar Elemental
.

This page contains information about a Guild Wars 2 element, mechanic, or feature that has been removed or replaced.
The information on this page does not apply to the current version of the game or the content is no longer available.
Notes
:
This content was only available during
Queen's Jubilee
(August 6, 2013).
Legendary Shurakk the Savage
Race
Ogre
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
The Crown Pavilion
(
Kryta
)
Legendary Shurakk the Savage
was one of the most dangerous and driven of
humanity
's foes.
Countess Anise
has used
mesmer
magic
to enchant a
watchknight
to look like him, to fight in
Queen Jennah
's gladiatorial arena. His segment of the arena eventually became replaced with a watchknight of
Justiciar Hablion
and the
White Mantle
.
Shurakk was originally from
Otyugh's Kraal
, was violent, and was a friend to
hawks
. His fighting style was close and personal.
Location
Historical location
Kryta
The Crown Pavilion (zone)
Event involvement
[Group Event]
Defeat Shurakk the Savage
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Creates Shockwaves
Stomps
Summons Hawks
Effects
Indomitable
- Cannot be
blinded
,
chilled
,
crippled
,
immobilized
,
knocked down
,
launched
, or
pushed back
. Cannot be removed.
Unshakable
- Gains
defiance
when targeted by
crowd-control skills
.
Blind
is 10% as effective.
Weakness
and
vulnerability
last 50% less time.
Behavior
Surrounded by a ring of
Hawks
that attack nearby targets.
Skills
Punch
- Basic melee attack.
Attract Hawks
- Melee attack where Shurakk slapped the target, applying
Covered in Seeds
.
Might of Shurrak
-
No skill description found.
Ogre Stomp
- AoE attack that
launched
anything caught in it back and inflicted
Crippled
(5s).
Shurakk Smash
- Slammed the ground with a fist, launching anything caught in it back  and causing the ground to rise up in a line in front of him.

and

PAGE: Legendary Sidony the Shadowkeeper
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Legendary_Sidony_the_Shadowkeeper
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
,
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
, and
Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity
.
Legendary Sidony the Shadowkeeper
Race
Ghost
,
Vision
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Bramble Pass
(
Straits of Devastation
)
Image(s)
Echo
of Sidony the Shadowkeeper.
Interactive map
Legendary Sidony the Shadowkeeper
, also known as the Immortal Leader,
is the
ghost
of a former
seer
leader from the previous
dragonrise
cycle who perished during the
seer-mursaat war
.
Biography
Early years
Sidony was the
doyen
, also known as the Immortal Leader,
of the
seer
fhogos
. He cried for war during the previous devastating rise of the
Elder Dragons
and allied with the
elder races
of
Tyria
to achieve this end. However, just before the seers were to aid the
mursaat
in the attack against the Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
, Sidony suddenly decided against seer involvement in the battle and left the mursaat and the
Forgotten
to fend for themselves while the seers focused on the creation of the
Shadowstone
and the
Bloodstone
instead. Sidony also stopped the fellow seer
Isgarren
from interfering.
The mursaat, who had suffered many losses alongside the Forgotten, never forgave the seers for this perceived act of betrayal, leading to a
bloody conflict
between the two races millennia later in which the mursaat would exterminate most seers from Tyria despite their numbers being fewer compared to the seers.
Since their creation at the hands of the seers, Sidony believed that the
magical
weapons Legavo and Mulmund would be essential in the seers' defense against extraplanar invaders. However, the mursaat would eventually ward their armors against the staves' unique magics, well before the weapons became known as the
Scepter of Orr
and the
Staff of the Mists
, respectively. Eventually, the
Six Gods
and their servants came, unabated and unhindered, and it became clear that the staves were not the miracles the seers had thought they were.
Towards the end of the war against the mursaat, Sidony was assassinated by the mursaat
Yagon
.
However, his
ghost
would continue to haunt
Tyria
long after his demise as his task had been left unfinished. Isgarren avenged Sidony's death by killing Yagon in turn some time after the mursaat's new capital of
Bava Nisos
had been erected near the site of the mursaat's triumph over the seers in the war.
Legacy
Secrets of the Obscure
In 1336
AE
, the
Pact Commander
began
investigating
the influence of the elder races on Isgarren's
Astral Ward
and the
Wizard's Court
. The Commander found a mysterious
artifact
from Isgarren's study and decided to take it to
Historian Tranton
, the foremost
Durmand Priory
expert on the seers. Thanks to Tranton's guidance, the Commander located the
Bloodstone Caves
and found Sidony's ghost manifesting due to the proximity of the artifact.
Sidony's mind was set in the past, reciting the speech from the previous
dragonrise
when the seers had rallied the other elder races to the fight against the Elder Dragons. Reliving past glory, the doyen duly tasked the Commander with cutting down the Elder Dragons'
champions
: the
Claw of Jormag
, the
Shatterer
, and
Tequatl the Sunless
. Upon completing the task, the Commander returned to Sidony who thanked them for ridding the world of a great evil and revealed that these actions allowed the seer's ghost to finally find rest. As a parting gift, the ghost activated the artifact which the Commander had been carrying, revealing it to be a
magical torch
which Sidony hoped would be of use to the Commander in their future adventures.
Janthir Wilds

.

During
Awakening

activated
that
weapon

Bloodstone Caves
.

Sidony

Notes
Sidony was initially referred to as both he
and she,
having a masculine voice but a female name. Given how earlier lore described the seers as
genderless
,
it is possible that both are true. However, most of the newer mentions of Sidony use he.
Trivia
A
doyen
is the senior ambassador by length of service in a particular country, or simply a senior member of a group, particularly one whose knowledge or abilities exceed those of other members.

Legendary Svánigandr

80

(

Svánigandr as a wolf.
Legendary Svánigandr
is an
extremely powerful
icebrood
wolf
that merges with multiple ice-coated
wolves
into a bipedal wolf form akin to
norn
's
Become the Wolf
. It is a disciple of
Sorge the Zealot
.
Location
Shiverpeak Mountains
Honor of the Waves
(
Explorable mode
;
Zealot
path)
Hall of Balance
Combat abilities
Abilities
Immobilizes
Bleeds
Effects
Skills
Swipe
— Basic melee attack.
Brutal Swipe
— Basic melee attack.
Bite
- Chunks of ice rain down from the ceiling, damaging anything within a red circle surrounding where they land.
Howl
- Svánigandr howls, inflicting
Immobile
(5s)
and forcing affected targets to perform a cowering animation that takes 2 seconds. This animation is not blocked by
Stability
, though it can be canceled using skills that
break stun
.
Leap
- Svánigandr
leaps
at the target, inflicting
Bleeding
(5s)
when it lands.

Trivia
Svánigandr is a play on the name of the Norse mythological creature
Fenrir Vánagandr
, a monstrous wolf beast.
In
Old Norse
, the language of the name of the
Sons of Svanir
originates from, Svánigandr would translate to "swan monster".

The legendary hydra Triq Griz Grolak is on the bounty board. Are you brave enough to take it on?

The
Legendary Triq Griz Grolak
is a
hydra
legendary bounty
in
Domain of Vabbi
, located east from The Necropolis Waypoint at
Forum Highlands
. Its bounty can be picked up at
The Necropolis
Bounty Board
near
Necropolis Waypoint
—
. As a legendary bounty, it will reappear on the bounty board 30 minutes after the bounty has retreated or has been defeated.

Crystal Desert

(only during related events)

(80)

Anomaly
:
Despite what its ability says, this hydra boss doesn't summon any minions.
Effects
1 offensive, 1 defensive, and 1 legendary-specific effect granted by
Unstable Magic Abilities
Skills
Charge
- The hydra charges at a foe.
Claw
- The hydra bites with two of its heads.
Fiery Meteor
- The hydra calls down two waves of fiery meteors, each striking a random location around. Enemies hit receive damage and are knocked down.
Tri-Elemental Breath
- The hydra breaths a different element with each of its heads, dealing high damage and knocking down targets.

Legendary Unbound Guardian
Race
Elemental
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Cavern of Unseen Lights
(
Bloodstone Fen
)
Image(s)
Interactive map  (Underground)
Legendary Unbound Guardian
is a powerful creature of energy akin to the
Vale Guardian
that appears in the
Cavern of Unseen Lights
.
Location
Heart of Maguuma
Bloodstone Fen
Cavern of Unseen Lights
Event involvement
[Group Event]
Disperse the Unbound Guardian
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Summons Seekers
Effects
(unlocked when casting
Magic Storm
)
Skills
Distributed Magic
- The Unbound Guardian distributes its magic to a point that must be absorbed before it unleashes devastating damage. These are indicated by green circle AoEs that slowly close, inflicting moderate damage and several debuffs (
1
Bleeding
(10s)
,
1
Poisoned
(10s)
,
1
Burning
(10s)
, and
Electrocuted
) to all targets standing within. Players may the use
Counter Magic
special action skill
to remove these debuffs and gain the
Enraged
effect.
Magic Storm
(at 75% health and below)
- Magic orbs erupt from the Unbound Guardian, releasing energy and damaging targets in a small area around their impact point. This skill is channeled for 10 seconds and has a 30 second cooldown.
Punch
- A punching attack.
Spawn Seekers
- At regular intervals, the Unbound Guardian will spawn
Seekers
in the arena.
Unstable Magic Spike
- The Unbound Guardian creates unstable spikes of magic, damaging enemies caught in the impact. These are indicated by blue circle AoEs.

Notes
Functions similarly to the Vale Guardian from
Spirit Vale
, without the split phases and platform damage.
Unlike the Vale Guardian encounter, the seekers spawned by the Unbound Guardian are invulnerable to damage and are immune to conditions.

Legendary Whispering Shadow
Race
Avatar
Level
80
Rank
Legendary

Kinfall Fractal
(

)

Portrait icon.
Legendary Whispering Shadow
is the final foe in the
Kinfall Fractal
. It is a
Voice of Jormag
through whom
Jormag
, the
Elder Dragon
of Ice and Persuasion, can speak from a great distance.
Location
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Kinfall Fractal
Combat abilities
Effects
Empowered
- Outgoing direct damage increased by 5%. Stacks in intensity.
Inevitable Darkness
- The whispering shadow has consumed
life-fire
from a player. Each effect consumed will be returned to a nearby player once Whispering Shadow's defiance has been broken.
Frozen Spikes
Devour's Light
Skills
Falling Ice
— A small piece of debris has fallen from the ceiling of the cave.
Frozen Teeth
— The whispering shadow creates multiple fissures in the ground that erupt with razor-sharp blades of ice. As health drops, more fissures are created.
Lofted Cryoflash
— Upon completing
Frozen Teeth
, the whispering shadow releases an icy explosion that severely damages and chills all enemies in the air.
Terrestrial Cryoflash
— Upon completing
Frozen Teeth
, the whispering shadow releases an icy explosion that severely damages and chills all enemies near the ground.
Freezing Fan
— The whispering shadow flash-freezes a large swath of terrain in front of itself, damaging everything in the area.
Freezing Vortex
— The whispering shadow summons multiple freezing vortices that spiral outward. As they move, they create
vitreous spikes
in their wake.
Vitreous Spike
— A large spike of ice erupts from the ground, launching targets into the air.
This can be used to avoid Terrestrial Cryoflash, but it may knock targets into aerial hazards.
Gorefrost
— Piercing spears of ice damage all enemies in their path. Targets damaged by Gorefrost may be interrupted, dazed, or stunned depending on the fractal tier. All icicles fire at the same target in an inward-facing circular sequence that tracks the target. As health drops, more projectiles are fired.
Guttering Light
— The whispering shadow becomes vulnerable to defiance damage and repeatedly targets enemies who carry life-fire. While channeling, Guttering Light damages all enemies in the life-fire's radius. Upon completion, life-fire is removed from the target and the whispering shadow gains a stack of Inevitable Darkness.
If no players have life-fire, the whispering shadow instead uses
Glacial Midnight
.
Glacial Midnight
— The whispering shadow assails all enemies with waves of supernatural cold and darkness. Each pulse of damage also grants it one stack of Empowered.
If the whispering shadow has 5 or more stacks of Empowered, all enemies are chilled and blinded.
If the whisperinng shadow has 10 stacks of Empowered, all enemies are also afflicted with vulnerability.
Stolen skills
Throw Enchanted Ice
Related achievements
Kinfall Fractal
:
Shatterstep
—
Defeat the
whispering shadow
without being hit by
Cryoflash
.
(5
)
See also
The Whisper of Jormag
Whisper of Jormag

E
Rare
1
Trivia
It was the first world boss to implement the new
Defiance bar
mechanics and the new particle effects management/rendering technique called "material replacement".
The material replacement system is a technique that allows for not only replacing texture data, but also animating them through shaders/texture sets over fixed durations of time, therefore creating visibly impressive effects attached to a model while still preventing framerate from dropping too much.

is the last of the
Ossa bloodline
, as well as a
legendary bounty
in the
Elon Riverlands
. He is located inside the
Ossa's Defeat
. His bounty can be picked up at the
Augury's Shadow
Bounty Board
.
In life, according to a tale told by
Master Sergeant Shadi
, when
Palawa Joko
retook
Elona
, Zehlon and the
Sunspears
who protected him tried to flee to
Amnoon
. However, Joko blocked and killed them, then
Awakened
Zehlon soon after to enforce the blockade for perpetuity.

CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Lextalion_the_Merciless

Lextalion the Merciless
Race
Ghost
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Titan's Vestibule
(
Bava Nisos
)
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
Their
souls were tempered, forged in wicked flame, but we... This is the form of our vengeance!
—
Lextalion the Merciless
Lextalion the Merciless
is a soul construct, similar to
Gorseval the Multifarious
.
Unlike other soul constructs, such as the
titans
forged in the
Foundry of Failed Creations
, Lextalion and Gorseval were formed naturally from vengeful souls, in Lextalion's case the souls of unworthy
mursaat
of
Bava Nisos
and the mursaat's victims whom
Manikaz
had sacrificed to the
keystone
on
Mercia
's orders.
Location
Janthir
Bava Nisos
Titan's Vestibule
(only during related events)
Events involvement
[Group Event]
Defeat Lextalion the Merciless
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Many Become One
Effects
(unlocked when channeling
Keystone's Vengeance
)
Protective Shadow
(when defiance bar is unlocked)
- The souls have channeled their strength, rending
Lextalion
invulnerable.
Shielding Fusion
(when defiance bar is unlocked)
- Gain 15% defiance damage reduction for every ghost devoured by Keystone's Vengeance.
Skills
Ghastly Rampage
- A ring of horrible, ghostly energy spikes up through the ground.
Harrowing Prison
- Captures enemies within a spirit prison and summons a spirit. Applies
Obstructed Soul
.
Haunting Echo
- Unleashes a shockwave of ghostly energy.
Keystone's Vengeance
(at 75%, 50%, and 25% health)
- Pulls enemies inwards then traps them under an impenetrable barrier and applies
Obstructed Soul
. Channels a devastating attack that defeats all enemies within if not interrupted. Consumes any spirits trapped within and gains
1
Shielding Fusion
per spirit.
Malefic Darkness
- Expands areas of darkness and inflicts
Malefic Darkness
to all caught within.
Spectral Impact
- Smashes the ground, dealing impact damage to foes in front and underneath.
Wicked Swipe
- Swipes the air, dealing damage at close range.
Stolen skills
Consume Plasma
Throw Crystal Shard
Throw Rock
Related achievements
Bava Nisos
:
Echo Evader
—
Defeat Lextalion without being hit by the Haunting Echoes shock wave more than 9 times.
(5
)
Bava Nisos
:
Pure Soul
—
Defeat Lextalion without being affected by
Malefic Darkness
for more than 10 seconds.
(5
)
Bava Nisos
:
Spirit Nutritionist
—
Defeat Lextalion without him consuming any spirits.
(5
)
Bava Nisos
:
Vengeance Denied
—
Prevent Lextalion from casting Keystone's Vengeance by breaking his defiance bar 5 times.
(3
)
Trivia
Lextalion's name likely refers to the legal doctrine of
lex talionis
or the law of retribution, often summarized as "an eye for an eye."

Notes
Kholer is hostile to the
Champion Cave Troll
that randomly spawns as an optional event nearby. If the two are pulled close enough, they will aggro each other and fight to the death. Without player interference, the troll will usually win.
Trivia
He is named after dungeon tester Dan Kohler.

======================================================================
PAGE: Livia
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Livia
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Sea of Sorrows
,
Living World Season 3
,
Living World Season 4
,
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
, and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Livia
Race
Human
Profession
Necromancer
Rank
Legendary
Location
Inner Sanctorium
(
The Wizard's Tower
)
Organization
Shining Blade
Astral Ward
Image(s)
Wayfinder Livia.
“
The time has come. You ready to fulfill a prophecy?
—
Livia
Livia
is an old and powerful
human
necromancer
who served as the third
Master Exemplar
of the
Shining Blade
. Like the rest of the Shining Blade, Livia has a narrow-minded devotion to the continuation of
Queen Salma's
bloodline
, no matter the means.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 3
and
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Having used the
Scepter of Orr
to prolong her life, Livia continues to zealously serve the
Krytan
throne to this day under the alias of
Exemplar Kerida
. She is one of two modern
Wayfinders
in the
Astral Ward
.
Biography
Guild Wars
Livia was reportedly born in 1064
AE
,
and first came to prominence in 1078
AE
. A royalist who was said to be willing to risk everything for peace, she had dedicated her life to the liberation of
Kryta
from its
White Mantle
puppet rulers during the
Krytan Civil War
. To this end, she sought a weapon of power that would bring an ultimate end to the war that tore apart her homeland. She approached the renowned
asuran
golemancer
Gadd
with an offer to help him research the
Bloodstones
in exchange for asuran technology, but she eventually
left his employ
due to ethical objections. Before doing so, she learned both about
necromancy
and the
Scepter of Orr
.
After leaving Gadd, Livia played an instrumental role in the
defeat
of
the Great Destroyer
,
champion
of the
Elder Dragon
Primordus
, alongside various
heroes
, and stated her intention to go to the then-sunken city of
Arah
to seek the Scepter of Orr. What happened after is not clear, but it is known she wielded the Scepter's power, for a time.
A year later, Livia used her credentials with the asura to convince the exiled golemancer
Zinn
and the eccentric
Blimm
to develop a
golem
that could help defeat the White Mantle's
mursaat
masters. Using energies extracted from a dead
Seer
,
they were able to
defend
Lion's Arch
from the brunt of the mursaat
magic
, and the royalist forces were victorious. With
Queen Salma
restored to power, the
Master Exemplar
of the Shining Blade,
Bartholos
, left to command the newly-formed
Seraph
in 1079
AE
, and Livia was promoted as the new Master Exemplar. No longer needed as a rebel group, the Shining Blade was appointed as the royal guard of all
future kings and queens of Kryta
.
Interim years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Secrets of the Obscure provides more details of Livia's post-GW1 actions
As Master Exemplar, Livia created the
Oath of Confidence
, a magical rite that prevented those who undertook it from revealing the secrets of the Shining Blade under penalty of death. She bound this ritual to
The Shining Blade
, a magical artifact of Seer origins. Being fully dedicated to the cause of ending the last mursaat, Livia never married or had children.
She eventually stepped down and was succeeded as Master Exemplar by Laurelai. Livia retreated into seclusion in the Shining Blade headquarters where she lived out her life and was reported to have died in 1157
AE
, at the age of 93.
Sea of Sorrows
However, in 1256
AE
, after the
Rising of Orr
, Livia showed up alive and well as the personal bodyguard to
Prince Edair
of Kryta, and held the rank of
Exemplar
within the Shining Blade. She was aboard the
Balthazar's Trident
which was attacked by
Dead Ships
, and used her magic to prevent
Risen
attackers from coming aboard.
Livia claimed to have studied much of
Orrian
magic after acquiring the Scepter of Orr, and had retained her youthful appearance. How she had managed such a feat was not known—some tales said she had sold her soul to become an immortal
lich
, and others claimed that Livia had sacrificed prisoners in the dungeons of
Divinity's Reach
and used their blood to give herself eternal youth and beauty. She became the subject matter of hundreds of legends, usually told around
Mad King's Day
, when children were trying to scare one another with spooky tales.
Living World Season 1
In 1326
AE
, Livia was still working for the Shining Blade as an Exemplar, this time under the alias of Kerida, and spent her time alternating between visiting Divinity's Reach and searching for the missing
aspects
of
Lazarus
, the last mursaat. She had formed a bond with
Countess Anise
, the present Master Exemplar, in what was referred to as a mother-daughter relationship, and she became one of the few people Anise truly respected. While having a small respite from dealing with
Scarlet Briar
, the
Pact Commander
visited
Logan Thackeray
's office to regroup with allies, one of them being Exemplar Kerida who, however, at this point still remained anonymous.
Living World Season 3
In 1326
AE
, after contact with Kerida was lost while she was tracking down the
Eye of Janthir
and one of Lazarus's aspects into a White Mantle
hideout
, Anise sent the
Pact Commander
to assist her. The Commander saved Kerida from a trap, and the pair begrudgingly
worked together
as they navigated the hideout. They defeated
Justiciar Araya
and the last remnants of the White Mantle before seizing a sword which held one of Lazarus's aspects, although the Eye of Janthir fled from their grasp.
After returning to Divinity's Reach, Kerida witnessed an exchange between Anise and the Commander and was shocked by Anise's decision to initiate the Commander into the Shining Blade's inner circle so they could reveal their plans without risking breaking the Oath of Confidence. However, she calmed down and went along with it, witnessing the Commander's
initiation
and even offering the Commander advice during the more dangerous parts of the ritual that involved combat.
After the Commander had been sworn in and had been told what Anise and Kerida were up to, the hunt for the Eye of Janthir commenced as it would lead them to the final aspect of Lazarus and, hopefully, to the rogue
god
Balthazar
whom the Commander wanted to catch. While the Commander headed for
Siren's Landing
, Kerida
traveled
to other potential locations the Eye might have fled to, including
Ember Bay
where she witnessed the rampage of a
Sloth Queen
.
Once the Commander had tracked the Eye of Janthir to
Abaddon's Reliquary
where Balthazar had hidden the final aspect in, Kerida joined the Commander's side. The pair braved many traps of the reliquary and
reached the innermost sanctum
only to discover that Balthazar had already left, although the Eye and the final aspect were still present. Seizing the opportunity and being anxious to reach the end of her lengthy quest, Kerida performed a ritual with the other aspects she had brought along to revive Lazarus in order to kill the last mursaat once and for all so he could never threaten her beloved Kryta again. During the ensuing battle, Lazarus recognized Kerida for who she really was, and the Commander was taken aback by the surprising reveal of Livia.
Despite a lengthy struggle, the pair nearly lost when Lazarus briefly gained an upper hand on Livia and intended to end her for good. However, the Commander successfully intervened and struck the final blow with the Shining Blade, saving Livia and defeating the distracted Lazarus. After showing the Commander a vision of Balthazar heading for the
Crystal Desert
, the Eye of Janthir faded, confirming the death of the last mursaat.
Livia and the Commander briefly exchanged words, and Livia wearily revealed how the Scepter of Orr had kept her alive for centuries so she could finish her mission. With the last mursaat truly gone, she could finally slow down but not before urging the Commander to go save
Tyria
from Balthazar's wrath.
Living World Season 4
Livia, disguised as Exemplar Kerida, was seen
defending
various locations in
Queensdale
from
Awakened
attackers along with other members of the Shining Blade in 1330
AE
.
Secrets of the Obscure
While
reclaiming entry
into
the Wizard's Tower
which was under siege by the
Kryptis
invaders in 1336
AE
, the Pact Commander crossed paths with Livia once again. She revealed that she had only reclaimed the Scepter of Orr with the
wizard
lord
Isgarren
's help, and had been working with the
Astral Ward
for many years since. For her service, Livia had been deemed a
Wayfinder
, an Astral Ward title which was also extended to the Commander. She also revealed that she had known about
Mabon
, a benevolent mursaat wizard, since joining the Ward and had ultimately come to view him as a redeemed figure rather than a villain like the rest of his kind.
After securing the Wizard's Tower, the Astral Ward moved on to their floating base of
Amnytas
in the clouds to reclaim the bastions that were under attack and to prevent further damage to
the World Spire
. The Commander helped Livia
collect
relics in the
Bastion of the Obscure
,
transport them
to the
Bastion of Knowledge
, and
defeat powerful Kryptis trying to seize the relics
. Livia also participated in
closing rifts
leading to the Kryptis' home realm of
Nayos
, and
summoned the Guardian of the Obscure
to help fend off the great Kryptis
Nourys, the Eyes of the Abyss
, which was trying to damage the World Spire. After Nourys had been
banished
back to Nayos, Livia appeared atop the World Spire along with other high-ranking Astral Ward to congratulate the Commander for a job well done.
Janthir Wilds
Act 1
Livia crashed the
second meeting
of the
Tyrian Alliance
in 1337
AE
to recommend the Alliance use the Scepter of Orr against the
titans
. She pointed out that Legavo had been used by
Vizier Khilbron
to control the titans in the past, and while she admitted no one could be certain the ancient artifact would still work on these new titans she felt it was worth the attempt. Queen Jennah opposed the idea, and in a close vote the Alliance leaders decided against using the Scepter.
Absolution
Livia uses
Legavo
to
open a portal
to
Bava Nisos
.
As the Alliance team tried and failed to get past the outskirts of the fallen mursaat city of
Bava Nisos
in 1338
AE
, Livia grew more impatient. Finally, she reclaimed Legavo from the Wizard's Tower and
headed
to the Bava Nisos gate. Isgarren, Anise,
Poised Arrow
,
Waiting Sorrow
, and the Commander intercepted her, and a tense conversation was interrupted by ambushing
Mistburned
. Livia ran off during the fight, and used the Scepter to force open the gate while everyone was distracted.
After passing through the gate themselves, the Commander and their allies found Livia battling unusually coordinated titan forces. The battle won, Livia noticed signs of a recent camp in the city's ruins. While the Commander searched the camp, and found signs it was established by a trio of White Mantle working under the orders of Caudecus from years ago, Livia and Anise got into an argument over Livia's actions. The group realized that a titan had grown and merged with the city, and the titanspawn there were acting as parts of its mass to defend its organs.
To give tempers time to cool, the Commander agreed to work with (and keep an eye on) Livia as she fought the titanspawn in the bowels of the city-sized titan, while the rest of the group continued their own investigations. Livia continued her fight when the Commander was called back to confer with the wizards, who had found another of the late mursaat wizard
Mabon
's memories suggesting a complicated history between him and the titan. The group
rejoined
near the
Mists Gate
in the heart of the city, where Livia found that the Scepter was ineffective against the
shadow creatures
that were coming out of the gate.
After Mabon's spirit arrived from the gate and warded it while assisting in defeating the shadow creatures, he told the assembled team how to seal the gate for good. He also revealed the truth about his past encounter with the titan, whom he had named
Saevus Saxum
, and how him showing the titan mercy in the past had led to the current predicament as Saevus had not only merged with the city but had also manipulated the three White Mantle to open the gate to the
Foundry of Failed Creations
in the
Realm of Torment
where new titans could be forged to invade Tyria. Livia used her Shining Blade connections to get the information the Commander needed to
track down
items important to each of the three former White Mantle who had become the current titans after Saevus's manipulations. Livia accompanied the Commander, the wizards, and Poised Arrow into the Realm of Torment, and helped break the connections between the three spirits and the gate. After the fight where the group banished the White Mantle's souls from the foundry, Livia gave the Scepter to Mabon so he could seal the gate from the inside.
With the titan threat ended, Livia and the others returned to Lion's Arch to report to the Alliance. The council's decision on Livia's punishment for her actions was postponed until after the victory celebration. While she commented that she had been "uninvited" from the party, she was present at the Guild Initiative Headquarters during it, on an upper balcony under guard.

Queensdale

Commander
. I was out for a stroll and just happened upon this pitched battle. I've heard
Joko
holds grudges a long time—perhaps you two should discuss
this
in person?

(otherwise)

Divinity's Reach
secured from attack?

Shining Blade
is prepared for many scenarios, especially attacks from within. But these attacks are simple displays of power to provoke fear.
Soon enough, Palawa Joko's going to remember what fear feels like.
You're one to talk about old grudges.

The Wizard's Tower
Acclimating?
With the Kryptis no longer a threat, I'm surprised you're still here.
(after completing
Sovereign of Nayos
)
Not for long—things are getting a bit dull here. And I'm hearing...rumblings in the
north
. Perhaps we'll cross paths
somewhere in the wilderness
, hm?
Let me ask something else.
Can't wait.
Well enough. Did I hear someone mention the
Scepter of Orr
?
Oh, that. You did. Just down the hall, actually, but I wouldn't get too close.
Why not?
The
Kryptis
invasion has it...stirring. The
ward
are just terrified of it. A little silly, really. They hear strange, spectral whispers all the time!
I didn't realize you surrendered it to the Astral Ward.
Watch yourself, "surrender" is a strong word.
Isgarren
didn't give me much of a choice, once he knew where I kept it. That was a hell of a lecture... But it is safer here. For me, and for everyone else.
That's good to hear.
So, about that
Shadowstone
I so painstakingly reforged...
Oh, that. The Astral Ward was keeping a close eye on you during that entire debacle,
Wayfinder
. You came pretty close to putting your hand into a cookie jar that bites. Hard.
Did you now?
A Shadowstone of that size, while volatile, is nowhere near the strength needed to be a true threat. But even then, nobody should drop it anywhere. Especially into volcanoes.
What even is a "Shadowstone"?
Shadowstones are magical conduits created by the
Seers
, Isgarren used the biggest one he could muster to power
the World Spire
—and that's why it's so important we keep it out of Kryptis control.
I have another question...
We'll do everything we can to prevent from that happening.
Noted.
I guess I shouldn't be surprised.
I have to go.

Notes
Lore discrepancy
:
Although Livia's birth year is implied by a Shining Blade document to be 1064
AE
, this would make her improbably young during the events of
Guild Wars
. This suggests that Livia was born significantly earlier than the document implies, intentionally or otherwise.
Livia seeking out the Scepter of Orr in
Arah
was one of several "hooks" that developers placed in
Ogden's Benediction
without necessarily knowing how they were going to be used.
This event was not mentioned at all throughout
Guild Wars Beyond
(suggesting it may actually have taken place
after
the Krytan Civil War, rather than immediately after the defeat of the Great Destroyer as suggested in the
Eye of the North
epilogue
).
When asked about what happened to Livia and the Scepter of Orr, continuity designer
Ree Soesbee
stated that she "couldn't answer that question" and that it was important that she couldn't answer it.
Her rank changes from Legendary to Veteran during the
Defeat the invading Awakened
event.
Trivia
Livia is
voiced
by
Courtenay Taylor
.
A
Pact
airship
, the
Lady Livia
, is named after her.
Some of the rumors about Livia's unnatural longevity are reminiscent of those told about
Báthory Erzsébet
.
In the novel
Sea of Sorrows
, Livia is described as having "White-streaked hair".
Livia's current alias is Kerida. In Spanish, the word for "dear" (an endearment Livia likes to use) is "querida".
Her combat abilities were intended to be a "fun call-back to
GW1
".
Livia wields the focus
King's Remembrance
, and the sword
Vengeance
.
As Kerida, she wears a
Protector's Helm
,
Banded Pauldrons
,
Draconic Coat
,
Banded Gauntlets
.
Banded Legs
, and
Banded Greaves
. She wields a
Wayward Wand Scepter
and
King's Remembrance
.
Livia wears the
Midnight Hunter Outfit
since the
Secrets of the Obscure
expansion.
See also
The
Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Livia
.

Spoiler alert:

personal story
,
Living World
and all
expansions
.
Logan Thackeray
Race
Human
Profession
Guardian
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Royal Palace
(
Divinity's Reach
)
Doric's Landing
(
Lake Doric
)
The Grotto
(
Thunderhead Peaks
)
Mist Warden Camp
(
Dragonfall
)

(
Eye of the North
)
