======================================================================
PAGE: Palawa Joko
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Palawa_Joko
======================================================================

This article is about the undead king.  For the actor at the
Garden of Seborhin
, see
Palawa Joko (actor)
. For the imposter, see
Definitely Not Joko
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
,
Living World Season 4
,
Twilight Oasis Fractal
, and
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Palawa Joko
Race
Awakened
Profession
Necromancer
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Mad King's Labyrinth
(
Mad King's Realm
)
Twilight Oasis Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Image(s)
Concept art by
Carlyn Lim
.
“
Gods, dragons, nations—soon all will grovel at the feet of Palawa Joko!
—
Palawa Joko
Palawa Ignacious Joko
(also known as
King Joko the Inevitable
,
the last Primeval King
,
Joko the Undying
,
the Scourge of Vabbi
,
Joko the Feared
,
Joko the Beloved
,
Joko the Transcendent
, and
Joko the Eternal Monarch of All
) is a powerful
undead
lich who has built an empire situated within the southern reaches of the
Crystal Desert
. He is the latest sovereign of
Elona
, enforcing his iron fisted rule with an
Awakened
undead army,
Joko Loyalists
, and the
Mordant Crescent
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
In 1330
AE
, Joko aids the former
god
Balthazar
in collecting souls for his
Forged
army, but is trapped within the
Domain of the Lost
. Following Balthazar's
demise
, Joko manages to escape his prison, kidnapping the
asuran
prodigy
Taimi
and after attempting to unleash the
Scarab Plague
on
Tyria
, is
devoured
by
Aurene
. In 1334
AE
, what may be Joko's spirit is brought to the
Mad King's Labyrinth
to entertain
Mad King Thorn
.
Biography
Early years
The past of Palawa Ignacious Joko remains elusive regarding his life before becoming
undead
and during his early years as a lich. Joko himself has presented various contradictory arguments on his origins, claiming to be the last of the
Primeval Kings
as well as having lived so long ago that the fig leaf was in style.
Mad King Thorn
's
Lunatic Courtiers
, however, suggest offhand that Palawa Joko and King Thorn
knew each other in life
. And according to the
Private Property of P.I. Joko
written by young "Iggy", Joko appears to have been the son of an
Elonian
diplomat (with the province of origin unknown) in the eighth century
AE
. In his youth, he accompanied his mother on her diplomatic tours and visited
Kryta
where, while suffering from boredom, he met with young Prince Oswald Thorn who was already showing signs of violence. Viewing the young Thorn ripping wings off of flies as amateurish for not utilizing torture to its fullest extent, Joko secretly taught the prince what creatures would survive torture and be of use afterwards, with the wording suggesting that Thorn should direct his sadism towards his servants instead. The young Thorn challenged Joko to a pun contest much to the latter's horror, and the pranks escalated into a clash between the two youths, the first battle to take place between the future lich king and Mad King.
Joko eventually began studying
necromancy
. According to
The Diary of Palawa Ignacious Joko
, the 17-year-old Joko viewed his fellow necromancer student Zuri as his first true rival. Enraged at Zuri challenging him for the premiere place by their teacher's side and for daring to question his skills, Joko resorted to stabbing Zuri in the back, killing him. Joko eventually left to pursue the path of the necromancer elsewhere, believing that his teacher had nothing left to offer him. This path would eventually lead him into becoming a lich.
The Scourge of Vabbi
The
earliest known mention
of Joko with a date is in 757
AE
,
when he constructed
the Bone Palace
in
the Desolation
, a sulfurous wasteland where only
demons
and the
undead
could survive, although he had discovered a way for the living to enter this area unscathed.
At some point he had
seemingly discovered
forbidden scriptures of the fallen
god
Abaddon
and eventually sealed them in
coffers
which were hidden deep within the
Vehjin Mines
.
However, scriptures found in at least one of the coffers turned out to be instructions to find a powerful wish-granting
djinn
in the
Forum Highlands
.
Whether Joko ever approached the djinn, who would only grant a wish once every hundred years, and what he may have asked for if he did, remains unknown.
In these early years, Joko feuded with Mad King Thorn of Kryta, with some squabbles nearly escalating into a full-scale war.
Some time after Thorn's death circa 825
AE
,
Joko discovered much to his disappointment that Thorn lingered on as a spirit lord in the
Mad Realm
and that death had not changed the Mad King's horrid personality—or his puns—to be any more bearable.
The rivalry between the two now undead tyrants continued.
In 860
AE
, Joko commanded hordes of
Awakened
followers raised from ancient tombs and made his move to expand his territory. The king of
Sahlahja
resisted Joko's advance for years until he died and was succeeded by his son, Prince
Sahlahjar
. When the new king was suddenly forced to lead a kingdom and face Joko's impending assault, the inexperienced Sahlahjar gave into his fears and cut a deal with the lich: in exchange for serving Joko, he asked his people to be spared. Shortly after the deal, however, Sahlahjar's citadel was overrun by the Awakened who killed everyone, including the king. Joko later reanimated Sahlahjar as his court jester, forcing the former king to serve him forever in death.
Intent on conquering
Vabbi
and using its riches to make himself even more powerful, Joko began the next stage of his campaign. He cautiously infiltrated the unsuspecting province through the Vehjin Mines and caught Vabbian defenders by surprise. With lightning-fast raids, Joko captured wealthy estates, driving his enemies before him. His victories and eventual subjugation of the province earned him the
sobriquet
Scourge of Vabbi.
With Vabbi under his control, Joko began a ruthless and systematic conquest of the rest of the nation, piece by piece. A unified Elona might have stopped him there, but the princes of Vabbi hid, and the
Istani
Sunspears
proved too few and too divided to halt the lich's relentless assault. Over many months, Joko drove back the armies of Elona, growing stronger with each victory. He proclaimed himself the nation's sovereign, beginning an era of tyranny and oppression. By 862
AE
, the
Kournan
army had been reduced to a struggling rebellion, an alliance of fugitives led by a single renegade
warmarshal
:
Turai Ossa
.
Battle of Jahai
Warmarshal
Turai Ossa
and King Joko duel above
the Battle of Jahai
.
Using the desperate tactics of hit-and-run warfare, Warmarshal Turai hunted Joko's army wherever possible, but his forces were eventually surrounded near the
Grand Cataract of Jahai
, where they prepared to make their final stand against Joko's army which consisted of Awakened and
corsair
mercenaries. Turai's scouts learned that Joko had insufficiently garrisoned his flank in his hurry to crush the resistance, however, and Turai believed a small but powerful group might be able to fight its way through and slay the lich while the rest of the army held the line against the Awakened horde.
The resulting
Battle of Jahai
lasted for days; each time night fell, the battleground remained unchanged, as neither army gave ground to the other. Turai's squad was ultimately able to reach Joko's camp just in time to witness the lich gloating about his impending victory. Playing to Joko's vanity, Turai challenged him to single combat. The lich could not resist the urge to fell the Warmarshal personally, and the two leaders faced off high above the warring armies. There, in full view of their respective followers, Turai bested Joko, and the undead lord fell from his lofty height. On the plains below, the resolve of the unliving hordes wavered and broke. Turai's forces cheered at the defeat of the enemy leader as they cut down the fleeing undead legions.
Though beaten, Joko could not be slain. Turai's bodyguards used powerful
magic
to bind the lich within a great tomb, and eventually built a
huge stone plinth
commemorating the victory at
Jahai Bluffs
while forming the
Order of Whispers
to watch over the tomb. There, Joko would lie forgotten, trapped with his hatred and weakness.
As centuries passed, however, Joko's legend would live on, and many villainous individuals attempted to commune with the spirit of the Scourge of Vabbi.
Return of the undead king
Centuries later, Turai Ossa's descendant,
Warmarshal
Varesh Ossa
, attempted to summon the dark god Abaddon and bring about
Nightfall
. The weakening of reality
freed Joko
, who fled to the
Sulfurous Wastelands
after being disturbed by
Spearmarshal
Kormir
of the
Order of the Sunspears
, who had dreamed that the Sunspears must travel to the monument to Joko's defeat to see what awaited them there. Being buried and bound for so long had cost Joko all of his power, however, so he was easily caught by the
Veldrunner
centaurs
shortly after his attempted escape.
The centaurs' Sunspear companions promised the lich freedom in exchange for knowledge on how mortals could traverse the Desolation. Joko revealed that this was possible using great
Junundu
wurms
, and with the help of the Sunspears and
heroes
he regained some control of his army and retook the Bone Palace from Varesh's rear Vanguard.
With his base of operations secure, the lich focused his efforts on
crushing the remaining rebellious Awakened generals
and
reuniting his scattered army
under his banner.
Kormir's
rise to godhood
upon the defeat of Abaddon in 1075
AE
was a blessing to the Sunspears and a beacon of hope to people across
Tyria
. Although her new power was a boon to those who followed her, it also ignited a jealousy that would lead to the downfall of the Order of the Sunspears. Ever an enemy, Palawa Joko's resolve to wipe them out intensified. Ruining the Sunspears, whose Spearmarshal had reached such great heights, would bolster Joko's ego and help secure his position as a true threat to Elona.
Following his release, Joko briefly visited
norn
lands and took part in
Magni the Bison
's
Fighting Tournament
. Recordings left by the renowned
asuran
golemancer
Zinn
hint that, around this time, it may have been Joko or an agent of his that commissioned the
golems
N.O.X.
,
P.O.X.
, and
R.O.X.
, which attempted to assassinate the leaders of the great human kingdoms of the world—
Emperor Kisu
of
Cantha
,
Princess Salma
of Kryta, and Princes
Ahmtur
,
Bokka
and
Mehtu
of Vabbi—in order to destablize those kingdoms.
This suggests that Joko was actively working toward his conquest of Elona only a few short years after his release.
Conqueror of Elona
By 1093
AE
, Palawa Joko had recuperated much of what remained of his forces. He began sending raiding parties into the borders of Vabbi, abducting citizens to further grow his army. Despite this, the
Vabbian princes
had refrained from acting out against Joko, while the Order of the Sunspears, still undermanned after the
Battle of Gandara
, only patrolled the borders, believing Joko would remain content to stay in the Desolation. By the end of the year,
Koss Dejarin
and
Lonai
, two famed Sunspears from the war against Abaddon, had mustered a group to confront Joko at the Bone Palace. The assault failed, and Koss and Lonai were turned into Awakened—the other Sunspears' fate remains unknown.
In 1135
AE
, Palawa Joko put his true plan into motion.
He began to spread lies across the nation, diminishing confidence in the Sunspears.
Following this, he launched his invasion. Having learned from his past mistakes, Joko dammed and diverted the
Elon River
, which both supplied his desolate domain with precious water and simultaneously deprived Kourna and Vabbi of that same resource. His forces burned crops and salted the soil, furthering the provinces' inability to grow food. At the same time, Joko's army placed bounties on the Sunspears, and the people, starved and disillusioned, began to seek the bounties out. Many Sunspears, fearing being villainized by the people they had sworn to protect, or enticed by Joko's offers of power and riches, betrayed the Order and became the first of the
Mordant Crescent
.
Starved and faced with a devastating drought and famine, the people of Vabbi and northern Kourna rebelled against their leaders, comfortably allowing Joko to crush any Elonian resistance. Vabbi surrendered in desperation, while Kourna and Istan later became
vassal states
to the undead lord who subsequently renamed the Istani capital of Kamadan as
Palawadan, Jewel of Istan
in his honor.
By 1175
AE
, Elona had fallen to Joko's armies.
During this time Joko's forces hunted down and enslaved local centaurs, using them as work force. The lich king killed many enslaved centaurs to further his experiments in bone manipulation, and ultimately used their bones to expand the Bone Palace.
With his position secure, Joko found and crippled
Tahlkora
, the last Spearmarshal of the Sunspears, by taking out her tongue and eyes as he wanted to stop her rebellious writings without turning her into a martyr for the remaining Sunspears to rally behind. When Tahlkora continued her defiance, the lich had her brought before him and resorted to killing and Awakening her so he could control her that way. However, much to both Joko and Tahlkora's surprise, the Awakened Tahlkora did not succumb to Joko's will unlike other Awakened before her. In frustration, Joko took away all but the tiniest spark of Awakening from Tahlkora and left the Spearmarshal's trapped spirit within her immobile Awakened body to rot on the remote
Yahnur Plateau
.
Founding an Empire
Statue of Palawa Joko in front of
the Necropolis
.
After conquering Elona, Joko forced tributes and loyalty from the Elonians, training them to accept him as the rightful ruler of Elona and promising wealth and fortune to those who joined his forces, with the ultimate honor being an eternity of servitude in undeath. The lich king had a harem of both men and women of various races in the
Forbidden Vault
, demanding their hand in marriage in exchange for protecting their respective communities; some of these betrothed joined the harem out of obligation while others were tricked or sold there.
Joko also had all of the Ossa bloodline brought to him, creating a living army to rival his undead one, and took pleasure in the irony of their servitude.
Along with this, Joko continued the defamation and destruction of the Sunspears, granting his new recruits prominent roles within his army.
Those who did not betray their vows were killed and recruited into the Mordant Crescent all the same as undead, and the Sunspears were scattered to the winds, leaving the survivors to travel as secretive hermits. Joko personally led the assault on
Twilight Oasis
, a haven for the remaining Sunspears, and eventually confronted and defeated
High Priestess Amala
with help from the Mordant Crescent, raising Amala as a powerful Awakened after being impressed with her combat prowess.
To bolster his already big ego and cement his position in Elona in order to elevate himself above the Sunspears and the former rulers of the three provinces, the lich king commissioned architects to build several statues in his honor throughout his united kingdom, the most grandiose being the
Colossus of Palawadan
in Istan, the
Great Palawa Joko Monument
in Vabbi, and
the Triumph of Palawa Joko
in the
Elon Riverlands
. The old monument which the Order of Whispers had once dedicated to Joko's defeat and entombment was renamed the
Monument to the Return of Palawa Joko
to show the Eternal King's triumph over his enemies.
With his conquest over Elona completed, Joko began rewriting history books, declaring that he had gifted magic to the races (including
sylvari
, when they were discovered), defeated Turai Ossa rather than the other way around, and even slain Abaddon and taken the fallen god's power.
In doing this, the lich king had outlawed all accurate history books, and burned the
Holdings of Chokhin
to the ground.
All places of learning became heavily monitored by the Awakened, especially in Vabbi. This false history was spread across Elona as full out truth, and those claiming otherwise were branded as heretics and traitors. Any who did not support the "god-king of Elona, the Crystal Desert, and all their environs" were exiled to prison farms, if not outright killed and, sometimes, Awakened into forced servitude.
At an unknown point in time, Joko had also
developed new necromantic magic
, which is why he had become so uniquely powerful. He was subtle about this new magic at first, but eventually his greed overcame him and he began to teach these practices to other
necromancers
, dubbing them
scourges
after himself. However, these necromancers realized the intention of these teachings and the exploiting of souls it would lead to, and rebelled against him so as to not further Elona's desecration. Some scourges remained in Elona with the intention of using Joko's own teachings against him, and took his teachings even further. Joko also showed interest in the
Scarab Plague
of the past, and he and his army used dark magic resembling it to frighten their opposition.
When Joko conquered Elona,
Central Tyria
had cut off all official contact and banned travel to the Crystal Desert and Elona.
Despite this, some small groups as well as the
Durmand Priory
had managed excursions into the Crystal Desert, and those who knew of the Order of Whispers knew that they retained access to Elona as well.
Refugees
would occasionally manage to escape Elona and reach Central Tyria, though the last group of such refugees had arrived around 1275
AE
. With the rise of the
Elder Dragons
Zhaitan
and
Kralkatorrik
, contact with Elona was further cut off. The
Desert Gate
, the main land route into the Crystal Desert from
Ascalon
, was sealed
by order of Queen Jennah
.
Path of Fire
Joko's banner, displayed throughout his kingdom.
After the deaths of Zhaitan and
Mordremoth
, Kralkatorrik flew south to Vabbi. In 1330
AE
, the rogue god
Balthazar
returned to the world in order to defeat the Elder Dragons and take their power. The ensuing chaos allowed Elonians to flee as far as Kryta for the first time in at least 50 years, with many disillusioned defectors attempting to escape north to the
Free City of Amnoon
and beyond.
The re-establishment of contact with Elona revealed how much control Joko exerted over his kingdom. Joko's northern domain had become enclosed by
the Bone Wall
with only the work farms along the Elon (where prisoners and exiles were sent) remaining outside. Even those loyal to Joko's regime could end up in these farms if Joko's whims demanded it, though many
offered themselves or their loved ones as concubines
in the hope of sparing their families.
The spread of propaganda promoting Joko as a god-king had furthered during the later years of his reign, expanding to Joko being the source behind not just Zhaitan's death, but Mordremoth's as well. The people of Elona were taught to honor the act of being Awakened, and to see it as their duty to yearn for such a "gloried" position. Citizens of Vabbi, perhaps all of Elona, were subject to continuous announcements by the
Awakened Affirmation System
promoting King Joko's ideals, teachings, and wants of the people, furthering to aid the brainwashing of the nation.
Balthazar approached Joko to find souls for his
Forged
army. In exchange for a share of the new recruits, Joko agreed. Together, they journeyed to the hidden city of
Kesho
to learn the
Forgotten
's magic of binding souls to armor, and then Joko guided Balthazar to the
Domain of the Lost
where those who had died traumatic deaths and forgotten themselves ended up before they could move on to their rightful resting place. Instead of fulfilling his end of the deal, however, Balthazar imprisoned the lich king in a cell in the Domain of the Lost with magic too strong for even
Grenth's Judge
to break.
When the
Pact Commander
arrived in the Domain of the Lost, Joko tried to bargain with them, pointing out that it would not be possible to defeat the Forged without the help of his Awakened army. The Commander refused the bargain and hatched a plan to seize control of Joko's army, who followed the Mordant Crescent in Joko's absence. Joko was last seen imprisoned in the Domain of the Lost, swearing to take revenge on those who had put him there, who had left him there, and who had dared take advantage of his absence.
Living World Season 4
Daybreak
After Balthazar's death, Palawa Joko escaped the Domain of the Lost and returned to Elona. He was initially angry at the Pact Commander for leaving him behind and deceiving the Awakened army to fight at
Kodash Bazaar
, and turned more serious as a result while plotting his revenge.
Upon his return, the lich king placed a bounty on the Pact Commander, though those who knew of Joko's imprisonment believed him to be an impostor. It was not until the Commander heard Joko's own voice over
Taimi
's communication device that
Dragon's Watch
finally believed Joko's return to be truth. Joko's propaganda had also furthered to proclaim that he was the one to
defeat
Balthazar atop Kodash Bazaar.
As the Commander traversed the
Domain of Istan
aiding
Spearmarshal Zaeim
's Sunspear rebellion, Joko kidnapped Taimi and brought her to
Fahranur
, the First City of the
Primeval Kings
where an ancient
Rata Novan
lab was. At an unknown point in time, the
Inquest
had discovered this lab which had been subsequently overrun by the Awakened. However, Joko did not have the knowledge to access the full lab. For this, he interrogated Taimi, threatening her with being made an Awakened to get her compliance.
With full access to the lab, Joko began a full-scale invasion on
Central Tyria
through the
asura gates
in Fahranur and another Elonian Inquest lab which did not need an exit gate at the destination (much like the Inquest's prototype
Gate Keno Decca Five
). To justify Elona's act of aggression against Tyria, Joko used the Commander's trickery over using his Awakened army against Balthazar in Kodash as a declaration of war.
With Taimi having outlived her usefulness, Joko had Awakened Inquest lock her into
Scruffy 2.0
and disable the golem's controls, intending for Taimi to suffocate inside while forcing the Commander to fight against the reprogrammed golem and watch her die an agonizing death. By the time the Commander's party reached the city and saved Taimi from the death trap, Joko and a mysterious figure had left Fahranur for an unknown destination.
A Bug in the System
While the destruction of the Fahranur gates did slow down Joko's assault, he had found yet another gate hub in
Rata Primus
to launch his attacks. However, the gate hub was not his primary reason for assaulting the massive Inquest base. Instead, Joko was looking to obtain their research on the Scarab Plague. He was personally on hand to collect the research in 1331
AE
and, in an uncharacteristic move, chose not to gloat. Instead Joko made the Pact Commander's party watch his hasty departure with the plague samples, which he intended to unleash on Central Tyria, and left
Commander Lonai
, the mysterious figure from Fahranur, behind to finish the party off. Joko would eventually learn that the Commander had successfully defeated Lonai and escaped from the trap, however.
Long Live the Lich
Upon entering the
Domain of Kourna
, Joko rallied his forces and ordered
Beastmarshal Oluwa Eranko
and
Troopmarshal Olori Ogun
to defend
Gandara, the Moon Fortress
where he attempted to unleash a third generation Scarab Plague upon the world after an earlier,
failed attempt
in Amnoon during which he had goaded the Pact Commander to meet him in combat in Gandara. He felt in control again and began joking at his enemies' expense, taking great pleasure in intimidating his Kournan subjects via announcements projected from various Joko statues found throughout the region.
Joko
meets
Aurene
.
The Pact Commander, together with the forces of Primeval Queens
Dahlah
and
Nahlah
, the
Olmakhan
, the Sunspears,
corsairs
and volunteers from Amnoon and Tyria, successfully snuck into Kourna through a portal and
established a foothold
, eventually
assaulting Gandara
to stop the plague from being spread. While the rest of the allied army was busy either fighting the Awakened or containing the plague, the Commander and
Braham
entered the Moon Fortress
via its sewers and were welcomed by projections of Joko who kept taunting them with illusions, traps, minions, and quips.
By this time Joko had become obsessed with the Commander, who had thwarted his schemes over and over again, and began viewing the Commander as a worthy nemesis whose demise he would savor. When the Commander and Braham reached him, the lich king revealed that the gauntlet he had put the pair through had been a trap to lure the Commander to him so he could end the hero's quest personally. However, Braham managed to take the Commander's place in the trap Joko had devised, leaving the Commander and Joko to duel.
After a lengthy clash during which Joko sent various minions and even scarabs against the Commander, the lich king was defeated. With nearby Awakened paralyzed and the traps disabled, the Commander and Braham believed that Joko's claims of immortality had been a ruse and that the Eternal King was truly dead. However, Joko caught the pair off-guard when he suddenly revived and trapped them in a paralyzing spell. Satisfied, the lich king gave a grand monologue about his and the Commander's roles as hero and villain. He pointed out the disastrous consequences of killing Balthazar and two Elder Dragons and how he would be viewed as a hero for slaying the Commander whose actions had brought Tyria closer to destruction. Before Joko had the chance to execute the Commander and Braham, however, the
dragon
Aurene
suddenly flew in and came to the pair's rescue, devouring the surprised lich. The reign of Palawa Joko, the God-King of Elona, had come to a sudden and definite end.
Legacy
Twilight Oasis Fractal
A
fractal
depicting events of Joko's assault on Twilight Oasis haven for the Sunspears appeared in
Dessa
's list of unstable fractals during the final years of Joko's reign. Urging adventurers to stabilize the Twilight Oasis fractal, she sent them into the fractal to relive the Mordant Crescent assault on the haven. Witnessing Joko's cruelty and Amala's tragic fate as well as the adventurers' eagerness to slaughter the Sunspears while playing the part of the Mordant Crescent, Dessa voiced her disapproval of the massacre although she appreciated how hard the adventurers were throwing themselves into her research.
Living World Season 4
While exploring Kourna after Joko's demise, the Commander discovered a statue with a voiced message which the late lich king had recorded during the assault on Gandara. In his message Joko mused about the origins of the universe and himself, and how he had supposedly been responsible for the creation of the gods and turning Kralkatorrik into an Elder Dragon.
Joko's death in the Battle of Gandara left a power vacuum in the United Kingdom of Elona and allowed the Awakened to gain freedom; however, many Awakened openly mourned the passing of their Eternal King and remained uncertain of their future despite efforts to ensure their safety in the changing political landscape. Several competing factions—including the
Free Awakened
,
Joko Loyalists
, the Mordant Crescent, the
Order of Shadows
, and the Order of the Sunspears—emerged to decide the future of the shattered nation at a
summit
in the
Fortress of Jahai
.
Meanwhile, opportunists such as an
Awakened mesmer
, who posed as Joko, appeared to the hopeful and confused Awakened in an effort to
sway the Awakened to follow them
, justifying these actions with the belief that Elonians were sheep who needed a wolf, Palawa Joko, to both lead them and keep order in the lich king's crumbling empire which was suffering from increased raids by Kralkatorrik's Branded. Ultimately most factions decided to put their differences aside and collaborate to build a new Elona, leaving Palawa Joko's reign behind them and facing a brighter future.
During the
Halloween
following the peace summit at Jahai,
Brigadier General Kernel
recruited the Pact Commander to aid with a scheme to gather various objects of "evidence" to make Mad King Thorn think that Joko was still active despite his Awakened troops' lessening presence; if Thorn ever learned the truth of his archrival's death, his mad tantrum could have disastrous consequences to everyone involved in both the Mad Realm and Tyria.
The Commander tracked down trophies of the feud between Joko and Thorn around Elona and the Mad Realm. Once all the trophies had been collected, the Mad King's minions presented a
trophy shelf
which contained Joko's tomes seized from the fallen lich king's armies. Some of the private journals found among the tomes shed some light on Joko's past and proved that many of his boasts had been mere fabrications to boost his ego.
Consuming Joko's lich magic granted Aurene the powers of resurrection, allowing her to
return to life
in 1332
AE
after Kralkatorrik had killed her in the battle of
Thunderhead Keep
. Aurene's return made Braham openly praise Joko for actually contributing something good to the world for a change after centuries of evil deeds.
Shadow of the Mad King 2021
Mad King Thorn
and the ghost of Palawa Joko, towering above the
Mad King's Labyrinth
as they argue.
By the
Halloween
of 1334
AE
, however, Thorn grew suspicious of Joko's lack of activity, which prompted the
Lunatic Court
's emergency task force to find a means to bring Joko's spirit to the
Mad King's Labyrinth
somehow to appease the mad monarch.
What seemed to be the spirit of Joko himself did eventually appear in the Mad King's Labyrinth, prompting King Thorn to replace his previous bantering partner,
Bloody Prince Thorn
, with Joko at the center of the labyrinth much to the prince's annoyance.
Thorn and Joko began exchanging taunts with gusto, and Joko appeared to enjoy his time both bullying Thorn while being simultaneously annoyed by the Mad King's pranks and puns. Curious of these developments due to having witnessed Aurene devouring Joko in Gandara, the Pact Commander sought out members of the task force to
press them for information
. The task force's testimonies differed wildly from one another, however, leading to multiple conflicting theories to consider.
In a report to
Magister Tassi
, the Commander had to choose which of the many theories for Joko's return they supported the most: the spirit could be the real Joko who had naturally passed on to the Mad Realm as his madness and ego would have drawn him there, a fragment of Joko's soul either recovered by the courtiers via a ritual or which had escaped death on its own when Aurene had devoured him, leftover fragments and memories of Joko bound into a projection of him, an echo of Joko created by
the Mists
, an alternate version of Joko from a different realm in the Mists, a
mesmer
illusion to conceal the truth, a
Mad Spirit
coerced by the courtiers into believing they are Joko, or an actual unaffiliated imposter making everyone think they are the real Joko.
Deciding on which theory to support for the thesis, the Commander and Tassi concluded their investigation and put an end to Joko's tale while the lich king's legacy, regardless of the labyrinth Joko's origins, would live on in the Mad Realm to keep the Mad King entertained.
Secrets of the Obscure
The
Wizard's Court
of
Amnytas
created artificial fractals to study alternate outcomes to canonical events and how these might have affected Tyria positively or negatively, hoping to find useful information and inventions from these alternate histories to better improve the present day Tyria's defenses against looming threats from the Mists. One of these fractals depicted a version of Elona where Balthazar had won and forced the Pact Commander to retreat, leaving Joko as the leader of the Elonian resistance against Balthazar's Forged army. When the wizards' fractals manifested on Tyria due to the breaking of
the World Spire
and the subsequent
Kryptis
invasion in 1336
AE
, the
Skyward Marches
region of this fractal became a permanent, floating landmass in the
Horn of Maguuma
alongside the fractal's inhabitants. Neither the fractal Balthazar nor Joko manifested, however, and
writings
by the now manifested fractal's inhabitants speculated on Joko's absence from the fight.

Notes
Unlike in the rest of his
Elonian
empire where public announcements come from the
Awakened Affirmation System
, Joko addresses his subjects directly in the
Domain of Kourna
via big and small statues of himself which have been spread throughout the settlements there.
Revenant
players
can briefly channel Joko as the
Legendary Prisoner Stance
in
The Hero of Istan
story instance.
Attempting to speak with Joko in the
Twilight Oasis Fractal
results in the player getting downed.
Trivia
Joko is
voiced
by
Nolan North
, who also voices the Human Male
Pact Commander
.
He also voiced Joko in the original
Guild Wars
.
In 2021 for
Halloween
, he replaced
Bloody Prince Thorn
in the
Mad King's Labyrinth
.
Joko has his own leitmotif, called "
Joko's Theme
", which was composed by
Maclaine Diemer
.
"Praise Joko" is an often used saying among Joko's
Elonian
subjects and has become a widely used
meme
among both developers and the player community.
Joko's full name is only ever mentioned once by himself.
Palawa
is a name for Aboriginal Tasmanians who historically suffered from diseases introduced by colonists, and
Jok
is a spirit of illness among
Acoli
belief.
When discussing who they would cast in a dream "movie-version" of
Guild Wars Nightfall
,
ArenaNet
would cast
Christopher Walken
as Palawa Joko.
In the
Long Live the Lich
episode, the motion capture for Joko was done by
Richard Sloniker
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
.
During the rewriting and rebuilding of the initial versions of the first three episodes of
Living World Season 4
due to a late shift in story focus, Joko's role was reduced as he was not intended to be the main villain of the restructured season story arc. Placing Joko's death earlier in the season at the height of his likability allowed the writers to have Joko address the questionable outcomes of the
Pact Commander
's actions without overstaying his welcome, help develop
Aurene
and progress her character arc, and give more room for the rest of the season's episodes to pay off some story debt.

has an article on

======================================================================
PAGE: Peitha
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Peitha
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Peitha
Race
Demon
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Memory's Hollow
(
Inner Nayos
)
Organization
Kryptis
“
We may be born of different rules, but strength recognizes strength.
—
Peitha
Lady
Peitha
of House Nephus is a
Kryptis
Lord from
Nayos
, the Realm of Dreams. She is the sister of
Cerus
and
Deimos
, the cousin of
Heitor
, and the niece of Midnight King
Eparch
and
Queen Labris
. Peitha feels empathy for her people under the tyrannical reign of her uncle, and has set her sights on the
Pact Commander
whose actions may help her advance her own goals and ulterior motives. She is curious about mortals and their emotions and has a playful if a bit unsettling side.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Following her
triumph
against Eparch in the Kryptis civil war, Peitha succeeds him as the new Midnight King of Nayos and ushers in an age of more peaceful relations between the Kryptis and the inhabitants of
Tyria
.
Biography
Early years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Peitha came to being as a
Kryptis
demon
alongside her brothers
Cerus
and
Deimos
in
Nayos
, the Realm of Dreams, at some point. She was assigned a noble rank in House Nephus by her uncle
Eparch
, the Midnight King. She, like the other Kryptis, served Eparch who aimed to find a way through the
Veil
into
Tyria
from which he had been exiled by the
Seer
wizard
Isgarren
. Before present times, Peitha only recalled hearing Isgarren's voice once when she was very little.
Peitha, along with her siblings and other higher Kryptis, tried to breach into Tyria multiple times as per Eparch's orders; each of them possessed several Tyrians in the past, and their first possessions in particular took them time to process the many emotions of their victims.
However, when Eparch did not lift a finger after Deimos was trapped in the
Bastion of the Penitent
and slain by raiders and the
Pact Commander
in 1330
AE
,
Peitha grew more disillusioned with the Midnight King's reign but chose not to oppose him openly like her mentor
Febe
had as Eparch was bound to punish anyone openly rebelling against him. She also began viewing Cerus as nothing but a tool for Eparch because the greedy Cerus, who yearned for the Midnight King's approval, lacked what little honor Deimos had possessed. Peitha considered Eparch and Cerus as despoilers of Nayos and the Kryptis, especially because the Midnight King had taken to not only intimidating his subjects into servitude but even devouring them.
Peitha first became aware of the
Pact Commander
after the latter had successfully
escaped
the
Domain of the Lost
and resurrected later that year. The Commander's return to Tyria created a subtle ripple through the Mists, which she felt from Nayos.
Secrets of the Obscure
Act 1
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Peitha sensed the Pact Commander's presence again in 1336
AE
when the Commander and an
Astral Ward
hunting squad led by
Arina
had been
sucked into a rift
. The hapless Tyrians were separated across Nayos in the ensuing chaos, and the Commander found themself alone in the hostile landscape of the corrupted
Temple of Febe
. As Cerus came to investigate the trespasser and began toying with his latest prey, Peitha invaded the Commander's mind and began communing with them telepathically from that moment on. She urged the Commander to utter Cerus's name to anger the Kryptis Lord, and then subtly helped the Commander escape Cerus into a nearby portal to
Skywatch Archipelago
which
Mabon
of the
Wizard's Court
had opened upon sensing the Commander's distress.
Act 2
Though she does not directly take part in the action during this act, Peitha continues to offer commentary to the Wayfinder.
On one occasion the player will overhear Peitha talking to
Eparch
.
Peitha:
“Scurrying like prey, waiting to be killed.”
Strange Voice:
Good.
<Character name>:
What? Peitha…?
Peitha:
“(sigh) Soon my king.”
She will inquire about
Aurene
when the Wayfinder makes mention of her upon first treading the halls of the Wizards tower. The player recounts that the prismatic dragon is currently slumbering.
She reveals that Eparch had once been to
the Wizard's Tower
as well. This story is relived in the
Lonely Tower Fractal
.
During this act, near the beginning of
Mabon's Fate
is the first time the player sees Peitha as a spectral image in the Wizards Tower.
After
Mabons
possession, and subsequent death, Peitha will comment that she senses Cerus’s presence. Thought it is unclear which specific Demon may have been possessing him, or if it was a collective.
Peitha:
His...callowness clings to the air. I feel the warmth of his breath collecting on your skin.
Peitha:
Disgusting. The dolt follows Eparch without a thought.
<Character name>:
Who are you talking about?
Peitha:
Did Eparch send him simply to cause havoc before the main event...?
Act 3
After Cerus snared
Isgarren
and the Pact Commander, Peitha intervened before her brother could deal the final blow. Revealing her disdain for the status quo in
Nayos
, Peitha betrayed Cerus and aided the Commander in killing him. She returned with them to the Wizard's Tower, wherein she stated her plan to stop Eparch from using the Kryptis as fuel for his planned rampage through the Mists.
After the Wayfinder finally located
Isgarren
it was revealed that Cerus, the demon first encountered at the start of Secrets of the Obscure, has possessed Isgarren. Before fighting the possessed
Seer
Peitha reveals Cerus is actually her brother.
Towards the end of the fight with Isgarren, Cerus pulls the Wayfinder and Isgarren into the
Temple of Febe
once more.
Peitha comes to taunt Cerus, at first feigning interest in his latest catch. She makes a reference to
Deimos
, the final boss in the
Bastion of the Penitent
raid, revealing that he and Cerus are related.
“Your honor died with Deimos, Eparch is foolish, and so. are. YOU.
Upon defeating Cerus; Peitha, Isgarren and the Wayfinder escape the
Temple of Febe
and reconvene in the tower.
Peitha reveals the demon civil war that is brewing in Nayos and her ultimate motivation to stop Eparch who has been feeding on the other Kryptis.
At first, Isgarren is indifferent, stating that without bodies Eparch wouldn’t be able to invade Tyria. However Peitha convinces him that with this power Eparch could invade Tyria to feast as well.
Upon further questioning Peitha reveals that she has known of the Wayfinder since they sojourned to the
Domain of the Lost
. They felt their presence again in the Temple of Febe and knew that with their combined power they could overthrow Eparch. She also sheds some light on the sibling dynamic between her and her brothers. Deimos having always been favored by Eparch, after his imprisonment Cerus tried to replace his brother but could never live up to his example.
Through the Veil
Some time after, Peitha contacted the
Wayfinder
again to inform them that the situation in
Nayos
was darkening and
Eparch
’s atrocities escalating. 
Upon arriving at the
The Wizard's Tower
to reconvene, the Wayfinder found out that Peitha and
Isgarren
were struggling to get along but still tried to work together. While Isgarren was not planning on joining the fight in Nayos, the immediate threat to Tyria due to
Kryptis
rifts was severe enough that he agreed to help, to Peitha’s relief. However, Peitha was worried about overwhelming the Kryptis with too many visitors too quickly, so she suggested sending in the Wayfinder first along with a few allies to gain the Kryptis’ trust. She mentioned that she and her allies had established a base in the outer reaches of Nayos, away from Eparch’s spies, and this is where the advance team would head to.
Before leaving the Wizard’s Tower however, the group was attacked by Kryptis coming from a powerful rift. Peitha went into the right accompanied by the Wayfinder, Isgarren and
Zojja
. There, they fought more Kryptis, as well as a general called
Sorrow
. Once the final wave of enemies defeated, Peitha and the Wayfinder ventured deeper into Nayos, while Isgarren and Zojja returned to Tyria. 
On their way to the camp, Peitha revealed that her old teacher used to be
Febe
, who also felt the same way as Peitha did now about Eparch, and he was killed.
“Will it take your arms and your toes on a platter to see? Those who follow Peitha will die at her feet.
Upon reaching the camp, Peitha and the Wayfinder once again were ambushed by Kryptis loyal to Eparch. When they finally cleared all the enemies from the camp, they were able to catch up with the
Astral Ward
team that had been lost, led by
Arina
, as well as
Ramses
, a Kryptis friendly to Peitha and the Ward. 
Peitha revealed that
Heitor
, her “cousin”, was responsible for the attacks, as an act of revenge for the Wayfinder and allies killing
Cerus
, her mate.
Following their discussion at camp,
Peitha
sent out the
Wayfinder
to scout
Inner Nayos
further, and help the
Astral Ward
and push back the
Kryptis
loyal to
Eparch
to send a message to their master. Using
Peitha
's name, the Wayfinder was able to convince some Kryptis, such as
Irja
to join the Astral Ward/Peitha alliance against their master,
Eparch
.
After securing the outskirts of
Inner Nayos
, and rallying some Kryptis to the cause,
Peitha
and the
Wayfinder
moved on to strike
Heitor
, Peitha's cousin. Any hope of rallying
Heitor
to the cause seemed to vanish when she absorbed
Irja
's life energy and killed them, leaving
Peitha
and the
Wayfinder
no choice but to kill her. While
Peitha
regretted that
Heitor
had to die, she told the
Wayfinder
not to worry, as
Heitor
was cruel, even though she was driven by fear.
The Realm of Dreams
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
By 1337 AE, after establishing a stronghold in the first area of
Inner Nayos
, the Astral Ward and local Kryptis reconvened at a
War Council
to decide how to push forward towards
Eparch
's forces. While
Isgarren
did not yet want to commit the full support of the
Astral Ward
,
Peitha
suggested that help may be found with local Kryptis, and that they would follow someone they respect, such as
General Nephus
. Peitha tasked the Wayfinder with gathering more information while assisting the local forces, until General Nephus would be found. The Wayfinder had to carefully navigate the next steps with Peitha, as if they did not manage to resolve the situation themselves by recruiting more Kryptis loyal to Peitha,
Isgarren
would likely order the total destruction of
Inner Nayos
and the
Kryptis
, in order to protect
Tyria
from their invasions.

You have questions.
Tell me about Heitor.
Heitor is powerful. Impulsive. Volatile. She's Cerus's former mate, tasked by Eparch to keep Inner Nayos secure. I imagine she is not pleased with me. Or you, for that matter.
Sounds ominous. One more thing.
I need to go now.
Who is Ignaxious?
One of Eparch's lapdogs. An ambitious one, with a bite. Ostensibly a general to Heitor, but I suspect he's also here to keep Heitor in line.
Intriguing. Let me ask something else.
Thanks. Talk to you later.
What's the deal with the barrier?
The beacons were all the security needed here. But Eparch's needs changed. He wanted absolute control over access to-and from-Inner Nayos. The toxic fog was his solution. Vile. Like Eparch himself.
Let me ask something else.
Thanks, Peitha. I need to go.
We'll talk later.

======================================================================
PAGE: Poised Arrow
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Poised_Arrow
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Poised Arrow
Race
Kodan
Profession
Ranger
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Harvest Den
(
Lowland Shore
)
Organization
Lowland Kodan
Lowland Kodan Council
Image(s)
Interactive map
Poised Arrow
, nicknamed Poky, is a
Lowland Kodan
and son of
Stoic Alder
and the late
Silken Weir
. He is expected to succeed his father as the next
Claw
of the Lowland Kodan one day.
Biography
Early years
Poised Arrow was raised in
Hearth's Glow
as a cub and left with his father
Stoic Alder
when his mother
Silken Weir
had died during the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
's rise. He is next in line to inherit the title of
Claw of Koda
in his community and is friends with at least a few
kodan
in
Harvest Den
.
Janthir Wilds
Act 1
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
In 1337
AE
, Poised Arrow
met
the
Pact Commander
,
Caithe
, and
Malice Swordshadow
soon after they had arrived in
Lowland Shore
. He was sitting in on a meeting of the
Lowland Kodan Council
members when he introduced himself to them. He expressed amusement at the circumstances of the Commander's arrival to Janthir and dubbed them 'Lost-Your-Way-Finder.' Stoic Alder requested that he chaperone the Commander, and he responded with reluctance.
Contrary to his father's wishes, Poised Arrow ultimately declined to chaperone the Commander during their exploration of the village, saying that a
Wayfinder
needed no assistance. He also expressed his desire to partake in the celebratory dance festival with his friend. At some point, he was given a communication device by Malice, and he summoned the Commander to inform them that their presence was requested by his father.
Act 2
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Godspawn
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Repentance
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add story for chapters 15 and 16.
After learning that the
Tyrian Alliance
, the
Astral Ward
and some other lowland kodan volunteers, who had teamed up to survey the northern isles of
Janthir
, had reached the outskirts of
Bava Nisos
, the ancient
Mursaat
city,
Poised Arrow
met with the
Commander
to travel to the outskirts of the city. Poised Arrow insisted they travel by boat, which didn't seem to be the Commander's first choice, but he was very excited about his first real voyage. After a rough journey, of which Poised Arrow spent the first part singing sea shanties, before eventually succumbing to sea sickness and spending the rest of the trip heaving, the duo arrived at the
Mistburned Barrens
, on the outskirts of the old Mursaat city.
There,
Poised Arrow
and the
Commander
met with
Isgarren
,
Anise
and
Waiting Sorrow
and formed a plan of action. Accessing the city was difficult, not least because of the
Spectral Agony
plaguing the surround area, following a major battle a long time ago between the
Seers
and the
Mursaat
. To find out more about the area, the Commander headed north into the valley. When the Commander came back, they informed the group that they had found a strange glowing wisp and wanted to investigate further, but had been unable to follow it into the valley filled with spectral agony. The party gathered again and set out together, Poised Arrow accompanying the Commander, Isgarren and Waiting Sorrow. Poised Arrow and the Commander felt the brunt of the spectral agony curse, not being as resistant to it as the wizards who were with them. As they progressed through the valley, they heard whispers of a voice that seemed familiar to some in the party, although Poised Arrow did not know who that was. After Waiting Sorrow suggested that the Commander use the Heart of the Obscure to help identify the mysterious wisp, the group was ambushed by a swarm of mursaat ghosts! As they fought the ghosts, Poised Arrow and the others witnessed as Isgarren and a mysterious new ghost called
Yagon
started fighting each other, as Yagon obviously held
Isgarren
responsible for the death of someone they knew. However, Poised Arrow then noticed the wisp had taken the form of mursaat ghost themselves. However, this particular mursaat seemed to be familiar to the rest of the party: an old wizard called
Mabon
. As the group watched as a vision of
Mabon
played out, Poised Arrow saw the impact this revelation was having on his friends,
Waiting Sorrow
especially. Later, the group decided to return to Anise to discuss next steps.
Absolution
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Despite being vastly out of his depth regarding the matters of the arcane nature, Poised Arrow played a major role during the finale of the titan crisis. Once the theft of the
Scepter of Orr
became apparent, the lowland kodan found himself in a party trying to contain the emergency caused by
Livia
. When the
Commander
arrived on the scene, he stood alongside
Countess Anise
and arguing members of the
Astral Ward
—
Isgarren
, Livia and
Waiting Sorrow
. Althought they failed to stop Livia from using the Scepter in an attempt to open a pathway to
Bava Nisos
, they were among the first members of the
Tyrian Alliance
to enter the city as they pursued the human necromancer. Poised Arrow helped defeat the defending
titanspawn
and secure
Mantle's Arrival
for the incoming Tyrian forces. With the situation seemingly under control, he stayed behind with his allies as the Commander separated from the party to investigate an abandoned yet relatively recent
White Mantle
camp. After another heated argument between Livia and the rest of the allies regarding the Scepter, he voiced his aversion towards such tensions among friends to the Commander.
Poky
did not participate in the subsequent push into
Bava Nisos
. Instead, he returned behind the front lines in
Mistburned Barrens
with
Countess Anise
,
Isgarren
and
Waiting Sorrow
. Once the
Commander
joined them, they witnessed the last of
Mabon
's memories. Seeing how the Mursaat spared the
last titan
's life, Poised Arrow began to view Mabon as an honorable individual. Soon he was forced to enter the fray again, this time as a part of a reinforcements group that rescued Livia from a
titanspawn
ambush near the
titan
's heart. Without much time to rest they were challenged by another force. During the ensuing invasion of
shadow creatures
from the now-opened
Mists Gate
, he shielded Anise from a lethal blow and helped his allies hold their ground until the arrival of
Mabon
.
Once the
Commander
finished their preparations, the party was able to push into the
Foundry of Failed Creations
through the
Mists Gate
. A simple lowland kodan not too long ago, Poised Arrow now found himself in a different realm, visibly taken aback by the reality within the
Realm of Torment
. The party traversed through the realm in hopes of finding and vanquishing whatever remained of the three
White Mantle
members:
Judge
,
Tatyanna
and
Ulrich
, as without this
Saevus
couldn't be truly defeated. Ultimately, they were successful and managed to safely return back to
Tyria
. Their actions put an end to the titan crisis. Like many of his friends, Poised Arrow was last seen in the
Guild Initiative Headquarters
in
Lion's Arch
at a celebratory banquet.

Poyaqui
Race
Hylek
Rank
Legendary
Location
Western Ward
(
Lion's Arch
)
Services
Belcher's Bluff
Image(s)
Route to Poyaqui in the cave tavern.
Loading screen of Grandmaster of Om instance.
Interactive map
Poyaqui
is a
hylek
located at
Western Ward
who provides an introduction to the
Belcher's Bluff
activity. He will become accessible as an opponent only after defeating all five of the other Masters of Om. He possesses the signature move
Spirits Vision
, which automatically punishes your opponent's next
Bluff
used within three turns, and, upon his defeat, will become available for future Belcher's Bluff activities.
Location
Kryta
Lion's Arch
Western Ward
(Southeast of the waypoint, in the tavern set up in the underground cave)
Gendarran Fields
Grandmaster of Om
(Small instance outside of Vigil Keep with just Poyaqui in it)
Historical locations
Kryta
Gendarran Fields
Vigil Keep
Lion's Arch
Trader's Forum
Maguuma Jungle
Labyrinthine Cliffs
Bazaar Docks
Combat abilities
Effects
Belcher's Bluff
Walkthrough
Poyaqui has two modes on playing
Belcher's Bluff
. He will mimic your moves, when he doesn't have his buff of
Spirits Vision
. When he has this buff, he won't mimic your behavior. You can use this on your advantage. Bring
Critique
as your signature skill and use bluff do damage him initially as long as he is imitating you. Then use bluff until he gets spirits vision. As long as he has spirits vision use drink water and belch as he won't copy this. When he mimics you behavior again, use drink and bluff to damage him further and remove cooldown from your skills.
Behavior
Using the same exact moves as your choices.
His signature skill
Spirits Vision
will automatically punished your
Bluff
within three turn.
When he used his signature, he will stop copy your moves.
Player Strategy
Start with
Critique
then
Bluff
three times.
Keep using
Bluff
until he has
Spirits Vision
.
During his
Spirits Vision
, use
Belch
and
Water
.
By now your health will be more than him. When he mimics you move again, use
Drink
or
Belch
.
Dialogue
Lion's Arch
Does my
omnomberry
juice dispenser intrigue you? I'll give you a hint. It's for a game called Belcher's Bluff.
I've defeated all five Masters of Om!
So you have! Then, are you ready, initiate? Do you have what it takes to best the Grandmaster of Om?
Yes!
Not quite yet.
I challenge you!
Hah! You're a tadpole. Do you know the secrets of the Masters of Om? No? Seek them out, challenge them, defeat them. Only then will you be worthy to challenge me, the Grandmaster of Om.
Can you tell me about the other masters?
There. I've sent you a pamphlet that describes our order and locations.
Thanks.
Very well. I will return, Poyaqui!
What is Belcher's Bluff?
Belcher's Bluff is a game of determination and deceit. Drinking this vile substance is detrimental to your health. Drink as much as you can, but be careful: whoever loses their stamina first concedes the match.
What are we drinking?
Raw omnomberry juice, chilled to sour perfection. The omnomberry grows only in
Orr
and is disgusting until cooked. Heat removes the toxins, but chilling it- oh, it makes for an entertaining game.
Can I bluff?
To win, you'll have to! You can fake a sip, but if your opponent catches you... well, that's another challenge entirely.
They can punish me?
Let loose a fearsome belch, and you punish an opponent who's not drinking as they should! Just be careful- once you belch, you'll have to drink three more times before you can belch again.
Can I drink anything other than juice?
Water is allowed, but only every four turns. If you can get away with it, it can put you ahead of the game. Additionally, it counts towards your drinks and your ability to belch!
I get the gist.
I'll be back.
Grandmaster of Om
(instance)
Does my
omnomberry
juice dispenser intrigue you? I'll give you a hint. It's for a game called Belcher's Bluff.
It's on.
Can you tell me about the other masters?
There. I've sent you a pamphlet that describes our order and locations.
Thank you!
What is Belcher's Bluff?
Belcher's Bluff is a game of determination and deceit. Drinking this vile substance is detrimental to your health. Drink as much as you can, but be careful: whoever loses their stamina first concedes the match.
What are we drinking?
Raw omnomberry juice, chilled to sour perfection. The omnomberry grows only in
Orr
and is disgusting until cooked. Heat removes the toxins, but chilling it- oh, it makes for an entertaining game.
Can I bluff?
To win, you'll have to! You can fake a sip, but if your opponent catches you... well, that's another challenge entirely.
They can punish me?
Let loose a fearsome belch, and you punish an opponent who's not drinking as they should! Just be careful- once you belch, you'll have to drink three more times before you can belch again.
Can I drink anything other than juice?
Water is allowed, but only every four turns. If you can get away with it, it can put you ahead of the game. Additionally, it counts towards your drinks and your ability to belch!
I get the gist.
I'll be back.

Prime Hologram

80

Scarlet's Alliance
Prime Hologram
is an advanced
hologram
created by and modeled after the
sylvari
Scarlet Briar
, as a last line of defense for her and
the Breachmaker
.
Location
Historical location
Lion's Arch (Enemy Controlled)
Sanctum Harbor
The Breachmaker
Story involvement
Living World Season 1
Episode 5:
Battle for Lion's Arch
Battle on the Breachmaker
Event involvement
Historical event
[Group Event]
Defeat Scarlet's Prime Hologram before the defenses activate
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Laser Attacks
Large Area of Effect
Behavior
Separates into
Dynamic
,
Static
, and
Synergetic Holograms
at 25% health. Reforms once all holograms are defeated.
Effects
Prime Light Construct
- Weak to
Prime Light Attunement
.
Determined
(at 25% health)
- Determined to survive and immune to damage.
Ultraviolet Light Construct
(at 25% health and below)
- Only weak to
feedback
from other Ultraviolet Light Constructs.
Ultraviolet Resonance
(at 25% health and below)
- Unless removed, a massive attack will be unleashed.
Skills
Prime Blast
- A large AoE stomp at the center of the arena. It
launches
back targets.
Prime Light Beam
- Sends a beam of holographic energy in a direction, leaving an
area of effect
that
dazes
and deals light damage.
Historical version
Abilities
Laser Attacks
Large Area of Effect
Effects
Unshakable
- Gains
defiance
when targeted by
crowd-control skills
.
Blind
is 10% as effective.
Weakness
and
vulnerability
last 50% less time.
Defiant
- Defiant
Prime Light Construct
- Weak to
Prime Light Attunement
.
Behavior
The Prime Hologram could only be damaged by players with
Prime Light Attunement
, which is acquired after all three
Assault Knights
were defeated. When its health reached 25%, it splits into three constituent holograms, the
Dynamic Hologram
, the
Static Hologram
, and the
Synergetic Hologram
.
Skills
Prime Light Beam
A wide linear attack that reaches from the center of the arena to the edge. The initial damage is extremely high and will down nearly ever player instantly. After landing, the attack remains on the ground as a pulsing AoE that deals heavy damage and
Dazes
players who enter it. As the fight continues, multiple beams will be laid down at once.
Prime Blast
A large AoE stomp at the center of the arena. It deals heavy damage and will down most players immediately, in addition to
Launching
.
Notes
Historical issues
[
verification requested
]
The following bugs existed whilst this content was current:
Even if the scorch marks left behind by the Prime Light Beam had disappeared, it could still hit players.
The following anomalies existed whilst this content was current:
Sometimes the Prime Hologram performed a surprised beam attack at 75% and 50% health on a direction that had no AoE indicator.

======================================================================
PAGE: Prototype Arsenite
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Prototype_Arsenite
======================================================================

This article is about the enemy encountered in
The Battle for Lion's Arch
.  For its historical version, see

Dysapoptosis

Unnamed defiance skill
— When defiance is broken, the prototype's shield malfunctions, forcing it to land and invert its attack pattern.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Empowered
(challenge mode only)
- Outgoing direct damage increased by 5%. Stacks in intensity.
Ley-Woven Shielding
- An experimental mechanism is generating tightly woven strands of ley-line energy to form an impervious barrier that prevents all damage. It doesn't look entirely stable.
Malfunctioning Ley-Woven Shielding
(upon breaking defiance bar)
- The mechanism generating the shield appears to be malfunctioning. The next time the shield is lowered, this prototype will become exposed and stunned for several seconds.
Power Transfer
(when near a
Watchknight Triumvirate
)
- This prototype is receiving an energy transfer from another assault knight, enabling its more powerful attacks even while shielded.
Skills
Dysapoptosis
- Prototype Arsenite unleashes a wave of toxic sludge that kills within seconds upon contact.
Exhaust Plume
- The fiery exhaust from an assault knight's jetpack is dangerous. Deadly, even.
Pernicious Vortex
- Prototype Arsenite conjures inflowing miasmatic winds that concentrate their deadly fumes in a small radius around itself. Distant targets take less damage, while targets drawn too close will take high damage and become
exposed
.
Pernicious Vortex
(challenge mode only)
- Prototype Arsenite conjures inflowing miasmatic winds that concentrate their deadly fumes in a small radius around itself. Distant targets take less damage, while targets drawn too close will take high damage, become
exposed
, and
empower
the prototype.
Toxic Slice
- Prototype Arsenite sweeps the area in front of it with its greatsword, dealing minor damage.
Relapse
- Prototype Arsenite slices through enemies with a backswing that deals increased damage.
Rupture
- Prototype Arsenite places a symbol in front of itself and then shatters it with an upswing of its blade, dealing high damage in a small area. Every other time this attack is used, it fires a Noxious Vaporblade at the furthest non-fixated target.
Noxious Vapor Blade
- A swirling blade comprised of toxic gasses slowly tracks its target. After a short delay or upon striking the target, the blade reverses course at high speed, striking everything in its path. Drops Boiling Aether at the location from which it reversed course.
Boiling Aether
- Scalding aether from the Mists collects into a growing pool. Upon reaching maximum size, the damage increases significantly. Dissipates after a short time.
Boiling Aether
(challenge mode only)
- Scalding aether from the Mists collects into a growing pool. Upon reaching maximum size, the damage increases significantly. Lingers until cleared.
Noxious Return
- A swirling blade comprised of toxic gasses returns to Prototype Arsenite, striking everything in its path for high damage.

Tri-Bolt
-
Prototype Indigo
targets three enemies with lightning orbs, which follow their targets then strike an area after a short delay. Every other time this ability is used, one of the three targets will also be targeted by
Tribocharge
.
Tribocharge
-
Prototype Indigo
tethers a target to a location. Movement away from this location builds up a charge. After a delay, the target is teleported back to the tethered location and takes increased damage based on the distance teleported. This attack creates
Boiling Aether
at the target's location just before teleportation.
Boiling Aether
- Scalding aether from the Mists collects into a growing pool. Upon reaching maximum size, the damage increases significantly. Dissipates after a short time.

(challenge mode only)

Volatile Static
- Prototype Indigo targets the fixated player with chain lightning. Each time the lightning jumps to a new target, it deals damage proportional to the distance of the jump.
Public instance
:
The Battle for Lion's Arch

Unnamed defiance skill
— When defiance is broken, the prototype's shield malfunctions, forcing it to land and invert its attack pattern.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Empowered
(challenge mode only)
- Outgoing direct damage increased by 5%. Stacks in intensity.
Ley-Woven Shielding
- An experimental mechanism is generating tightly woven strands of ley-line energy to form an impervious barrier that prevents all damage. It doesn't look entirely stable.
Malfunctioning Ley-Woven Shielding
(upon breaking defiance bar)

Dual Horizon
- Prototype Vermilion swings its hammer in a circle, generating dangerous gravitational fields. Targets inside the ring receive
Tidal Torment
. Targets outside the ring receive
Ergo Shear
. Targets hit by the ring take high damage, receive a random effect, and are knocked in a random direction.
Gravitational Wave
(upon failure)
- Prototype Vermilion exposes its singularity core, causing ripples in the fabric of reality that damages everything in a large area.
Exhaust Plume
- The fiery exhaust from an assault knight's jetpack is dangerous. Deadly, even.
Mass Driver
- Prototype Vermilion strikes enemies before it with a wide swing of its hammer.
Kinetic Impact
- Prototype Vermilion strikes enemies with another swing of its hammer.
Gravity Hammer
- Prototype Vermilion accelerates its hammer into the ground with extreme force, causing moderate damage in a large area. Every other time this skill is used, it creates Boiling Aether at the attack's location.
Boiling Aether
- Scalding aether from the Mists collects into a growing pool. Upon reaching maximum size, the damage increases significantly. Dissipates after a short time.
Boiling Aether
(challenge mode only)
- Scalding aether from the Mists collects into a growing pool. Upon reaching maximum size, the damage increases significantly. Lingers until cleared.
Spaghettification
- Prototype Vermilion unleashes tidal forces that violently stretch enemies, tearing them into long, thin strands before obliterating them completely.

Pyroxis
Race
Destroyer
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
The Crown Pavilion
(
Kryta

Image(s)
Location of Boss Blitz bosses.
Pyroxis
was one of the most dangerous and driven of
humanity
's foes.
Countess Anise
has used
mesmer
magic
to enchant a
watchknight
to look like it, to fight in
Queen Jennah
's gladiatorial arena.
Pyroxis was originally from
Avernan Volatile
. Its fighting style was conjuring lava and flames, front and left.

======================================================================
PAGE: Qadim
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Qadim
======================================================================

This article is about the NPC in
Mythwright Gambit
.  For the NPC in
The Key of Ahdashim
, see
Qadim the Peerless
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Mythwright Gambit
and
The Key of Ahdashim
.
Qadim
Race
Djinn
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Mythwright Gambit
(
Crystal Desert
)
The Key of Ahdashim
(
Crystal Desert
)
Image(s)
Qadim dual wielding his
War Gavels
“
The djinn will rise, with me as their one true ruler—he who can craft life from the dregs of this world!
—
Qadim
Qadim
is a fire
djinn
rival of
Zommoros
and a collector of exotic specimens. He is the third and final boss as well as the main antagonist in the
Mythwright Gambit
and
The Key of Ahdashim
raids
. Many of his specimens have escaped him and can now be found in
Jahai Bluffs
, where the
Order of Shadows
has placed a
bounty
on them.
Biography
Early years
As a fire
djinn
, Qadim viewed himself superior to what he considered to be lesser races and advocated djinn supremacy. He collected various living beings from all around
Tyria
as curiosities to add to his menagerie within his lamp although it is not known if he did so for his own amusement or with the intention to show them off to someone. Qadim's collection did not grow old while in captivity for unknown reasons, thus denying them release via death.
Qadim and his water djinn rival
Zommoros
competed against one another by making many wagers over the centuries with Qadim winning all of them. Losing one particular wager against Qadim forced Zommoros out of
Elona
.
Mythwright Gambit
In 1331
AE
, Qadim learned of the existence of Zommoros's
Mystic Forge
and the
Mythwright Cauldron
within it via unknown means. Wishing to investigate it further and claim it for his own, the fire djinn challenged Zommoros to another wager in which their respective hand-picked champions would fight in three encounters. If Qadim's champions won, the fire djinn would get the Mystic Forge for himself; if Zommoros's champions won, however, the water djinn's debts to Qadim would be cleared. Zommoros accepted the wager and lured in would-be tourists whom he bound with a contract to fight in his name as raiders. To add even more stakes to the wager, Zommoros suggested that he would relinquish his ownership of the raiders to Qadim if he was defeated while Qadim would have to pay Zommoros everything the water djinn owed him if Zommoros won instead. Qadim accepted the added stakes, believing that he would win effortlessly.
However, Zommoros's chosen raiders surprised Qadim by defeating the fire djinn's three champions ranging from the menacing
Conjured Amalgamate
to the skilled
largos
twins
Kenut
and
Nikare
. The victorious water djinn confronted Qadim and demanded that his debt be cleared and that he also be paid what he had owed the fire djinn in return. Angered at being bested and realizing what the true power within the Mystic Forge was, Qadim revealed his plan to not only add Zommoros to his menagerie of exotic specimens but also take over the Mythwright Cauldron and use its power to create living, legendary creatures. Horrified, Zommoros revealed that he had deliberately built the Mystic Forge to conceal and protect the Mythwright Cauldron from falling into the wrong hands and warned Qadim not to attempt creating life with it as it could result in twisted horrors emerging.
Ignoring djinn laws and the water djinn's warning, Qadim breached the Mythwright Cauldron where Zommoros and the raiders confronted him in battle. Using his magic, Qadim imprisoned Zommoros in his lamp where the water djinn remained until the raiders broke him free. Upon escaping the fire djinn's lamp, Zommoros aided the raiders in their fight against Qadim with his magic and learned that the fire djinn's ultimate plan was to unleash the horrors created with the Cauldron to wipe out the "unworthy" races from the face of Tyria and become the supreme ruler of all djinn. Disgusted, Zommoros called Qadim mad for succumbing to lust for power.
After a fierce battle, Zommoros and the raiders prevailed over Qadim who fell into the Mythwright Cauldron. However, they witnessed the fire djinn emerging in a shadowy form soon after and gleefully fleeing from the realm while Qadim's lamp lost its power. Zommoros speculated that exposure to the Cauldron's magic had changed Qadim in unexpected ways although he could not say how.
Word of the clash between Zommoros and Qadim spread to
Jahai Bluffs
in Elona where the
Order of Shadows
placed
bounties
on dangerous specimens which had appeared in the region after escaping from Qadim's menagerie.
The Key of Ahdashim
Primary article:
Qadim the Peerless
Sometime after Qadim's escape from the Mystic Forge, he had grown in power due to his exposure to the Mythwright Cauldron's magic. Zommoros eventually received word that Qadim had entered the city of Ahdashim. All of Zommoros's attempts to communicate with the city were blocked, however, and he grew worried about his rival's intentions.
The water djinn decided to contact the raiders, who had helped him defeat Qadim before, via a letter. He urged them to investigate Ahdashim and keep it safe in his stead and to seek out the Key of Ahdashim and the four djinn cardinals for more information. Unbeknownst to Zommoros, however, the now former fire djinn had slain the cardinals of
Water
and
Fire
and corrupted the others, as well as dismantled the Key in order to gain access to the magic within the
Well of Ahdashim
so Qadim could gain control over all the djinn.
The raiders eventually managed to reassemble the Key of Ahdashim and defeated both the corrupted cardinals and the transformed Qadim the Peerless. Unlike his first defeat at the hands of the raiders, Qadim the Peerless' form was shown erupting into magic, presumably defeated for good. Instead of granting elemental magic to four cardinals like before or taking all the magic into herself, the Key decided to spread magic to all the surviving djinn throughout the
world
as she had come to realize that no djinn should lord over others lest they be corrupted like Qadim and the cardinals had been.

Qadim the Peerless
Race
Djinn
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Sovereign's Stadium
(
The Key of Ahdashim
)
Image(s)
Portrait
“
I emerged from the Forge with greater vision. The future will be what I deem it to be.
—
Qadim the Peerless
Qadim the Peerless
is the evolved form of
Qadim
after he was infused with the chaotic
magic
of the
Mythwright Cauldron
. Believing himself to have become a greater being, he intends to consume and twist the magic of the
Well of Ahdashim
to rule over all
djinn
and build a new world in his image.
Biography
Early years
Primary article:
Qadim
The Key of Ahdashim
Sometime after Qadim's
escape
from the
Mystic Forge
, he had grown in power due to his exposure to the
Mythwright Cauldron
's
magic
. His rival
Zommoros
eventually received word that Qadim had entered the city of Ahdashim. All of Zommoros's attempts to communicate with the city were blocked, however, and he grew worried about Qadim's intentions.
The water
djinn
decided to contact the raiders, who had helped him defeat Qadim before, via a letter. He urged them to investigate Ahdashim and keep it safe in his stead and to seek out the
Key of Ahdashim
and the four djinn
cardinals
for more information. Unbeknownst to Zommoros, however, the evolved Qadim had slain the cardinals of
Water
and
Fire
and corrupted the others, as well as dismantled the Key in order to gain access to the magic within the
Well of Ahdashim
so Qadim could gain control over all the djinn.
The raiders eventually managed to reassemble the Key of Ahdashim and defeated both the corrupted cardinals and the transformed Qadim the Peerless. Unlike his first defeat at the hands of the raiders, Qadim the Peerless's form was shown erupting into magic, presumably defeated for good. Instead of granting elemental magic to four cardinals like before or taking all the magic into herself, the Key decided to spread magic to all the surviving djinn throughout the
world
as she had come to realize that no djinn should lord over others lest they be corrupted like Qadim and the cardinals had been.

This article is about the Queen of the Kryptis.  For the axe, see
Labrys
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure

Queen Labris
Race
Demon
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Forced Hand
(
Nyedra, Dreamer's Sanctum
)
Eventide's March
(
Inner Nayos
)
Organization
Kryptis
“
You have no idea the commitment required to ensure the survival of our people. The strength of will. To do that which needs doing, no matter the cost. I have the strength.
—
Queen Labris
Queen Labris
, Mother of
Nayos
, is a powerful
Kryptis
noble and the mate of Midnight King
Eparch
. She also serves as the commander of Eparch's forces.
Information collected by the
Astral Ward
suggests that Labris and Eparch may be the aunt and uncle of
Cerus
,
Deimos
, and
Peitha
.
The Kryptis view Labris as a powerful and dangerous opponent, and they respect her.
Kryptis speculate whether Eparch becoming increasingly unpredictable and impatient has something to do with senility and that Labris may actually be ruling behind the scenes.
Biography
Early years

expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Secrets of the Obscure
Stub. Key elements of this npc article are incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
When
Cerus
was confronted by Peitha in the
Temple of Febe
and learned that she intended to betray the royal family and topple Eparch, he told her that they should have given her to Labris to eat.

R'tchikk
