R'tchikk
is a
skritt
and an
Astral Ward
scout. She is a close partner to
Gladium
and often helps in translating her physical gestures for clarity. The pair of scouts are first encountered by the Commander in the fourth chapter of the
Secrets of the Obscure
story-line,
Voices beyond the Veil
.
Biography
Early years
When R'tchikk was younger, she was caught in an asuran trap and experimented on, causing her to become more intelligent than other
skritt
. She left the skritt and ultimately found her place in the Astral Ward.
Secrets of the Obscure
Act II
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
The Commander first meets R'tchikk in the
Command Center
, where she introduces herself as a fan. She takes part in reclaiming the
Wizard's Tower
, as part of
Mabon
's group, and there asks the Commander to help locate dangerous artifacts.
Act III
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
R'tchikk and Gladium assist the Commander in investigating the
Bastion of the Obscure
, where they find an artifact showing a memory of Mabon and
Isgarren
. She later takes part in the battle to cleanse Isgarren atop the
World Spire
.
The Midnight King

.

Eparch
.

Dialogue
In
Outer Ring
,
The Wizard's Tower
during
Return to Secrets of the Obscure
Initial dialogue
R'tchikk:
Wayfinder
! R'tchikk is very glad you are helping. The
museum
lacks much, and many friends are busy across
Amnytas
. We three will put our heads together, like
skritt
, and make a good plan.
What did you have in mind?
R'tchikk:
R'tchikk and
Glade
have devised a three-part strategy. Part one: The wayfarer helps Amnytas, wizards, and new Mists friends. We get more shinies for museum.
That makes sense. It'll help to clear up any trouble. What else?
R'tchikk:
Part two: Wayfarer's deeds and relics become must—have display pieces. We make more shinies for museum. Part three: Grand reopening! Great celebration! After R'tchikk finishes all the cleaning...
It sounds like I have my part lined up. Good luck with the cleaning!
Gladium
:
(Gladium gives you a knowing look and flashes a thumbs-up. It seems like things are going according to plan.)
Good seeing both of you—I'll look forward to that reopening.
(awards
Returning to Horn of Maguuma
)
I hope things are less hectic here with the change in
Kryptis
leadership.
R'tchikk:
R'tchikk and Glade are busy always! Diplomats to take on tour, ward expeditions to visit Tyria... there is always more to do. But it is much nicer in times of peace.
That it is.
Good to see you, too. I have some shopping to do; be right back.
Subsequent dialogue

======================================================================
PAGE: Ramses
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Ramses
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Ramses
Race
Demon
Level
80
Rank
Normal
Champion
Legendary
Locations
Memory's Hollow
(
Inner Nayos
)
Inner Sanctorium
(
The Wizard's Tower
)
Organization
Kryptis
Ramses
is a
Kryptis
rebel and
Peitha
's closest ally. He has keen interest in
Tyria
and its people. While voicing his disdain for
Eparch
's suffocating rule, he displays knowledge of the
sylvari
.
Researcher Selly
mentions Ramses in her notes, stating that he is approachable and verbose, open about his ideas and opinions and charming, although physically unappealing.
Biography
Early years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
After they learnt about
Arina
and her squad of
Astral Ward
members being
stranded
in
Nayos
in 1336
AE
, Ramses and
Peitha
tracked them down to offer their assistance. Ramses stayed with the squad and helped them better acclimate to the Nayosian environment, while Peitha set her sights on the other person who had stumbled into Nayos alongside Arina's squad: the
Pact Commander
.
Secrets of the Obscure
Through the Veil
Ramses was supposed to be on guard duty when Peitha and the Pact Commander, who had earned the
Wayfinder
title as an ally of the Astral Ward,
arrived
in
Inner Nayos
later in the same year. Instead, he was occupied by an incoming assault of enemy
Kryptis
deeper in the
Astral Ward Camp
. Sensing fresh surge of emotions from the pair, Ramses was quick to notice that his master Peitha must have ventured to
Tyria
again. He informed the party that
Heitor
, Peitha's cousin, was responsible for the attack and leading the Kryptis forces in the region. During an introduction with the Wayfinder, Ramses's heightened senses once again made themselves known as he pointed out a "curious" smell of theirs. Later, during a private conversation Ramses explained another curious ability of Kryptis. He shared that the Wayfinder was a subject of many "dreams." He followed up with an explanation of that phenomenon, agreeing that it bore similarities to experiences of
sylvari
. At the same time, Ramses was clearly upset that the access to this ability was currently disrupted, for which he blamed
Eparch
, Midnight King of the Kryptis. Before ending the conversation with the Wayfinder, Ramses proclaimed his absolute faith in Peitha.
Once the Wayfinder was done with fieldwork in Inner Nayos, they were contacted by Ramses through telepathic means—a skill he has been taught by Peitha. He informed the Wayfinder that their actions had made an impact in the area and had left Heitor's soldiers "quaking," commending their work. Through his senses he managed to notice one more thing—a "disturbance, just out of sight"—and asked the Wayfinder to investigate it prior to returning to the camp. Upon arrival, an unusual Kryptis named
Irja
was found; not aggresive, instead cowering before the Wayfinder out of fear that they might be acting on Eparch's orders. Ramses then offered aid to the anxious Kryptis, providing comfort and encouraging her to interact with the members of the Astral Ward.
Later, Ramses was one of the individuals who joined an assault on
Heitor's Gate
, which resulted in the defeat of general
Ignaxious
, as well as a subsequent push deeper into the structure. At some point he was involved in scouting tasks, supposedly due to his extraordinary senses. He was able to reveal Heitor's presence inside, but not much else. Lack of proper knowledge of remaining enemy numbers prompted a decision to clear out the outside premises of any adversaries before heading inside. That delay has turned out to have terrible results. When the party finally made their way inside the structure, they were met with innumerable bodies—Astral Ward and Kryptis alike—and a scream of Irja, the friendly Kryptis encountered earlier. Unfortunately, she was killed by Heitor before the very eyes of the party as Heitor wanted to make a point about the fate that befell anyone opposing Eparch. A fight broke out in which Heitor was ultimately slain. Ramses remained by Irja's side as allied troops investigated the site in search for survivors and information.
The Realm of Dreams

Raul, Avatar of the All Seer
Race
Construct
,
Seer
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Temple of the Blessed Mantle
(
Eternity's Garden
)
Organization
Seer constructs
Raul, Avatar of the All Seer
is a projection of
the All Seer
. In life, Raul was the guard to the
doyen
Sidony
,
who they were extremely close with. They also practiced as an astrologer.
When the seers abandoned
Castora
to keep it from the
mursaat
, Raul stayed behind to watch over the island alongside several other seers, acting as the All Seer's avatar by offering their soul essence to protect the island.

's

and governs the
Bone Pits
in the
Underworld
. In 1330
AE

Hall of Judgment

Dhuum
like they had done
before
. However, the assault ended unexpectedly for the Reapers in the end, forcing the raiders to take their place to finish the ritual.

armor

armor
, this translates to -99.8%

damage
.

hitbox
, which is considered medium.

======================================================================
PAGE: Reeg, the Blighter
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Reeg,_the_Blighter
======================================================================

Reeg, the Blighter
Race
Titan
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Blightbringer's Court
(
Mount Balrior
)
Convergence: Mount Balrior
(
Mount Balrior
)
Image(s)
Interactive map
Reeg, the Blighter
is a
Greerling
titan
found alongside
Greer, the Blightbringer
and
Gree, the Bringer
in the
Mount Balrior
raid
and
convergence
.
Location
Janthir
Convergence: Mount Balrior
Mount Balrior
Blightbringer's Court
Combat abilities
Abilities
Poison
Area Control
Boon Corruption
Effects
5
Might
(30s)
- Increased outgoing
damage
; stacks intensity.
(Granted by
Ereg, the Enabler
during challenge mode, quickly stacks to 25)
Protection
(30s)
- Incoming
damage
decreased by 33%; stacks duration.
(Granted by
Gree, the Bringer
during challenge mode)
Behaviors
If there are non-players within Reeg's attacks range, Reeg uses
Empowering Blast
on
Greer
.
Flee every 25% of their health.
Skills
Aura of Corruption
- A pool of putrescence constantly seeps from the cracks in the Greerling's armor, damaging and poisoning anything that gets too close to it.
Blob of Blight
- Reeg targets up to 3 foes, preferring the most distant combatants. He slams his fist down, creating a damaging sphere that will then fire a blob of blight that follows the targeted enemy for 12 seconds, firing multiple smaller projectiles as it moves. If it hits a targeted foe, it will also create a stationary blob of blight that fires projectiles for several seconds.
Blob of Blight
- If one of Greer's blobs of blight hits a targeted player, it explodes and places a stationary blob of blight that will continue to fire projectiles for several seconds.
Enfeebling Miasma
- Reeg exhales lingering clouds of poisonous gas that travel outward to form 3 roughly conical areas. The gas clouds stay in place for several seconds, poisoning foes inside them.
Empowering Blast
- Fires a projectile of corrupted life energy at Greer. If it hits Greer, it grants him 2
empowered
stacks. If it hits a player, it will knock them back and continue on. If it is blocked by a player, the projectile is destroyed.
Eruption of Rot
- The Greerling places a stacking indicator on several players. If the indicated number of players are within the circle when time runs out, the
rot eruption
is stabilized and only does damage. If a player is within more than one stacking circle, they are instantly killed. If there are not enough players within the circle, a
rot eruption
occurs and the players are given a debuff of
Infectious Rot
and the Greerling gains 2 empowered stacks.
Rot Eruption
(Failed Green) - Rot walls burst up from the ground, knocking players back and creating a
noxious blight
at the eruption point. Players hit by a rot eruption take damage and gain poison, bleeding, and a stack of
infectious rot
.
Applies
Infectious Rot
, which lasts for 20 seconds or until the player is downed.
Noxious Blight
(Boon Corrupt AoE) - Noxious blights form persistent pools of corruption on the ground. Foes in a noxious blight take continuous damage, and their boons will be corrupted while they remain in the noxious blight.
Rain of Spores
- The Greerling targets 3 players, preferring the farthest away, and then fires a torrent of exploding spores along a line that moves towards the initial position of the target. The spores explode on impact, inflicting damage, poison, and confusion.
Scattering Sporeblast
- The Greerling rapidly fires exploding spores in a cone in front of it. Players in this cone will be directly targeted by the spores.
Stomp the Growth
- The Greerling stomps 3 times in quick succession. The first two stomps hit all targets in a circle around the impact point dealing damage, inflicting poison, and applying vulnerability. The third stomp creates a
wave of corruption
.
Wave of Corruption
- An expanding circular shockwave thats spreads outward. Players hit by the wave of corruption take damage, gain vulnerability, are knocked down, and have a number of their boons corrupted. This attack cannot be blocked, but players can avoid the effects by jumping over it.
Stolen skills
Consume Plasma
Throw Gunk
Throw Vine

======================================================================
PAGE: Riannoc
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Riannoc
======================================================================

Riannoc
Race
Sylvari
Level
30, 80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Secrets in the Earth
(
Omphalos Chamber
,
Lychcroft Mere
)
The First Vision
(
Lychcroft Mere
)
“
What good is power when it's afforded to you by an evil master? What good is an army when you're standing against love, honor, and loyalty?
—
Riannoc
Riannoc
(1302 to ≈1304/1305
AE
) was a
firstborn
sylvari
, and the first sylvari to die. He is described as being "valiant as the shining sun" by the
Pale Tree
. He was idealistic and direct, having no fear of death or even a real understanding of what fear is, due to
the Dream
being so young. Riannoc's
Wyld Hunt
was to face
Mazdak the Accursed
, for which he was given
Caladbolg
. He traveled north alone to face Mazdak despite the warnings of
Trahearne
, stating that Mazdak was too great a threat to leave unchallenged.
While in the north, Riannoc met a young
human
boy named
Waine
and took him as a squire. He sought to confront Mazdak and his army of
Risen
in
Lychcroft Mere
with Waine at his side. However, Waine was too scared to fight with Riannoc. When exhausted by Mazdak's overwhelming Risen, Waine took Caladbolg and left, leaving Riannoc defenseless when Mazdak himself finally showed up.
When Riannoc's death was discovered, it was felt deeply by the sylvari,
described by
Caithe
as being "like a part of the Dream was torn away". The Pale Tree says it was as though "the sun dimmed, and the Dream wept."
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 3: Justice for Riannoc

Living World Season 3

Knight of the Thorn
The First Vision

Combat abilities
Behavior
Has two skillsets, and can begin the encounter with either skillset.
Gains
Berserk
at 75% health.
Switches to whichever skillset he wasn't using before at 25% health.
Briefly gains
Determined
at 25% health, and gains
Quickness
for the remainder of the fight.
Effects
Skillset 1 (daytime)
Arcing Slice
-
Burst
.

fury
. Deal more damage to foes with low health.

-
Chain

Slash your foe.

-
Chain

Slice your foe.

- Slash at your foe. This attack restores endurance if it hits.

clone
at its first target and then
bounces
to enemies and allies, dealing less damage to foes per hit and granting
might
to allies.
Whirlwind Attack
- Whirl in a target's direction, slashing foes along your path.

Symbol
.

symbol
that damages foes while granting
resolution
to allies.

-
Daze
and
weaken
nearby enemies. Grant
fury
,
might
, and
swiftness
to yourself and nearby allies.

Arcing Slice
-
Burst
.
Deliver a circular attack to foes around you, and gain
fury
. Deal more damage to foes with low health.
Spatial Surge
- Shoot a beam at your foe. The farther away they are, the more damage you deal.
Hundred Blades
- Repeatedly strike multiple foes. The last strike does extra damage.

- Throw an illusionary blade that creates a
clone
at its first target and then
bounces
to enemies and allies, dealing less damage to foes per hit and granting

to allies.

======================================================================
PAGE: Risen Priestess of Lyssa
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Risen_Priestess_of_Lyssa
======================================================================

This article is about the
meta event
foe.  For the story foe, see
Risen Priestess of Lyssa (Ossuary of Unquiet Dead)
.
Risen Priestess of Lyssa
Race
Risen
Profession
Mesmer
Level
76, 78
Rank
Legendary
Location
Cathedral of Eternal Radiance
(
Malchor's Leap
)
“
My goddess was mere prey for the dragon. Despair!
—
Risen Priestess of Lyssa
The
Risen Priestess of Lyssa
is a powerful
Risen
mesmer
that controls the
Cathedral of Eternal Radiance
from the
Altar of Lies
. She was once the high priestess of
Lyssa
in
Orr
, but is now a servant of
Zhaitan
. She is protected by a magical barrier and cannot be fought until the cathedral's three outer seals are captured. Defeating her is the final objective of the
Cathedral of Eternal Radiance
meta event
, and a
Glorious Chest
will spawn upon her death.
Location
Ruins of Orr
Malchor's Leap
Cathedral of Eternal Radiance
Event involvement
[Group Event]
Kill the Corrupted High Priestess
(78)
Combat abilities

Chaos Bolt
Summons Illusions
Reflects Projectiles
Effects
Determined
(40s)
— Determined to survive and immune to damage.
Skills

- Projectile attack that
bounces
causing a random
condition
:
Bleeding
3s
Confusion
3s
Poisoned
3s
Burning
3s
Blinded
3s
Teleport
- Randomly
evades
and summons clones
Block
-
Reflects
attacks
Corrupted Gaze
grants
Determined
and
Dazes
targets facing her, summoning
Risen
when targets are dazed

- creates one of the following Illusions
Corrupted Illusion
Veteran Corrupted Illusion
Champion Corrupted Illusion
(new string)
[
sic
]

Altar of Lies
and summons clones of players that are teleported

Notes
Bug
:
Sometimes facing the Risen Priestess of Lyssa will continue to Daze you even if she's no longer channeling
Corrupted Gaze
.
The Risen Priestess of Lyssa herself isn't actually very dangerous, the real threat comes from the
Risen
and Corrupted Illusions she spawns.
If the event scales up enough for the Priestess to summon
Champion Corrupted Illusions
watch out, they're especially deadly.
If
Summoned Allies
or Ranger
pets
are facing the Priestess while she's channeling Corrupted Gaze they'll get
dazed
and cause additional
Risen
to spawn just like players do.
See also

======================================================================
PAGE: Rox
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Rox
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 1
,
Living World Season 2
,
Guild Wars 2: Heart of Thorns
,
Living World Season 3
,
Living World Season 4
, and
The Icebrood Saga
.
Rox
Race
Charr
Profession
Ranger
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Front Line
(
Domain of Kourna
)
Olmakhan Camp
(
Dragonfall
)
Dragon Response Mission: Bloodtide Coast
(
Bloodtide Coast
)
Hall of Monuments
(
Eye of the North
)
Organization
Blood Legion
Destiny's Edge (norn)
Dragon's Watch
Olmakhan
Pick Warband
Image(s)
Rox's appearance during
Living World Season 3
and first half of
Living World Season 4
.
Rox's appearance during and after the second half of
Living World Season 4
.
“
If I kill
Scarlet
, I'm in the warband. Definitely.
— Rox
Rox
, previously known as Rox Pickheart
and Rox Whetstone,
is a
charr
gladium
who works discreetly as a
free agent
for the
imperator
's office in the
Black Citadel
. A good friend of
Braham Eirsson
, she has lately become a member of the
Dragon's Watch
guild
. Rox has adopted the albino
devourer
Frostbite
as her pet.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
and
The Icebrood Saga
.
During the campaigns against the
lich
Palawa Joko
and the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
, Rox decides to leave Dragon's Watch and the
High Legions
and joins the
Olmakhan
tribe of charr.
Biography
Early years
Rox became a member of the
Pick Warband
following her leaving the
fahrar
, and she took the name Rox Pickheart. She and
Rytlock Brimstone
shared a past with Rytlock being fond of her although the exact nature of their relationship remains unclear. Rox and her fellow warbandmate
Nonus Pickaxe
were best friends since the fahrar and eventually became mates.
Rox worked with her
warband
on mining duties but lost them and her pet
devourer
Skewer
in a cave-in.
As a result of the accident she became a
gladium
and was expected to join a new
warband
to restore her position in the eyes of fellow charr.
Rox had already been unusually superstitious compared to other people, spitting on the ground for good luck, which Rytlock had noted as one of her many good luck charms since her childhood.
She became even more superstitious after the accident that had killed her warband as she survived on almost pure luck.
Living World Season 1
Rox being briefed by
Rytlock Brimstone
.
Flame and Frost
In early 1326
AE
, Rox began trials to join Rytlock's
Stone Warband
. She was provisionally named Rox
Whetstone
during this period. Her first assignment as Whetstone was to retake the
North Nolan Hatchery
from the
Molten Alliance
. There she rescued a baby albino
devourer
Frostbite
, who became her new animal companion despite her initial reluctance, and befriended the
Pact Commander
who helped her in her task. She later accompanied the Commander,
Braham Eirsson
and the
Vigil
on several raids into the
Molten Facilities
to free prisoners and dismantle the Molten Alliance's mining operations there.
Clockwork Chaos
Rox was part of the
official delegation
from the
Black Citadel
during the
Queen's Jubilee
and
tried to lift Braham's spirits
after the norn had been dumped by his ex girlfriend
Ottilia
. Rox came to the defense of
Divinity's Reach
when it was attacked by a
sylvari
terrorist who became known as
Scarlet Briar
. Later, she was given an assignment to
track and kill
the mysteriously empowered
Tequatl the Sunless
, and she brought back the beast's tail as proof of her kill.
Tower of Nightmares
Rox traveled to the
Tower of Nightmares
in late 1326
AE
with Braham, where she met
Kasmeer Meade
and
Marjory Delaqua
for the first time and helped end the schemes of the
Toxic Alliance
by bringing the polluting tower down. During this time the group learned that both the Molten and Toxic Alliances, as well as the
Aetherblades
whom Marjory and Kasmeer had been investigating earlier, were all working for Scarlet Briar for reasons that remained unclear at the time.
Battle for Lion's Arch
Following those events, Rox was part of the group that began tracking down Scarlet to end the threat which the mad sylvari's
multiracial alliance
posed to
Tyria
. Following her earlier successes, Rox was given one final task by Rytlock: she would be granted automatic membership into the
tribune
's warband if she killed Scarlet herself. However, Rox passed up her opportunity to do so during the
Battle for Lion's Arch
in 1327
AE
as she chose to tend to Braham whom Scarlet had injured during the
final confrontation
. Before her demise, Scarlet used her flagship,
The Breachmaker
, to successfully breach the
ley lines
underneath Lion's Arch and redirect their magic to the
Heart of Maguuma
to feed the
Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
, however, leaving the victory over her bittersweet at best.
Fearing Rytlock's wrath and expecting him to kill her for her failure, Rox gave up the name Whetstone and her chance to become a member of the Stone Warband for good. Although she avoided meeting with Rytlock in the following months, she nevertheless believed she had made the right choice putting the safety of her friends over any other ambitions even if it would have repercussions for her in the charr society in the long run.
Once again a gladium, Rox continued to pursue the new threat of the now rising Elder Dragon Mordremoth in the aftermath of Scarlet Briar's attack along with a group including the Pact Commander, Braham, Kasmeer, Marjory and the asuran prodigy
Taimi
. At some point during this time period she was recruited by
Imperator
Smodur the Unflinching
, or someone in the Imperator's office, to act as a free agent on a discreet mission which she kept hidden from her companions.
Living World Season 2
Gates of Maguuma
Rox grouped up again with Braham, Taimi, Marjory, Kasmeer and the Pact Commander to look into disturbances in the
Brisban Wildlands
which led them into the
Maguuma Wastes
and the
Zephyrite
airships that had crashed there. Following the information they had, she helped track down the
Master of Peace
and saved him from the rogue sylvari
Aerin
who was showing symptoms of madness similar to Scarlet.
Entanglement
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
The Dragon's Reach: Part 1
Smodur the Unflinching
and Rox witness Rytlock's
Foefire
cleansing ritual
attempt
in
Barradin's Vaults
.
Having become a
free agent
in charr society, Rox finally gathered enough courage to confront Rytlock to ask for his help in convincing
Imperator
Smodur the Unflinching
about the threat Mordremoth posed to charr and all of Tyria. She accompanied Rytlock, Smodur and the Commander to the
Old Duke's Estate
to try to end the
Foefire
curse with Rytlock's sword
Sohothin
and a reassembled magical crown and rid
Ascalon
of the
ghosts
plaguing it. The ritual had an unexpected twist, however, when Sohothin was swallowed by
the Mists
, forcing Rytlock to jump after the sword to reclaim it. Rox expressed worry over Rytlock but Smodur comforted her that Rytlock was not one to give up easily and would return to them eventually.
The Dragon's Reach: Part 2
Once the Commander's companions had each convinced their respective racial leaders to attend
the World Summit
at
the Grove
to discuss the threat Mordremoth posed, the summit was interrupted by Mordremoth's champion, the
Shadow of the Dragon
. The dragon's minions injured the
Pale Tree
while the dragon itself focused on the Commander who was separated from the others. After the Shadow of the Dragon had been driven off and the Pale Tree had been severely weakened due to her injuries, the Commander informed the group about the vision of an
egg
which was sought after by Mordremoth's minions.
Echoes of the Past
Realizing that the Master of Peace would know more about the mystery, the group decided to go track him down before the dragon minions would catch up to him. They entered
Camp Resolve
in search of the Master of Peace and witnessed the Pact making preparations to take the fight to Mordremoth before the Elder Dragon had time to strengthen its forces. The group met with various members of Destiny's Edge who were accompanying Marshal
Trahearne
in the preparations.
While in the camp, Rox was seen
talking
with the mysterious charr soldier
Monti Scythescrape
who she seemed to share a history with although both of them refused to elaborate on the matter and tried to direct the conversation elsewhere. When questioned by the Commander, Rox chose to lie rather than tell the truth although she accidentally let it slip that her mission apparently involved spying on someone whom she did not elaborate upon. The matter was quickly brushed aside by more pressing matters, however, as the threat of Mordremoth loomed ever closer.
Tangled Paths
The group eventually
tracked down
the Master of Peace in
the Silverwastes
and saw him being fatally injured by Mordremoth's minions. Caithe stole the egg from the dying man and fled before anyone could stop her, leaving the group confused. They learned that the egg was apparently important to Tyria's future and should not fall into the wrong hands as it belonged to
Glint
, a
dragon
prophet who had begun a plan to save Tyria from the consumption of the Elder Dragons.
Point of No Return
Following Caithe's trail, the group ultimately cornered her in a cave in the far Silverwastes. Caithe tried to justify her actions with the egg and revealed the shocking secret that sylvari had originally been intended to serve Mordremoth before the Pale Tree had shielded them from the dragon's influence. The confrontation ended abruptly, however, when the Shadow of the Dragon breached the cave in its hunt for the egg. Once again, the dragon champion separated the Commander from the others, intending to finish the Commander off once and for all. Rox provided help with torches which she occasionally tossed into the arena to remotely assist the trapped Commander who killed the beast with everyone's help.
Caithe had escapeed with the egg once again during the battle, however. Braham decided to hurry back to Camp Resolve before the Pact Fleet's launch to try to talk to
Destiny's Edge
about Caithe. However, the group was too late as the fleet had already launched with Destiny's Edge aboard it. The fleet was shot down from the sky by Mordremoth who had set up a trap for them, however, and several sylvari turned into Mordremoth's minions and betrayed their Pact comrades after hearing the dragon's persuasive call.
Heart of Thorns
Rox assisted the Pact Commander, Braham, Taimi, Kasmeer, Marjory and the sylvari
Canach
with entering the
Heart of Maguuma
after Mordremoth had torn the Pact fleet from the sky, and assisted tracking the whereabouts of Destiny’s Edge. She reunited with Rytlock Brimstone, who appeared from the Mists during a major
Mordrem
attack. Rox had several questions about the tribune's new
revenant
magic; however, Rytlock neglected to answer her inquiries. Rox stayed behind with a grieving Braham after Eir’s death from a Mordrem Vinetooth while the rest of the group pressed on deeper into the jungle.
Rox assisted the Commander in tracking the remaining members Destiny’s Edge to
Dragon's Stand
. After rescuing
Zojja
and
Logan Thackeray
from a
Mordrem Pod
, she protected them along with Kasmeer who used a portal to relocate the injured Destiny's Edge members to a safe location. Rox did not participate in the final battle with Mordremoth which resulted in the Elder Dragon's demise.
Living World Season 3
Out of the Shadows
Rox returned to
Hoelbrak
to
attend Eir's funeral
in 1329
AE
after locating Eir's dire wolf companion
Garm
in the Heart of Maguuma. The Pact Commander invited Rox to the new guild named
Dragon's Watch
to carry on the work of Destiny's Edge. Following the memorial, Rox left Hoelbrak with Garm to locate Braham in
Bitterfrost Frontier
.
A Crack in the Ice
The Commander eventually traveled to the frontier as well and
met with
Braham, Rox and Garm in an ancient cave which was said to hold a magical
jotun scroll
similar to the one used by
Asgeir Dragonrender
that had rendered a
tooth
from the Elder Dragon
Jormag
's mouth.
The group located an ice beast guarding the scroll which froze Rox in a block of ice during the battle. Braham got hold of the scroll with which he enchanted his mother's bow and helped the Commander kill the beast, after which Rox was freed from the ice. Witnessing a verbal clash between the Commander and Braham who was furious of Dragon's Watch being formed over continuing Destiny's Edge in honor of Eir, Rox decided to accompany Braham on his quest to kill Jormag to keep him safe.
Interim months
Following his success with the tooth, Braham made plans to rally the norn who celebrated him and take the fight to Jormag. During this time, he formed his own guild and named it
Destiny's Edge
to honor Eir's memory. Rox followed Braham to Hoelbrak and managed to convince him to instead take a scouting party north to assess the situation first before mobilizing a larger force, while the Commander was busy dealing with threats elsewhere.
Braham and his guild eventually tracked down and surrounded Jormag, only to witness the Elder Dragon suddenly roaring in anguish and returning to the ice much to their confusion. Braham suspected that the Commander had had something to do with this turn of events and wrote a letter to Taimi inquiring about the matter. The norn had put Braham under pressure to kill Jormag, and he felt he had to live up to Eir's legend and could no longer back down.
After Braham had failed to fulfill his promise of killing Jormag, some norn began calling him Braham Vowbreaker. Ashamed of his failure, Braham left the rest of his Destiny's Edge guild members behind in Bjora Marches and headed back to Hoelbrak with Garm and Rox to think on his next move, and Garm retired.
Living World Season 4
Daybreak
After losing their chance at Jormag, Destiny's Edge returned to Hoelbrak to think of their next course of action, and Garm retired. While there, Braham and Rox witnessed strange portals opening and never-before-seen
undead
pouring out to invade Hoelbrak. After beating back the probing assault, the pair decided to investigate the source of these
Awakened
and entered one of the portals, ending up in a strange asuran lab before taking another portal which led them in the middle of a would-be cave in which threatened to bury a group of people under a pile of rubble.
After Braham had used his guardian magic to save the group, the pair realized they had stumbled onto the Pact Commander, Rytlock, Canach and an unfamiliar face who introduced himself as
Spearmarshal Zaeim
. Braham and Rox learned that the Awakened were working for the undead king of
Elona
,
Palawa Joko
, and were seemingly pouring out of the ruins of
Fahranur, the First City
which the Commander's party was presently investigating. Taimi had been kidnapped by what appeared to be Joko himself, and the Commander was trying to save her before she would be harmed. Deciding to set his grudge against the Commander aside for the time being and focus on saving Taimi, Braham joined the Commander as did Rox.
The group eventually fought their way deeper into the city and encountered
Inquest
technology as well as asura who had been turned into Awakened servants of Joko. In the heart of the complex they discovered Taimi who was trapped within
Scruffy 2.0
which had been reprogrammed to not only suffocate her but also force the golem to fight against the Commander as part of Joko's elaborate revenge for being slighted by the Commander in their
earlier encounter
. While Braham and the Commander faced Scruffy in combat, the rest of the party held off Awakened reinforcements which rushed to the location.
After the battle was over and Taimi had been saved, Rox expressed how much she had missed her old friends and how happy she was to be reunited with them. She tried to soften Braham to the Commander although her efforts were in vain due to the norn still holding a grudge. She and Braham also explained what they had been up to, including the brief Awakened invasion attempt on Hoelbrak which had led them to Fahranur to begin with. Worried that Joko gaining Inquest tech would lead to more Awakened invasions of Tyria, the pair decided to stay in Elona and aid the Commander in further investigations about what Joko was actually planning.
A Bug in the System

Logan Thackeray
, Rytlock Brimstone,
Taimi
, the
Pact Commander
,
Zafirah
,
Caithe
, and Rox look on as
Aurene
becomes
an
Elder Dragon
.
The Icebrood Saga
When both Jormag and Primordus's respective armies began battling one another and invading territories throughout Central Tyria in 1333
AE
,
Ash Legion
's Imperator
Malice Swordshadow
and Blood Legion's new Imperator
Crecia Stoneglow
reached out
to the Olmakhan to request their assistance. Although the Olmakhan were suspicious of the idea of
United Legions
that the two imperators were proposing after a brutal civil war between the legions, as well as an atoning Flame Legion led by the new Imperator
Efram Greetsglory
joining as allies, the tribe decided to send Rox as their representative to hear the legions' plan. Crecia called the Pact Commander to attend the meeting on
Bloodtide Coast
as a neutral mediator.
However, the negotiations had to be put on hold when Jormag's
icebrood
invaded the coast in an effort to reach Lion's Arch from the south. Rox and the Commander barely had time to exchange words as they were soon called to join Crecia and Malice to assist the Lionguard in evacuating survivors and defending the
Stormbluff Beacon
lighthouse from the invaders.
During the
battle
, Crecia apologized for the unexpected turn of events. Rox took it in stride, however, reminding everyone that things tended not to go according to plan whenever the Commander got involved, so she had actually expected some sort of surprise. She also promised that, despite misigivings from the Council of Five, the Olmakhan would join the fight as saving Tyria—and the charr race as a whole after the civil war that had nearly cost them everything—was a greater cause than any single faction's needs.
The party converged around a nearby moored Lionguard ship after defeating
Tribune Splitwall
, the
Frost Legion
champion leading the invading icebrood. Rox voiced out what Boticca and the rest of the Olmakhan had suspected: that the campaign to unite the legions was Malice's version of the deposed Blood Legion Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
's
All-Legion Rally
to promote herself as a new candidate for
Khan-Ur
. Malice was quick to point out that she did not desire the title as she only cared about Ash politics and felt that spying on equals was much more interesting. Malice also complimented Rox on always being reliable much to the latter's delight.
While talking with the Commander, Rox revealed that her and Frostbite's life with the Olmakhan since Dragonfall had been peaceful and that she had heard about what had happened in Tyria since Kralkatorrik's defeat. She admitted to sometimes missing the action although she had come to prefer her new way of life. Rox revealed that she and Braham had been writing letters to one another a year ago and she had noticed that he had changed since their last meeting; she had wanted to give him space upon learning about his involvement with the
Spirits of the Wild
and Primordus. Rox also expressed hope that the Olmakhan and Efram's new Flame Legion would figure out their new relationship in the months to come as both factions shared the goal of saving Tyria and the charr.
Rox eventually departed from Bloodtide Coast and traveled to the
Eye of the North
to exchange ideas with other races' representatives and coordinate Olmakhan to assist Tyrians against the invading
destroyers
and icebrood. While conversing with the Commander, she admitted that ever since losing her mate Nonus, she had been fighting nonstop to distract from the pain and how joining the Olmakhan and learning their way of life had finally allowed her to heal. Rox also pointed out that the one good thing to come out of the charr civil war was new leadership as some of the old guard imperators had held the charr race back from changing with the rest of Tyria.
End of Dragons

.
Reason:
Expand biography.
After the events of End of Dragons, when the Commander and friends gather in the Dead End Bar, Rox tells the Commander that she and the Olmakhan are enjoying peacetime - and she has found a new partner, an Olmakhan lifebinder who is 'about the kindest person you'll ever meet'.

Notes
Rox was introduced with the
March 26th 2013 game update
.
Both Braham and Rox have unique facial features not available in
Total Makeover Kits
.
Trivia
Rox was originally
voiced
by
Rachel Robinson
. Since the release of
A Bug in the System
she is voiced by
Misty Lee
.
In original scripts for the ending of
Living World Season 1
, which could be found in the
gw2.dat
, it was revealed that Rox and
Rytlock
are half-siblings via their mother who, while dying, had made Rytlock promise to look after Rox.
As this is never referenced in content that has been intentionally released to players, it cannot be considered
canon
, but it undoubtedly informs all the interactions between Rox and Rytlock in Living World Seasons 1 and 2.

personal story
,

, and all
expansions
.
Rytlock Brimstone
Race
Charr
Profession
Warrior
Revenant
Rank
Legendary
Locations

(
Imperator's Core
)

(

)

(

)

)

)

Help stabilize the front line
Image(s)
Rytlock as a warrior.
“
Our strength will shake the foundations of the world.
—
Rytlock Brimstone
Rytlock Brimstone
is a
Blood Legion
Tribune
stationed in
Black Citadel
as part of its vanguard, and a member of the
Stone Warband
,
Destiny's Edge
and
Dragon's Watch
. He wields the legendary sword
Sohothin
.
In
Domain of Kourna
, Rytlock serves as a
renown heart
vendor.
Biography
Early years
As a cub, Rytlock was the youngest of the striplings whom the smallest cubs of his
fahrar
were called. He was often pejoratively called "Runtlock", and his parents left him to struggle with bullies on his own. Eventually Rytlock and the other striplings banded together with him as their leader and made the bullies stop by teaching them "a few lessons." Since that time, Rytlock could never tolerate bullies of any kind.
This band of striplings eventually formed the
Stone Warband
in the
Blood Legion
and was composed of about a dozen males and dozen females, with Rytlock selecting the surname Brimstone for himself.
While in the fahrar, Rytlock and his warband mate
Crecia Stoneglow
often spotted
ice elementals
coming down from
Shiverpeaks Pass
in
Grothmar Valley
.
Despite being of the Blood Legion, Rytlock also spent some time in the
Iron Legion
's territory in
Ascalon
as a cub. He used to explore around
Grendich Courthouse
, believing there to be treasure.
Rytlock got to know
Rox
of the
Pick Warband
from the time she was a cub, and learned of her superstitious beliefs and use of good luck charms and rituals.
Rytlock had several cubs over the years with different female charr, with the eldest being the son
Ryland Steelcatcher
whom he had sired with Crecia. He was not supposed to be a part of their lives but, unlike his own parents, he did visit the cubs occasionally to see how they were doing as he did not want them to suffer from bullying or bully other cubs.
At some point Blood
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
selected Rytlock and Crecia to infiltrate the
Flame Legion
and sabotage its operations from the inside. The pair carried out their mission with diligence even though Rytlock had to restrain himself from killing any Flame Legion member he encountered in the garrison where they were stationed in.
While on the mission, Crecia and Rytlock discovered that the garrison had received
Prince Rurik
's lost sword
Sohothin
which Flame Legion agents had discovered on the
Ring of Fire
islands and were intending to deliver to Flame Imperator
Gaheron Baelfire
. Crecia objected to Rytlock's plan to steal the sword as it would compromise their mission and put them both in danger; despite Crecia's warnings, however, Rytlock killed the guards protecting the sword and begged her to leave with him. Intent on keeping up her cover, Crecia stabbed Rytlock in the leg and sounded an alarm to make herself look less suspicious to the Flame Legion who chased after Rytlock. Despite the pursuit and suffering from the wound, Rytlock successfully returned to the
Blood Legion Homelands
.
Bangar was initially furious at Rytlock for blowing his cover and disobeying orders until he learned that Rytlock had stolen Sohothin. Deciding to use the magic sword as a propaganda tool against the Flame Legion, the Blood Imperator sent Rytlock and the Stone Warband on the front lines. Although Rytlock used the sword with diligence at first, his fame as a legendary Flame Legion killer and wielder of Sohothin gradually emboldened him and made him ignore his superiors' orders while he also grew distant from his warband. Bangar summoned Rytlock to the
Blood Citadel
where he voiced his displeasure and that he had considered executing the reckless warrior for the acts of defiance. Although Bangar justified his arguments with Rytlock's disobedience having needlessly endangered his fellow soldiers in battle, he also made it clear that he did not like Rytlock believing he was better than the Blood Imperator himself.
Instead of executing the warrior or making him a
gladium
as punishment, however, Bangar promoted Rytlock and sent him on "a tour of the legions" so the warrior's presence would "motivate" the other
High Legions
. As he delivered the punishment masked as a reward, Bangar enjoyed the thought of Rytlock, who did not hold a high opinion of the other legions at the time, being miserable abroad more than he enjoyed winning their verbal clash. For the longest time Rytlock considered this encounter with the Blood Imperator as his greatest failure. He would later learn that Crecia had only stabbed him to take suspicion off herself, and he continued reminiscing about her in the following years.
Female charr from the three High Legions were drawn to sleep with Rytlock not only because of his fame and Sohothin but also because of his looks.
Rytlock became interested in
human
history over the years and studied it for ways to end the
Foefire
curse, as well as Sohothin's significance as a weapon.
Edge of Destiny
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand lore with book's events.
Destiny's Edge
meets
Glint
.
During his tour of the High Legions, in 1319
AE
, Rytlock Brimstone was assigned to assist Iron Legion's
Centurion
Korrak Blacksnout
raiding party of three hundred soldiers in tracking down human caravans passing through the
Blazeridge Gap
. When questioned, Rytlock claimed he had left his warband and was working with the Iron Legion of his own accord, though Korrak criticized that Rytlock was around because his warband and legion couldn't stand Brimstone's arrogance.
Shortly after an argument, a human scouting party led by
Logan Thackeray
, ambushed the charr forces and forced a landslide to bury most of their forces alive.
With the death of Centurion Korrak Blacksnout at his own hands, Rytlock took charge of the remains of the group and pursued Logan's, trapping them in a dead-end. However, the two groups were quickly forced to team up when they were attacked by
Chiefling Ygor
and his
ogres
, with only Rytlock and Logan surviving at the end of the initial skirmish. Joined by the
Firstborn
sylvari
Caithe
, the three fled from more ogres led by Ygor's vengeful father
Chief Kronon
, and then
destroyers
to
Lion's Arch
.
Rytlock refused to tell Logan how he had acquired Sohothin which was a human relic. After destroying a building during the clash over the ownership of the sword, the pair and Caithe were thrown into jail by the
Lionguard
and were offered to fight for their freedom in a
gladiatorial arena
by
Captain Magnus Irondawn
. They fought as Edge of Steel, and were undefeated until a group called Dragonspawn's Destiny—which included the
norn
Eir Stegalkin
, the dire wolf
Garm
, and the
asura
Snaff
and
Zojja
—bested them. The victors commissioned the trio to join forces with them to help defeat the
Dragonspawn
, a
champion
of the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
.
While on the mission, Logan, who had begun bonding with Rytlock during their many fights together, apologized to Rytlock for his earlier actions and gave him his approval to wield Sohothin. Rytlock, who had been talking with Snaff about the strengths of brotherly bonds the night before, was taken aback by Logan's earnesty and admitted in his own way that he had begun viewing Logan as a blood brother. To signify the growing friendship and brotherhood between them, Rytlock gave Logan a Blood Legion pendant, symbolically making him an honorary member of the Stone Warband.
In return, Logan gave Rytlock a scarf recently gifted to him by
Queen Jennah
.
After the united group had defeated the Dragonspawn, the
guild
Destiny's Edge
was formed. They went onto many great accomplishments, until their battle with the Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
, which resulted in the deaths of both Snaff and
Glint
after Logan had left to defend
Ebonhawke
and
Queen Jennah
from the
Ogre Revolt
. The group parted ways on bad terms thereafter.
After the split of Destiny's Edge, Rytlock was stripped of his rank and assigned menial work for his perceived desertion of his post and treachery of siding with humans, but rose quickly through the ranks of the Blood Legion by challenging and killing his superiors that insulted him for loving humans and taking their position until he reached the rank of
Legionnaire
, promising to himself he'd rise to Centurion in the same way soon. He felt guilt over failing to both protect Snaff and kill Kralkatorrik, and blamed Logan for abandoning the guild in its time of need, as well as his own weakness for trusting a human, for the defeat. Since then, Rytlock began to hate Logan as the human he had once considered a brother in arms now represented Rytlock's greatest failure.
Ghosts of Ascalon
Rytlock led the Blood Legion in an assault on a Flame Legion holdout south of the
Loreclaw Expanse
in 1324
AE
and witnessed
Ember Doomforge
and
Gullik Oddsson
killing a shaman and destroying a
Flame Legion Effigy
. After the Flame Legion had been routed, the pair and their associates were brought in for questioning. Although Ember admitted to working for
Imperator
Malice Swordshadow
of the
Ash Legion
, she refused to tell the Blood Legion officers about the nature of her mission. Rytlock intervened to stop the interrogation as he would be dining with the Ash Imperator at the end of the week and could ask her about the mission in person.
The tribune listened to Ember's story about having found two human prisoners—
Dougal Keane
and
Riona Grady
—near the
Dragonbrand
and her intentions to transport them for questioning with her associates Gullik and
Kranxx
. One of the prisoners was missing much to Ember's dismay, however, making Kranxx explain how he and the prisoners had been attacked by a stray member of the Flame Legion and Riona had given pursuit. Upon learning that a human was on the loose, Rytlock ordered his troops to sweep the area for Riona who was eventually brought back bruised.
Rytlock insisted on the group accompanying his troops in their march until they reached
Ascalon Basin
where his forces set up a camp. The tribune called for Ember and told her that he would let her group depart on a supply skiff and advised her not to travel east where his forces were headed to. Before leaving, Rytlock mentioned Ember sharing her grandmother's eyes with a smile, implying that he may have been aware of the group actually working for Ember's grandmother,
General Almorra Soulkeeper
of the
Vigil
, on a discreet mission.
Personal story
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Missing story from charr Personal Story and dungeons.
Logan Thackeray
saves Rytlock in the
Citadel of Flame
.
By 1325
AE
, Rytlock had become the most renowned Blood Legion soldier in Ascalon. After Caithe made several disastrous attempts to reform Destiny's Edge in order to battle the Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
whose influence was spreading into
Central Tyria
, Rytlock and Logan had a chance encounter at the
Citadel of Flame
, where both commanded groups seeking to defeat
Gaheron Baelfire
and investigate rumors of a powerful weapon the Flame Legion had that the
Pact
could use in the campaign against the Elder Dragon. After Logan saved Rytlock's life and they
defeated Gaheron
with the help of the
Pact Commander
, relations grudgingly improved between the two of them. After helping Zojja
stop
Kudu
's plans in the
Crucible of Eternity
, they left for
the Ruined City of Arah
to confront Zhaitan. With the legendary guild reunited, they joined forces with the Pact Commander to
defeat
the Elder Dragon aboard the
airship
named
The Glory of Tyria
.
Living World Season 1
In 1326
AE
, Rytlock took part in a
defense quorum
to address the threat of the
Molten Alliance
. After this, he gave Rox several missions to see if she was worthy of joining the Stone Warband. The missions included hunting the dragon champion
Tequatl the Sunless
which had become more powerful since the defeat of Zhaitan and bringing back its tail, acting as a charr representative during the
Queen's Jubilee
, and slaying
Scarlet Briar
. Rox failed in her
final task
, saving her friends
Braham Eirsson
and
Marjory Delaqua
instead, causing Rytlock to dismiss her as a
free agent
. Despite this, the two continued having an amiable relationship.
During Rox's warband trials, Rytlock's activities included the investigation of the
Energy Probes
scattered throughout Central Tyria, and monitoring the threat posed by Scarlet.
Living World Season 2
Rytlock recoils as Sohothin disappears into
the Mists
.
In 1327
AE
, after years of research with the
Durmand Priory
, Rytlock came to believe he had found the correct combination of elements to reverse the Foefire curse. Prior to
the Exodus
, the
Six Gods
had given the Ascalonians two magical swords—Sohothin and
Magdaer
—and a magical
crown
. After gathering up the crown fragments which the Pact Commander had sought for him, Rytlock headed into
Barradin's Vaults
to
perform the ritual
, but failed, and the sword was lost to
the Mists
. Since it was impossible to dispel the
Ascalonian ghosts
without Sohothin, Rytlock had to quickly follow the sword into the portal, which closed up behind him.
Heart of Thorns
Act 1
Rytlock reappeared on Tyria from a mist portal as a
revenant
during the
Pact
's campaign against the Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
in the
Heart of Maguuma
. Using the new skills he had acquired in the Mists, he helped the Pact Commander's party to defeat an attack from Mordremoth's minions. Rox was interested in Rytlock's new powers, but the tribune did not divulge information on his experiences and instead urged the group to focus on finding the missing Destiny's Edge and ending the threat Mordremoth posed to the world.
The Commander decided to let Braham and Rytlock scout ahead for the missing members of Destiny's Edge. Rytlock used his new powers to locate Eir Stegalkin and witnessed Faolain stabbing her and leaving her to be killed by Mordremoth's Vinetooth minion. Rytlock helped the Commander and the group defeat the vinetooth and was forced to continue on before the other members of Destiny's Edge could meet the same fate.
Act 2
After the death of Eir, Rytlock and the group encountered Caithe and the newly corrupted Vinetooth Faolain. With the egg being dropped, Rytlock distracted Faolain while the Commander snatched the egg and fled. The group later split up again, and Rytlock left with one half of the group to track down the other members of Destiny's Edge. The group reunited with the Commander after the egg was safely placed in
Tarir, the Forgotten City
, but at the time the group had lost track of
Taimi
who had wandered off investigating an
Exalted
outpost which held a map depicting ley-line hubs, giving the group the location of Mordremoth as well as the lost asuran city
Rata Novus
.
Act 4
After tracking down and rescuing both Zojja and Logan, Rytlock helped the Commander take down the vinetooth Faolain. They found Trahearne who was trapped by the Jungle Dragon, and in order to defeat Mordremoth, the Commander needed to enter the mind of the dragon through Trahearne. Rytlock expressed his wish to not enter the mind of Mordremoth due to his prolonged experiences in the Mists and instead stayed behind to protect the bodies of those who entered. In the mind of Mordremoth, the dragon tried to demoralize and kill the Commander with a Blighted version of Rytlock whom the Commander successfully turned against the dragon. Rytlock later witnessed the death of both Trahearne and Mordremoth by the Commander's hands.
Living World Season 3
At Eir's memorial in 1329
AE
, Rytlock put forth the idea that Destiny's Edge was done and it was time to form a new guild. This led to the formation of
Dragon's Watch
. When Rytlock and the Commander stopped by
Rata Novus
at Taimi's request, he received a summons from the Black Citadel via
General Almorra Soulkeeper
, revealed to be the latest in a string of such summons, all of which he had crumpled up and ignored. The Commander sent him to get word to their other comrades, and he arrived in
Bloodstone Fen
with Marjory Delaqua in time to help with the attempt to "rescue" Caudecus from the White Mantle at their colosseum stronghold.
After learning that Primordus was active, Rytlock met with the Commander and Taimi in Rata Novus and participated in a simulation fight against Destroyers. Upon leaving the dragon lab, he was cornered by two members of the
Adamant Guard
who informed him that he had been stripped of his rank and charged with dereliction of duty, and that they were under orders to bring him back to the Citadel for questioning about his new revenant magic, by force if necessary. Rytlock complied peacefully, assuring the Commander that he would straighten out the situation. For the rest of the season, he was absent and only mentioned in passing.
Path of Fire
Act 1
Rytlock finds
Balthazar
and
Sohothin
in
the Mists
.
At some point, Rytlock managed to give the Black Citadel a report on his recent adventures and he was allowed to keep his title of Tribune. He also stopped wearing his revenant blindfold, as he "got used to the voices in his head." Rytlock and
Lady Kasmeer Meade
arrived in Elona before the Commander. Upon the Commander's arrival in the
Free City of Amnoon
, Rytlock still withheld his reasons for pursuing Balthazar.
Later, Dragon's Watch split up to learn more about the
Forged
. Rytlock, Canach, and the Commander invaded the main forged camp in the
Crystal Oasis
, though Balthazar was nowhere to be found. The group followed Agent Kito to the
Desert Highlands
, where they confronted Balthazar, who expressed that he would spare Rytlock. After the death of Vlast, Rytlock had no choice but to tell his story. When Rytlock found Sohothin in the Mists, its flame had been extinguished. Nearby, he saw a disheveled stranger in chains who reignited Sohothin's flame. As a favor, Rytlock used Sohothin to free the stranger from his chains, who then opened a portal that allowed Rytlock and the stranger to escape the Mists. It was only when Balthazar called Rytlock "friend" that he realized the stranger was Balthazar. Notwithstanding, Rytlock vowed to make things right.
Act 2
After listening to Vlast's recordings, Rytlock determined that the guild's next destination was Glint's Lair. There, Rytlock sensed the power of Glint, whose memory crystals reacted to Rytlock's presence. Rytlock was hesitant to allow the party to destroy the dragonsblood spear, Kralkatorrik's weakness, but ultimately allowed it to prevent Balthazar from getting it. Upon seeing the remains of Snaff's golem nearby, Rytlock blamed himself for his repeated failures in the loss of Glint, Snaff, and Eir Stegalkin. Nonetheless, Rytlock remained steadfast and followed the Commander back to the Mists.
Within the
Tomb of the Primeval Kings
, he was dismissive of the notion that the
Six
could help against Balthazar but still followed the Commander through the tomb where he expressed admiration for the size of the treasury. When within the mists, he appeared a distance away from the rest of the group and later threatened to tear apart the
Handmaidens of Kormir
when they appeared. When they arrived in
The Sanctum
, Rytlock was disrespectful of the library and
Kormir
herself. He, along with the rest of the group, witnessed Kormir's reenactments of Balthazar's imprisonment and
Abaddon
's demise along with the goddess of truth's departure.
Act 3
He was present for the revival of the Commander after they had been killed by Balthazar, and he expressed disbelief that they were alive. After the group decided on the next course of action, he, the Commander, Canach, and Kasmeer went to
Joko's Domain
and killed
Archon Iberu
. They used Kasmeer's mesmer magic to disguise themselvesa s Iberu and Awakened retinue to trick
Grand Vizier Utumishi
and the Awakened military leaders into gathering an army to confront Balthazar in the
Domain of Vabbi
. During which, he had a battle of wits with Canach, and was given the (albeit temporary) nickname of Cuddles.
Preceeding the battle with the Forged, Rytlock figured out Kormir's cryptic message for how to defeat Balthazar, and for the first time handed Sohothin over to someone else, hoping that the Pact Commander could use it to slay Balthazar once and for all before remaining with the Awakened army in fighting off the Forged.
Following Balthazar's death, Rytlock attended the party at Amnoon, where he pressured the Commander to give a speech and advised telling the crowd the unfortunate truth about Kralkatorrik becoming stronger from eating Balthazar's released magic, despite the celebrating atmosphere.
Living World Season 4
Daybreak
After Balthazar's
defeat
at
Kodash Bazaar
, Rytlock, Canach, and Taimi commandeered
Head Rancher Bedar
's dolyak ranch south of Amnoon to use as a base camp from which to search for Aurene and track Kralkatorrik. While Canach headed to Amnoon to collect the winnings he had made from betting that the Commander would defeat Balthazar, Taimi and Rytlock requested that the Commander meet them at the ranch to begin looking for Aurene. As the group spoke, however, a
Brandstorm
formed over the city of Amnoon during the conversation, with Rytlock noting that the Elder Crystal Dragon did not seem to be nearby.
Taimi replied that they would've been
Branded
if Kralkatorrik had flown over, and Rytlock sarcastically asked what could go wrong with giving an Elder Dragon Balthazar's magic. Heading into the city, the group saw citizens and refugees encased in
Branding crystals
to be transformed into minions of the dragon along with Aurene, now grown, battling with a
Branded wyvern
. Breaking open the crystals and freeing the civilians inside, Rytlock proceeded into the city, where Canach began to aid them in battling the Branded. Slaying the lieutenants of the dragon infused with
Zhaitan
's magical spectrum of death, the Commander and Rytlock
battled
a Branded
Deputy Qais
and following that, attempted to drive off the wyvern Aurene had been battling. With the wyvern eventually slain, the group approached Aurene, as she began to glow with magic and showed a vision to all present. The vision, depicting an army of Awakened within an ancient city attacking Tyrian forces, resulted in the Commander moving to Istan to try to find the First City,
Fahranur
, while the rest of
Dragon's Watch
present would clean up remaining Branded in Amnoon.
At some point, Rytlock learnt that the Commander had been
captured
by the
Mordant Crescent
, describing it as "[pulling] a Thackeray", and went with the
Sunspears
of Istan to help the Commander escape the
Mordant Crescent Great Hall
. Arriving after the Commander's escape, they told the gathered allies that the lich
Palawa Joko
had captured Taimi. Horrified by this news, Rytlock insisted that they go to the First City immediately to rescue the asura prodigy.
Once within the city, Rytlock noted that it had seen better days, with
Spearmarshal Zaeim
explaining the city's history. Moving forward, they encountered hostile Awakened which were quickly dispatched of quickly. As the Commander dived into the
Eternal Reservoir
with a pair of goggles, Canach asked Rytlock why he was never so spontaneous, earning a growl from the charr. After reaching a trap involving falling bells, they were soon deactivated and the group crossed the pits safely, into an area with Branded and Awakened battling each other. Canach noticed Taimi's hair bow on the ground, with Zaeim describing Taimi as ingenious for figuring out how to lay a trail there. Rytlock warned the Sunspear not to tell Taimi that.
Proceeding further into the city, they found a room full of
Inquest
asura
corpses, which soon were Awakened while Canach attempted to explode the gate to further in the city. As a colossal
Awakened abomination
battled the group, the ceiling caved in but was stopped by
Braham Eirsson
and his companion Rox, with Braham trying to argue with the Commander about Taimi being in danger now because of them. The Commander, however, cut the norn off, explaining that if Braham wanted to save Taimi, he would be coming with them. He begrudgingly agreed to this, and they proceeded further into the city, where they encountered a swarm of Awakened troops summoned by Joko trying to keep them away. The Commander and Braham headed into the chamber where Taimi was while the rest of the group, including Rytlock, stayed behind to battle the Awakened. Eventually, the Commander rescued Taimi and they regrouped, attempting to determine what to do next. Rytlock chose to contact Marshal Thackeray of the Pact and warn him of the impending threat of Joko.
Long Live the Lich
While the Commander was preparing the landing site for the invasion of
Gandara
, Rytlock met up with Rox and the
Olmakhan
and readied them for battle. The Charr group arrived after the Primeval Dynasty army, where Rytlock took point to the surprise of the Olmakhan.
Rytlock led the front while the Commander was helping and making new allies. The advance on the fortress was stalling as he needed high-grade explosives to bring down the bridge towers.
Faren
raided an enemy camp for the supplies, nearly dying in the process and needed to be rescued by Rox. He kept the Awakened of the back of the invasion force during the final fight in Gandara.
A Star to Guide Us
Rytlock helped reignite the extinguished sword Sohotin. Afterwards he enters the Mists to gather information on behalf of the commander. He was going to look for ways to reverse the damage done by Kralkatorrik.
