======================================================================
PAGE: Troopmarshal Olori Ogun
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Troopmarshal_Olori_Ogun
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
Troopmarshal Olori Ogun
Race
Awakened
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Eastern Front
(
Domain of Kourna
)
“
I can think of no greater honor than to follow the storied archon into battle.
—
Troopmarshal Olori Ogun
Troopmarshal Olori Ogun
is one of the
Awakened
generals in
Palawa Joko
's army. He is the commander of the Awakened infantry regiment.
Biography
Path of Fire
After
Awakening
, Olori Ogun served
Palawa Joko
faithfully as a troopmarshal in charge of the Kingdom of
Elona
's infantry who also oversaw the training of newly Awakened recruits. The arrival of the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
and its
Branded
horde and the invasion of the fallen
god
Balthazar
's
Forged
army in 1330
AE
depleted the numbers of the Awakened army who were waiting for Joko to return and issue orders to them.
The
Pact Commander
and members of
Dragon's Watch
, disguised as
Archon Iberu
and Awakened via a
mesmer
spell, arrived at the troopmarshal's camp to convince him to lend his infantry to thwart Balthazar's advance. Ogun had received news of the archon's plans from
Wurmmarshal Osa Ekolo
in advance and was willing to lend his forces to the cause. However, he pointed out that out that his newly Awakened troops were not combat ready yet, and so he could not leave his post for fear that the Forged base nearby would overrun and disrupt the supply lines he had been ordered to defend.
To aid the troopmarshal, the disguised Commander
assaulted
the Forged base in
the Foundry
with a small squad of Awakened troops and destroyed the portal within that Balthazar had been using to send in Forged reinforcements. After the Forged threat had been neutralized, Ogun honored the deal and pledged his infantry to the archon for the defense of
Kodash Bazaar
where Balthazar's army was headed to confront Kralkatorrik.
Ogun met with
Beastmarshal Oluwa Eranko
and Wurmmarshal Osa Ekolo at the western gate of Kodash. The marshals dispatched soldiers to the other three gates as well, but chose to remain in their present location themselves to wait for the archon. The Commander, still under disguise, went to the marshals and issued orders to them. Ogun's troops were instructed to disrupt Forged infantry.
Balthazar arrived soon after with his army headed by a giant machine called the
Warbeast
. The marshals
faced the Forged
in combat, each one commanding their forces valiantly. However, they were unable to stop the Warbeast and some of its escorts from invading Kodash, forcing the Commander to give chase to the war machine before it could kill Kralkatorrik.
After Balthazar had been slain and the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
had ended in the Awakened army's favor, news eventually spread about the disguised archon's real identity being the Pact Commander. Despite realizing that they had been tricked by an outlander, the marshals decided to wait for Joko's return and oblige should he demand retribution for the Commander's actions.
Living World Season 4
Palawa Joko returned after Balthazar's demise and planned to unleash an improved
Scarab Plague
on
Central Tyria
to have his revenge on the Pact Commander for leaving him behind in the
Domain of the Lost
. The lich king bred scarabs in the
Domain of Kourna
and ordered Olori Ogun to travel to
Gandara, the Moon Fortress
along with Beastmarshal Oluwa Eranko and prepare their cavalry and infantry to face the eventual invasion of the Pact Commander's allied forces who would be trying to stop the release of the improved plague. Ogun remained unaware of the Commander's presence in the area, however, when the Commander used
Blish
's technology to stay unseen during the allied forces'
scouting
of the outskirts of Gandara in 1331
AE
.
By the time the Commander's allies assaulted Gandara, Ogun did not lead the defense outside. Instead he had been subjected to a dose of
ley energy
which turned him more aggressive and unstable.
Ogun roamed the
Eastern Front
, still issuing orders to nearby troops and assaulting anyone who came nearby, and the
Order of Shadows
at the
Allied Encampment
placed a
bounty
on his head. Ogun ultimately came to blows with bounty hunters and was felled. He was succeeded as troopmarshal by his second-in-command
Pelu Egan
.

For bounty map:

In the
Yoruba language
"olori" means "leader" and "ogun" means "soldier", which corresponds with his role. The other two Awakened marshals also have names that translate from Yoruba related to their roles.

PAGE: Uenno
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Uenno

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
Uenno
Race
Asura
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Wizard's Ascent
(
Skywatch Archipelago
)
Outer Sanctorium
(
The Wizard's Tower
)
Bastion of Knowledge
(
Amnytas
)
Organization
Astral Ward
“
I didn't leave the colleges for more incompetency.
—
Uenno
Uenno
, the Historian of the Ward,
is a high-ranking
asuran
member of the
Astral Ward
and one of its oldest mortal members. She was the one who introduced
Zojja
to the Ward after they met in
Kryta
. Uenno helps attune the Commander's communication device to arcane fluctuations so the Commander and the Astral Ward can keep in touch remotely.
Biography
Early years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Uenno was an esteemed
asuran
professor in the
College of Synergetics
, but got tired of the bureaucracy after years of frustration with the system and went on a long sabbatical to explore the world beyond the stuffy labs and debate halls of
Rata Sum
. She lived along the outskirts of
Divinity's Reach
for some time, teaching statics to the locals. She noticed
the Wizard's Tower
while visiting
Garenhoff
, and
Mabon
, a
mursaat
member of the
Wizard's Court
, had noticed her curiosity and approached her before recruiting her. To this day, Uenno still collects stipends from the
Arcane Council
, as they think she is in a jungle somewhere, studying plants.
Uenno accompanied Lyhr on an expedition to the
Twin Serpent Mountains
in the
Underworld
.
At some point, Uenno and the wizard
Isgarren
met
Frode
, a broken
norn shaman
of
Wurm
, and took him into
Amnytas
and the
Astral Ward
.
While traveling in
Kryta
many years later as a senior member of the Ward, Uenno met another asura,
Zojja
, in a tavern. Eventually, Uenno had asked Zojja if she needed a ride wherever she was traveling; Zojja agreed, as at that point in life she had nothing better to do. Together, the two asura traveled to Garenhoff where Uenno was supposed to meet with an old friend. While waiting for Uenno, Zojja was admiring the Wizard's Tower. Later, the pair traveled to the
Black Citadel
after visiting the southern
Shiverpeaks
, where Uenno, as in all other places they had stopped at, had friends she needed to visit, being increasingly suspicious to Zojja. Their final stop was just outside
Smokestead
, where Uenno introduced Zojja to Mabon while also giving her a lecture about personal security, and they took Zojja to Amnytas.
Secrets of the Obscure
Act 1
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
After the initial Kryptis assault on the Beacon of Ages had been pushed back and the Commander had been successfully pulled out of Nayos, Frode introduced the Commander to Uenno at the local command center on
Skywatch Archipelago
. They discussed their plan to confront and cleanse the Kryptis-possessed wizards, starting with Dagda. As the Ward worked to pinpoint Dagda's location, Uenno tweaked the Commander's communications device to pick up astral fluctuations so the Commander and Ward could stay in touch remotely and call upon one another for aid when needed.
Act 2
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
Act 3
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information.
The Midnight King

.

Ultraviolet Hologram
Race
Hologram
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Battle on the Breachmaker
(
The Breachmaker
)
Organization
Scarlet's Alliance
Ultraviolet Hologram
was a third and final form of the
Prime Hologram
, the principle boss for the
Battle for Lion's Arch living story
. After getting the Prime Hologram down to 25% hp, it will split into the
Dynamic Hologram
, the
Static Hologram
, and the
Synergetic Hologram
. Once they are defeated, they reform into the Ultraviolet Hologram. The Ultraviolet Hologram cannot be injured directly, and can only be damaged by killing
Microprime Holograms
.
Location
Historical location
Lion's Arch (Enemy Controlled)
Sanctum Harbor
The Breachmaker
Story involvement
Living World Season 1
Episode 5:
Battle for Lion's Arch
Battle on the Breachmaker
Event involvement
Historical event
[Group Event]
Defeat Scarlet's Prime Hologram before the defenses activate
(80)
Combat abilities
Abilities
Laser Attacks
Large Area of Effect
Effects
Unshakable
- Gains
defiance
when targeted by
crowd-control skills
.
Blind
is 10% as effective.
Weakness
and
vulnerability
last 50% less time.
Defiant
- Defiant
Ultraviolet Light Construct
- Only weak to
feedback
from other Ultraviolet Light Constructs.
Ultraviolet Resonance
- Unless removed, a massive attack will be unleashed.
Skills
Prime Light Beam
A wide linear attack that reaches from the center of the arena to the edge in four directions. The initial damage is extremely high and will down nearly ever player instantly. After landing, the attack remains on the ground as a pulsing AoE that deals heavy damage and
Dazes
players who enter it.
Ultraviolet Blast
Used if
Microprime Holograms
are not killed fast enough. It targets every player in the area and deals massive damage.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ura
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Ura
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Ura
Race
Titan
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Convergence: Mount Balrior
(
Mount Balrior
)
Steamshrieker's Court
(
Mount Balrior
)
“
We are all godspawn, but I am Ura. And I am far stronger than my stock.
—
Ura
Ura, the Steamshrieker
, or
Godscream Ura
, is a
titan
imperatrix who is respected by both
Decima, the Stormsinger
and
Greer, the Blightbringer
. She is the third and final boss of the
Mount Balrior
raid
. Ura's spheres of influence are geysers, rage, and boiling steam.
She claims to be far stronger than her stock. She is willing to reason with her enemies and is able to command her weaker godspawn brethren to obey her.
Biography
Early years
Ura and her fellow
titans
Greer, the Blightbringer
and
Decima, the Stormsinger
traveled from the
Foundry of Failed Creations
in the
Realm of Torment
to
Tyria
through a
Mists
portal which was located deep within the
mursaat
city of
Bava Nisos
. Once the
magical
wards around the ravaged city vanished following the mursaat
wizard
Mabon
's
death
in 1336
AE
, the titans were free to spread out from the city.
The trio traveled south to
Janthir Syntri
and began spreading their corruption across the island. Ura chose
Mount Balrior
as her base of operations; unlike the other titans who spread their titanspawn around Janthir, Ura chose to keep her spawn mostly confined to the mountain. In the ensuing months, the titans began collecting and feeding on local
bloodstone
shards to increase their strength and regenerate. Their power surged during unusual storms known as
Mists Tides
when it was safer for them to feed on the shards.
Janthir Wilds
Act 2
In 1337
AE
, members from both the
Lowland Kodan
and the
Tyrian Alliance
began taking a foothold on Janthir Syntri to combat the titans' spreading corruption that was also manifesting as the troubling
Mistburn
phenomenon infecting local flora and fauna. While their allies were busy preventing Decima and Greer's continued drainings of the island's remaining bloodstone shards to empower themselves, the
Pact Commander
's party decided to use the knowledge gained from their bloodstone shard experimentation to neutralize many of the shards with the
Heart of the Obscure
and
defeat the titan pair
at the same time in their lair.
Before the party could deal the finishing blow against the weakened pair, however, they were forced to stand down when Ura made herself known via both commanding the titans to retreat to her and raising a steam wall between the fleeing titans and the pursuers. She addressed the Commander, claiming to be more powerful than the two titans, and offered the party a chance to retreat as long they let the titan pair go as she believed the Commander would listen to reason and not risk the exhausted party's lives in battle. After the Commander reluctantly backed down, Ura kept her end of the bargain and allowed the party to flee, while the heavily injured Greer and Decima retreated to her side at
Mount Balrior
.
In a discussion that followed in the Wizard's Tower, the wizard lord
Isgarren
was surprised to learn of Ura's involvement while identifying her as the titan imperatrix.
Godspawn
Ura stayed on
Mount Balrior
with most of her titanspawn, drawing power from
bloodstone
shards there, while the gravely injured Greer and Decima used the time to heal themselves. The titan trio stayed separated in their designated areas around the mountain, using bursts of hot air and energy to emit stormlike tones which both allowed them to communicate across great distances and prevent the Tyrian Alliance and the lowlanders from figuring out the content of their messages.
Assisted by the
Durmand Priory
member
Farrah Baelseeker
's research where they
fought
against one of Ura's greater titanspawn and observed its fluctuating strength based on local weather phenomena, the Commander's party discovered how the
Mists Tides
' presence strengthened titans and allowed them to regenerate whereas the lack of such storms made them more vulnerable.
Realizing that an upcoming lull in storms would give them a chance to corner the titans and permanently kill them, the party began
ascending the mountain
a few days later, now also aided by the
jotun
wizard
Dagda
, Stoic Alder's spear master
Barbed Vale
, as well as
Scholar Glenna
on the sidelines. However, a sudden and unexpected storm moved towards the mountain at that time, blocking the further reinforcements from crossing the bay and forcing the assembled party to continue the trek by themselves.
The lengthy climb up the mountain, and back-to-back battles against the weakened but still dangerous Greer and Decima as well as the three titans' numerous spawn exhausted most members of the party, however, leaving only the Commander, Dagda, and Waiting Sorrow to continue onward until they reached the apex of the mountain. As the Commander touched a suspiciously unguarded and large bloodstone shard, Ura finally appeared in person. The titan imperatrix smelled Greer and Decima's corpses on the trio's garb, and became enraged that the Tyrians had not stayed away after their earlier parley. Using the
Heart of the Obscure
, the Commander neutralized the bloodstone shard and plugged the emerging geysers with the drained fragments while the trio slowly whittled down the titan's strength. Ura threatened to not only drag the trio to the
Foundry of Failed Creations
to build her fallen kin anew but to also pay
Harvest Den
a visit for this slight and avenge her kin by spilling lowlander blood. Her boasts only strengthened the trio's resolve to end her threat, however.
Unlike Greer and Decima who had panicked in their final moments, Ura remained bemused even as she drew her last breath. The titan imperatrix remarked that the trio's triumph would be short-lived because the door the titans had come through still remained open, leaving her opponents to ponder the implications of her foreboding words. The alliance would later learn that Ura had referred to a particular gateway deep within the city of
Bava Nisos
which had once been populated by the
mursaat
.

Janthir

In the raid Ura has ?
armor
(1374 toughness and ? defense).
In the raid Ura has a 1,000 range diameter hitbox, which is considered large.
For the
Undefeated: Mount Balrior
achievement,

must be disabled.
Trivia
Ura is
voiced
by
Julie Nathanson
.
Ura likes her fellow godspawn, being worshipped, and ascendancy.
Ura may be inspired by
Urash
, the Mesopotamian goddess regarded as the personification of the earth who was the mother of many other Mesopotamian deities.
Ura may also be inspired by
Uranus
, the primordial deity who fathered the
Titans
in ancient Greek mythology.

Vale Guardian
Race
Elemental
Mech. race
Plant
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Spirit Vale
(
Forsaken Thicket
)
Image(s)
Click to enlarge.
Interactive map
“
Pylons. Did they power those beings...or create them?
—
Spirit Vale#Dialogue
The
Vale Guardian
is the first boss in the
Spirit Vale
raid
wing. It is an
elemental
of mysterious origin, either powered or summoned by the pylons in the Vale.
Location
Heart of Maguuma
Forsaken Thicket
Spirit Vale
Mysterious Pylons
Walkthrough
Primary article:
Spirit Vale#Vale Guardian
Combat abilities
Behavior
Vale Division
- At 66% and 33% health remaining, Vale Guardian will become invulnerable, move to the center of the arena, and disappear, spawning a
Red
,
Green
, and
Blue
Guardian at their respective colored pillars. During this split phase, the Vale Guardian is not present in the arena and will only return once all three colored guardians are defeated. Each of these lesser guardians has one of the signature abilities of the Vale Guardian, and all besides the Green Guardian have other special mechanics.
The Red Guardian summons
Seekers
and only takes heavy damage from conditions.
The Green Guardian uses
Unstable Magic Spike
(blue teleport circles)
The Blue Guardian uses
Distributed Magic
, however only 3 players are needed to nullify it and can gain
Blue Pylon Power
which needs to be stripped (boon removal).
At the beginning of this phase, players will become attuned to the pylon color of the third of the platform they are standing on (
Pylon Attunement: Red
,
Pylon Attunement: Green
, or
Pylon Attunement: Blue
). Players will take heavy periodic damage if they are standing in the area of a color they are not attuned to. Pylon Attunement effects are removed once the Guardian Split phase ends.
An additional segment of the floor lights up (dealing periodic damage) in rotation after splitting and rejoining.
Gains Magic Storm skill after first split.
Effects
(unlocked when casting
Magic Storm
)
Magic Aura
- Deals periodic damage to all enemies within 1500 units.
Skills
Bullet Storm
- The
Vale Guardian
sends slow-moving projectiles at you that deal damage on hit.
Casts multiple, slow moving, white orbs causing moderate damage. This ability is only present when the Vale Guardian is split into the three minor guardians.
Distributed Magic
- The
Vale Guardian
distributes its magic to a point that must be absorbed before it unleashes devastating damage.
Vale Guardian summons a green circle which slowly 'closes', which if insufficient players are present inside, deals devastating damage to the entire player group. At least 4 players who are not
downed
or
defeated
need to be in the circle before it closes to avoid the massive group damage. These players will be dealt a moderate amount of damage when the skill ends. Distributed Magic appears in the third of the arena the Vale Guardian was in when it casted it.
Magic Aura
- The
Vale Guardian
pulses energy every 3 seconds, damaging all foes in range.
A passive damaging aura effect present throughout the Vale Guardian fight.
Magic Storm
- Magic orbs erupt from the
Vale Guardian
, releasing energy and damaging targets in a small area around their impact point.
An ability which casts multiple high-damage aoes on the ground. Casting this ability also grants Vale Guardian a breakbar. Attack will automatically end after 30 seconds. Attack has a 30 second cooldown.
Punch
- Punches foes.
Spawn Seekers
-
No skill description found.
At regular intervals, the Vale Guardian will spawn
Seekers
in the arena.
Unstable Magic Spike
- The
Vale Guardian
creates unstable spikes of magic, teleporting enemies caught in the impact.
Vale Guardian casts electric-like blue AoEs on the ground, teleporting and damaging players caught inside them.
Unstable Pylon
- Vale Guardian renders the Blue portion of the ground unstable, damaging players standing on these portions periodically.
Unstable Pylon
- Vale Guardian renders the Green portion of the ground unstable, damaging players standing on these portions periodically.
Unstable Pylon
- Vale Guardian renders the red portion of the ground unstable, damaging players standing on these portions periodically.

Notes
Vale Guardian has 1910
armor
(687 toughness and 1223 defense).
Compared to regular
Raid
bosses with 2597
armor
, this translates to +36%
power
damage
.
Vale Guardian has a 140 range diameter hitbox, which is considered medium.
Though its race in lore is an elemental, mechanically it is a
plant
. This was an intentional choice by the developers for most raid bosses so no sigil/potion would provide a race bonus against them.
Blue teleport circles can be blocked with
Aegis
.

.
Varinia Stormsounder
Race
Charr
,
Icebrood
Rank
Elite
Legendary
Locations
Sacnoth Stream
(
Grothmar Valley
)
Cold War
(
Drizzlewood Coast
)
Organization
Frost Legion
Dominion
Blood Legion
Image(s)
Icebrood Varinia Stormsounder.
Interactive map
“
With Bangar's true leadership and vision, the charr have evolved!
—
Varinia Stormsounder
Varinia Stormsounder
is the Minister of Morale for the
Blood Legion
. Hers is the voice that can be heard
announcing the status
of
meta events
throughout
Grothmar Valley
. She spends much of her time during the
All-Legion Rally
micromanaging the
Metal Legion
concert
, from attempting to secure high-profile
charr
guests such as
General Almorra Soulkeeper
, to approving which songs the band may sing and even dictating lyrics in order to promote
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
's ideals.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
.
During the
Icebrood War
between the
United Legions
and the
Dominion
in
Drizzlewood Coast
Varinia begins spreading propaganda via speakers to demoralize the United Legions and goad them to join Bangar's Dominion. She later undergoes a transformation into a powerful
icebrood
and
fights
in the ranks of the newly created
Frost Legion
, falling in combat while stalling the
Pact Commander
's group from pursuing
Ryland Steelcatcher
deeper north.
Biography
Prologue: Bound by Blood
Acting as the Minister of Morale for the
Blood Legion
, Varinia Stormsounder did her part to
promote
Imperator
Bangar Ruinbringer
's ideals with her propaganda messages that could be heard throughout
Grothmar Valley
during the
All-Legion Rally
in 1332
AE
. She also got
involved
with the
Metal Legion
concert, approving which songs the band may sing and even dictating their lyrics in order to promote Bangar's message of
charr
unity and Blood Legion superiority.
Even after the concert turned out to be a rousing success, the minister showed her disappointment to the band members by telling them that they could have used Bangar's name a few more times in their songs.
No Quarter
Once Bangar had made his ambitions to usher in a new age of charr supremacy known and formed the
Dominion
with the charr soldiers who agreed with him, Varinia joined her imperator's side to wage the
Icebrood War
. By 1333
AE
, the Minister of Morale had traveled to
Drizzlewood Coast
where the Dominion faced the
United Legions
in battle. Varinia used various methods, such as speakers and balloons spreading propaganda leaflets, to spread Bangar's message in order to make an increasing number of soldiers defect to the Dominion's side.
Varinia accompanied
Tribune
Ryland Steelcatcher
and
Centurion
Cinder Steeltemper
to a
parley
with the United Legions' leadership after the Dominion had taken over most of the territory in Drizzlewood Coast. During the talks she was quick to correct Ryland by stating that the Dominion forces followed Bangar, not him, while she also told everyone present that Ryland's accomplishments on the battlefield were only a taste of Bangar's reach and how the Blood Imperator would soon claim the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
for the Dominion.
After the parley had ended with neither side reaching common ground, Ryland's group returned north to continue the war effort against the United Legions. At some point, Varinia became corrupted by
dragon energy
and joined the ranks of the newly formed
Frost Legion
which consisted of Dominion charr who had been turned into
icebrood
. The transformed minister waited by the bridge at
Wolf's Crossing
to ambush an enemy party—consisting of
Crecia Stoneglow
,
Rytlock Brimstone
,
Smodur the Unflinching
, and the
Pact Commander
—that had been trying to apprehend Ryland who had fled after the
second attempt at a parley
between the two armies had ended disastrously.
Varinia proudly announced her presence to the surprised party and presented the mixed army of corrupted charr and icebrood as the Frost Legion. The minister also reminded her enemies that she had warned them during the first parley how Ryland's campaign had only been a taste of Bangar's power and how the charr had evolved with the Blood Imperator's leadership and vision. Facing the party in battle alongside other soldiers of the Frost Legion, Varinia fought with all her might but was ultimately felled after a fierce struggle. Despite her death, the rest of the Frost Legion managed to hold the bridge by using artillery strikes to force the Commander's party to retreat and thus prevented the party from pursuing Ryland deeper north for a time.

— Periodically generates a barrier that reflects missiles.
Icy Echoes
— When hit, causes the attacker to deal damage to allies in a radius around themselves. No attacker can cause this effect more than once every 2 seconds.
Lethal Coalescence
— Periodically targets a foe with a massive burst of frost magic. The damage done by this attack is distributed among all enemies caught in the blast.

Dialogue
Before progress in the
Journeykin Outpost Renown Mastery
Greetings. Vast Caldera here. Master hunter. We keep watch on the land, and we do not hunt recklessly. Watch what you kill around here.
Can I help in any way?
Been some unwanted creatures in the area, there has. You can sniff them out of hiding with your journeykin—warclaws I think you call them? You can also help our hunters on hunts or feed journeykin.
I'll take a look.
Can you give me a rundown on the area?
Vast Caldera:
Welcome to the journeykin outpost. Here we train our journeykin. The land has felt...disturbed of late. We would greatly value your help with the strange threats.
(only in the top right corner of the UI)
Vast Caldera:
The main outpost this is. We have supplies for hunting and the care of journeykin.
Vast Caldera:
Feeding the journeykin with our freshly caught prey would be helpful.
Vast Caldera:
The journeykin eat much, and so they produce much scat. Picking it up and taking it to compost would be much appreciated.
Vast Caldera:
Outside our main outpost there have been sightings of strange creatures not of this land. Please hunt them down for us if you're able. Concerning, they are.
Can you let me know how I'm doing? (Show Renown Mastery Achievement)
Afraid I can't right now.
Before completing the heart
I am happy to see you again, friend. You have been a great help to us.
You're welcome. Anything else you need?
(Same as "Can I help in any way?" above.)
Can you give me a rundown on the area?
(Same as before.)
Can you let me know how I'm doing? (Show Renown Mastery Achievement)
Can I buy supplies?
(only if previously completed the heart with the same character)
Afraid I can't right now.
After completing the heart
Proved yourself a real help today, you did. You've earned a rest, or some leisure time, perhaps.
Can I buy supplies?
Can you let me know how I'm doing? (Show Renown Mastery Achievement)
Thanks, I'll try.
After completing the heart
(
Journeykin Outpost Renown Mastery
tier 3)
Vast Caldera:
Looking rather comfortable together. If you didn't catch it earlier, she recognized you by scent when you arrived.
Vast Caldera:
First, they'll recognize their tamer by smell, their most refined sense. Then, by voice: tone, mannerisms, cadence.
Vast Caldera:
Given enough time, she'll recognize your footfalls long before you come into her sight.
Vast Caldera:
I'd suspect you're not far from witnessing such. Continue returning to her, and you'll see for yourself.
(
Journeykin Outpost Renown Mastery
tier 4)
Vast Caldera:
Obvious now, isn't it? How the time spent nurturing her has been repaid. First, by earning her trust. Then, her respect.
Vast Caldera:
And ultimately, her love. For love cannot stand without trust and respect as its foundation.
Vast Caldera:
You are now unique to each other. And upon one another, you may rely. That is the greatest gift this life can bestow...
Vast Caldera:
To shoulder no longer the burden of walking the world alone.

Dialogue
At Ruined Procession after event
Thank you for my freedom, mortal.
You're welcome.
At Ruined Procession upon completion of
Find the djinn vessel before the Brandstorm overwhelms the area
, after acquiring
Story of a Djinn
Thank you for freeing me. In time to protect these humans, no less. Now I go to taste freedom to the fullest. I doubt that we will meet again.
Vemyen, nemesis of
Ziya the Radiant
, can I ask a favor of you?
Where did you learn our names?
It's a long story. Someday Ziya will regret all the grief she gave you.
Is that so? I'm intrigued. Ask your favor.
These people are vulnerable while this Branding lasts. Can you stay here?
Djinn are free creatures by nature—free as fire, free as air. Though this ground may be sacred to humans and centaurs, I have no particular affection for it. I was, after all, trapped here for years.
I don't mean forever. Just until these storms have passed.
Why would I ever agree to that?
You love mortals. You collect their coins. And yarn, bad books, and so on.
You are oddly knowledgeable, for a mortal. All right, I agree to a brief sojourn.
Thank you.
What an interesting mystery.
Someday we'll all understand.

“
How delightful! A crowd. You will be first to watch my glorious transformation.
— Vevina
Vevina
, the Countess of Obedience, is one of the
Nightmare Courtiers
seeking
Faolain
's
Nightmare Tree
to fulfill her desire to be unquestionably adored and obeyed. Her pride even demanded the
Pale Tree
obey her. Instead, the Pale Tree humiliated her, and Vevina lost favor with her admirers. She left
the Grove
seeking the Nightmare Court's ability to twist the minds of others.

Trivia
When Vevina is absorbed by the Nightmare Tree, the room turns pink and purple.
Vevina kills Leurent in Fyonna's (removed) path, and is killed by Fyonna in Leurent's path.
Interestingly, it is possible for Vevina to occasionally survive her encounter with Fyonna, provided that the player and/or their party members interupt Fyonna and her spiders in a timely fashion. However she possesses no additional dialogue, and cannot be interacted with thereafter.
Vevina wields a
Verdant Staff
.
She wears
Snapdragon Epaulets
,
Nightmare Court Robes
,
Snapdragon Leggings
, and
Snapdragon Boots
.

Viirastra
is a mysterious
norn
in the
Shattered Observatory
. She and her
clones
can be found playing games together before she asks the player to "join in."
Location
The Mists
Fractals of the Mists
Shattered Observatory
Combat abilities
Abilities
Casts Spells
Plays Games
Behavior
Splits into 4 clones at 66% and 33% health. All clones are vulnerable only to
defiance bar
break.
Effects
(locked between 66%–33% and 33%–0% health until hit by a
Globolla Marble
)
Stun
— Vulnerable while broken.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Stun
(only at 66% and 33% health)
— Disabled when broken.  Occurs when defiance is broken.
Skills
Astral Surge
- Explodes with astral energy.
Globolla Marble
(when below 66% health)
- Globolla marbles refresh Nova Launch when they bounce on party members, but explode if they connect with the ground.
Mib Ring
- Damages foes who stand too close.
Obliterate
(Tier 3+ and immediately after each split phase)
- Downs any foe that does not dodge or block.
Taw Shot
- Shoots a damaging ball of astral energy.
Teleport Lunge
- Teleports to a new corner, creating astral explosions along the way.

Blinding Tuft
Quotes
More strange new friends. Wonderful!
Stay. Let's play a game! Let's entertain one another!
Two arms...two legs...a head...like that little one from earlier. He was much too serious. I was happy to see him go.
Watch this! You won't believe your eyes, truly!
Oh, I see this is much too easy for you. Let's make it more exciting. With marbles!
Such fun! It's fantastic, isn't it?
The game is almost over, but the best part is always the ending!
I know all the tricks, see?
You dropped it. Let's give it another try!
(laughs) What fun! I need a rest.
Here, I'll give you this orb. That impatient little man with the sharp teeth gave it to me--I want you to have it!
Come back soon, and we'll play another game together!

======================================================================
PAGE: Vlast
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Vlast
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
.
Vlast
Race
Dragon
Rank
Legendary
Locations
The Sacrifice
(
Tomb of the Primeval Kings
,
Salt Flats
,
Brightwater Inlet
,
The Sunderlands
,
Diviner's Reach
,
Stampede Uplands
,
Auburn Hills
,
Lifeblood Ravine
)
Bottle Your Emotions
(
College of Synergetics
,
Tarir, the Forgotten City
,
Brokentooth Maw
,
Salt Flats
,
Naksi Bay
)
“
I feel trapped in this role...in this "legacy." I yearn for freedom, but I cannot turn away from these responsibilities.
—
Vlast
Vlast
, given the name
Gleam
by the bards,
is
Glint
's first
scion
, and
Aurene
's older brother. Wishing to be freed of the burden of his mother's
legacy
yet understanding the importance of his role, he continues to fight against the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
's growing influence in the
Crystal Desert
no matter the cost.
Biography
Early years
Glint
's first
scion
was born inside the city of
Kesho
in the
Elon Riverlands
.
He would later appear in
Glint's Lair
where he was attacked by
Destroyers
in 1078
AE
and was defended by
humans
and the
Brotherhood of the Dragon
.
Some time after this, he returned to Kesho where he would be attended by
Forgotten
and, eventually,
Exalted
.
Bards called the first scion Gleam, although he chose the name Vlast for himself, which the people of the
Crystal Desert
eventually adopted.
In the following years stories about Vlast became legend, and Tyrians outside the desert who had heard bards tell legends of Gleam did not know if the scion was still alive or if he was even real and not just a fairytale.
In the decades that followed, Vlast was raised by
Sadizi
and other Exalted in the safety of Kesho. Due to them not knowing how to socialize him properly, he never bonded to the mortal races the same way as his mother had done.
Vlast grew more and more weary of the role Glint had placed on him but dutifully continued playing his part in her grand plan for the salvation of
Tyria
. However, he sometimes hoped to be freed of the burden of responsibility and roam free without worry.
After
Kralkatorrik
had slain Glint in a
battle
against
Destiny's Edge
in 1320
AE
, Vlast began exterminating the Elder Dragon's
Branded
minions, which had begun appearing in greater numbers in the Crystal Desert, and keeping them contained in the
Dragonbrand
. His actions in the
Desert Highlands
made him a hero in the eyes of the locals, and his true name became known throughout the Crystal Desert and passed on to legend in the southern regions. The skirmishes with veteran Branded wounded Vlast, however, leaving behind
slivers
which would later be used as ammunition for
makeshift weapons
so travelers and locals could continue the fight against the Branded in honor of Vlast.
Vlast sensed the
birth
of
Aurene
, Glint's second scion, in 1329
AE
and shared an empathic link with her. He was both happy and reserved about her existence, welcoming a sibling into the world but also hoping that her closer bond with mortals would allow her to become more successful and less jaded than he was. At this time he became aware of the
Pact Commander
whom Aurene had chosen as her
champion
.
Path of Fire
Vlast impaled by
Balthazar's Sword
.
Balthazar
arrived in the Desert Highlands in 1330
AE
with the intention of hunting down and capturing Vlast and the
Dragonsblood Spear
which he hoped to fell Kralkatorrik with to gain the Elder Dragon's power. While the spear was safely hidden in Glint's Lair, Vlast fought against the invading
Forged
army in
Salt Flats
, protecting the locals and refusing to let the former
God of War
succeed in his plans which could threaten the existence of Tyria. The scion was eventually forced to flee to the
Godfall Tower
where he faced both Balthazar and the
Herald of Balthazar
in combat.
The Pact Commander arrived on the scene to witness the end of the
battle
and fought against the Herald while Vlast defended himself against Balthazar who successfully bound the scion with a spell. After defeating the Herald, the Commander came to Vlast's aid and challenged the God of War, only to turn out to be severely outmatched.
As Balthazar prepared to deliver the killing blow on the incapacitated Commander, Vlast managed to break free from the spell while the God of War was distracted and dove to take the hit instead, sacrificing himself to save the Commander's life. The scion crystallized and exploded into fragments, making Balthazar vanish and leaving behind memory crystals which scattered throughout the highlands and which the Commander would later access to learn more about Vlast's past and what he had hoped to accomplish.
The Commander later
discovered
recordings of Vlast while exploring Kesho, learning about the scion's frustrations and diligence to carry on his mother's legacy to the very end.
Story involvement

The Sacrifice
Act 2

(voice only)

(as a vision)

trailer.

Related achievements

Vlast from the Past
—

(5
)
Notes
Lore discrepancy
:
The
Book of Vlast
by
Josso Essher
states that
"When the Branded came, [Vlast] was the first to throw himself into the fray. [...] The people of Elona told tales for generations, both of hope and of fear toward their 'protector.'"
The suggestion that Branded had existed in the desert for multiple generations (with the average period of a generation being about twenty-thirty years), instead of only about a decade, conflicts with most of the known lore, including
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire—Road to the Desert
which states that
"1320
AE:
An Elder Dragon Moves In. Kralkatorrik, the Crystal Elder Dragon, wakes from its slumber and heads directly for the desert region, leaving a corrupted swath of Branded landscape and creatures in its wake."
while the Path of Fire story takes place in 1330
AE
as per the story journal.
While a render by
Ronald Kury
on his ArtStation page shows Vlast having green scales, Vlast's actual GW2.dat model has golden scales.
Trivia
Vlast is
voiced
by
Tim Blaney
.
Gleam was always meant to be something that bards coined for the
scion
and that the name would not fit his personality. The name Vlast was chosen as the
dragon
's true name because it was sharper, more percussive, and closer to his mother's original name (Glaust) to highlight his wilder, rougher edged nature.
See also
The
Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Baby Dragon
.

Waiting Sorrow
Race

Elementalist

Organization
Wizard's Court
Lowland Kodan
Image(s)
Appearance with
Sorrow's Claws
.
Waiting Sorrow with the
Arcane Flow
aura.
“
For beings that know "all," I don't know enough. Not about myself.
—
Waiting Sorrow
Waiting Sorrow
is an ancient female
kodan
wizard. She is a former member of the
Wizard's Court
, and former leader of the
Bastion of the Celestial
,
having been
Dagda
's mentor and close friend. She left her fellow wizards relatively recently on somewhat precarious terms.
Before leaving, Waiting Sorrow crafted the
Heart of the Obscure
, a device that channels emotions and can open and seal rifts, and left it behind for another wizard to find. She was the mentor, as well as close friend or even soulmate, of the ancient female
jotun
Dagda
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Since leaving the Wizard's Court centuries ago, Waiting Sorrow has observed the lives of kodan to protect them from the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
. Adopting the identity of a healer named
Fading Aurora
, she has settled among the
Lowland Kodan
of
Harvest Den
and has earned a place in their council. Because of the rising
titan
activity emerging from
Janthir
that threatens not only the Lowland Kodan but
Tyria
itself, Waiting Sorrow is eventually forced to reveal her true identity and returns to the Wizard's Court, her future uncertain. After the titans are eliminated,
Poised Arrow
gives her the new name
Waiting Dawn
.
Biography
Early years
Life in the north
Waiting Sorrow was born to the
kodan
in the far north millennia ago. She was
magically
adept from an early age, receiving visions and images, and she would become the youngest
Voice of Koda
for her community in that era. As she acclimated to her role, she bonded with and eventually became the mate of
Southward Claw
, the
Claw of Koda
of that community.
When the
Elder Dragon
Jormag
rose and created a lengthy
Dragonstorm
which affected the denizens of the north, the various kodan tribes were forced to seek shelter from the raging blizzard in the caves together and grew increasingly hungry over the following years as they had access to fewer and fewer game to feed their people. As the storm picked up,
Koda
's voice turned angry and began affecting some of the Voices, Waiting Sorrow included. She began losing herself and could no longer trust the visions which she identified as false. She came to believe that she was not necessarily hearing Koda's voice anymore. She hated having a destiny and the burden that came with it, and she worried that she would become a threat to her community, so she fled before she would hurt her loved ones. Waiting Sorrow would have been consumed by the
Rage of Koda
in her growing madness and grief if not for her meeting with the
Seer
wizard
Isgarren
who helped sever her tie to
the Mists
to remove Koda's burden from her as he helped her
ascend
and turned her immortal.
However, the ascension ended up affecting Waiting Sorrow's memories too as she distanced herself from her former life and community, while Isgarren let her roam alone for a time. Southward Claw, against the wishes of the Voices of the other tribes, made the tough choice to lead his eventual
Lowland Kodan
tribe south to
Janthir
to seek new hunting grounds instead of waiting for his mate's return.
Waiting Sorrow eventually met with the
Forgotten
Vass
and began traveling with her near
Frostgorge Sound
where they observed the highland kodan settling the area as the Elder Dragons fell to slumber. At some point Isgarren, who had been banished from the Seer horde for interfering with mortal lives one time too many, approached the pair and invited them to join him and his
mursaat
friend
Mabon
in the
Wizard's Court
, a fledgling organization of ascended wizards whose task would be to guard
Tyria
from Mistborn threats. However, Isgarren's offer was both sympathetic and pragmatic: as he had witnessed Waiting Sorrow's growing arcane power, he realized that he could not let her go again.
Life as a wizard
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing lore.
As a wizard, Waiting Sorrow helped design the defenses of
the Wizard's Tower
which Isgarren had acquired. According to her, the sentient tower always liked her best out of all the wizards. She founded the
Bastion of the Celestial
in
Amnytas
, a floating chain of islands acting as the base for the wizards as well as their eventual
Astral Ward
disciples. She became a mentor and dear friend to
Dagda
, a female
jotun
with magical abilities who was saved during the jotun civil war and who underwent ascension to become a wizard herself. Waiting Sorrow and Dagda used the
Cosmic Observatory
fractal
as a research facility to study the artificial fractals created by the wizards.
Waiting Sorrow also created the
Primal Maguuma
fractal at some point but did not reveal what purpose it was for.
Some time after the Astral Ward's founding in 604
AE
, the
demonic
Kryptis
of
Nayos
possessed the first
Warden of the Wizard's Court
,
Taryn
.
This ultimately led
Eparch
, the Midnight King of the Kryptis who had once been Isgarren's ward before his banishment, to usher the first major Kryptis invasion attempt into Tyria circa 635
AE
so he could have his revenge on Isgarren for denying him a seat in the Wizard's Court.
The Kryptis horde
targeted
the Wizard's Tower with Eparch himself leading the incursion alongside his powerful lieutenants
Cerus
and
Deimos
. The assault led to the deaths of the wizards
Akeem
and Vass and culminated in Isgarren and Eparch's second confrontation. Waiting Sorrow was so traumatized by the Kryptis' actions and the deaths of her fellow wizards that she could neither speak nor move, leaving the rest of the wizards to carry on the fight without her. Isgarren and his allies were ultimately able to force the Midnight King and the Kryptis back into Nayos despite the tower sustaining heavy damage in the ensuing clash.
When interviewed for the multi-volume
History of the Wizard's Court
and asked to tell the interviewer about Akeem and Vass in 716
AE
, painful memories of her friends' demise surfaced in Waiting Sorrow's mind, and she left the interviewer's company after uttering how it would have been nice to have known the two late wizards better.
As time passed, Waiting Sorrow began to harbour reservations against the wizards, as a desire to know more about herself and her past grew and haunted her. Upon voicing her reservations to Isgarren, the
Seer
asked the kodan if she would like to put an end to her immortal life, to which Waiting Sorrow declined, refusing to believe that there was not a path forward that did not involve moving to the next wave of existence. This conversation would only further the kodan's desire to depart from the order.
Waiting Sorrow crafted the
Heart of the Obscure
, a mysterious device capable of opening and sealing rifts in
the Mists
by channelling emotions, as well as pointing the user to the nearest rift, deciphering mursaat language, neutralizing
bloodstone
shards, exorcising
ghosts
, and channeling touchstones into souls among other things. She left this device on her desk in
The Wizard's Tower
.
Waiting Sorrow, still a wizard, finally abandoned the order in secrecy some time around
the Searing
of
Ascalon
.
She left behind a
letter
for her close friend and apprentice,
Dagda
, to find. The kodan wizard left in a hurry, fearing that Isgarren would stop her if he knew, as the immortal wizards are forbidden from leaving the order with their powers intact.
Later on some Astral Ward members would find the exact date of Sorrow's departure vague, referencing that it had happened "over a century ago" in the present day.
Interim years
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing lore.
In 1165
AE
, Jormag rose once somewhere beyond the
Far Shiverpeaks
in the latest dragonrise, creating a years-long
Dragonstorm
as they began creating
icebrood
and pushing south. Waiting Sorrow, plagued by fragmented visions of her past life and guilt for abandoning her bearkin, decided to aid them while also masking her magic so Isgarren or her fellow wizards could not locate her.
Janthir Wilds
Act 1
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Primary article:
Fading Aurora
Act 2
After Poised Arrow was injured during the initial fight with the titan Decima, he called Waiting Sorrow for help.  She created a portal to bring the party to the Commander's homestead, and used her healing magic to repair the damage to Poised Arrow's leg.  While there was still some tension between mentor and student, the two were able to talk things out.
Waiting Sorrow and Poky stayed at the homestead while the Commander worked with researchers in
Janthir Syntri
.  But once Malice and Caithe learned that bloodstone might be linked to the titans somehow, Sorrow recruited some other kodan to stay with Poky and joined the group at the Bloodstone Research Camp.  She believed the Heart of the Obscure should be able to neutralize bloodstone shards, which would keep the titans from drawing power.
The neutralization worked, though not exactly safely. Sorrow needed to shield the party each time a shard was neutralized.  The team returned to the homestead for final planning, but not before the Commander told Sorrow they had deduced the wizard's true identity as the
Voice
of legend.  Sorrow begged them to keep that information secret.
When Stoic Alder arrived at the homestead to join in the battle, Sorrow confessed in part to him -- not that she was the ancient Voice, but that she was a wizard.  He agreed to postpone further discussion until after the titans were dealt with.
Greer and Decima would have fallen had Ura not interfered at the last minute.  Sorrow was one of the team members who voted to pull back until more could be learned of Ura and her powers.
After a short discussion in Harvest Den, Waiting Sorrow asked the Commander to back her up for another discussion Sorrow wasn't looking forward to -- a discussion with
Isgarren
.  The two portaled back to the
Wizard's Tower
, easily bypassing the Tower's wards since Sorrow was the one who set them up centuries before.
Sorrow sent the Commander in first to report on the titan battle, then walked in during that report, revealing that she still lived.

Trivia
Waiting Sorrow is
voiced
by
Tina Huang
.
As a millennia-old kodan, Waiting Sorrow was born during a time when most kodan had two names, a tradition still in use by the kodan of
Still Waters Speaking
. Their first name, referred to as "
Koda
's Promise" is based on a characteristic wished by the family or tribe of the newborn kodan cub, such as "humble", "stoic", "aiding", or "noble". The second name, referred to as "Koda's Gift", is one of nature, such as "mountain", "thunder", "cloud", or "cove". A handful of
sanctuaries
, however, used pliable names which changed based on circumstance and mood, describing singular things and lacking a first or last name. These sanctuaries would eventually become established in the
Shiverpeaks
possibly following
Jormag
's awakening.
Waiting Sorrow, based on her name, might have belonged to one such sanctuary.

summons deadly ice tornadoes.

- The
Whisper of Jormag
targets a foe with a massive burst of frost magic. The damage done by this attack is distributed among all enemies caught in the blast.
Slithering Rime
- The Whisper of Jormag rapidly spins, launching multiple icy explosions around itself and radiating out multiple orbs of spectral ice in straight lines.
Spreading Ice
- The
Whisper of Jormag
targets all enemies with deadly ice shards. While a target may evade their own shards, they may not evade others' shards.
Vicious Slam
- Inflict
Knockback
to nearby players and slam your staff, launching all players into the air and spawning an icy spike underneath their original positions after a delay.

Wrathbringer
Race

The
Wrathbringer
is a powerful
Branded
minion of the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
that has been sent to subdue the
Thunderhead Keep
. Like the
Branded Riftstalkers
, it is capable of keeping open
rifts
created by Kralkatorrik from
the Mists
. Unlike most Branded, the Wrathbringer was not a corrupted creature, but instead a hand-forged lieutenant by Kralkatorrik to replace
The Shatterer
.
It resembles a
Bristleback
with the tail of an
Arrowhead
.
After its death, it is reanimated into the
Death-Branded Wrathbringer
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Xera
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Xera
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Stronghold of the Faithful
and
Living World Season 3
.
Xera
Race
Human
Profession
Mesmer
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
Stronghold of the Faithful
(
Forsaken Thicket
)
Organization
White Mantle
Image(s)
Xera's illusionary form.
“
For hundreds of years we have hidden in plain sight, living in your cities, manipulating your politics. Soldiers, farmers, scholars—all swearing allegiance to the cause...all waiting for the moment to strike. Our time is now. We rise from the shadows to claim what is ours!
—
Xera
Xera
is the High Inquisitor of the
White Mantle
. She serves as the final boss of the
Stronghold of the Faithful
raid wing.
Biography
Early years
Xera was a faithful servant of the
White Mantle
cult, loyal to the
mursaat
or Unseen Ones, and whose main goal was to resurrect the last of them,
Lazarus the Dire
. She eventually learned what had really happened to the last mursaat. Lazarus had been betrayed, his power turned against him and, to survive, he had to split his soul into five
aspects
. Each of those aspects had been hidden and scattered across the
Maguuma Jungle
and sites sacred to the White Mantle.
While Xera went to search for these aspects,
Caudecus Beetlestone
,
Legate Minister
, worked to undermine
Queen Jennah
's leadership of Kryta and secretly orchestrated centaur and bandit attacks, rising as a political rival to Jennah. In Xera's absence,
Confessor Esthel
, supreme leader of the White Mantle, named Caudecus her second-in-command for discrediting Jennah.
High Inquisitor Xera was dedicated to her task, and purged nonbelievers among the White Mantle, many of whom worked under Justiciar Caudecus. Xera's investigations prompted her to write to Caudecus to express her concern over how many of the Justiciar's followers were straying from the goal of serving the Unseen Ones.
In 1325
AE
, the
Shining Blade
and the
Hero of Shaemoor
raided a White Mantle hideout in Kryta
and killed Confessor Esthel. Caudecus succeeded her, becoming Confessor and leader of the White Mantle, angering Xera.
. While Xera was loyal to the original goal of the White Mantle, Confessor Caudecus was more focused on the Krytan throne.
The White Mantle further splintered into those loyal to High Inquisitor Xera, who wanted to serve the mursaat and were focused on Operation Rebirth to restore Lazarus; and those loyal to Confessor Caudecus, who wanted the White Mantle to rule Kryta. Once the five aspects of Lazarus were found, Xera,
Matthias Gabrel
, and other like-minded zealots decided to continue working on Operation Rebirth as their primary objective behind Caudecus's back, as they relocated to the
Stronghold of the Faithful
in the
Forsaken Thicket
.
Stronghold of the Faithful
By 1329
AE
,
Scholar Glenna
and a group of raiders assisting the
Pact
entered Forsaken Thicket while investigating strange disappearances in the region and discovered a heavy White Mantle presence. They fought their way past sentries to the Stronghold of the Faithful. Xera's attempts to stop them by sending both the cultists and her creation, the
Keep Construct
, to fight them proved to be futile. She even accidentally slipped up during her gloating by revealing the White Mantle's plans to revive Lazarus, which clued the raiders in on what she and the cultists were really up to.
As the raiders fought the Keep Construct, Xera realized that killing them was not working, and proceeded to stall them. She drew power from nearby
Bloodstone
shards to enhance her
mesmer
magic
and, when the Keep Construct was defeated, she used her power to amplify the resulting explosion to destroy the stronghold. She used her magic to levitate fragments of the stronghold in the air to create the Twisted Castle, a labyrinth. The raiders had to fight their way through the maze that defied reality, teleporting from platform to platform, while Xera's magic twisted their minds.
When the raiders managed to navigate through the twisted castle, Xera finally confronted them herself and fought them as a last resort to buy more time for Lazarus to be fully revived. Although she perished in battle, the High Inquisitor had ultimately succeeded in stalling the raiders long enough so that they did not find any mursaat in the revival chamber by the time they got there.
However, unbeknownst to Xera and the raiders at the time, before Xera had left for the Stronghold of the Faithful,
one of Caudecus' lackeys
had secretly swapped one of the artifacts imbued with Lazarus's aspects with a fake to ensure that the ritual to revive the mursaat would fail even if Xera could carry it out.
Without this artifact, the real Lazarus could have never been resurrected. Despite Xera's fall, her ominous words about an uprising ultimately came to pass once Caudecus led the White Mantle assault in the
Siege of Divinity's Reach
in 1330
AE
.
Location
Heart of Maguuma
Forsaken Thicket
Stronghold of the Faithful
Twisted Castle
Walkthrough

mastery—may help.

======================================================================
PAGE: Yagon
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Yagon
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Yagon
Race
Ghost
Profession
Mesmer
Rank
Champion
Legendary
Location
Seer's March
(
Mistburned Barrens
)
Organization
Bloodstone ghosts
Image(s)
Yagon using the
shadowcraft
skill.
“
You can banish me, but not my gift. My gift to this world is eternal. When all the world is quiet, the choir will continue its everlasting lament.
—
Yagon
Yagon
is a
mursaat
ghost
. In life, she was
Isgarren
's rival and the creator of
Spectral Agony
that caused the
Seers
' downfall. She was responsible for the death of
Sidony
, the leader of the Seers, and was herself felled by
Isgarren
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Yagon has managed to survive her fall due to the effects of the
bloodstone
and continues haunting the
Mistburned Barrens
which still suffer the lingering effects of Spectral Agony. Upon sensing Isgarren's return to the region, she arrives to enact her greater plans to spread the agony farther and make the last Seer, her nemesis, suffer. Thanks to the
Pact Commander
's use of the
Heart of the Obscure
, however, Yagon is finally
slain
for good although not before taunting the party with the unknown dangers lurking deep inside the fallen mursaat city of
Bava Nisos
.
Biography
Early years
Yagon was a gifted
mursaat
researcher and mage who participated in the millennia-spanning
Seer-mursaat war
. According to the Seer
Isgarren
, Yagon was vile even by mursaat standards. She had a mate, Arch Steward
Celsa
.
The mursaat witch successfully assassinated the Seer leader
Sidony
while luring the attacking Seers into a trap in the
Valley of Agony
in
Mistburned Barrens
where she unleashed her greatest invention,
Spectral Agony
which she considered her gift to
Tyria
, on them. This assault effectively ended the war in the mursaat's favor as the few remaining Seer survivors could not challenge the might of the entire mursaat army directly afterwards.
The mursaat city of
Bava Nisos
was built near the site of Yagon's triumph, while the valley remained steeped in endless agony of the tormented Seer ghosts for millennia as Yagon wanted them to suffer for eternity.
Isgarren, despite having been exiled by Sidony before the fateful attack, still chose to avenge his kin's fate. He gained passage into Bava Nisos undetected and killed Yagon in broad daylight in revenge. Yagon was among her peers and family, and the assassination was executed at the height of preparations for the Ceremony of the Rabbit's Moon, catching the mursaat unaware.
A Ceremony of Passing was held by Yagon's mate Celsa in the late mursaat mage's memory two days later at
the Golden Lake
, and all mursaat were forbidden to speak for the duration of the following sunrise until sundown.
Celsa issued an order to bring Isgarren back alive if possible as mursaat tradition dictated that the final life-breath of the enemy should be gifted to the mate of the victim.
However, Isgarren remained elusive, and Celsa could not avenge Yagon before most of the mursaat fell to the
titans
and the
Chosen
heroes
centuries later.
Janthir Wilds
Unbeknownst to Isgarren, Yagon had managed to linger on as a spirit tied to the
bloodstones
which the mursaat had experimented on. Some time after the
fall
of Isgarren's mursaat ally
Mabon
, the wards Mabon had placed around the isle containing Bava Nisos and Mistburned Barrens fell, allowing interlopers to enter the desecrated grounds after centuries of isolation. Mabon's lingering
memory
also sought out the
Janthir
region in
wisp
form, eventually guiding the
Pact Commander
and their allies, including Isgarren, to retrace the path he had once taken. This path led to the party
confronting
Yagon who, unlike many mursaat bloodstone ghosts, was not oblivious to her surroundings but very aware of everything happening around her. She sent mursaat ghosts to swarm the party and also taunted Isgarren by revealing how ruthlessly the Seer had murdered her in front of her family as she attempted to sow seeds of discord among Isgarren's allies. The party was able to thwart the ghost onslaught while Isgarren fought Yagon on the cliffs before the mursaat witch escaped.
After surviving the ambush, the Commander continued exploring the barrens and followed the vision of Mabon around while neutralizing the smaller bloodstones along the way. During this time, the Commander also aided the
Astral Ward
team headed by the
djinn
Alya
and the
sylvari
Head Researcher Rhianwyn
in exploring the Valley of Agony and trying to neutralize the Spectral Agony there. However, the agony was resistant to these attempts even if it could be dissipated for a while. During one of these excursions the explorers came across Yagon herself as she
possessed a jade construct
in an attempt to slay the party and add them to her chorus of torment. The Ward ultimately managed to banish Yagon although it would only give them a temporary reprieve as the mursaat witch would eventually return to continue her experiments.
Following Mabon's trace led the Commander and the rest of their party—including Isgarren, and the
kodan
pair
Poised Arrow
and
Waiting Sorrow
—to the very gates of Bava Nisos itself. Yagon interrupted the reunion as she returned to taunt Isgarren; tensions between her and the Seer came to a head when she accused the late Mabon of cowardice upon his
demise
which had occurred a year earlier. She once again showed her awareness of her surroundings as she disparagingly called out each member of Isgarren's party in turn, even recognizing the Commander as a
champion
of an
Elder Dragon
. Infuriated by her callous words, the party fought the wicked remnant, while Isgarren made sure to point out that he would make sure that the world remembered Yagon as a vile coward. After Yagon had been sufficiently weakened, the Commander used Waiting Sorrow's invention, the
Heart of the Obscure
, to seal away what remained of the mursaat witch. As Yagon fell, she taunted the party by teasing the existence of
unknown dangers
still lurking deep inside Bava Nisos.
Location
Janthir
Mistburned Barrens
Seer's March
(only during related events)
Story involvement
Janthir Wilds story
Repentance
Into the Valley of Shadow
Penance for the Past
Event involvement

(80)

.

from Tyria before she completes her foul ritual.

)
Trivia

voiced

.
Yagon is the first explicitly confirmed female
mursaat
.
"Yagón" (יָגוֹן)
means 'agony, grief, sorrow' in
Hebrew
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Yao
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Yao
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
and
Guild Wars 2: Secrets of the Obscure
.
In this
Canthan
name, the
family name
is Zhou.
Yao
Race
Human
Profession
Mechanist
Level
80
Rank
Veteran
Legendary
Location
North Lab
(
New Kaineng City
)
Organization
Xunlai Jade
Image(s)
Hologram of Yao during The Future in Jade.
Interactive map
“
Mizuki
. I guess I jumped the gun on my last introduction... I'm Yao—your local, mostly friendly agender engineer.
— Yao after
Destroy the corruption before the system overloads!
Zhou Yao
is an
agender
engineer
with a cynical outlook on life.
They
are a former member of the
Jade Brotherhood
.
Yao is fond of
cats
and holodramas.
They highly value smarts, openness and drive as traits, and are deeply loyal to those who have helped them.
Biography
Early years
Zhou Yao was affiliated with the
Jade Brotherhood
in their adolescence, having been found and taken in by
Park Chul-Moo
after their run-in with local ruffians. However, they eventually parted with the organization for the opportunity to work with Joon, souring their relations with the Brotherhood's leader, and began employment with
Xunlai Jade
in 1843
CC
(1333
AE
). When
Renho
informed Chul-Moo about Yao's defection from the Brotherhood and asked for permission to eliminate them, Chul-Moo issued strict commands that Yao should only be monitored and left unharmed or "the person responsible will see it returned—threefold."
Despite leaving the Jade Brotherhood after an argument with Chul-Moo,
Yao continued to have friendly relations with other Brotherhood members, admitting that the organization had been a family for them before they met Joon.
Yao was interested in
dragonjade
use in medical technology, and expressed an interest in becoming project leader of the MedTech department in Xunlai Jade. While lacking formal education, Yao proved themself to be a gifted
engineer
by designing their own prosthetic arm. They were also defensive about their history with the Jade Brotherhood and their apprenticeship with Xunlai Jade founder
Yu Joon
, identifying themself as her 'protégé' and a 'vital asset' to Xunlai Jade's operations. This created conflicts with Joon's other protégé
Mai Trin
, who considered Yao to be "possessive" of Joon's attention. Yao also established friendships with fellow jade tech engineers
Nozomi
, another ex-Brotherhood member,
and
Elora
, the latter of whom Yao worked with and took under their wing.
End of Dragons
Act 2
Prior to meeting members of the
Tyrian
Dragon's Watch
in 1845
CC
(1335
AE
), Yao had been attending to reports of Jade mechs going haywire across
New Kaineng City
. They became acquainted with the
asura
Gorrik
through Joon's introduction, who also updated them on the situation involving Dragon's Watch. Later, Yao attended to distressed yelling from citizens at the harbors of New Kaineng City and came across the
Pact Commander
,
Marjory Delaqua
, and Mai Trin
subduing
a rogue Jade Tech Loader. Yao's deployment of assistant Jade Spiders to analyze the rogue mech went awry when they became corrupted as well, forcing the party to destroy all three jade mechs.
Following the battle, Yao thanked the three and freely offered their
Jade Bot
to the Commander after they had expressed interest. Yao directed the Commander to
Lead Plant Engineer Hu
and the Jade Bot service stations for security and safety clearances. Though unable to meet the Commander in person, Yao
instructed
them through a Jade Bot hologram to approach Elora for further assistance.
Later on, Yao became instrumental in the recovery of New Kaineng City's power plant when an
unknown corruption
in the jade tech threatened its systems. They collaborated with
Officer Mizuki
and adventurers in
stopping the rogue mechs' rampage
. Although the two had been suspicious of one another at first, Yao and Mizuki befriended one another during the conflict, and Yao offered to help Mizuki with future problems if any arose.
Act 5
During the battle at
the Jade Sea
, Yao was called in by Joon for the delivery and assembling of extractor parts, though they did not partake in the ultimate confrontation with the maddened
Elder Dragon
Soo-Won
due to their understandable inexperience with world-ending catastrophes. Yao was, however, present at the
post-battle assembly
at
Arborstone
, where they indicated great enthusiasm in assisting Joon's research for jade energy solutions, travelling to
Tyria
, as well as helping Dragon's Watch in future endeavors.
What Lies Beneath
Months after the conclusion of the battle against Soo-Won, Yao received word of the Jade Brotherhood discovering unusually powerful jade in
Gyala Delve
—alongside concerning reports of missing members and aggressive wildlife. They hastily left their post and duties at Xunlai Jade to Joon's great dismay, and upon their arrival in the jade mining facility, they were attacked by their rogue jade mech
Finn
. The commotion led the Commander to their aid and, unfazed by the attack and chalking it up to Chul-Moo's "management style", Yao reprogrammed the mech's learned behaviours.
Yao soon reunited with Chul-Moo when the latter was attacked by a crazed
Jin-Lee
. Forced to subdue their old colleague, they had a brief dispute with Chul-Moo over his hesitancy to take responsibility over the disturbing situation and left with Finn to investigate more of the mines shortly after.
Reconvening with the Commander, Rama and Gorrik at
Luxon Terminus Fort
, Yao came to Chul-Moo's defense whenever Rama questioned his leadership, creating some contention between the two. The tension was quickly resolved when the team encountered the apparent source of the
haze
—an
oni
called the
Ravenous Wanderer
—and attempted to fend it off, saving Rama in the process by having Finn shield him from an attack.
The team was ultimately forced to retreat when the oni began regenerating itself and the Commander blacked out. Yao waited together with their allies as the Commander regained consciousness, after which they were reprimanded by Joon and separately invited to help Gorrik with his plan to subjugate the oni.
Deep Research
After the run-in with the oni, Yao remained in Gyala Delve. Here, they frequented Gorrik's temporary lab and twice interrupted his
voice recordings
. They at first attempted to derail his case notes with a news broadcast impression to "lighten the mood." The second time around, they offered insight into Chul-Moo's psyche, briefly expressing that he was "torn up" about his crew before they were dismissed by Gorrik. Gorrik also mentions that Yao comforted him after his hallucination of
Blish
.
What Lies Within
Within weeks, Yao, Gorrik, and Taimi presented the Recollector to the Commander. After a brief sojourn, Yao
met
the Commander at the entrance of the Yong Reactor, where they offered a listening ear to the conflicted hero about Mai Trin's sacrifice. They ended up joining the Commander when going down into the mines to lure the Ravenous Wanderer away from the ley line. After some distressed calls from Chul-Moo, memories from the ley-powered jade from both Yao and Chul-Moo offered some insight into Yao's past with the Jade Brotherhood. After the oni was
defeated
for good, Yao was one of the options for the Commander's
dinner date
, where they admitted that they had never been on a date before, even platonic ones.
End of Dragons
By 1336
AE
, Taimi realized that communications had become critically important to solving Cantha's power problems. She, Joon, and Yao collaborated to create an upgrade to Taimi's communicator system to make remote talk easier. The upgraded service chip was then shipped to their various allies, including the Commander, as the update could not be patched remotely. After
applying the upgrade
, the Commander tried calling various allies and friends, including Yao. However, Yao had difficulties responding before the connection cut off, and the Commander assumed that the engineer must have been down in the jade mines again.

Trivia
Yao is
voiced
by
Ry Chase
.
Yao's visual appearance has been confirmed by the official
Guild Wars 2
twitter account.
As Yao is agender (
non-binary
), they are referred to with "they/them" pronouns.
窑
(
Pinyin
:
Yáo
) means "kiln", a reference to the
engineer
profession.
瑶
(
Pinyin
:
Yáo
) also means "precious jade".
Yao considers the Commander to be "cute"
and "a keeper".
Yao is usually seen wielding a rifle with the
Jade Tech skin
. However, during the
New Kaineng meta
they can be occasionally seen wielding a sword with the
Dragon's Jade Lacerator Skin
and a warhorn with the
Dragon's Jade Harbinger Skin
, despite engineers not being able to use warhorns. Yao also uses a
Jade Tech Pistol
in the End of Dragons Launch Trailer.
In the End of Dragon's launch trailer, Yao uses a
Jade Mech CJ-1
to show off the
Mechanist
proffession mechanic. Yao never uses this mechanic in-game, but they did build
their own mech.
In New Kaineng, Yao is ranked as a veteran. In Gayla Delve and instances in the final act of End of Dragons, they are ranked Legendary.
It's implied that Yao doesn't have or know any blood family, as Chul Moo asks a young Yao why their parents let them stay out so late
and offers them a place to stay.
Judging by the fact Yao ended up joining the brotherhood when they were young, it's likely they took this offer.

Notes
Zane has 2323
armor
(1100 toughness and 1223 defense). Compared to regular
Raid
bosses with 2597
armor
, this translates to +12%
power
damage
.
Zane has a 80 range diameter
hitbox
, wich is considered small.
Zane's
Unrelenting Assault
attack will always target the player furthest away from him (within ~2000 units), keep this in mind while trying to manage his agro.
Zane's
Hail of Bullets
will deal devastating damage to the cage if the attack cleaves it, pay close attention to never stand between Zane and the cage in order to prevent this from happening.
He wields two pistols resembling the
Pact Fleet Pistol
.
His movements resemble that of a
Revenant
and a
Thief
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Zohaqan
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Zohaqan
======================================================================

Zohaqan
Race
Djinn
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Location
The Ruined Paths
(
Sandswept Isles
)
Image(s)
Click to enlarge.
“
Heartless fools! You will pay in blood!
—
Zohaqan
Zohaqan
is a powerful water
djinn
, capable of commanding the elements of water, air, and earth. His power to summon powerful storms have earned him the moniker, The Stormcaller. He lives deep within the
Cove of Zohaqan
on
Sandswept Isles
.
Biography
Zohaqan's full history is unknown. In recent years he had fallen in love with a
human
man, Nakis, in the
Crystal Desert
—possibly near or from
Whispering Grottos
as a letter from Zohaqan to Nakis was left there.
Together the two fled to
Sandswept Isles
to live in peace. During their time together they had etched their silhouettes into
a tree
from where they watched the sunrises together. However, the
Inquest
captured and killed Nakis, sending Zohaqan into a blinding rage.
By 1331
AE
, Zohaqan had come to despise all mortals alike, believing them all to be treacherous beings. The
djinn
constantly built storms around the isles to kill all inhabitants.
Olmakhan
elders
Rhona
and
Yowen
led an
expedition
to Zohaqan's
cove
where they confronted the djinn with intentions to stop him and find out what had invoked his rage. Zohaqan was not willing to stop what he had begun, however, and he viewed the elders' demands as disrespectful. The enraged djinn
attacked
the elders' party but was defeated after a long clash. Although Rhona was hopeful that the djinn was gone for good, Yowen was less certain as she believed Zohaqan would be powerful enough to return to spread his rage throughout the isles. Adventurers would eventually learn the reason for the djinn's rampage but Zohaqan would refuse to listen to reason and stubbornly continued his campaign every time he returned to his cove.
Location
Crystal Desert
Sandswept Isles
The Ruined Paths
Cove of Zohaqan
(only during related events)

- Points at an area, then unleashes high-damage projectiles towards the ground in a triangular pattern in that area. Projectiles become symbols, and explode.
Ice Storm
- Moves both hands towards the air, then lightning strikes the ground at random areas.
Water Wall
- Blue circles appear around Zohaqan, applying
Chilled
and
Slow
. Immediately after they dissipate, an explosion occurs at the original location of the "wall".
